title: Extension: opening a story shows a BLACK page + polluted URL (#commentsContainer&site-content) — sidecar/overlay not loading
description: USER-REPORTED (2026-06-25): in extension web mode, opening a test/bookmark goes to a BLACK page; the URL bar shows a polluted fragment e.g. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/22/us/politics/jd-vance-iran-negotiations.html#commentsContainer&site-content . Two coupled symptoms: (1) URL pollution — #commentsContainer is STILL appearing despite the ic98 fix (4a929a9e) that was supposed to bound the hydrate sweep to the article; PLUS a new '&site-content' joined onto it (site-content is NYT's own <div id=site-content> wrapper / skip-link target — captureDom() at content.js:498 bakes location.href incl. hash into the stored page URL). (2) The app overlay/sidecar 'no longer loads at all' — user sees BLACK (the boot-cover backdrop never tears down because first content never paints), not the app-primary full overlay. Likely entangled with recent xink.6 cached-paint decoupling (9e105c49/06055e0b) and/or mopz.4 boot-cover teardown. BLOCKS the user from verifying anything else (incl. the blg5.19 CDP question). Investigate: where the residual #commentsContainer + &site-content come from; why the overlay paints black; reproduce headfully with a NYT-shaped fixture (skip-link #site-content + comments sibling + a tab driven to a hash-polluted URL) and assert a CLEAN url + a non-black painted overlay.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND + URL-POLLUTION HALF FIXED & VERIFIED (RED->GREEN headful, 2026-06-25). The residual '&site-content' is the tell: NYT wraps the article in <div id=site-content> and its scrollspy stamps location.href with #site-content the moment the main content is in view (on first paint AND as our capture sweep scrolls the article). ic98 correctly bounded the sweep so it never reaches the comments (#commentsContainer), but scrolling the ARTICLE still triggers the main-content spy, and ic98 restored only scroll position after the sweep, NEVER the hash (its own commit says so). So the URL was left polluted. The backend ALWAYS drives a fragment-stripped URL (StripUrlFragment), so any fragment at capture time is page-JS scroll noise, not user intent. FIX (extension/content.js captureDomHydratedInner): snapshot cleanHref=location.href.split('#')[0] before the sweep; after the scroll-restore, history.replaceState back to cleanHref. captureDom() reads location.href just after, so the CAPTURED page identity (cache key / bookmark / reader) is cleaned too — which breaks the vicious cycle behind the BLACK PAGE: a polluted #commentsContainer bookmark/cache was re-opened and drove the tab into NYT's comments/subscription region. REPRO: added a one-time #site-content scrollspy to the article-comments.html fixture (Program.cs) + a validity assertion to the headful 'fragbound' gate. RED on pre-fix (url=...#site-content, spy fired), GREEN after (url clean, spy still fired). linksfirst (reader no-flash) still GREEN — no capture regression. REMAINING / cannot self-verify: the literal NYT 'black page' needs the user's logged-in NYT session to reproduce end-to-end (per Verification Doctrine I will not claim it verified). The URL-pollution root that feeds it is fixed and gated. Keep OPEN pending the user's NYT re-test of the black-page symptom.
close_reason: FIXED + verified end-to-end on REAL nytimes.com with the user's logged-in account, on the user's EXACT reported URL (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/22/us/politics/jd-vance-iran-negotiations.html). RED->GREEN (capture sweep confirmed running, 137 paragraphs hydrated, in both): RED with the k985 hash-restore disabled -> live tab URL = '...jd-vance-iran-negotiations.html#commentsContainer' (the exact scrollspy pollution reported); GREEN with the fix -> URL stays CLEAN (no #commentsContainer / #site-content / &site-content), tab is NOT a chrome-error 'black' page, and the overlay reader renders the real article. Logged-in run (NYT subscriber session via tools/nyt-login) on the same URL: URL clean, reader rendered 4148 chars; SCREENSHOT /tmp/verify-ext/fragbound.png READ — shows the real article prose ('As Vice President JD Vance entered the fifth hour of negotiations...'), no black page, no regwall. Root cause: the page's own scrollspy stamps location.href with #commentsContainer/#site-content as the capture sweep scrolls the article; ic98 bounded the sweep but never restored the hash. Fix (extension/content.js captureDomHydratedInner): snapshot a fragment-free href before the sweep, history.replaceState back to it after — cleans both the live address bar AND the captured identity (cache key/bookmark/reader), breaking the polluted-bookmark->black-page cycle. Gate: VERIFY_FRAGBOUND_TARGET on the fragbound mode (real-site branch). Also deterministically RED->GREEN on the local #site-content scrollspy fixture. NOTE: the reader showed 'By Joe Rayment' as the byline on the logged-in page (account-chrome pickup) — cosmetic, unrelated to k985.

title: Lock the multi-article gate GREEN end-to-end and add it to the DEFAULT verify-ext run
description: Finalize the multiload mode as a first-class DEFAULT phase in scripts/verify-extension.sh (alongside section/reader/navigate/linkenter/hijack) so the multi-article transition is permanently covered and cannot silently regress. Tighten every assertion to the USER-VISIBLE OUTCOME: reader-rows word-overlap A vs B; top-level tab URL==B; the Shift+R loop-liveness + fresh-nonce reload; the lazyload below-the-fold marker on the cross-document path; the cached-revisit full body. Add a screenshot whose CONTENT is asserted. Write the close note with the exact actions (load A, Back, Enter uncached B, Shift+R, revisit cached B) and observed outcomes. This bead makes 'green gate == done' true for this path.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/verify-extension.sh headful under Xvfb passes ALL phases incl. the new default multiload phase: A opens+reads A; Back restores A's section; Enter UNCACHED B reads B (overlap(B)>overlap(A), tab URL==B); Shift+R re-renders B without freezing (fresh nonce); the lazyload article shows below-the-fold prose; cached-revisit B shows the full body. dotnet build --no-incremental clean (0 new first-party warnings). Phase is in the default loop. Close note states actions + observed outcomes verbatim.
close_reason: DONE + VERIFIED. multiload is now a first-class DEFAULT phase in scripts/verify-extension.sh (section + reader + navigate + linkenter + hijack + multiload). The phase drives the full journey in ONE session and asserts USER-VISIBLE OUTCOMES (no logs): A opens+reads A (tab on A); Back restores the section; Enter UNCACHED B reads B (overlap vs list 1.00->0.36, tab URL==B); Shift+R re-renders B without freezing (fresh per-load nonce 1->2); the >40-anchor lazyload article shows the below-the-fold marker; CACHED revisit of B shows the full body. Screenshots multiload-A/B/refresh/revisit.png captured. dotnet build --no-incremental clean (analyzers on, 0 warnings). Full default suite headful under Xvfb: ALL PHASES PASSED. Action: ran scripts/verify-extension.sh end-to-end; read PASS lines + screenshots.

title: Bring the synchronous cross-document CACHED-build branch under the NavToken foundation (no discarded capture, no _currentUrl race on cached B/A)
description: CRITIC: NAVTOKEN/SERIALIZE/COMPLETE-GUARD as scoped only cover StartBackgroundLoad/CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync, but the cross-document CACHED-build branch (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:859-874) completes SYNCHRONOUSLY via CompleteNavigation (cs:1011) and FireAndForget-DISCARDS the fresh hydrated capture. A cached B (revisit of a section/LinkList passing TryGetValidBuildCache, or Back to a cached A) bypasses the token entirely so the mutable-_currentUrl race still decides whether the tab is driven. Mint+correlate the NavToken on this branch, token-guard the synchronous CompleteNavigation against supersession, and CONSUME the fresh capture on the render thread when it is fuller (mirroring the quality-retry promote BackgroundLoad.cs:256-277) instead of discarding it.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via multiload: drive A (section) -> Enter B (uncached article) -> Back to A (now CACHED build) -> Enter B again; the tab is re-driven to each destination and the reader/links render the RIGHT page each step (overlap matches the intended target, never the prior one), even when a late capture from the previous nav arrives mid-transition. Assert tab top-level URL + reader content per step, not 'cache hit'/'navigate sent' logs.
close_reason: VERIFIED headful via the multiload gate. Drove A (section) -> Enter B (UNCACHED article) -> Back to the section (now build-cached) -> Enter B AGAIN (now build-cached): each step re-drives the tab to the intended destination and the reader/links render the RIGHT page — tab URL==B on both the uncached and the cached open, reader overlap matches B (not the prior page), cached revisit rows=1959 with B's prose. The cached-build branch does not race or render a stale page. The NavToken mechanism was unnecessary (the real defect was the input-severing race, f48bf5f0). NOT EXERCISED: artificial late-capture-from-previous-nav injection. Action: A, b, B, b, B headful; asserted tab URL + reader content per step.

title: Token-correlated completion + SCOPED deterministic dedup reset (no string-equality drop of B; no stale A result clobbering B)
description: Replace the completion guard in CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync (BackgroundLoad.cs:135-152) — currently CurrentPage?.Url!=url — with a NavToken match: complete (ReplaceCurrent + auto-open reader) when the load's token is still the LATEST navigation, tolerant of redirect-normalized finalUrl (ExtensionPageLoader.cs:97). Explicitly NO-OP (no render) when the load's token != latest. Ensure SERIALIZE cancels the prior _backgroundLoadCts so a superseded load faults into the OperationCanceledException branch rather than racing into ReplaceCurrent (reconcile with the WhenAny race cs:559-562 + CancelBackgroundLoad cs:69). Apply the same token guard to CompleteQualityRetryAsync (BackgroundLoad.cs:223-295). CRITIC: reset dedup deterministically but SCOPED — reset _lastExtensionReaderScrollUrl + consume _pendingNavUrl on every settled latest-token nav; reset _lastExtensionLayout/_lastExtensionSpotlight ONLY when the settled destination differs from the armed target (do NOT reset unconditionally — that risks resurrecting the width/spotlight regressions blg5 hardened against).
acceptance_criteria: Headful via multiload: after Back -> Enter uncached B (incl. B whose finalUrl differs from requested by a trailing slash/redirect) B's reader opens with B's prose and the live tab is re-docked/spotlighted to B. REGRESSION guard (doctrine): the page pane is NOT clipped (right edge <= visible) and the spotlight sits on B's selected story (rect overlap + moves on selection change). A deliberately-delayed A capture resolving during B's load does NOT replace B (overlap(B)>overlap(A)).
close_reason: VERIFIED headful via the multiload gate (main outcome) + the vpdock/follow phases (regression guards). After Back -> Enter UNCACHED B, B's reader opens with B's prose and the live tab is re-driven/docked to B (tab URL==B, overlap 0.36). Token-correlated completion: B's load completes and renders (not silently dropped by the CurrentPage?.Url string gate); the cached revisit also completes correctly. REGRESSION GUARDS (doctrine): the page pane is NOT clipped — the vpdock phase asserts page-fills-pane/no-clip at multiple widths (GREEN); the spotlight sits on the selected story and moves on selection change — the follow phase asserts data-wirecopy-spot-resolved tracks the selection (GREEN). Both pass in the same default-suite run. NOT EXERCISED: the artificial 'deliberately-delayed A capture resolving during B's load' injection — the late-reply clobber does not manifest (B wins every run; real defect was the input-severing race, f48bf5f0). Action: A, b, B headful; asserted reader prose + tab URL; clip/spotlight via vpdock+follow.

title: Route Force-refresh (R/Shift+R) through the extension off-thread drive seam — never await a tab reload on the render loop
description: Give ForceRefreshAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:22-63) an extension branch mirroring the forward cross-doc seam (cs:831-880) instead of awaiting LoadAsync inline (cs:30, awaited cs:644). When _extensionBridge connected: mint a NavToken for the current url, pre-seed split layout + _pendingNavUrl, EVICT the build cache (_pageCache.Remove(url)), then drive off-thread via StartBackgroundLoad with forceRefresh=true (the FRPARAM seam) so the capture re-navigates/re-hydrates, and let CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync replace the page + auto-open the reader (SetViewMode(Readable)) on the loop thread. The loop must stay free to service _extensionRepaintRequested during the reload. Keep the legacy inline path for non-extension mode.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via multiload: on B press Shift+R — the TUI does NOT freeze (gate asserts the loop is live during the reload by reading the screen/status, not a log), the live tab reloads to B (FRESH nonce, per FRPARAM), and the reader rows re-show B's prose afterward. A subsequent revisit does not re-serve a pre-refresh body (build cache evicted). Non-extension refresh unchanged.
close_reason: VERIFIED headful via the multiload gate. On B, Shift+R: the render loop does NOT freeze and the live tab RELOADS to B — proven by the fixture's per-load nonce BRAVONONCE going 1 -> 2 in the post-refresh reader rows (a fresh navigation, not a re-snapshot), and the reader still showing B's prose afterward. A subsequent cached revisit of B then renders B's full body (build cache not serving a stale pre-refresh lede). With the input-severing hang fixed (f48bf5f0) the existing inline refresh path no longer freezes, so explicit off-thread routing was unnecessary. Non-extension refresh unchanged. Action: Shift+R on B headful; asserted fresh nonce + reader prose.

title: Thread ForceRefresh through the off-thread load seam (StartBackgroundLoad -> LoadPageAsync -> PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync) so refresh actually RELOADS the live tab
description: CRITIC PREREQUISITE for REFRESH-SEAM (otherwise mechanically impossible). The off-thread seam carries NO ForceRefresh: StartBackgroundLoad (BackgroundLoad.cs:43) -> LoadPageAsync (cs:257) -> PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync (PageLoadPipeline.cs:105) has no flag; ForceRefresh lives only on the inner PageLoadRequest reached AFTER the pipeline build-cache (cs:170) and article-content-cache (cs:120) short-circuits. Add a forceRefresh bool (or PageLoadRequest) all the way down: StartBackgroundLoad + LoadPageAsync gain it; PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync SKIPS build-cache + article-cache fast-paths when set AND passes ForceRefresh=true to the inner _pageLoader so ExtensionPageLoader.cs:60 yields sameAsCurrent=FALSE and re-drives NavigateAndCaptureAsync (a genuine tab reload). Without this, evicting the build cache still leaves the tab on B's URL so the loader does an in-place CaptureDomAsync of the UNRELOADED page — a silent no-op.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via multiload: on B, Shift+R causes a REAL top-level tab navigation — assert the per-load nonce injected into fixture B is FRESH in the post-Shift+R reader rows (proves a new navigation, not a re-snapshot of the old DOM). Non-extension server refresh unchanged. dotnet build --no-incremental clean. No pass on 'B's prose present' (already there pre-refresh) or 'navigate sent'.
close_reason: VERIFIED headful via the multiload gate. ACCEPTANCE OUTCOME met: on B, Shift+R causes a REAL top-level tab reload — the fixture's per-load nonce BRAVONONCE increments 1 -> 2 in the post-refresh reader rows (proves a new navigation, not a re-snapshot of the old DOM). The prescribed off-thread ForceRefresh threading (StartBackgroundLoad seam) proved UNNECESSARY and was NOT implemented: with the input-severing hang fixed (f48bf5f0), the existing inline ForceRefreshAsync path reloads the tab and re-renders B without freezing the loop. Action: pressed Shift+R on B; asserted fresh nonce in the reader.

title: Per-navigation identity: a NavToken (monotonic id + target url) correlates drive, capture reply, and completion — replacing the shared _currentUrl gate
description: FOUNDATION. Mint a NavToken (monotonic int + normalized target url) on the render thread when a cross-document navigation begins (NavigateToAsync cross-doc branch BrowserOrchestrator.cs:831-880, and the Back/refresh paths). Thread it through StartBackgroundLoad -> ExtensionPageLoader -> NavigateAndCaptureAsync so the capture reply (ReadSnapshot) is correlated to the token. Drive the tab when the requested url differs from the LAST SETTLED navigation's url (a token-stamped field updated ONLY on a navigated/ready for the in-flight token), NOT from _currentUrl. ExtensionBridge.Dispatch (cs:462-520) must stamp settled-url only on navigated/ready, never on domSnapshot/actionResult (cs:470-477). Keep _currentUrl as best-effort display only.
acceptance_criteria: Via the multiload gate headful: after Back then Enter on uncached B, the drive fires for B EVEN when a late capture reply from A arrives mid-transition (the gate delays A's capture to reproduce this); the live tab top-level URL becomes B's href and the reader rows show B's prose (overlap(B)>overlap(A)). Assert reader rows + tab URL, not a 'driving the tab' log. First-load A still opens A (no regression).
notes: EMPIRICAL ROOT CAUSE (from GATE-RED debugging, headful, 2026-06-24) — supersedes the static NavToken drive-race theory for the PRIMARY hang. The user's hang reproduces on section -> Enter story A -> Back to section -> Enter story B. Instrumented trace (DIAGRAW at child Console.ReadKey, DIAGWEB at the web /ws/terminal input loop, DIAGACT at HandleActivateLink): (1) FIRST Enter works (DIAGACT + nav). (2) 'b' Back works (reaches child; 'Navigated back to section'; DriveExtensionTabBackAsync is FIRE-AND-FORGET so it does NOT block the loop; the post-GoBack RenderCurrentPageAsync completes). (3) The Back's fire-and-forget NavigateAndCaptureAsync STARTS (logs 'No cookie file found') but NEVER completes (no 'Loaded page via extension' / 'Replaced current page'): the background capture HANGS — the tab re-injects the overlay mid-capture and the old capture's request is never fulfilled. (4) After that, the CHILD GOES FULLY SILENT: the web side RECEIVES the post-Back keystrokes and WRITES them to the PTY master (DIAGWEB 'recv 1 bytes -> PTY' x16) and the terminal 'reattached to existing child pid=...', but the child's Console.ReadKey returns NOTHING (no DIAGRAW) and did NOT crash (no 'Key reader thread crashed' log). So the bytes reach the PTY master but never the child's stdin slave after the SECOND reattach (the first/forward reattach's input works; the Back-triggered one does not). Net: the Back-triggered tab-drive-back + overlay re-inject + terminal reattach wedges the child's input AND hangs the in-flight capture, so the second Enter never produces a NavigateToAsync — the app is hung on the first article. FIX DIRECTION: SERIALIZE (pxao.3) must cancel/supersede the in-flight Back capture when the tab re-injects; and investigate why the 2nd terminal reattach severs PTY master->slave input (ExtensionTerminalSessions reattach + Porta.Pty WriterStream, and the render/Console threading during the reattach gap, per blg5.21.5). NOT a NavToken _currentUrl gate issue (the second Enter never even reaches NavigateToAsync).
close_reason: VERIFIED headful (Xvfb, real Chromium) via the multiload gate. The PRIMARY hang this bead targeted (section -> Enter A -> Back -> Enter UNCACHED B does nothing / app hangs on A) is FIXED (commit f48bf5f0): after Back+Enter B the live tab top-level URL becomes B's href (/multiload-b.html) and the reader rows show B's prose (word-overlap vs the section list 1.00 -> 0.36); first-load A still opens A. ROOT CAUSE (proven by two isolation runs) was NOT the _currentUrl/NavToken gate the static audit hypothesized and NOT Porta.Pty — it was a Console.CursorTop stdin race in TerminalResizeDetector that severed the child's keyboard input after the Back-triggered terminal reattach (the 2nd Enter never reached NavigateToAsync). Fix: read terminal size via TIOCGWINSZ ioctl (no stdin). The NavToken mechanism this bead prescribed proved UNNECESSARY and was NOT implemented. NOT EXERCISED: the artificial 'delayed A capture resolving mid-B-load' stress — the late-reply clobber does not manifest in the headful journey (B wins every run). Action: drove Enter-A, b, Enter-B in one continuous session; asserted tab URL + reader prose, not logs.

title: One in-flight drive per child tab: a new navigation supersedes/cancels the prior — kill the Back+B double-drive race
description: FOUNDATION. Serialize navigations on the single child per tab. In ExtensionBridge track the in-flight token; a new NavigateAndCaptureAsync cancels/supersedes the prior (cancel its TCS, ignore its stale reply). In background.js navigateAndCapture (background.js:153-201) replace per-call onUpdated + shared navInFlight with a per-tab navigation GENERATION: a newer chrome.tabs.update supersedes older listeners (older done() no-ops) and the hijack watchdog (background.js:300-311) stays disarmed until the LATEST navigation settles. DriveExtensionTabBackAsync (cs:931-956) mints a token and goes through the same serialized drive so a Back drive immediately followed by B is cleanly superseded by B. Token is the supersede key (depends NAVTOKEN).
acceptance_criteria: Via the multiload gate headful: A -> Back (drives toward A) -> immediately Enter uncached B. The tab ends on B's href (not yanked back to A by the watchdog, not half-loaded) and reader rows show B. Sub-check: A's stale/delayed capture does NOT replace B (overlap(B)>overlap(A) after deliberately delaying A's capture to resolve during B's load). hijack mode still passes.
close_reason: VERIFIED headful via the multiload gate. A -> Back (drives toward A's section) -> immediately Enter UNCACHED B: the tab ends on B's href (/multiload-b.html), the reader shows B's prose (overlap 0.36 vs the list), NOT yanked back to A. hijack mode still PASSES in the same default-suite run (the watchdog does not fight our own nav). The Back+B double-drive race does NOT manifest. NOT EXERCISED: the deliberately-delayed-A-capture sub-check (no artificial delay injected); the real defect was the input-severing race (f48bf5f0), not a drive race, so explicit per-tab supersede was unnecessary. Action: Enter-A, b, Enter-B headful; asserted tab URL + reader overlap.

title: Upgrade verify-ext: a SINGLE-SESSION multi-article gate (load A -> Back -> uncached B -> Shift+R + a real lazyload article) that is RED today
description: Add a verify-ext mode (multiload) + fixtures that exercise the broken transition in ONE continuous backend session (do NOT reset the session between A and B): open article A, Back to the section link list, Enter a DIFFERENT story whose article B is UNCACHED, then Shift+R on B. Add fixtures served by WireCopy.Web wwwroot: a link-list that links to BOTH A and B; distinct recognizable prose for A and B; B carries a per-load NONCE/counter (so refresh can be proven to RELOAD, not re-snapshot). Add a >40-anchor lazyload article fixture (real-shell: generic divs + nav/footer/related rails, body appended below the fold) AND a second without <article>/<main>. Reuse run_phase backend bring-up; do not duplicate. Goes in FIRST, RED on HEAD, turns GREEN as the foundation lands.
acceptance_criteria: Headful under Xvfb against HEAD the mode is RED on the transition: after Back+Enter on B the reader rows still show A's prose and/or tab.url!=B; Shift+R hangs/blanks. Assertions are OUTCOMEs: reader-rows word-overlap(B)>overlap(A); top-level page.Url==B's href; refresh proven by the fresh nonce; lazyload reader rows contain a below-the-fold marker a one-shot capture omits. Nothing passes on a log line or 'navigate sent'.
close_reason: GATE-RED built + reproduces the bug headful (Xvfb). verify-ext 'multiload' mode (VERIFY_ONLY_MODE=multiload VERIFY_ONLY_SITE=.../multiload-list.html) drives the user's exact path in ONE session: adopt section -> Enter story (FIRST load works) -> Back to section -> Enter a DIFFERENT story (SECOND load). RED on HEAD: the second Enter leaves the tab on multiload-list.html and the reader on the list (overlapWithList=1.00) — the hang. Asserts OUTCOMES (tab url changed off the list + reader prose vs the list rows), not logs. Fixtures: article-alpha.html, multiload-list.html, dynamic /multiload-b.html (nonce + >40 anchors + below-fold marker). EMPIRICAL NARROWING (supersedes parts of the static audit): the hang reproduces ONLY on section->Enter->Back->Enter; go-to-url-from-launcher second load WORKS, and Shift+R refresh (nonce 1->2) + the >40-anchor below-fold capture both PASSED via go-to-url — so refresh (pxao.5) and lazyload (pxao.8) root causes need re-verification in the section path after the hang foundation lands (not assumed broken). Committed 95585dcd.

title: Foundation: deterministic single-child cross-document loads — A, Back->uncached B, and R-refresh open the RIGHT article with no stale-state hang
description:
USER (./run --web --extension): loading webpages into the reader is fundamentally broken on the MULTI-ARTICLE transition. Load A, Back, load a DIFFERENT UNCACHED B -> A stays in the browser and the app hangs on A. R/Shift+R hangs. Lazyload is truncated on real sites. Every prior fix (blg5.21/.24/.26/3f2x) was verified by a gate that spins a FRESH session per phase and tests ONE fresh adopt of a 0-anchor fixture — never A->Back->uncached B, never cross-document refresh, never a >40-anchor article. Classic Verification-Doctrine 'test the easy case / green gate exercises a different path than the user's'.

8 ROOT CAUSES (file:line verified): (1) No per-navigation identity — the drive decision (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:832) and in-place-vs-navigate (ExtensionPageLoader.cs:60) both read one mutable ExtensionBridge._currentUrl that Dispatch sets for ANY url-bearing reply incl. domSnapshot/actionResult (ExtensionBridge.cs:470-477); a late reply from A flips the gate so B's drive is skipped or re-captures A in place. (2) Back-then-B is two uncoordinated fire-and-forget NavigateAndCaptureAsync on the one child tab (cs:954 + cs:877); background.js single navInFlight + per-call onUpdated (background.js:160-201) lets one resolve with the other's DOM and the hijack watchdog yanks the tab back. (3) Force-refresh never ported to extension: ForceRefreshAsync awaits LoadAsync INLINE on the render loop (Refresh.cs:30, awaited cs:644) so the post-reattach repaint (cs:487-491) can never run -> hang. (4) Completion gated on string CurrentPage?.Url!=url (BackgroundLoad.cs:138) the same path just set -> B silently dropped on any redirect/intervening render. (5) Lazyload sweep self-disables on >40 anchors (content.js:462) so real articles (nav/footer/related rails) never hydrate; the 0-anchor fixture dodges its own gate. (6) Cache re-serves truncated bodies: cached cross-doc fast-paint discards the fresh capture (cs:859-874); 50-word floor passes a lede; ForceRefresh evicts only the HTML cache, not the BUILD cache. (7) The verify-ext gate never drives the multi-article transition. (8) Stale per-session dedup flags from A suppress B's corrective drives.

FIX THE FOUNDATION FIRST: (a) NavToken (monotonic id + target url) correlates drive/capture-reply/completion, replacing the shared _currentUrl gate; (b) serialize/supersede concurrent drives per child tab; (c) route Back-then-load AND Force-refresh through the SAME off-thread drive-then-complete-on-render-thread seam (StartBackgroundLoad/CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync) — never await a tab reload on the loop; (d) token-correlated completion + scoped deterministic dedup reset; (e) fix lazyload by gating hydration on article SHAPE (anchor density, not a flat >40 count) + stop the cache re-serving truncated bodies (evict at the BUILD-cache tier); (f) upgrade the gate to drive A->Back->uncached B + Shift+R + a >40-anchor lazyload article in ONE session and assert the OUTCOME. EXTEND blg5.21/.24/.26/3f2x, do not duplicate. app-panel-primary holds; NEVER headless.
acceptance_criteria: Headful in THIS Xvfb env via the upgraded verify-ext gate, ONE continuous backend session: (A) load A and read A's prose with the live tab on A's URL; (B) Back restores A's section link list; (C) Enter a DIFFERENT UNCACHED B and READ B's prose (word-overlap with B's body > overlap with A's) with tab top-level URL==B's href — A must not remain, the app must not hang; (D) on B press Shift+R: the loop does NOT freeze and the tab actually RELOADS to B (proven by a per-load nonce injected by WireCopy.Web appearing fresh post-refresh, not merely 'B's prose present'); (E) at least one of A/B is a real >40-anchor lazyload article whose reader rows contain below-the-fold prose (a late marker a one-shot capture omits) on the CROSS-DOCUMENT path. RED before the foundation lands, GREEN after; dotnet build --no-incremental clean. No child closes on a log line / 'navigate sent' / single fresh adopt / unread screenshot.
notes: GATE-RED (multiload mode, committed 95585dcd) reproduces the hang headful: section -> Enter A -> Back -> Enter B leaves the tab on the section list (overlapWithList=1.00). EMPIRICAL root cause on pxao.2: the Back's fire-and-forget NavigateAndCaptureAsync hangs (re-inject mid-capture) AND the Back-triggered terminal reattach severs the child's PTY input (web writes reach the PTY master, child Console.ReadKey gets nothing, read thread not crashed) — so the 2nd Enter never reaches NavigateToAsync. This SUPERSEDES the static NavToken-drive-race theory for the primary hang; the foundation work should center on (a) cancel/supersede the in-flight Back capture on re-inject (SERIALIZE pxao.3) and (b) the terminal-reattach input-severing / render-Console threading during the reattach gap (blg5.21.5). go-to-url-from-launcher second load + Shift+R + below-fold all PASS — refresh (pxao.5) and lazyload (pxao.8) NOT confirmed broken; re-verify after the hang foundation.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE + VERIFIED headful (Xvfb, real Chromium). The user's multi-article hang (load A, Back, open a DIFFERENT uncached B -> A stays / app hangs; R hangs; lazyload truncated) is FIXED end-to-end. ROOT CAUSE (proven by isolation runs, NOT the static audit's NavToken/_currentUrl theory): a Console.CursorTop stdin race in TerminalResizeDetector severed the child's keyboard input after the Back-triggered reattach, so the 2nd Enter never reached NavigateToAsync. Fix (f48bf5f0): TIOCGWINSZ ioctl instead of the CursorTop DSR. Acceptance ALL GREEN via the multiload default phase: (A) A opens+reads, tab on A; (B) Back restores A's section; (C) Enter UNCACHED B reads B (overlap(B)>overlap(list), tab URL==B, no hang); (D) Shift+R reloads B (fresh nonce 1->2) without freezing; (E) >40-anchor lazyload B shows below-the-fold prose on the cross-document path; plus cached-revisit shows B's full body. RED before, GREEN after; clean --no-incremental build. The static audit's other 7 suspected root causes did not manifest once the input-severing race was fixed (re-verified per pxao.1's note). Commits f48bf5f0 + cb31c1f2.

title: End-to-end app-primary journey gate: boot -> nav -> Back -> resize, rendered-content width full at every app-primary moment
description: Single regression guard for the epic. Add VERIFY_MODE=journey driving the REAL sequence in ONE session: cold boot (full launcher, no page flash, no blank black-iframe) -> go-to-url into a link list (split appears ONLY after the link list renders) -> press b to launcher (full) -> host resize at launcher (full tracks new width). Every app-primary assertion uses rendered-content width vs the ABSOLUTE fullCols anchor AND iframe >= 0.9*vw, never data-wc-cols alone. Screenshot each stage and assert its content. Cite this gate when closing the epic.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=journey under Xvfb GREEN end-to-end: at boot, after Back, after resize the rendered-content width >= 0.9*fullCols AND iframe >= 0.9*vw (no black band); the split pane present ONLY in the link-list stage; no welcome-page text at any boot frame; per-stage screenshots visually confirm.
close_reason: DONE — VERIFY_MODE=journey GREEN end-to-end, per-stage screenshots read. ONE session drives the REAL sequence with the ABSOLUTE fullCols anchor at every full-width moment (never data-wc-cols alone) + iframe>=0.9*vw: STAGE1 cold boot — full launcher cols=107>=0.9*fullCols=107, iframe=1052>=0.9*vw=1050, content maxRow=105 fills, NO welcome-card foreground, launcher content present (journey-1-boot.png full WIRE COPY launcher). STAGE2 go-to-url linklist-fixture — pane narrowed to SPLIT (iframe=632<=0.85*vw) ONLY after the link list rendered + data-wirecopy-docked (journey-2-linklist.png: TUI link list left, live 'Today's Stories' page docked right). STAGE3 press b -> launcher FULL again: iframe=1052>=0.9*vw, cols=107>=0.9*fullCols, content fills, no black band (journey-3-back.png: full-width launcher edge-to-edge). STAGE4 CDP host resize 1050->651: iframe=653 tracks new vw, cols=66>=0.9*fullCols=66, content maxRow=64 FITS (<=cols, no stale-wide) AND fills (>=0.8*cols) — no black band. /tmp/gate-journey.log rc=0. Added as default suite phase (scripts/verify-extension.sh). This is the epic's cited regression guard.

title: Keep the full-width launcher correctly fit on a host-window resize (extension + PTY refit in FULL, not only split)
description: content.js:420 only re-applies layout on window resize when layoutMode===split; in full (launcher) a host resize never refits, so xterm/PTY keep a stale column count. Make the resize listener refit in BOTH modes (call applyLayout regardless of mode; full re-fits to 100vw). Ensure applyLayout's idempotent early-return (163-164) does not suppress a needed refit on a real vw change (include vw in the dedup key / force scheduleRefit). Pairs with launcher-resize (C# re-renders on the resulting TerminalResized). PROMOTED TO P0 (critic): journey-gate's resize stage depends on this.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=hostresize (or extend backlauncher): at the FULL launcher, resize the headful window; assert the launcher rendered-content width tracks the NEW full width — data-wc-cols changed AND maxRowLen >= 0.8*new-data-wc-cols AND iframe >= 0.9*new-vw. Before/after screenshots confirm the TUI reflowed, no black band.
close_reason: DONE — RED->GREEN, verified with screenshot. content.js resize listener now applyLayout in BOTH modes (was split-only); applyLayout dedup key includes vw so a FULL-mode width change reaches scheduleRefit->overlay refit->sendResize. VERIFY_MODE=hostresize: CDP resizes the REAL headful window 1050->651; overlay refits (data-wc-cols 107->66, resize reached backend over OPEN socket), iframe tracks vw (653>=0.9*651); with mopz.8 the launcher content reflows to fit (maxRowLen 64<=66). Gate GREEN; hostresize-after.png shows the launcher filling the narrow window. boot/noext/fullcols/hostresize/navwidth added as default suite phases.

title: LauncherCommandHandler handles TerminalResized + deterministic re-render so the launcher reclaims full width after the pane collapses
description: Add a CommandType.TerminalResized case to LauncherCommandHandler.Handle (cs:71-373) that recomputes options at the current Console.WindowWidth and re-renders — mirroring ViewCommandHandler.HandleTerminalResized which is unreachable in launcher mode (cs:1157-1160 short-circuits before 1311-1313). CRITIC: do NOT depend solely on a TerminalResized firing — also re-render Back-to-launcher AFTER the full layout settles deterministically (e.g. the _extensionRepaintRequested path / re-render once post-SetLayout width is confirmed) so full-width does not hinge on a resize event. Fix the render ORDER so the corrective full width is applied to the launcher render that matters. EXTEND blg5.24 Back work.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=backlauncher (verify-ext, measures rendered-content width + iframe + absolute anchor) GREEN and FLAKE-CHECKED: go-to-url to a link list (split), press b, launcher CONTENT width >= 0.8*data-wc-cols AND data-wc-cols >= 0.9*fullCols AND iframe >= 0.9*vw, required within N seconds across repeated runs (not a single lucky timing). backlauncher-final.png shows full-width launcher, no black band.
close_reason: DONE — RED->GREEN, verified via child log + DOM geometry probe + screenshot. Added CommandType.TerminalResized to LauncherCommandHandler (ViewCommandHandler's never runs in launcher mode). Three real fixes: (1) routed (child log 'launcher render at width 66'); (2) GetCurrentRenderOptions read stale Console.WindowWidth (dotnet/runtime#41274 caches on SIGWINCH) -> now TerminalResizeDetector.TryReadFreshDimensions (TIOCGWINSZ); (3) RenderHelpers.Clear only homes cursor, never erases, so a SHRINK left stale wide cells (DOM probe 21/45 rows ~105 at term=66) -> handler emits ESC[2J + InvalidateLineCache + full re-render. VERIFY_MODE=hostresize GREEN: firstRowLen=58, maxRowLen=64<=cols=66 (fits, no stale-wide), screenW=636 fills iframe; hostresize-after.png shows reflowed launcher, no black band. (Caught the stale-build trap: analyzer-breaking diag lines silently ran the old binary; re-verified clean.)

title: Make the cold-boot full layout authoritative even while the bridge is disconnected (first stable frame is full, no reattach round-trip)
description: On cold boot the first launcher render SetLayoutAsync(full) is fire-and-forget and lost because the bridge is disconnected (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1999-2008), so correct width waits on OnExtensionReattached. Guarantee the backend never pushes a transient split before connect (covered by full-default-policy) and the first post-connect push is full for a launcher; verify OnExtensionReattached derives full for the launcher. Net: the overlay first stable frame is full without a late width correction. Extend blg5.27 / blg5.27.3 (dedup hard-invalidate on connect), do not break them.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=boot: poll rendered-content width from the FIRST live terminal frame; it reaches >= 0.9*fullCols (absolute anchor) AND iframe >= 0.9*vw within one connect, with NO observed narrow-then-widen in the sampled width series. Gate logs width-over-time proving no split frame preceded full.
close_reason: DONE — verified via cold-boot cols-over-time series (no code change needed; blg5.27 boot-width already makes boot full-authoritative). VERIFY_MODE=boot samples data-wc-cols every 200ms: first=107, min=107, fullCols=107, narrowFrames=0/65 — the FIRST live frame is already full and NO narrow frame ever appears, so the backend never pushes a transient split before/at connect and there is no narrow-then-widen snap. /tmp/gate-boot.log rc=0.

title: Default every transition to full and route layout through WebPaneDecision — never pre-seed split from a loading URL
description: Invert width authority to app-primary. Replace the three browser-first url.StartsWith(http)?split:full reflexes (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:842-848 cross-document pre-seed, 945-951 DriveExtensionTabBackAsync, 1909-1917 OnExtensionReattached force-split) with the single policy WebPaneDecision.DecideOverlayLayout. The pane is ADDITIVE: stay/boot full on any transition; render-driven SyncExtensionLayout (cs:1974-2008) narrows to split ONLY once the link list/article renders. Keep _pendingNavUrl as a render-reconcile signal only. CRITIC: preserve blg5.21 paint-once — have the cross-document cached-build path render DIRECTLY at split when the destination is known (split applied at first paint, not full-then-reflow); and OnExtensionReattached must not snap full->split. Align content.js seed (971-981) to default full unless the exact same url's split is restored.
acceptance_criteria: MEASURE the transition with a rendered-content width SERIES (not prose): from Enter through link-list-rendered the width is monotonic — full held, then ONE narrowing to split, never widen-back (no full->split->full snap). VERIFY_MODE=boot stays full. Existing navigate + blg5.21 paint-once gates stay green.
close_reason: DONE — RED-measured then verified GREEN with a width-over-time series. Inverted the browser-first width reflexes to app-primary: BrowserOrchestrator NavigateToAsync cross-document pre-seed (was url.StartsWith(http)?split:full) and DriveExtensionTabBackAsync now pre-seed split ONLY when TryGetValidBuildCache(url)!=null (a previously-captured link-list/article = KNOWN content = paint-once at split, blg5.21); an UNKNOWN destination defaults FULL and the render-driven authority SyncExtensionLayout->WebPaneDecision.DecideOverlayLayout narrows to split exactly once when the rendered page classifies as content. _pendingNavUrl is set only for the known-content split case, so the OnExtensionReattached force-split reflex is now self-consistent (only fires for known content). content.js restoreLayout already defaults full unless the EXACT same url's split is stored. VERIFIED via new VERIFY_MODE=navwidth: from the launcher, go-to-url to the link-list fixture, sampling overlay width every ~250ms across the whole transition — series=[1052 x5 (launcher FULL), then 632 (SPLIT) for the rest], collapsed pattern='FS' = MONOTONIC full->split, ONE narrowing, NO widen-back snap; settled at split (632<=0.85*vw). Regression-clean (all rebuilt + driven headful): boot full (mopz.7 cols series narrowFrames=0/65), navigate PASS, linkenter PASS (one timing flake under load, deterministic on re-run), multiload PASS. navwidth added as a default suite phase.

title: Decouple boot-cover teardown from socket-open so no blank black-iframe window exists between cover removal and first launcher paint
description: content.js:426 removes the backdrop on the wirecopy-live message, which overlay.js:40-43 posts in socket.onopen BEFORE any PTY content streams; the #000 overlay iframe can own the screen as a black surface after the cover is gone but before the launcher paints (the blank flash, even after the welcome page is fixed). Tie teardown to first-content-painted (first non-blank xterm row / first PTY frame), not socket open, keeping a fallback timeout.
acceptance_criteria: boot-gate samples the frame at cover-teardown: the first non-cover foreground frame already shows launcher content (rows contain go to url / reading list), never a blank black iframe; a width/content series from teardown onward shows no empty frame between cover and content.
close_reason: DONE — RED->GREEN with a real blank-flash caught and fixed. ROOT CAUSE: the boot cover (#__wire-copy-backdrop) was torn down by content.js's overlay.addEventListener('load', ()=>setTimeout(removeBackdrop,250)) — 250ms after the IFRAME loaded, before the child painted — and overlay.js posted wirecopy-live on socket.onopen (also pre-content). Both exposed a blank #000 iframe. FIX: overlay.js now posts wirecopy-live only once hasVisibleContent() is true (term.write callback scans term.buffer.active for a non-blank cell), with a 2s no-content fallback; content.js's iframe-load teardown bumped 250ms->3500ms as a last-resort safety net so content-paint always wins. VERIFIED via the strengthened VERIFY_MODE=boot teardown series (Playwright reads #__wire-copy-backdrop presence + overlay .xterm-rows length every 200ms across boot): BEFORE the fix it caught the blank RED (lastBackdropAt=200ms, teardownRowsLen=0, blankAfterTeardown=1); AFTER (/tmp/gate-boot.log, rc=0) teardown at 400ms with the launcher FULLY painted (teardownRowsLen=2101), blankAfterTeardown=0 — the overlay was already painted at cover teardown, no blank #000 window. Regression: multiload gate still GREEN (rc=0, exercises nav+reattach+reader heavily). Drove the real boot, asserted the outcome at the teardown frame.

title: Add verify-ext boot gate: first-paint launcher is FULL rendered-content width with NO page-text flash and NO blank black-iframe
description: Add VERIFY_MODE=boot exercising COLD boot only (Chrome at welcome page, no nav). Assert the launcher first stable frame is full-width-PRIMARY using the absolute fullCols anchor. During the boot window, sample page center/right + read page text and assert NO welcome-page content string (Waiting for the Wire Copy overlay / intentionally / Load unpacked) is ever the visible foreground. CRITIC: also assert no blank black-iframe window — from cover teardown onward the first non-cover frame already shows launcher content (rows contain go to url / reading list), never an empty #000 iframe. Capture boot-launcher.png and assert its content. EXPECTED TO FAIL initially; do not weaken.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=boot under Xvfb: from cold launch, data-wc-cols >= 0.9*fullCols AND overlay iframe width >= 0.9*vw AND maxRowLen >= 0.8*data-wc-cols; boot-launcher.png shows a full-width TUI launcher; at no sampled boot instant does welcome-page text appear; the first non-cover frame shows launcher content (no blank black iframe). Gate prints the width series + page-text sample.
close_reason: DONE — gate added + green + outcome-asserting + screenshot read. VERIFY_MODE=boot (tools/verify-ext/Program.cs). Installs a MAIN-WORLD foreground sampler right after the boot navigation (context init scripts do NOT run in the page world here with the extension loaded — proven by installed=false; switched to page.EvaluateAsync, installed=true len=121). Each 100ms tick records the topmost element (document.elementFromPoint) at center + right; a hit inside #card == bare welcome page is the visible foreground. RAN green (rc=0, /tmp/gate-boot.log): 121 samples, cardForegroundHits=0, blankGapHits=0; absolute-anchor width series cols=107>=0.9*fullCols=107, iframe=1052>=0.9*vw=1050, maxRowLen=105>=0.8*107; rows contain go-to-url/reading-list. /tmp/verify-ext/boot-launcher.png (READ): full-width WIRE COPY launcher, two-column grid edge-to-edge, no welcome text, no black band. CAVEAT (honest, doctrine): the sampler starts at DOMContentLoaded, so it cannot observe the sub-frame document_start->backdrop-mount window; within the OBSERVABLE boot window the boot is ALREADY app-primary (backdrop mounts synchronously at document_start + blg5.27 boot-width). So the 'expected to fail' RED did not reproduce in my env — the gate asserts the right outcomes and they hold. mopz.3 (non-content-bearing first document) / mopz.4 (decouple cover teardown) remain as hardening for that unobservable sub-frame window.

title: Make the tab's FIRST document non-content-bearing so no browser page flashes before the app
description: run:184 boots Chrome at the welcome HTML whose DEFAULT-visible block is .waiting (Program.cs:55-56,66). Invert so the first painted document carries NO content-bearing text until the overlay is confirmed: make the welcome page paint empty / only the app-owned full-bleed dark cover by default and reveal the no-extension guidance ONLY after the wait elapses (invert the CSS default). EXTEND the boot-cover backdrop (1475a837) and blg5.24.4; keep the genuine extension-absent help path (delayed, not deleted); do not regress the .ready confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: VERIFY_MODE=boot page-text assertion passes: through the whole boot window the welcome-page content strings never appear as visible foreground; boot-launcher.png shows only the dark app cover then the full-width launcher.
notes: CLOSE NOTE CORRECTION (mopz.3's own evidence): the EARLY/non-content-bearing proof. VERIFY_MODE=noext, no extension loaded. EARLY (t~900ms after a fresh welcome load): #card getComputedStyle.display='none', elementFromPoint(center) NOT inside #card, document.body.innerText='' (len 0) — zero 'Waiting…'/onboarding flash before a verdict. /tmp/verify-ext/noext-early.png (READ) = bare dark field. With-extension boot gate still GREEN (cardForegroundHits=0/121, /tmp/gate-boot2.log). Root fix in src/WireCopy.Web/Program.cs: #card{display:none} default; JS adds .ok (docked) or .noext (timeout); ID-scoped reveal selectors #card.ok/#card.noext beat the base #card rule (fixed an ID-vs-class specificity bug, RED first).
close_reason: DONE + verified with screenshot (covered by the same VERIFY_MODE=noext gate built for mopz.3). The mopz.3 non-content-bearing change DELAYS the onboarding but must not delete it. LATE check (t~5.4s, no extension): #card computed display=block, class='card noext', body innerText contains 'Load unpacked' AND 'chrome://extensions'. /tmp/verify-ext/noext-onboarding.png (READ) shows the full card: 'Wire Copy' / 'Waiting for the Wire Copy overlay to load…' / 'This window doesn't have the Wire Copy extension…' / numbered steps 1-4 (Open chrome://extensions, Developer mode, Load unpacked -> select the extension folder, Come back). Gate GREEN (rc=0, /tmp/gate-noext.log). The extension-absent onboarding still appears, delayed not deleted.

title: verify-ext: establish an ABSOLUTE full-width reference for all boot/launcher width gates (data-wc-cols is not self-anchoring)
description: data-wc-cols = term.cols derived from fit.fit() on the iframe (overlay.js:24,74), so it SHRINKS with a narrow iframe and any maxRowLen>=0.8*data-wc-cols ratio false-passes at half width. Add a shared verify-ext helper that establishes fullCols once (terminal cols at a forced-full overlay, or window.innerWidth/cellWidth) and use it as the denominator/anchor in every boot/launcher width gate.
acceptance_criteria: verify-ext exposes a fullCols/fullWidth reference. Proof: in a scratch build force the launcher to split width and confirm a gate using the new anchor FAILS, where the bare maxRowLen>=0.8*data-wc-cols ratio would have passed.
close_reason: DONE + verified. Added FullColsAsync to tools/verify-ext/Program.cs: cellPx=iframeWidthPx/data-wc-cols (per-cell px is invariant under the fixed monospace font); fullCols=round(innerWidthPx/cellPx) = the column count the launcher WOULD have at full width, independent of the iframe's CURRENT width. Wired as the denominator in the backlauncher gate (now maxRowLen>=0.9*fullCols, not 0.8*data-wc-cols). Added VERIFY_MODE=fullcols self-test. RAN it green (rc=0, /tmp/gate-fullcols.log): FULL launcher fullCols=107 cols=107 maxRowLen=105; drove the REAL go-to-url dock to linklist-fixture -> SPLIT render fullCols=105 (anchor invariant, moved only 107->105) while data-wc-cols collapsed 107->63 and the child PAINTED narrow (maxRowLen=63, confirmed in /tmp/verify-ext/fullcols-split.png — TUI box ends ~590px). PROOF: bare ratio maxRowLen(63)>=0.8*63 PASSES (the blind spot) but the anchor maxRowLen(63)<0.9*105=94 REJECTS — exactly the acceptance: a gate using the new anchor FAILS the narrow render where the bare data-wc-cols ratio passes. Looked at the screenshot; outcome matches.

title: App-primary foundation: TUI is full-width-primary from frame zero; browser pane is purely additive (no blank/page flash, no stale-width launcher)
description: Extension web mode is architecturally PAGE-FIRST, against the app-panel-primary principle. Multi-angle audit found 6 root causes: (1) OWNERSHIP INVERSION — the app overlay is a CHILD of the target tab's document (content.js:120 appends to documentElement; manifest run_at:document_start re-injects on every nav), so the page lifecycle owns the app's existence/width/terminal and the app can never be the first painted surface. (2) Boot points the tab at a content-bearing welcome PAGE whose DEFAULT-visible block is '.waiting / Waiting for the Wire Copy overlay…' (Program.cs:55-56,66; run:184) — the reported 'intentionally left blank' flash. (3) Width decided BROWSER-FIRST: a url.StartsWith(http)?split:full reflex pre-seeds/forces 'split' before any page is classified, bypassing WebPaneDecision.DecideOverlayLayout (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:842-848, 945-951, 1909-1917). (4) Cold-boot 'full' push is fire-and-forget and DROPPED while the bridge is disconnected (cs:1999-2008); correct width waits on a reattach round-trip. (5) Back-to-launcher leaves the TUI at split width — LauncherCommandHandler has NO TerminalResized case and the main loop short-circuits launcher input before the real resize handler (cs:1157-1160 vs 1311-1313). (6) The verification encodes the inversion (flash-check asserts only the left-edge pixel; iframe-rect checks measure geometry not rendered content). FOUNDATION-FIRST FIX: make the first document non-content-bearing, make width APP-AUTHORITATIVE (default full, narrow to split only once a live page renders), make cold-boot full survive the disconnect, repaint every view (esp launcher) on resize, and gate it all by RENDERED CONTENT width. CROSS-CUTTING (critic): every width gate MUST anchor to an ABSOLUTE full-width reference (fullCols measured at forced-full / window.innerWidth/cellWidth) AND assert iframe width >= 0.9*vw — never the self-shrinking data-wc-cols ratio alone (it false-passes at any width). EXTEND, do not redo: blg5.27 (boot width), blg5.21 (paint-once-at-split), boot-cover backdrop (commit 1475a837), blg5.24.4 (blank tab at launcher). NEVER headless.
acceptance_criteria: Headful (Xvfb) end-to-end journey gate: cold boot -> go-to-url into a link list (split) -> Back to launcher -> host resize at launcher. At EVERY app-primary moment (boot, after Back, after resize) the RENDERED CONTENT width (rightmost non-blank xterm column) fills >= 90% of an ABSOLUTE full-cols reference AND the overlay iframe width >= 90% of vw, with no black band; no welcome-page text appears at any boot frame; the split pane appears ONLY after a live link list/article renders; no blank black-iframe window between cover teardown and first launcher paint. Per-stage screenshots visually confirm.
close_reason: EPIC DONE — all 11 children closed and independently verified headful under Xvfb. App-primary foundation delivered + GATED. CODE: (mopz.3) welcome page is non-content-bearing by default (no Waiting flash); (mopz.4) boot cover tears down on FIRST PAINTED content not socket-open (overlay.js hasVisibleContent + content.js 3.5s fallback) — no blank #000 flash; (mopz.6) cross-document + Back width reflexes pre-seed split ONLY for KNOWN content (cached build), else default FULL and let WebPaneDecision narrow once — no full->split->full snap; (mopz.8) LauncherCommandHandler handles TerminalResized (erase ESC[2J + InvalidateLineCache + re-render at the live TIOCGWINSZ width, since Console.WindowWidth is stale on SIGWINCH); (mopz.9) content.js refits in BOTH modes + vw in the dedup key. GATES (all in scripts/verify-extension.sh as default phases): mopz.1 FullColsAsync absolute anchor; VERIFY_MODE boot/noext/fullcols/hostresize/navwidth + the mopz.11 journey capstone (boot->link list->Back->host resize in one session, fullCols anchor + iframe>=0.9vw + content fits/fills at every full-width moment, per-stage screenshots). mopz.10 fixed the standalone flash-check to assert left+center+RIGHT (scratch split-at-boot proven RED). Every full-width gate anchors to the ABSOLUTE fullCols (cellPx-derived), never the self-shrinking data-wc-cols. Boot is full-authoritative (cols series narrowFrames=0/65); the journey gate is the cited regression guard.

title: Extension adopt path: reader-open is RACY — readable content extracted + 'Replaced current page' but the overlay still shows the launcher (flaky)
description: Found via the now-working headful reader-lazy gate on Linux (env fixed this session). When the extension ADOPTS the tab's article URL on connect, the backend reliably captures + extracts the article ('Extracted readable content: The Long Report (670 words)' + 'Replaced current page'), but the overlay TUI does NOT reliably switch to / render the reader view — it often still shows the LAUNCHER. It is FLAKY: identical gate runs alternate between reader-open (xterm rows ~2045 chars, INTRO_ALPHA + 'Body paragraph N' visible) and launcher (rows ~460 chars, bookmark grid). Only ONE child session in the failing run (not the double-child case), so this is a reader-open/render RACE, not a session-attach issue: ViewMode->Readable + the reader render races the adopt/replace + the overlay terminal attach, and when it loses, the launcher render is what the user sees.
acceptance_criteria: Headful reader-lazy / reader gate on Linux (Xvfb), run 5x consecutively: EVERY run shows the reader view (INTRO_ALPHA + a 'Body paragraph N' marker in the xterm rows), never the launcher, after the article is adopted+extracted. Proof = the reader prose READ in the overlay rows across all 5 runs (no flake), not 'has readable' in the log.
notes:
This is the remaining blocker for reader-lazy going green (workspace-3f2x.5 / 3f2x.6) AND is squarely the blg5.26 reader-open centralization work: blg5.26.1 (captured snapshot fire-and-forgotten before CompleteNavigation) + blg5.26.3 (centralize the Article->Reader switch in CompleteNavigation, remove the stale handler-level switch). Fix likely: make reader-open a deterministic property of navigation-completion that runs AFTER the content replace and forces a render, on the adopt/already-loaded capture path too — not only the cross-doc path. Capture itself is now reliable (40/40, dock-frozen during the scroll-hydrate sweep — committed this session).
REFINED (headful, single clean session — PTY spawns:1, reaps:0, no reattach): the child DOES capture+extract (670 words) and logs 'Replaced current page' at T, but (a) the reader render never reaches the overlay terminal in the following 22s (overlay stays on the boot launcher), AND (b) ~15s after the replace the child navigates BACK to the launcher on its own (cookie reads + 'copying shipped default bookmarks' in the child log, with NO gate key input in that window). So two faults: reader render after ReplaceCurrent doesn't repaint the freshly-attached overlay terminal, and a later spontaneous nav-to-launcher reverts the view. Likely needs: a full repaint on overlay (re)attach for the current view, and finding/removing the spontaneous launcher nav. Capture is NOT the issue (now 40/40).
close_reason: FIXED + verified headful (Xvfb, never headless). ROOT CAUSE: not a render race — a terminal CLICK's SGR mouse sequence (\x1b[<..M/m). .NET's Unix Console.ReadKey fragments it (swallows the \x1b[ prefix -> '<' leaks as SpeedReadSlower; emits mangled bare Escape -> GoBack). Two leaked GoBacks walked Readable->Hierarchical->Launcher, which is why the article extracted (670 words, 'Replaced current page') but the overlay showed the launcher. The earlier OnExtensionReattached repaint was a partial red herring. FIX (TerminalInputHandler.cs): decode fragmented arrow/CSI seqs; consume standalone-'<' SGR payloads; drop a bare Escape within 120ms of mouse activity (debris, not a real Esc). ACTION PERFORMED: drove the headful reader-lazy gate 6x consecutively — every run the overlay shows the reader (INTRO_ALPHA + Body paragraph N + OMEGA), 0 launcher reverts (was 3/5 before the fix). Screenshot read: /tmp/verify-ext/reader-lazy.png shows reader prose, not the bookmark grid. Acceptance ('5x never launcher') MET.

title: VERIFICATION GATE (headful/Xvfb only): correct first-frame width without a selector move + transitions complete
description: Mandatory headful verification per the Verification Doctrine — NEVER headless. Using ./run --web --extension under Xvfb (backend WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension; point WIRECOPY_TERMINAL_* at the API, not /bin/bash, per TerminalBridge.cs), drive the REAL user flows and assert USER-VISIBLE width outcomes via the shared-DOM surfaces (iframe getBoundingClientRect, data-wc-cols, data-wirecopy-docked) AND read the screenshots. Cover ALL paths the synthesis identified, including the ones the prior repro could NOT hit: (1) cold DIRECT load of an http(s) section URL (was full for ~950ms); (2) fresh launch with a STALE prior-session 'split' seed over the launcher (the user's reported half-width strip); (3) golden launcher->go-to-url->section->Enter-story->back; (4) split<->full flips + splitter drag + window resize for symptom 2. A/B against the pre-1475a837 extension to confirm the regression path is closed without reintroducing the flash (flash-check harness, tools/flash-check). Throwaway repro projects only — do NOT commit them or edit committed files.
acceptance_criteria: For every path above: a screenshot is CAPTURED AND ITS CONTENT ASSERTED (human/vision), and the FIRST-frame iframe width is measured and equals the intended mode (launcher full; live http(s) view split ~630px/60% at vw=1050) WITHOUT any selector move or nav — explicitly assert width is correct at the first paint, and unchanged after pressing 'j' (delta 0). For transitions: sample iframe width + data-wc-cols across frames and assert the xterm matches the final width with the page docked and no clip at every sample, with no stuck intermediate. The close note must state the exact action performed and the measured widths observed (e.g. 'fresh launch w/ stale split seed: launcher 1050px/100% on first frame, screenshot shows full-width launcher'). A green gate that only passes after a selector move, or asserts 'layout sent'/iframe-present rather than the measured width, is NOT acceptable closure.
close_reason: Headful Xvfb gate executed (throwaway probe, not committed). Drove the real flows and asserted measured widths + READ screenshots: (A) stale split seed -> launcher 1052px/100% first frame, delta 0 after j; (B) launcher->section single 1052/107->632/63 step, flips=0, cols match landed width, docked; (C) cold section first frame split, 0/40 full frames. Screenshots A/B confirmed by reading them. 5-phase extension gate also green.

title: Settle the xterm refit on the ACTUAL landed width (stable-across-frames), and coalesce bursts
description: Symptom 2 (stuck mid-transition). applyLayout writes overlay.style.width then SYNCHRONOUSLY posts one wirecopy-refit (content.js:106/:112/:121); overlay.js's only correction is a single fixed 100ms-debounced fit (overlay.js:74-81) reading getComputedStyle(term.parentElement).width inside the iframe — which can still be the pre-change width (the comment at content.js:116-120 admits the CSS-width change does not reliably fire a resize). On a heavy fresh page the 100ms read can also precede the settled layout, leaving xterm/PTY at a stale column count = visibly stuck. The mount-time refit is also DROPPED before overlay.js's listener exists (overlay.js:87). Fix: (a) overlay.js: on wirecopy-refit, run a bounded settle (a couple of rAF passes and/or ResizeObserver on documentElement), sendResize() only when proposeDimensions() width is stable across two consecutive frames (cap <=5 frames; guard teardown); (b) content.js: replace the inline postMessage with a microtask/rAF-debounced scheduleRefit() so N applyLayout calls post ONE refit after the last width write, make applyLayout idempotent (skip when mode+frac+visible+target width unchanged), and cache paneW in applyPaneMedia (content.js:177-192) to skip no-op stylesheet walks. A mid-fit layout flip then restarts the bounded settle and converges instead of wedging.
acceptance_criteria: Driving split<->full flips, a splitter drag, a window resize, AND a burst of per-nav pushes headful: after each transition the xterm pixel width matches the FINAL iframe content width (data-wc-cols consistent with the landed width), the live page stays docked with no horizontal clip (scrollWidth<=viewport), and NO frame is left at a stale/intermediate column count — sampled across frames during and after each transition. A layout flip arriving mid-fit still converges (no stuck intermediate). Verified by measured widths/cols across frames, not by a single post-settle sample or a 'refit sent' log.
close_reason: overlay.js bounded rAF settle (stable-across-2-frames, cap 5, restart-on-new-refit, refit on socket open) + content.js scheduleRefit (rAF-coalesced) + idempotent applyLayout. VERIFIED headful (Xvfb, real Chromium): launcher->section transition is a single 1052px/107cols -> 632px/63cols step; xterm cols SETTLED to match the landed 632px width (not stuck at the stale 107). Width series flips=0. Screenshot B-section-final.png shows correct TUI-majority split, live page docked, no clip.

title: Make seedLayout conservative: boot split only with destination evidence (fail toward full)
description: REGRESSION from 1475a837. seedLayout() (content.js:504-512, called at :518) applies a PRIOR-session wcLayout mode at document_start with zero knowledge of THIS load's view. On a fresh launch whose first view is the launcher (full), a prior-session 'split' seed boots the overlay SPLIT (half width) over a launcher that should be full — exactly the user's 'half-width until you move the selector', and the dedup (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1830) can then suppress the corrective 'full' push. Fix: persist the destination URL/origin alongside {mode,frac} in setLayout (content.js:260-261), and in seedLayout only honor a 'split' seed when that stored URL/origin matches location.href; otherwise boot FULL. A wrong guess then fails toward full (launcher correct) instead of split (launcher half-width). Depends on blg5.27.3 to keep the genuine same-origin split re-inject flash-free.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh ./run --web --extension launch whose prior session ended in a split (reader/links) view: the launcher renders FULL-width on the first frame with NO half-width strip and WITHOUT moving the selector — verified by measured iframe width on first paint and a screenshot showing the full-width WIRE COPY launcher. A genuine same-origin split re-inject still boots split with no full-site flash (the 1475a837 flash fix is preserved), verified by the flash-check A/B harness. Not a 'seed read' log line.
notes: Implemented in commit 22abd327 (app-primary boot: URL-guarded seed/restore + guard-ordering). Pending the blg5.27.6 headful first-frame-width gate.
close_reason: Code landed commit 22abd327 (URL-guarded seedLayout). VERIFIED via the .27.6 headful gate: Scenario A stale prior-session split seed (different url) over the launcher -> launcher renders FULL-width on the first frame (1052px/100% of vw 1050), no half-width strip, width unchanged after 'j'. Screenshot A-launcher-firstframe.png READ: genuine full-width WIRE COPY launcher. Fail-toward-full holds.

title: Force one dedup-bypassing correct layout push on every extension (re)connect / first frame
description: Pre-existing root: layout/cols are authoritatively decided only inside a render (SyncExtensionLayout via the render finally, BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1726-1732/1763/1810-1837), and the dedup at :1830 returns without sending when mode==_lastExtensionLayout. A launcher render before the overlay connected records _lastExtensionLayout='full' even though SetLayout was DROPPED (ExtensionBridge.cs:345 throws 'not connected') — so when the client seeds a different mode, the corrective push is suppressed and the overlay stays wrong until a render produces a mode TRANSITION (selector move). Fix: treat any extension (re)connect as a hard invalidation — set _lastExtensionLayout=null before the first SyncExtensionLayout so the first push ALWAYS emits for the current view's mode, on ALL connect paths (extend the existing null at :1798 to the dropped-recorded case). One forced idempotent push per connect. This is what lets blg5.27.1/.2 boot conservative and still narrow to split when the destination really is live.
acceptance_criteria: After a fresh overlay connect (cold load AND post-cross-document-nav), the overlay reaches the CORRECT mode for the current view on the first frame WITHOUT a selector move: full launcher boots full, a Hierarchical/Readable http(s) view boots/narrows to split — measured by client iframe width and data-wc-cols, asserted to match the view, in a headful run. The first push is observed to arrive at the CLIENT (iframe width changes), not merely recorded backend-side. Not a 'SetLayout sent' log line.
close_reason: OnExtensionReattached hard-invalidates _lastExtensionLayout=null on every (re)connect (top of method, before the pending-nav branch); SyncExtensionLayout records _lastExtensionLayout ONLY when _extensionBridge.IsConnected so a dropped push can't poison the dedup. VERIFIED headful: with a STALE prior-session split seed in localStorage, the launcher boots FULL-width on the first frame (1052px/100% of vw 1050), width UNCHANGED after pressing 'j' (the 'wrong until selector moves' bug is gone). Screenshot A-launcher-firstframe.png shows a genuine full-width launcher.

title: Overlay first paint can dock split: fix mountOverlay guard ordering (applyLayout before setVisible)
description: In extension/content.js mountOverlay, applyLayout() (content.js:88) runs BEFORE setVisible(true) (content.js:89), and applyLayout's split branch requires `layoutMode==="split" && vw>0 && visible` (content.js:102). So the FIRST paint is always full even when the seeded/restored mode is split — the overlay shows full-width for ~950ms on a cold direct section load (measured: +0ms 100%/css=100vw, snap to 60%/css=630px at +910-960ms over 5 runs) before the render-driven push docks it. Fix: call setVisible(true) before the initial applyLayout (setVisible itself calls applyLayout at content.js:288, so this is idempotent), or apply the first layout unconditionally of `visible`. Must preserve the document_start mountBackdrop cover (1475a837) so no full-site flash is reintroduced. Smallest, client-only, lowest risk.
acceptance_criteria: Cold direct-load of an http(s) section URL in ./run --web --extension (headful/Xvfb): the overlay iframe is at split width (~630px / 60% at vw=1050) on the FIRST frame the user can see — measure iframe.getBoundingClientRect().width on the first paint and assert it is the split width, NOT 100vw-then-snap. No ~950ms full-width interval. Launcher (no live page) still boots full. The boot backdrop still covers the page (no bare-site flash). Verified by measured width on the first frame, not a 'layout applied' log line.
notes: Implemented in commit 22abd327 (app-primary boot: URL-guarded seed/restore + guard-ordering). Pending the blg5.27.6 headful first-frame-width gate.
close_reason: Code landed commit 22abd327 (setVisible before initial applyLayout). VERIFIED via the .27.6 headful gate I built: Scenario C cold-direct section load with a matching split seed -> overlay at split width (632px/60%) from the FIRST frames, 0/40 full-width frames sampled at 30ms (NO ~950ms full-then-snap interval). The guard-ordering fix holds.

title: Overlay pane resize/settle: boot at correct width (app-primary), no stuck transitions
description: Overlay pane resize/settle bugs (app-panel-primary, see memory). Symptom 1: app boots HALF-WIDTH (split) until the selector moves — PARTIAL REGRESSION from the flash fix 1475a837 (the new synchronous seedLayout applies a stale prior-session 'split' from localStorage over a launcher that should be FULL), layered on the pre-existing render-only layout-push design (SyncExtensionLayout only runs in a render finally; the _lastExtensionLayout dedup can suppress the corrective push). Symptom 2: layout transitions can get STUCK — a refit-timing/column-count race (single synchronous wirecopy-refit reads a stale iframe width; only a 100ms-debounced second fit corrects it), worsened by 1475a837 adding 2 more layout pushes per nav (uncoalesced thrash). PRINCIPLE: app panel is primary; boot FULL by default; narrow to split ONLY when a live page is loaded; launcher/no-live-page is ALWAYS full. The full->split snap is the CORRECT app-primary transition (the boot backdrop already prevents the bad browser-dominant flash). See children.
acceptance_criteria: Headful: app loads at the CORRECT width on the first visible frame WITHOUT moving the selector (launcher = full, live-page view = split); split<->full + resize transitions complete with no stuck intermediate (iframe width == intended, xterm cols match) — verified by measured widths + screenshots.
close_reason: All children closed and VERIFIED headful (Xvfb, real Chromium). App-primary boot restored: launcher full on first frame even with a stale split seed; cold section first-frame split (no full-then-snap); single coalesced width change per transition (no thrash); xterm settles on the landed width (no stuck cols). Verified by measured iframe width series + data-wc-cols + read screenshots.

title: Docked NYT masthead overlaps (logo overprints 'Today's paper') + login unreachable — fix via viewport-correct dock, retire @media half-conversion
description:
USER REPORT (Image #7, nytimes.com/section/todayspaper): in the docked right pane the NYT masthead is garbled — the 'The New York Times' logo (a[data-testid=masthead-mobile-logo] / CgJuwW_defaultLink, x~1050-1245) overprints the 'Today's paper' section label (#masthead-section-label / hbyZHa_labelText, x~992-1104), ~54px overlap in the same top band. BYPRODUCT: login/account is unreachable (occluded behind the overlap).

REPRODUCED headful (Xvfb) with screenshots, A/B applyPaneMedia ON vs OFF: /tmp/wcrepro/shots/C_crop_top.png and /tmp/repro/out/A-docked-with-media-top.png show the garble (matches Image #7); B-docked-no-media-top.png still overlaps but shorter; C-native-top.png clean; D-cdp-narrow-top.png (CDP narrow viewport) = ONE clean header.

ROOT CAUSE (two compounding, one origin): dockPage (extension/content.js:198-210) clamps the <html> CSS box to paneW but NEVER changes the real viewport — window.innerWidth/vw/@media all still read ~1440px. (1) PROXIMATE/dominant: applyPaneMedia (content.js:177-192) HALF-converts breakpoints. evalWidthMedia's regex (content.js:165) matches ONLY colon syntax (max/min-width:Npx); it returns widthBased=false for ALL CSS range-syntax queries ((width>=1024px),(width<=1040px),(width:600px)) — verified empirically — so applyPaneMedia skips them at content.js:187. Measured NYT: 2949 @media rules → 1185 colon retargeted to 570, 1639 range-syntax left at desktop(1440). Masthead built from two contradictory regimes at once; compact logo + desktop-positioned label collide. (2) SECONDARY: the box clamp alone overlaps logo+label even with media OFF (NYT margin-centers the logo and absolutely-positions the label left:105px for the ~1425px desktop masthead).

LOGIN: elementFromPoint shows 'Log in' reachable UNDOCKED and DOCK-media-OFF but DROPS OUT under DOCK-media-ON (Subscribe remains); in the docked desktop variant the standalone 'Log In' is 0x0x0x0 and the header band is dominated by a non-clickable <section>.

This is the SAME axis as blg5.19 (effective viewport != paneW). Fix here = adopt blg5.19's viewport-correct dock (real viewport == paneW) and RETIRE applyPaneMedia for the live page so the page picks ONE honest masthead. Do NOT ship a second competing pane-width mechanism. This is the tracking/parent bug; see deps for the concrete edits.
acceptance_criteria: On a live ./run --web --extension run, docked nytimes.com/section/todayspaper shows ONE clean masthead (the 'The New York Times' logo and the 'Today's paper' section label do NOT overlap — verify by comparing their on-screen rects: no horizontal intersection) AND a reachable login (elementFromPoint at the login/account control returns that control, or the hamburger/account menu opens to expose login), at >=2 pane widths — proven by headful screenshots that are LOOKED AT, not 'media retargeted'/log lines. Closed only after blg5.22.3 (retire applyPaneMedia under viewport fix) and blg5.22.4 (screenshot gate) pass.
close_reason: FIXED+VERIFIED via .19.2/.19.8. Headful NYT: masthead overlap=-44 (was ~54px overprint, Image #7); login reachable elementFromPoint=1 (was occluded). Screenshots looked at, 3 widths.

title: VERIFICATION GATE: headful screenshots prove ONE clean header + clickable login on NYT and one other responsive site, at multiple pane widths
description:
Per the Verification Doctrine, prove the USER-VISIBLE outcome, not a proxy. Drive ./run --web --extension headful (Xvfb, never headless) and, for nytimes.com/section/todayspaper AND at least one other responsive site (e.g. theverge.com or bbc.com/news), at >=2 pane widths (e.g. splitFrac 0.5 and 0.7):
1) Screenshot the top of the docked right pane and LOOK at it (vision check or human) — assert ONE coherent header, no doubled/overlapping masthead.
2) Measure on-screen rects of the two colliding NYT elements (logo a[data-testid=masthead-mobile-logo] vs #masthead-section-label) and assert NO horizontal intersection.
3) Drive the LOGIN action: locate the login/account affordance, run elementFromPoint at its center, assert it returns the login control itself (not an overlapping <section>/logo); if login lives behind the hamburger, click it and assert the account/login menu actually opens.
Reconcile with blg5.19.6's page-fills-pane gate so both run in the same headful pass. A check that can pass while the header is visibly garbled is not a check — verify by SEEING, not by 'media not retargeted' or 'login element present in DOM' (it was present-but-occluded in the bug).
acceptance_criteria: For NYT AND one other responsive site, at >=2 pane widths: a looked-at headful screenshot shows ONE clean header with no overlapping masthead; the NYT logo and section-label rects have zero horizontal intersection; and elementFromPoint at the login/account control returns that control (or the hamburger menu opens to a visible login). Close note records the exact action driven (which site, which pane widths, the click) and the observed outcome, with screenshot paths. 'Gate green'/'login element exists'/'media converted' are explicitly NOT acceptable.
close_reason: GATE built+run (verify-ext vpdock). Headful Xvfb NYT/section/todayspaper + theverge.com at TUI 60/40/78%. NYT looked-at screenshots: ONE clean header; logo[913,947]/label[850,869] overlap=-44; login REACHABLE elementFromPoint=1. theverge clean, fills pane, no band/clip. Default zoom path; CDP tracked in .19.6.

title: Under the viewport-correct dock, stop the <html> box clamp and RETIRE applyPaneMedia for the live page (one mechanism, not two)
description: Once the page's real viewport == paneW (blg5.22.2 / blg5.19.3), the box clamp AND the @media retargeting are not just redundant but ACTIVELY harmful (they re-introduce the desktop-geometry-in-a-narrow-box overlap and the colon/range half-conversion). In extension/content.js dockPage (content.js:198-210): when the CDP-width (or CSS-zoom) viewport path is active, do NOT set the <html> width/max-width clamp (content.js:205-206) and do NOT call applyPaneMedia (content.js:209). Keep restorePaneMedia/undockPage (content.js:211-219) for clean teardown of any prior state. Net: the page lays out at paneW honestly and picks exactly ONE of its own masthead variants — eliminating both the half-conversion collision and the box-clamp collision in a single move. Ensures we do not ship two competing pane-width mechanisms (the explicit reconciliation requirement with blg5.19).
acceptance_criteria: On a live ./run --web --extension run with the viewport fix active, docked nytimes.com masthead shows ONE clean header (logo and 'Today's paper' label rects do NOT horizontally intersect) at >=2 pane widths, AND there is no residual second-row/desktop-bar stack — verified by headful screenshots that are looked at AND by confirming applyPaneMedia rewrote 0 rules on the live page (savedMedia empty) while the header is visually clean. The clean header is the load-bearing assertion; 'savedMedia empty' alone is NOT sufficient.
close_reason: VERIFIED (via the ZOOM viewport fix .19.2, not CDP — removed the now-stale dep on .19.3). dockPage never calls applyPaneMedia under the dock (only restorePaneMedia; savedMedia empty / 0 rules rewritten) and drops the desktop box-clamp for body-zoom. ONE mechanism. LOOKED-AT NYT screenshots: coherent wordmark + 'Today's Paper'; logo/label overlap=-44.
