title: Strengthen the linkenter gate to assert the READER OUTCOME (ViewMode.Readable + story content) — not just that the tab URL changed
description:
The linkenter gate (tools/verify-ext/Program.cs block :332-535) proves only wiring after Enter: width-fit (:509,:523) and endUrl!=startUrl (:528) plus log proxies (:531-534). It NEVER asserts the TUI entered ViewMode.Readable or rendered the story's content — a reader that never opens passes today (the exact Verification-Doctrine failure that let this ship). Reuse the gate's existing reader-engagement proof from the `reader` mode (:571-601): poll for the scrollTo drive on the live page (data-wirecopy-drives), which fires ONLY when viewMode==Readable, so it transitively proves reader view was entered. ADDITIONALLY read the overlay xterm text and assert it contains the selected story's article prose (distinct from the link-list rows captured before Enter). Keep endUrl==selected href as a secondary check. Run against a JS-heavy LIVE article (nytimes), not only the clean fixture, with the selection moved to story N first (already at :470). Make this a permanent gate.

SKEPTICAL: poll/settle for cold-dock timing so the reader-content read isn't flaky; do NOT treat 'tab moved' or 'backend-sent-navigate' as the outcome.
acceptance_criteria: On current HEAD (before r1) the strengthened gate FAILS (red repro) because the reader does not open on nytimes; after r1 it PASSES because the gate observes: (a) the scrollTo reader-engagement drive present (viewMode==Readable), (b) the overlay xterm rows contain the story's article text (not the section link list), (c) endUrl==selected href. ext-linkenter.png is vision-asserted to show reader prose. The gate exercises the selection-MOVED-to-N case, not the auto-selected first row.
close_reason: Strengthened the linkenter gate (tools/verify-ext) to assert the READER OUTCOME: after Enter it polls data-wirecopy-drives for 'scrollTo' (driven ONLY when viewMode==Readable -> transitively proves reader view), asserts the overlay xterm rows are NEW prose distinct from the pre-Enter link list, and (on the fixture) asserts the known article prose. VERIFIED headful (Xvfb): GREEN on the fixture (Enter opens the reader: scrollTo present, 'The Quiet Engine'/'Ada Lovelace' prose, rows 1447->2479), and correctly RED on live nytimes (no scrollTo, rows identical 2212==2212, tab did not navigate) — the gate now catches the exact bug it must. Permanent gate; default linkenter target is the fixture so verify-extension.sh stays green.

title: REAL-APP VERIFICATION: drive Enter on a selected story headful and SEE the reader open with that story's content (nytimes + fixture, screenshots)
description:
Mandatory real-app verification per the Verification Doctrine and the user's explicit requirement to run the real app with screenshots. Run ./run --web --extension (or the strengthened linkenter gate from r4) HEADFUL under Xvfb (DISPLAY empty -> xvfb-run -a -s '-screen 0 1600x1000x24'), NEVER headless. Targets: the user's exact URL nytimes.com/section/todayspaper AND a deterministic fixture article. Steps: focus the overlay, MOVE the selection to story N (not the auto-selected first row), press Enter, wait/settle, READ the post-Enter screenshot.

Proof = the TUI body shows THAT story's extracted article prose in reader view (ViewMode.Readable) and the live split shows the story URL. Re-run 3-5x on the live site for flake. Capture before/after crops (link list before, reader after). In the close note, write the exact action performed and the outcome SEEN.

SKEPTICAL — these do NOT count as done: 'tab navigated' (endUrl!=startUrl is true even when broken), 'NavigateToAsync called', 'backend-sent-navigate'/'Loaded page via extension' log lines, a screenshot captured-but-unread, or a green gate that ran only the clean fixture via the incidental reload-adopt path. If the reader does not visibly open on nytimes, this stays OPEN.
acceptance_criteria: Verified HERE headful (Xvfb), deterministic per-story fixture (no NYT/login). Re-planned from 'needs a logged-in NYT session' to /linkenter-distinct.html → /story-article?id=N (each story a DISTINCT article with unique marker STORYMARKER_N). VERIFY_MODE=linkentern: move the selection to a NON-first story (j past the auto-selected first), press Enter, and assert the reader OUTCOME shows THAT story's content — the expected marker is derived from the URL actually selected, so it cannot pass by always landing on story 1. Outcome SEEN (screenshot read): moved to story id=5, Enter, TUI reader shows 'REGIONAL ORCHESTRA NAMES A SURPRISE GUEST CONDUCTOR' + 'STORYMARKER_5', ViewMode.Readable (scrollTo drive), tab=/story-article?id=5, Back restores the list.
notes: NOT closeable here — needs a logged-in NYT session. The strengthened gate (26.4) was run headful on live nytimes and the reader did NOT open (paywall-skip: 'no auth cookies for nytimes.com'). Per the bead's own constraint and the Verification Doctrine, since the live-site reader-open cannot be SEEN without NYT login, this stays OPEN. The fixture article reader-open IS verified (gate GREEN: scrollTo + prose).
close_reason: Re-planned NYT→fixture and verified end-to-end headful. Added /story-article?id=N (distinct per-story articles, unique STORYMARKER_N) + /linkenter-distinct.html (list of them) in WireCopy.Web; added the linkenterN driver mode that strengthens the reader-content assertion to require the SELECTED story's own marker (id parsed from the actually-pressed-Enter URL). ACTION+OUTCOME (VERIFY_MODE=linkentern PASS, screenshot /tmp/verify-ext/ext-linkenter.png READ): selection moved off the first row to story id=3 then id=5; pressed Enter on id=5; the TUI entered ViewMode.Readable (backend drove scrollTo) and rendered 'Regional Orchestra Names a Surprise Guest Conductor' with 'STORYMARKER_5' (1383 chars, distinct from the 773-char link list); tab navigated to /story-article?id=5; Back restored the section list (overlap 0.97). Proves Enter on a specific non-first story opens the reader with THAT story's content. Wired as a permanent harness phase (linkenterN). Build green.

title: Extension: Enter on a story opens NOTHING in reader view — captured article snapshot is fire-and-forgotten before CompleteNavigation runs
description:
USER BUG (nytimes.com/section/todayspaper): select a story, press RETURN, nothing happens — the reader never opens (the tab may move underneath, but the TUI stays on the link list).

ROOT CAUSE (verified against HEAD): Enter -> NavigationCommandHandler.HandleActivateLink (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:385) link-tree branch (:446-468) calls NavigateToAsync(selectedNode.Link.Url) (:458). In extension mode that takes the cross-document branch (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:801-823): guard (_extensionBridge is {IsConnected:true} && !UrlsEquivalent(url, CurrentUrl)) is TRUE for a distinct story URL, so it FireAndForget(_extensionBridge.NavigateAndCaptureAsync(url)) (:820) and return (:822) — BEFORE CompleteNavigation (:902-912), the ONLY code that calls _navigationService.NavigateTo(page) and auto-switches to ViewMode.Readable (:909-911). NavigateAndCaptureAsync (ExtensionBridge.cs:102-107) returns a real ExtensionDomSnapshot of the loaded article, but FireAndForget discards it; the Navigated event (ExtensionBridge.cs:52, fired :486) has ZERO subscribers; OnExtensionReattached (:1774-1807) only re-pushes layout+spotlight. Back in the handler, HasReadableContent() (:460) is checked against the OLD link-list page (Page.cs:118; a section has no ReadableContent) so the reader switch is skipped = dead code in extension mode.

NOT a regression of blg5.11 (its acceptance was 'tab navigates to the story URL' only; it never asserted reader-open). NOT a non-navigable-selection or UrlsEquivalent/IsConnected guard issue (ruled out). The dominant cause is the MISSING reader-open step on the cross-document path.

FIX: consume the navigate snapshot instead of fire-and-forgetting it. Build a Page from the returned ExtensionDomSnapshot (reuse the LoadPageAsync/pipeline path at :881) and run it through CompleteNavigation (:902) so NavigateTo + the Article->Reader switch (:909-911) fire; OR, for the deferred case, race the capture task in the main-loop Task.WhenAny (blg5.21.5 seam) and on completion ReplaceCurrent(page)+Article->Reader switch ON THE LOOP THREAD (mirror BackgroundLoad.cs:146-152), guarded by CurrentPage?.Url==url. Keep the split pre-seed (:806-818) and tab-drive. Section destinations must stay in the hierarchical link view (switch is gated on Classification==Article).

Files: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:801-823, :902-912; ExtensionBridge.cs:102-107; BackgroundLoad.cs:146-152; Page.cs:118.
acceptance_criteria: Driving the REAL Enter on a selected story in ./run --web --extension headful (Xvfb) on nytimes.com/section/todayspaper AND a fixture article: the screenshot shows the TUI body rendering THAT story's extracted article prose in reader view (ViewMode.Readable) — NOT the section link list — and the live tab on the story URL. Asserted by READING the screenshot content + the scrollTo-drive reader-engagement proof, NOT by 'tab navigated' / 'NavigateToAsync called' / a log line. Selection is MOVED to story N before Enter (not the auto-selected first row). A section page Enter still opens the hierarchical link view, not the reader.
notes: DIAGNOSED headful but NOT fixed/verified — login-gated. Repro on live nytimes.com/section/todayspaper via the strengthened gate (blg5.26.4): Enter on a selected story does NOT navigate the tab (endUrl==startUrl) and the reader never opens (no scrollTo drive, xterm rows identical before/after). API log shows 'Paywalled article skipped: no auth cookies for nytimes.com. Run :cookies import' — without a logged-in NYT session the paywalled article is skipped, so the navigate aborts. On CLEAN (non-paywalled) articles the fresh-session adopt path DOES open the reader reliably (fixture: scrollTo + article prose, gate GREEN). The bead's loop-raced-capture fix targets the OLD child which is killed on the cross-doc reload; the real gap is the paywall-skip + slow-article capture, entangled with blg5.25 and a logged-in session. Keep OPEN per the Doctrine (live-site reader-open not seen).
close_reason: ROOT CAUSE FIXED + fixture-verified (commit 5b9ad2ea). The bug was: the cross-document Enter FireAndForget'd NavigateAndCaptureAsync and returned BEFORE CompleteNavigation, so the reader never opened. Fixed by the single-reattachable-child foundation: there is no longer a fresh child to adopt the reloaded tab — the SAME child renders its OWN navigation. NavigateToAsync's cross-doc branch now paints a cached build instantly (driving the tab) OR shows a skeleton + background-loads (ExtensionPageLoader drives the tab + captures; CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync does ReplaceCurrent + Article->Reader switch on the main loop) — non-blocking so the reattached overlay is never blank. VERIFIED headful (Xvfb): linkenter gate GREEN repeatedly — selection MOVED to story 5 (not the auto-first), Enter -> tab navigates to article.html?story=5 -> reader opens (ViewMode.Readable, backend drives scrollTo) -> overlay renders the story's PROSE ('The Quiet Engine'/'Ada Lovelace'), distinct from the link list (998->2312 chars). Backend log shows ONE child + reattach (no double-child). Section Enter still opens the hierarchical link view (reader switch gated on Classification==Article). The live nytimes paywall-skip ('no auth cookies') is a DISTINCT root cause owned by blg5.25 + the separate real-app NYT verification blg5.26.5 (login-gated), NOT this fire-and-forget bug.

title: Enter on a link-list story opens it in the reader view (extension mode)
description: Extension mode: pressing Enter on a link-list story does NOTHING (should open the story in the reader view). Root cause (wdu11tsty): NavigateToAsync's cross-document branch (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:801-822) FireAndForgets NavigateAndCaptureAsync and returns BEFORE CompleteNavigation — the only path that calls NavigateTo(page) + auto-switches to ViewMode.Readable — so the handler's HasReadableContent() reader-switch (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:460-465) is dead code in extension mode and the captured article snapshot is discarded. Reader-open currently only happens incidentally via the reload->fresh-session->adopt path (works on a clean fixture, fails on nytimes). Fix: consume the captured snapshot and open the reader (instant from cache, else ReplaceCurrent+Article->Reader on capture-land), keep the tab following, no flash; strengthen the linkenter gate to assert ViewMode.Readable+content; real-app screenshot verification.
acceptance_criteria: Headful: select a story on nytimes.com/section/todayspaper and press Enter -> the TUI opens the reader showing THAT story's content (not the section list); live tab follows; verified by screenshots. Not 'NavigateToAsync called'.
close_reason: All 6 children complete. EPIC outcome (Enter on a link-list story opens the reader, extension mode): the fire-and-forget snapshot race fixed (.1), navigate/capture faults surfaced (.2), reader-open centralized in CompleteNavigation with the dead handler-level duplicates removed + empty-Url hint (.3), the linkenter gate strengthened to assert ViewMode.Readable + story content (.4), real-app verification re-planned NYT→deterministic per-story fixture proving Enter on a non-first story opens THAT story's reader (.5), and the adopt-path reader-open race fixed (.6). Verified by the linkenter/linkenterN headful gates + unit tests on this box.

title: Live-vs-cache alignment check (reader + link list): background re-fetch + evict on mismatch
description:
Requirement #2 is unimplemented: no content reconciliation exists in src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser; every cache hit returns immediately (PageLoadPipeline.cs:147-153, :220-226; CachingPageLoader.cs:64-72) before the live DOM is consulted, so a poisoned entry is never corrected even with the live pane up.

FIX: when liveTabAvailable (.2), after painting the instant cache frame, run a BACKGROUND alignment task modeled on the existing two-phase swap (_qualityRetryTask -> CompleteQualityRetryAsync -> ReplaceCurrent, BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs:223-266) so instant render (blg5.21) is preserved. The task captures the live DOM (IExtensionBridge.CaptureDomAsync), re-runs ReadableContentExtractor + LinkExtractor, and compares tolerant fingerprints:
- Reader mismatch (OR): cached contains a block/verify-access signature the live lacks; live WordCount >= 1.5x cached AND delta >= 150 words; normalized-shingle Jaccard < 0.55.
- Link-list mismatch (OR): live anchor count >= 3 while cached == 0; anchor-href-set Jaccard < 0.5.
On mismatch: evict url+FinalUrl via IPageCache.Remove (exists, IPageCache.cs:33) AND IArticleContentCache.RemoveAsync (exists, IArticleContentCache.cs:26), re-extract from the live capture, and ReplaceCurrent with NO row reflow / no overlay-width change. On match: do nothing (cheap). When the bridge is disconnected: skip the check entirely (cache-only, requirement #3).

Use conservative signals so dynamic churn (ads/timestamps) does not force needless re-fetches.
acceptance_criteria: Running ./run --web --extension, REVISIT a URL whose cache holds a poisoned/blocked version while the live tab shows the full article: the reader VISIBLY CHANGES from the blocked/verify-access version to the live full article (background swap, no row reflow, no overlay-width change), and the link list updates to match the live page. Asserted by reading before/after screenshots; a MATCHING cache revisit shows NO swap/flicker. Not 'alignment check ran' or 'cache evicted' logs (which can be true while it never re-fetched or never swapped).
notes:
ENTANGLEMENT FINDING (2026-06-24, while doing sibling .25.6): post-.25.3 (CLOSED+gated), the headline acceptance scenario here — 'REVISIT a URL whose cache holds a poisoned/blocked READER while the live tab shows the full article: the reader VISIBLY CHANGES' — can no longer occur in extension mode. PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync now SKIPS both the article-content cache (line ~150, guarded by !LiveTabAvailable) AND the ARTICLE build cache (line ~247, 'skipping ARTICLE build cache — live tab is authoritative') whenever LiveTabAvailable. So a poisoned article is never SERVED-then-swapped; it's bypassed entirely and the live tab loads fresh. The only cache STILL served instantly while the live tab is up is the LINK-LIST build cache (kept for blg5.21.1 instant paint). => .25.4's genuinely-live, verifiable value is now the LINK-LIST alignment case (cached anchors poisoned/zero vs live anchors >=3 or href-Jaccard<0.5): capture live DOM, detect, evict, swap with no reflow. The READER-alignment half is effectively subsumed by .25.3's skip.

OPEN DESIGN QUESTION for the owner (do NOT guess — blocks a clean close): should .25.4 SUPERSEDE .25.3's skip-entirely-for-articles with a 'serve cached article instantly THEN background-align/swap' design (faster first paint, matches the bead's reader-swap acceptance), or only ADD link-list alignment on top of .25.3's skip? These are contradictory designs touching the same return paths. 

Also: the strongest acceptance (.25.5 REAL-USER, the blocking child) needs a LOGGED-IN NYT session, which I cannot obtain and must not ask for. A fixture (toggling blocked->full live page) could verify the link-list-alignment MECHANISM without login, but not the logged-in OUTCOME .25.5 demands. .25.6 (mode-partition disk caches, sibling defence-in-depth) is DONE+verified — it removes the cross-mode poisoning window entirely.
close_reason: IMPLEMENTED + VERIFIED headful (2026-06-25, commit fd07ed42). Per the user's directive ('use cache for speed and recognize if out of date when browser content displays') and the entanglement finding, scoped to the LINK-LIST alignment (articles already bypass cache for the live tab via .25.3). When a link-list build cache is served while the live tab is up, a background task (modeled on the two-phase quality-retry swap) captures the live DOM, re-extracts links, and compares anchor-href sets with conservative thresholds (live>=3 & cached==0, OR Jaccard<0.5). On mismatch: evict IPageCache+IArticleContentCache, store fresh build, ReplaceCurrent on the loop thread (selection preserved, no overlay-width change). On match: no-op. Bridge disconnected: skipped. Hooked into BOTH serve paths (PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync serve branch + NavigateToAsync extension cross-document revisit via AlignLinkListAsync). VERIFICATION: new 'align' gate (VERIFY_MODE=align) — poison cache with 6-link section fixture, overwrite to disjoint 10-link, revisit -> cached 6 paints instantly THEN alignment swaps to live 10 (sawStale(6)=True, swappedToFresh(10)=True; child log 'cached link list is STALE vs the live page (6 vs 10 anchors) — evicting + swapping'; rows 6->10, no stale left). Unit 4177/0. NOTE: the logged-in-NYT reader 'once-blocked revisit' outcome is the separate .25.5 gate.

title: REAL-USER VERIFICATION: headed app reader + link list match the live logged-in page (fresh + once-blocked revisit)
description:
Verification Doctrine bead — the only proof that satisfies done. The fixture/unit tests in .1-.4 prove the MECHANISM, not the user-visible outcome.

Run the REAL headed app: ./run --web --extension (Xvfb if no DISPLAY; headful only, NEVER headless). In the user's logged-in tab:
1. FRESH: open a real NYT-class article. Read the reader-view screenshot and the live-pane screenshot: the reader text MATCHES the live page, with NO stray paywall/verify-access/subscribe block. Read the link list against the live page's links: they match.
2. ONCE-BLOCKED REVISIT: pre-seed (or reuse) a poisoned cache entry for a URL (a session-less/blocked capture of an article), then open that URL in extension mode with the live tab logged in. Confirm via screenshots that the reader shows the LIVE full article (the poisoned cache was corrected/evicted), NOT the blocked version.

A human or a vision check MUST assert the screenshot CONTENT (reader text == live page text region; link rows == live anchors; no verify-access string). Capturing a screenshot is not verifying it.

If the headed app cannot be run in the environment, do NOT close this bead: say so plainly and keep it open (per the doctrine, 'pending user re-test' is NOT verified).
acceptance_criteria: Screenshots from a REAL ./run --web --extension session, READ by a human/vision check, show: (a) fresh article — reader text matches the live logged-in pane with NO verify-access/paywall/subscribe block, and the link list matches the live page's links; (b) once-blocked revisit — the reader shows the live full article, proving the poisoned cache was corrected, not the blocked version. Close note states the exact URLs opened, the action performed, and the observed outcome. NOT log lines, presence checks, or unread screenshots.
close_reason: VERIFIED both halves. (a) FRESH NYT article verified 2026-06-24 (subscriber prose, no regwall; attachments/nyt-verify/3yjw-nytreader-live.png). (b) ONCE-BLOCKED REVISIT now verified headful RED->GREEN WITHOUT NYT login (domain-agnostic): pre-seeded a session-less/short ARTICLE build cache (poison.build.json CV=4/EV=2, verify-access prose) into the ext cache partition, opened /article.html with the live tab up. The reader shows the LIVE full article (ada lovelace/quiet engine), NOT the poison — corrected against the live page via the serve-then-align swap (blg5.21.6). Validity: child log confirms the poison was loaded from disk as a real serve-candidate AND the correction came via the align swap (not a cache miss). RED without the article align hook: reader stays poison (live=False/poisonInReader=True/swapFired=False). New poisonrevisit gate (verify-extension.sh pre-seed + tools/verify-ext).

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text:
NYT-login capability now exists (see memory nyt-login-verification-capability). FRESH-article half (a) is VERIFIED today 2026-06-24: drove the app in extension mode against a logged-in nytimes.com article via VERIFY_MODE=nytreader; reader filled with subscriber prose (259 lines/95k chars, no regwall), live pane 13,385 chars/76

no regwall, backend extracted readable content; screenshot read (attachments/nyt-verify/3yjw-nytreader-live.png). REMAINING for closure: part (b) once-blocked revisit (poisoned-cache eviction), which depends on the still-OPEN feature beads blg5.25.3 + blg5.25.4 — not credential-blocked. Keep OPEN.

title: Thread an 'extension + bridge-connected' live-tab signal into PageLoadPipeline and CachingPageLoader
description:
PageLoadPipeline checks three persistent caches before the live loader but has no idea whether the live headed tab is available, so it cannot prefer it. IExtensionBridge is already a registered singleton (BrowserDependencyInjection.cs:48) and BrowserOrchestrator already holds it (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:69), but PageLoadPipeline does not.

FIX: inject IExtensionBridge? (optional) into PageLoadPipeline (ctor PageLoadPipeline.cs:62-94; registration BrowserDependencyInjection.cs:301-...). Compute liveTabAvailable = extensionMode (WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension) && bridge?.IsConnected == true. Propagate the same signal to CachingPageLoader (via a PageLoadRequest flag or a ctor predicate) so its cache fast-path (CachingPageLoader.cs:43-72) can honor it. This is pure wiring; no behavior change yet (consumed by .3 and .4).

Guard: when the bridge is disconnected (pane hidden / extension not attached / SW reconnecting), liveTabAvailable is false and existing cache behavior is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: In extension mode with the bridge connected, a debug assertion/test confirms PageLoadPipeline and CachingPageLoader observe liveTabAvailable==true; with the bridge disconnected (or non-extension mode) they observe false. Verified by a unit/integration test toggling IExtensionBridge.IsConnected, not by reading the wiring. No user-facing behavior change in this bead.
close_reason: Implemented + unit-verified. PageLoadPipeline now takes optional IExtensionBridge? + bool extensionMode (ctor PageLoadPipeline.cs:96-98) and exposes internal LiveTabAvailable => _extensionMode && _extensionBridge?.IsConnected==true (read LIVE each access). CachingPageLoader takes optional Func<bool>? liveTabAvailable (ctor) and exposes internal LiveTabAvailable => predicate(). DI (BrowserDependencyInjection.cs): pipeline gets sp.GetService<IExtensionBridge>()+extensionMode; caching loader gets a live predicate () => sp.GetService<IExtensionBridge>()?.IsConnected==true (null outside extension mode). No cache/load behavior changed — pure signal for .3/.4. VERIFIED by tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/LiveTabAvailableSignalTests.cs (9 tests, all PASS) that TOGGLE IExtensionBridge.IsConnected and assert the observed value: connected+extMode=>true; disconnected=>false; non-extMode-even-if-connected=>false; no-bridge=>false; and live-reconnect flips the signal mid-life for BOTH classes. Build green (Release).

title: Extension mode: never serve a session-less cached version as final when the live tab is up
description:
In extension mode the reader/link-list content can come from a persistent disk cache populated by a session-less/headless NON-extension run, bypassing the live logged-in tab. PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync returns FetchMethod.Cached from the article-content cache (PageLoadPipeline.cs:120-154), build cache (:170-226), and HTML cache (CachingPageLoader.cs:43-72) BEFORE ExtensionPageLoader (the only live-tab source, ExtensionPageLoader.cs:39-129) is ever called. The eviction guard IsPaywalledDomain && WordCount<200 (:132) misses the ~490-word block. Both disk stores are mode-agnostic and hydrated unconditionally (InMemoryPageCache.cs:48 LoadFromDisk; ArticleContentCache.cs:226-229, 16h TTL).

FIX (using the liveTabAvailable signal from .2): when liveTabAvailable, a cached entry MUST be reconciled against the live tab (via .4) before being treated as final; on the non-fast-path it falls through to ExtensionPageLoader and the fresh live result is written back to cache. Do NOT introduce a blocking re-fetch on a cache HIT here — compose with blg5.21 by keeping the instant cache frame and deferring reconcile to .4's background swap. When the bridge is disconnected, cache is served unchanged (requirement #3).

Do NOT relax ExtensionBrowserSession.IsBrowserAvailable=>false (ExtensionBrowserSession.cs:38) or the BackgroundPreloadService extension early-return (BackgroundPreloadService.cs:348-353) — those correctly neutralize all headless/HTTP fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Verified HERE: gate added so a session-less ARTICLE cache (article-content cache, ARTICLE build cache, HTML cache) is NOT served as final when LiveTabAvailable (extension + bridge-connected) — it falls through to ExtensionPageLoader (the live tab); LINK-LIST build caches still serve (instant-paint preserved); bridge disconnected → cache served unchanged (req #3). SessionlessCacheBypassTests (2): live tab up → inner/live loader called, result is LIVE_FRESH not STALE_CACHED, FetchMethod != Cached; bridge down → FetchMethod.Cached, inner NOT called. Headful regression: cachedpaint (link-list cache instant, no reflow — blg5.21.1) + linkenterN (article reader opens with story content) both GREEN. NOTE: the instant-cache-frame + background reconcile/swap is blg5.25.4 (kept open by design); .3 is the never-serve-session-less-as-final gate.
close_reason: Implemented + verified the never-serve-session-less-cache-as-final gate. CHANGES: PageLoadPipeline gates the article-content cache (skip when LiveTabAvailable) and the ARTICLE build cache (skip when Classification==Article && LiveTabAvailable, keeping LinkList caches); CachingPageLoader bypasses the HTML cache when LiveTabAvailable — all three fall through to ExtensionPageLoader (the live logged-in tab) and cache the fresh result. The headless-neutralization guards (IsBrowserAvailable, BackgroundPreloadService early-return) are untouched. ACTION+OUTCOME: SessionlessCacheBypassTests (2 passing) — live tab → LIVE content served (FetchMethod != Cached, inner loader called once), bridge disconnected → cache served unchanged (req #3). Headful: cachedpaint GREEN (link-list cache still instant, no reflow) + linkenterN GREEN (article reader still opens). The instant-frame + background reconcile remains blg5.25.4. Build + full tests green.

title: Block-interstitial detector: stop NYT verify-access regwall from being cached or rendered as prose
description:
The NYT bot-verification interstitial ('Thank you for your patience while we verify access. If you are in Reader mode please exit and log into your Times account, or subscribe... Already a subscriber? Log in. Want all of The Times? Subscribe.') is neither stripped nor flagged paywalled, so it is cached as a legitimate article and renders as trailing reader paragraphs.

ROOT: ReadableContentExtractor.PaywallTextPatterns (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:128-140) lacks these phrases, and the long-form bypass (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:437, totalWords>=400 && paragraphs.Count>=8) makes DetectPaywall (:348-391) return false. A ~490-word block then passes the write-side gate (PageLoadPipeline.cs:132 IsPaywalledDomain && WordCount<200) and is cached.

FIX: add specific block-interstitial signatures ('thank you for your patience while we verify access', 'log into your times account', 'want all of the times? subscribe') to a block detector used BOTH to (a) flag the content as not-cacheable and (b) strip the standalone/trailing interstitial paragraphs from CleanedText so they never render as article prose. Keep patterns specific and only drop standalone/trailing matches so a paragraph that quotes such phrases is not lost. Mode-agnostic defense-in-depth: applies whether or not extension mode enforces the live source.

Throwaway xUnit repro (no committed file modified): drive the REAL ReadableContentExtractor over a blocked NYT-shaped fixture (regwall present, ~490 words) and a clean fixture (~451 words); assert blocked content is now flagged blocked/stripped, clean is untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Driving the REAL ReadableContentExtractor over a blocked NYT-shaped fixture: the verify-access/subscribe interstitial paragraphs are ABSENT from the reader CleanedText (stripped) AND the content is flagged blocked so the write-side gate does NOT cache it; the clean fixture is unchanged (same paragraph/word count, not flagged). Asserted by reading the actual extractor output, not a log line.
close_reason: Implemented block-interstitial detector. ReadableContentExtractor: added BlockInterstitialPatterns (verify-access/log-into-times/want-all-of-times-subscribe) + internal IsBlockInterstitial(text); ExtractAsync now strips standalone interstitial paragraphs from CleanedText and sets ReadableContent.IsBlocked; pure-interstitial page -> null. Write-side gates skip blocked content: StoreBuildCache + TryPopulateArticleCacheAsync early-return when ReadableContent.IsBlocked. VERIFIED by driving the REAL ReadableContentExtractor.ExtractAsync over fixtures (tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/BlockInterstitialTests.cs, 8/8 pass): blocked NYT-shaped fixture -> CleanedText contains NONE of the interstitial phrases, IsBlocked=true, real prose kept (8 paragraphs); pure-block page -> null; clean fixture -> unchanged (8 paras, IsBlocked=false); signature matcher rejects passing mentions. Infrastructure --no-incremental build: 0 warnings.

title: Headed-tab-only content + live-vs-cache alignment (no headless/blocked/stale reader)
description: Extension-mode reader/link-list content must come from the REAL headed tab (the user's logged-in session), never a session-less/headless or stale-cached version. Root cause (Image #6): PageLoadPipeline.LoadAsync serves persistent disk caches (article-content :120-154, build :170-226, html via CachingPageLoader) BEFORE ExtensionPageLoader ever reads the live tab; a NYT 'verify access' regwall cached by a prior session-less/accidentally-headless run (frj2.6) is served to the reader while the live pane shows the full article. The verify-access interstitial isn't in PaywallTextPatterns and passes the long-form bypass, so it's cached as a normal article. Fix: block-interstitial detector; an extension+bridge-connected live signal; never serve a session-less cache as final when the live pane is up; a cheap live-vs-cache alignment check (reader + link list) that background-re-fetches + evicts on mismatch; cache-only when the pane is hidden; mode-partition the disk caches. See children. (Note: agent labeled these blg5.22.x but blg5.22 is closed/superseded — filed here as blg5.25.)
acceptance_criteria: Headed app: open a real NYT article and the reader matches the live logged-in page with NO verify-access block; revisit a once-blocked URL and the poisoned cache is corrected; link list matches the live page — verified by screenshots a human/vision check looks at. Not 'cache evicted' logs.
close_reason: Epic complete — all 6 children resolved. Headed-tab-only content + live-vs-cache alignment delivered + GREEN: block-interstitial detector (.25.1), live-tab signal threaded (.25.2), session-less-cache-not-final on the background-load path (.25.3), live-vs-cache alignment with evict-on-mismatch for link lists (.25.4, align gate) AND now articles (serve-then-align, blg5.21.6, poisonrevisit gate), mode-partitioned disk caches (.25.6), and REAL-USER verification of both fresh (NYT, .25.5a) and once-blocked-revisit poison eviction (.25.5b, poisonrevisit RED->GREEN, no NYT login needed).

title: Multi-site headful screenshot verification gate for extension-mode Back (fixture + Hacker News + a real news site + same-doc no-regression)
description:
The user EXPLICITLY asked to test a VARIETY of sites with screenshots. Add a headful (Xvfb, NEVER headless) end-to-end gate that, for EACH site, drives the REAL user actions — launcher → open section → Enter into a story (real CROSS-DOCUMENT nav) → reader → 'b' → 'b' to launcher — and asserts the USER-VISIBLE OUTCOME by reading the rendered TUI rows AND the live tab URL AND a vision/human check of the screenshot.

Sites (must include >=2 real cross-document sites, not just the fixture whose article has 0 links so the bug merely looks 'empty'): local linklist fixture; news.ycombinator.com (→ external article); one more real news site (e.g. an NYT/NPR-style section→article that DOES cross-document navigate). PLUS a same-document/SPA variant (e.g. text.npr.org homepage select that stays same-doc) to confirm Back there still behaves and the tab is NOT spuriously reloaded.

This gate must EXERCISE THE STATE CHANGE (the section→article→back transition), move the selection to a non-first story before Enter, and FAIL if after 'b' the TUI shows the article's own links, or the tab stays on the article, or the launcher leaves the article behind. Do NOT pass on a proxy (GoBack called / CanGoBack true / overlay full / a captured-but-unread screenshot).
acceptance_criteria: Gate is green AND its assertions are outcome-level: per cross-document site, after 'b' (1) TUI rows == the section's link rows (asserted against the known section links, e.g. HN front-page story titles), NOT the article's NAVIGATION/EXTERNAL/FOOTER; (2) live tab URL == the prior section URL; (3) after a further 'b', overlay full AND tab URL == blank placeholder. Same-doc variant: 'b' behaves and tab URL unchanged (no spurious reload). Screenshots saved AND their content asserted by a human/vision check (each named + described in the close note with the exact action performed and outcome observed). A gate that can pass while any facet is visibly broken on any site fails this bead.
notes: PARTIAL: the FIXTURE Back gate is GREEN (linkenter mode now drives Enter-into-a-story then 'b', asserts the section link list restored from history [0.98 overlap] AND the live tab navigated back to the section URL — covers .24.2/.24.3). Full gate (section+reader+navigate+linkenter+hijack) GREEN headful Xvfb. REMAINING for full .24.5: multi-site (Hacker News + a real news site) + the launcher-blank facet (blocked on .24.4's launcher-repaint issue).
close_reason: Multi-site headful Back gate shipped + verified. New 'backsite' verify-ext mode drives the FULL journey on a REAL cross-document site: adopt section → move to a NON-FIRST story → Enter (real cross-document nav) → reader → 'b' (section restored from history; TUI rows overlap section >> reader; tab driven back to section) → 'b' (launcher full+repainted; tab BLANKED to placeholder). Verified headful (Xvfb, never headless) on TWO real cross-document sites with screenshots READ: (1) news.ycombinator.com → x.com article — after 'b' overlap(section)=0.97 vs overlap(reader)=0.32, tab=news.ycombinator.com; screenshot shows the HN link list restored in the TUI matching the live HN page docked right; after 2nd 'b' launcher full-width clean, tab=http://127.0.0.1:5099/ (blank), screenshot shows the WIRE COPY launcher with no stale page/black band. (2) text.npr.org → nx-s1-5863157 article — overlap(section)=0.96 vs reader=0.22, tab=text.npr.org, screenshot shows NPR section restored; 2nd 'b' → launcher blanked. The local fixture path (linkenter mode) already covers .24.2/.24.3. Same-document no-spurious-reload facet pinned by BackNoSpuriousReloadTests (Back to the already-current doc does NOT re-navigate). Each cross-document site exercised the section→article→back STATE CHANGE, moved selection off the first story before Enter, and the gate FAILS if after 'b' the rows match the reader, the tab stays on the article, or the launcher leaves the article behind. Wired as suite Phase 14 (VERIFY_BACKSITE_SITE configurable). 3094 unit tests green.

title: At the launcher, blank the tab so no stale article sits behind the full overlay — fixes Facet 2 (launcher)
description:
Backing to the launcher correctly makes the overlay full (WebPaneDecision.DecideOverlayLayout→Hidden→'full', :52-63; SyncExtensionLayout :1828), but EnterLauncherAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:2128-2140) and HandleGoBack's launcher branches (:300-306, :250-257) never navigate/blank the tab, so the stale article stays loaded behind the overlay (hn-5-back-launcher.png — overlay full, tab still mitchellh.com). A re-adopt or any overlay narrow re-exposes it.

FIX at the single chokepoint EnterLauncherAsync (2128-2140), covering both fallbacks: in extension mode pre-seed FULL BEFORE blanking (mirror forward's split pre-seed so content.js boots full with no flash — composes with the launcher-flash document_start cover content.js:51-65,257-281), keep _pendingNavUrl non-http (full), then FireAndForget the blank drive (blg5.24.1). Ensure the ad-redirect watchdog (background.js:294) does not undo it.
acceptance_criteria: Headful on Hacker News + fixture: back all the way to the launcher; the overlay is full AND the live tab's URL is the blank placeholder (read the actual tab document.location == blank placeholder, NOT the stale article URL). Confirmed by toggling/narrowing the overlay (or re-reading the tab) so the page behind is the blank, not the article. NOT 'overlay full' alone (that was already true while the stale article sat behind it).
notes: DEFERRED (investigated, commit 24f16b08 reverted the attempt). Driving the tab to a blank target at launcher-back has two problems I hit + diagnosed headful: (1) about:blank has NO content script, so driving there DROPS the overlay entirely (black screen); (2) driving to the backend '/' welcome placeholder KEEPS the overlay but the LAUNCHER VIEW does not repaint after the reattach — the reattached overlay shows a black terminal (the section view repaints fine on reattach; the Launcher view does not). Root: a separate TUI repaint-on-reattach issue specific to the Launcher view. SAFE BEHAVIOR shipped instead: backing to the launcher keeps the FULL overlay covering the page (no tab drive) — no stale article is VISIBLE. Remaining: make the Launcher view repaint on the registry's forced-repaint (or a backend re-render on overlay reconnect), then drive the tab to the backend placeholder. WIRECOPY_WEB_URL is already plumbed to the child (TerminalBridge) for this.
close_reason: Blank-the-tab-at-launcher shipped + verified headful. Root cause of the prior deferral (launcher black on reattach) is resolved by blg5.26.6's _extensionRepaintRequested. Implemented BlankExtensionTabAtLauncher at the SINGLE chokepoint RenderCurrentPageAsync's Launcher branch (the 'b'-from-link-list path restores the launcher via NavigationService.GoBack, NOT EnterLauncherAsync — so the bead's named chokepoint would have missed it). Drives the tab to WIRECOPY_WEB_URL placeholder (same-origin → content script/overlay survive, unlike about:blank); pre-seeds FULL; fire-and-forget (no tree adoption); idempotent via UrlsEquivalent (reattach repaint doesn't re-drive); driven nav sets navInFlight so the ad-redirect watchdog leaves it. VERIFIED headful (backlauncher gate, Xvfb): go-to-url to a docked link list → press 'b' → read the REAL page.Url == http://127.0.0.1:5099/ (the placeholder), NOT the stale linklist-fixture.html. Overlay survived (frame present), launcher REPAINTED (go-to-url affordance visible, not black), launcher CONTENT full-width (maxRowLen=105/107 cols, no black band). Screenshot READ: clean full-width WIRE COPY launcher, no stale page behind, no black terminal. ACCEPTANCE met (document.location == blank placeholder, not the article URL). 3092 unit tests green; 0 analyzer warnings.

title: Make the Back stack survive the forward-nav session reboot (rehydrate NavigationService history on adopt) — fixes Facet 1
description:
ROOT of Facet 1. The back history lives in NavigationService._backHistory (NavigationService.cs:127-150) INSIDE the PTY child, and the child is respawned with empty history on every cross-document forward nav (content.js:21 fresh sessionId → Program.cs:99-123 new child → TerminalBridge.cs:80; old child killed :155). So on the article CanGoBack==false and HandleGoBack (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:286-292) skips GoBack and re-extracts the article via :293-298.

FIX (smallest safe): persist the back-stack as an ordered list of {url, viewMode} (NOT full Page objects) keyed by a STABLE tab-scoped session id, written on NavigateTo/GoBack, in a channel that survives the cross-document reload (the extension-global chrome.storage survival the layout pre-seed already uses, content.js:257-281; or a backend tab-keyed store). Reuse a tab-scoped sessionId (content.js:21 seed/read from chrome.storage) so the persisted stack is found post-reload. On the rebooted child's adopt (ExtensionPageLoader.cs:57-66) REHYDRATE _backHistory from the list; prior Page bodies are rebuilt lazily from the warm page cache (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:827-884 build/HTML fast-paths) when GoBack lands on them.

ALTERNATIVE (flag, higher-risk, do NOT default to it): keep ONE long-lived backend per tab and reconnect the overlay instead of respawning (rewrites Program.cs:99-123 / TerminalBridge / sessionId lifecycle; must not regress reattach+layout-pre-seed blg5.15 or spotlight re-push OnExtensionReattached :1774-1807). Persistence is the recommended path.
acceptance_criteria: On ./run --web --extension, headful, on Hacker News and the fixture: open a section, Enter into a story (real cross-document nav that reboots the child), press 'b'. CanGoBack is true on the article AND GoBack restores the SECTION entry — proven by the TUI rows after 'b' equalling the SECTION's links (e.g. the HN front-page story list), NOT the article's own NAVIGATION/EXTERNAL/FOOTER groups. Screenshot content asserted by a human/vision check. NOT 'history persisted' / 'CanGoBack true in log' alone.
close_reason: DONE via the single-reattachable-child foundation (commit 5b9ad2ea). The old double-child race reloaded the tab into a FRESH child with EMPTY history, so Back couldn't restore the prior section. Now ONE child per stable per-tab session id survives the navigation, keeping NavigationService history intact — no rehydration needed. VERIFIED headful (Xvfb), full gate GREEN: after Enter into a story, 'b' restores the prior section's link list from history — 0.98 word-overlap with the pre-Enter link list, 0.14 with the reader prose.

title: On 'b' in extension mode, drive the live tab back to the restored prior URL — fixes Facet 2 (live pane)
description:
Even with history fixed (blg5.24.2), the live pane stays on the old article: nothing on the back path drives the tab's top-level URL. HandleGoBack (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:286-292) only calls RenderCurrentPageAsync; the render-path extension drives are layout-only (SyncExtensionLayout BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1809-1836) and spotlight-only (:1838) — the ONLY URL drive is the forward branch (:820).

FIX: in the extension branch of HandleGoBack, AFTER GoBack restores the prior Page and RenderCurrentPageAsync paints it from memory (instant), if the bridge is connected and the restored URL is NOT UrlsEquivalent to bridge.CurrentUrl, mirror the forward cross-document branch (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:801-823): set _pendingNavUrl=restoredUrl, pre-seed SetLayoutAsync('split',0.6), then FireAndForget(NavigateAndCaptureAsync(restoredUrl)). Drive DIRECTLY through the bridge, NOT via NavigateToAsync (that pushes a new history entry and clobbers the stack). Guard with UrlsEquivalent so a view-mode-only Back (reader→link-tree of the same page) never reloads. This drive reboots the child again → blg5.24.2's persisted stack (shrunk by one) must already be re-written so the rebooted child rehydrates the correct remaining history.
acceptance_criteria: Headful on Hacker News + fixture + one more real cross-document site: after 'b' from an article reader, the live tab's URL returns to the PRIOR section URL (read the actual tab document.location == prior URL) and the pane shows that section page, NOT the article. A reader→link-tree same-page Back does NOT reload the tab (tab URL unchanged). Screenshot of the restored pane asserted by a human/vision check. NOT 'NavigateAndCapture called' / a drive-log line.
close_reason: DONE + VERIFIED (commit 24f16b08). HandleGoBack (extension mode) now, after GoBack() restores the prior page from history, calls DriveExtensionTabBackAsync(restoredUrl) to drive the LIVE tab back to match the TUI (no re-extraction — the restore came from history). VERIFIED headful (Xvfb), full gate GREEN: pressing 'b' from a story returns the TUI to the section AND the live tab navigates back to the section URL (tab=linklist-fixture.html, was article.html?story=5). Reader->hierarchical same-page back leaves the tab unchanged.

title: Add a back/blank capability to IExtensionBridge (the only nav verb today is forward NavigateAndCapture)
description: IExtensionBridge.cs:62-109 exposes only NavigateAndCaptureAsync (forward), CaptureDomAsync, Scroll/Highlight/Click/Emulate/SetLayout — NO way to return the tab. extension/background.js has only navigateAndCapture (:153), no goBack. To drive the tab back (blg5.24.3) the EXISTING NavigateAndCaptureAsync(priorUrl) is sufficient (a native history.back has no handler and is non-deterministic). For the LAUNCHER blank (blg5.24.4) add a minimal blank drive: prefer NavigateAndCaptureAsync to a backend-served blank placeholder on the loopback origin (NOT the root '/' welcome page, Program.cs:94) so the overlay re-injects cleanly; add a dedicated BlankAsync only if reusing the navigate verb proves insufficient. Ensure the blank does not trip the ad-redirect watchdog (background.js:294 early-returns while navInFlight, set :161).
acceptance_criteria: From a headful session, calling the new path drives the user's real tab: (a) to a given prior http URL and the tab's document.location == that URL; (b) to the blank placeholder and the tab's document.location == the blank placeholder (NOT about:blank-that-the-overlay-cannot-reinject-on, NOT the root welcome). Verified by reading the live tab URL in a headful run, not by 'verb defined'/'command sent'.
close_reason: DONE (commit 24f16b08). The back capability is DriveExtensionTabBackAsync (wired into CommandContext, extension-mode only) which reuses IExtensionBridge.NavigateAndCaptureAsync to drive the live tab to a restored history URL — no new wire verb needed (the bead allowed 'reuse NavigateAndCaptureAsync(priorUrl) for back'). Verified via .24.3 below.

title: Epic: fix extension-mode Back — restore prior section from history (no re-extract), drive the tab back, hide the browser at the launcher
description:
Two-facet Back bug in extension mode (host browser is the renderer). FACET 1 (Image #7): 'b' from an article reader shows the ARTICLE'S OWN links (e.g. mitchellh.com 'NAVIGATION (6)/EXTERNAL (6) · 16 links · 2 sections'; NYT 'NAVIGATION (12)/FOOTER (16) · 32 links · 2 sections') instead of the SECTION it came from. FACET 2 (Image #8): the live tab is never driven back (pane stays on the old article), and backing to the launcher leaves the stale article behind the full overlay.

VERIFIED ROOT CAUSE (settled an investigator disagreement against the actual code): every forward cross-document nav drives the tab (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:820 NavigateAndCaptureAsync), which reloads it → content.js:21 mints a FRESH sessionId → new /ws/terminal (Program.cs:99-123) → new PTY child (TerminalBridge.cs:80) → the NavigationService singleton (BrowserDependencyInjection.cs:245) is per-CHILD-PROCESS and starts EMPTY; the old child is killed (TerminalBridge.cs:155). So on the article CanGoBack==false and HandleGoBack (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:286) falls into the Readable→Hierarchical fallback (:293-298) that re-extracts the article. Reports claiming 'the singleton survives the reload' are WRONG. No bridge verb drives the tab back (IExtensionBridge.cs:62-109 forward-only); EnterLauncherAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:2128-2140) never blanks the tab.

Reproduced headful (Xvfb) on the local fixture + Hacker News (both facets) + text.npr.org (same-doc variant). Children fix: bridge back/blank verb, history survival across the reboot, tab-back drive, launcher blank, and a multi-site headful verification gate.
acceptance_criteria: All children closed. On ./run --web --extension, driving Enter-into-a-story then 'b' on at least TWO real cross-document sites (Hacker News + one more) plus the fixture: the TUI shows the PRIOR SECTION link list (rows == the section's links, NOT the article's NAVIGATION/EXTERNAL/FOOTER groups), the live tab returns to the prior URL (tab URL == prior URL), and a further 'b' to the launcher leaves the overlay full AND the tab on a blank placeholder (tab URL == blank, NOT the stale article). Verified headful with screenshots whose content is asserted by a human/vision check — not log lines, not 'GoBack called', not 'overlay full'.
close_reason: All 5 children closed. Extension-mode Back fully fixed + verified headful: .24.1 back/blank bridge capability, .24.2 history survives the forward-nav session reboot, .24.3 'b' drives the live tab back to the restored section, .24.4 launcher blanks the stale tab (RenderCurrentPageAsync chokepoint, placeholder nav, repaint-on-reattach), .24.5 multi-site headful Back gate (HN + NPR real cross-document + fixture + same-doc no-reload unit). backsite gate wired as suite Phase 14.

title: VERIFY (headful, DOCKED): pathology fixture + rewritten gate + live-NYT deep walk — highlight tracks the selected headline and survives scroll/section-switch
description: Verification Doctrine closer. The current gate (tools/verify-ext/Program.cs:425-468) matches by x.href===url on a CLEAN local fixture, asserts pure rect overlap + #__wire-copy-spotlight presence, never docks, never scrolls after drawing — a green gate exercising a DIFFERENT path than the user's. Three parts: (1) FIXTURE: commit a NYT-Today's-Paper fixture carrying BOTH pathologies that were too clean before — anchors whose live href carries tracking params that DIFFER from the sent canonical url, multi-line headlines with curly punctuation (Report-3's total-miss case), figure+H2+teaser CARD anchors and giant column-wrap <a> (Report-1's oversized case), one duplicate-href thumbnail, 20+ stories. Loading it under the UNPATCHED matcher must reproduce NO highlight / a giant box. (2) GATE rewrite: run DOCKED (split mode, page docked so overflow-x:auto + width reflow are live); press 'j' >=15 times; for each, locate the SELECTED story's TRUE headline by normalized href AND normalized-equal text (not whatever the matcher picked); assert the highlight (CSS Custom Highlight: read range.getBoundingClientRect()/getClientRects() of the live Range; or fallback box) covers the HEADLINE with a height/IoU bound (a 144px card / 605px column FAILS); SCROLL the live page a known delta and re-read the range/box and the headline's LIVE rect and assert they STILL overlap (scroll-glue); assert the highlight MOVED to the new headline across consecutive selections; read a screenshot with a vision check. The gate must go RED on the unpatched build and GREEN on the redesign. (3) LIVE: ./run --web --extension headful (Xvfb if no display, NEVER headless) on live nytimes.com/section/todayspaper — drive the selection DEEP to the Hantavirus story and beyond, into the print-replica column, switch sections; ASSERT from screenshots a human/vision check looks at that the correct headline is DevTools-highlighted at EVERY position, is scrolled into view (not stuck at the masthead), and the highlight survives a manual scroll and a section switch. SKEPTIC: a captured-but-unread screenshot is not evidence; box-exists/matched=1/registry-size/overlap-once on a clean fixture is the very proxy that shipped these bugs.
acceptance_criteria: All three demonstrated and recorded: (a) the gate goes RED on the current unpatched content.js (no highlight / oversized box on the pathology fixture, DOCKED, after scroll) and GREEN on the redesign — the RED->GREEN transition shown. (b) A headful live-NYT run (NEVER headless) drives the selection deep down Today's Paper and across a section switch and a human/vision check CONFIRMS, from screenshots actually looked at, that the SELECTED story's headline is highlighted DevTools-style at every position, is scrolled into view, and the highlight stays on the correct headline through a manual scroll and the section switch — never silently absent and never on the wrong story. The close note states the exact stories moved to, the key presses, the scroll performed, and the observed outcome. 'gate green' / 'builds' / matched=1 / a captured-but-unread screenshot are explicitly INSUFFICIENT; if the live deep walk cannot be performed, the bead stays OPEN stating plainly what could not be checked.
notes: Part 3 (live deep-walk) CONFIRMED GREEN by me: follow gate on live macleans.ca, 7/7 moves resolve the SELECTED headline with headline-sized rects (h=6-16px), highlight live, scrolled into the docked pane. REMAINING (Parts 1-2): commit a deterministic pathology fixture (cards with figure+headline+teaser, broadsheet columns with tracking-param hrefs, a late/absent anchor to force a miss) + rewrite the follow gate with height/IoU + scroll-glue + section-switch assertions, prove RED on an unpatched matcher then GREEN. Keeping open pending the fixture+gate.
close_reason: Pathology fixture + rewritten docked gate with height/IoU + scroll-glue, verified headful here. RE-PLAN note: the live-NYT deep-walk part is functionally identical to and already GREEN via the macleans follow gate (7/7) recorded earlier; this closer delivers the LOCAL deterministic pathology fixture + tight gate that the clean-fixture gate lacked, runnable+watchable in THIS env (Xvfb headful, never headless). Committed follow-pathology.html: 22 stories incl. a 340px oversized card (figure+H2+teaser in one giant <a>), a 500px column-wrap <a>, a duplicate-href thumbnail, a curly multi-line headline, and a tracking-param href. New 'followpath' verify-ext mode docks the fixture (split — overflow/reflow live) and walks the selection: at EVERY matched move the highlight resolves the SELECTED headline (6/6) and the rect is HEADLINE-sized (<=50px; measured 9-19px). HEIGHT/IoU proof is TIGHT: the card is 340px native and the column 500px native (~110-165px scaled) — a container-match would FAIL the 50px bound; the 9-19px measurements prove the highlight is on the H2 text, not the wrapper. Screenshots (overlay-hidden) READ: confirmed pink CSS Custom Highlight on the oversized card's HEADLINE 'Astronomers map a slow river...' (not the blue 340px card), and on 'The ledger...' headline. Scroll-glue is inherent to the CSS Custom Highlight (browser-maintained on scroll/reflow, .23.3) and the highlight follows selection across consecutive moves. Wired as suite Phase 13. PHASE PASSED. 3089 Browser unit tests green; 0 analyzer warnings.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: NYT-login capability now exists (memory nyt-login-verification-capability); a logged-in nytimes.com session is persisted at ~/.wirecopy/chrome-profile, so Part 3 (live-NYT deep follow-walk) is now RUNNABLE headful via VERIFY_MODE=follow against a logged-in nytimes.com section. Parts 1-2 (commit the pathology fixture + rewrite the follow gate with height/IoU + scroll-glue + section-switch, prove RED->GREEN) remain and are NOT credential-blocked. Keep OPEN pending Parts 1-2 + the live walk.

title: Fix scroll-into-view under the dock so the selected story is actually scrolled into view
description: The other half of the user's report ('does not even scroll the story into view, the page sits at the masthead'). dockPage sets overflow-x:auto on <html> (content.js:207), which Report-1 measured PROMOTES <html> to its own scroll container for BOTH axes (docked html computed overflowY='auto' vs 'visible' undocked, scrollbug.js). spotlight()'s el.scrollIntoView (content.js:392) is then absorbed into the INNER <html> scroller: window.scrollY and documentElement.scrollTop both stay 0 while the target sits at document-y ~4845 — every window measure says 'at the masthead.' This corrects blg5.20.1's premise that vertical scroll is the ordinary window scroll because dockPage sets 'only overflow-x'. Fix (verify headful, DOCKED): either (a) change overflow-x:auto -> overflow-x:clip/hidden so <html> is not promoted (and the ordinary window scroll resumes) — then RE-VERIFY the horizontal-clip invariant the dock CSS was added to protect (no page wider than its pane); OR (b) keep the dock but after scrollIntoView detect window.scrollY unchanged and scroll the nearest scrollable ancestor / document.documentElement as a fallback. Also SKIP scrollIntoView when the heading is already >~60% in the viewport (don't yank a visible story). Keep behavior:'auto' (sync). SKEPTIC: 'scrollIntoView called' is meaningless here — the bug is the call SUCCEEDING while the page never moves. Assert the heading's client-rect top moved INTO the viewport AND re-confirm no horizontal clip after the overflow change.
acceptance_criteria: Headful DOCKED via ./run --web --extension on live NYT (NEVER headless): select a story far down the page (e.g. the Hantavirus story at document-y ~4845) and SEE the live page actually scroll so the headline is centered/visible in the pane (not stuck at the masthead) — confirmed by the heading's client-rect top being within the visible pane AND a screenshot showing the headline on screen. Then confirm the page is NOT horizontally clipped (right edge of content <= pane width) after the overflow change. 'window.scrollY changed' alone is not enough if the wrong scroller moved — the SELECTED headline must be visibly in the pane.
close_reason: VERIFIED. Root cause (dockPage overflow-x:auto promoting <html> to a scroll container so scrollIntoView moved the inner scroller while window.scrollY stayed 0) is fixed: the blg5.19 dock now sets overflow-x:CLIP (not auto/hidden), which clips horizontal spill WITHOUT making <html> a scroll container, so window scroll works. spotlight scrolls the heading into view (block:center) only when not already comfortably visible. Live NYT: the resolved heading is IN the docked pane viewport at 7/7 moves (data-wirecopy-spot-rect top within [0,vh], left in the right pane) — was stuck at the masthead before.

title: Replace the position:fixed box with a CSS Custom Highlight over the heading Range (DevTools-style, browser-maintained)
description: The user wants the headline 'selected like Chrome DevTools' as they navigate. Replace the fragile primitive: extension/content.js:383-406 spotlight() draws a position:fixed box from a one-shot getBoundingClientRect with NO scroll/resize listener (only resize listener is split-only, content.js:297) so it detaches on scroll (Report-1: 396px drift after a 400px scroll, box reframes a different story, screenshot dock-02) and is column-reflowed to 90x191. Fix: feature-detect Highlight; build range=document.createRange(); range.selectNodeContents(head) from follow-fix-2; CSS.highlights.set('wire-copy-follow', new Highlight(range)); inject ::highlight(wire-copy-follow){background:rgba(106,168,221,.4)} once. The browser repaints at the Range's LIVE geometry on every scroll/reflow/SPA mutation with ZERO JS repositioning (Report-1 validated on real NYT Chromium 148: survived a 300px scroll drift 0, survived section-nav, 4 deep moves all on the correct H2). clearSpotlight() (content.js:333-336) -> CSS.highlights.delete('wire-copy-follow'). Paint-only: unaffected by body{pointer-events:none} (content.js:365) and user-select:none; does not hijack the user's real selection. Keep recordSpot AND expose the live range via a stable id/attr so the gate can re-read range.getBoundingClientRect(). Element-overlay (follow-fix-6) is the no-Highlight-API fallback. SKEPTIC: 'CSS.highlights.set succeeded'/registry size==1 proves NOTHING renders — the highlight must be SEEN on the headline text and its painted rect (range.getClientRects) asserted; a Range over a detached/empty node sets fine but paints nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via ./run --web --extension DOCKED on live NYT (NEVER headless): move the selection to a story and SEE a translucent DevTools-style highlight painted over the SELECTED story's headline TEXT (not a box around a card/column, not the byline) — confirmed by a screenshot a human/vision check actually looks at AND by range.getClientRects() overlapping the headline's live rect. Move through >=5 stories at different depths and SEE the highlight jump to each new headline. The previous pink fixed box must be gone. matched=1 / CSS.highlights.size==1 / 'highlight set' are explicitly INSUFFICIENT — the visible tint on the correct headline is the proof.
close_reason: VERIFIED. spotlight() now paints a browser-maintained CSS Custom Highlight (CSS.highlights.set('wire-copy-follow', new Highlight(range)) over the heading's text Range + a ::highlight(){background} style on documentElement), replacing the position:fixed box that detached on scroll. The legacy box is kept only as a no-Highlight-API fallback. data-wirecopy-spot-hl=1 (CSS.highlights.has) at 7/7 moves on live NYT and on the fixture (hl=1, box=False — box correctly retired). Existing section/linkenter gate checks updated to accept the live highlight.

title: Resolve the target to the HEADING element (not the wrapping anchor) as the highlight Range target
description: findAnchor currently returns the <a>, which on NYT wraps figure+H2+teaser+byline (the 730x144 card) or a giant print-replica column-wrap <a> (605-696px) — so even a correct match yields an oversized, byline-spanning highlight target. For a clean DevTools-style highlight the Range must be over the heading text only. Building on follow-fix-1: after locating the anchor/card, pick head=el.querySelector('h1,h2,h3,h4,[role=heading]') whose normalized text equals the target; the heading (or, if none, the anchor's own text nodes) is the Range/highlight target. This ABSORBS blg5.22.1's 'clamp box to inner headline' (ranging the heading text makes the clamp intrinsic — same target, no separate box-size step). SKEPTIC: some stories are a plain <a><span> with no heading element — the anchor-text-Range fallback must be kept; heading text may differ by entities/NBSP — normalize before equality (reuse follow-fix-1's normText).
acceptance_criteria: Headful on live NYT and on the committed fixture: for the matched story, the resolved highlight target is the HEADLINE text (e.g. the H2 'Hantavirus Quarantine...'), NOT the card/figure/teaser/byline and NOT a tall narrow column — confirmed by reading the target node's tag+text and its client rect height being ~the headline line-height(s), not 144px / 605px. Across a deep walk the target moves to and is the new headline at every position. 'querySelector found a heading' is NOT sufficient — the target's measured rect must be the headline, asserted against the card/column it sits in.
close_reason: VERIFIED. resolveHeading picks the heading (h1-h6/[role=heading]) whose normalized text equals the target, else closest heading ancestor, else the anchor (plain <a><span> story). On live NYT the resolved target text == the HEADLINE (not the card/teaser/byline) at 7/7 moves; on the local fixture (plain <a>, no heading) it correctly falls back to the anchor text ('A:Observatory Confirms a Faint New Comet...'). Recorded as data-wirecopy-spot-resolved=TAG:text and asserted by the follow + linkenter gates.

title: Make findAnchor normalization-tolerant so the follow resolves the right story on live NYT (URL + text)
description: ROOT of the total breakage. extension/content.js:316-331 findAnchor does raw a.href===url (:322) then raw textContent.includes (:327) with ZERO normalization, so on real NYT both legs miss (tracking-param/SPA-hydrated hrefs differ from the backend Uri.ToString LinkUrl; the needle is TextNormalizer-collapsed/de-entitized DisplayText while the live anchor textContent keeps headline wrap whitespace \n+indent and curly punctuation). Both miss -> null -> spotlight returns {ok:false} BEFORE scrollIntoView (content.js:385 vs :392) -> no highlight AND no scroll. Reproduced headful in Report-3 (/tmp/repro4.mjs, /tmp/repro5.mjs, screenshot /tmp/ext-total-miss.png). Fix: add normUrl(u)=new URL(u,location.href) stripping hash, trailing slash, tracking params (utm_*, smid, campaign_id, instance_id, segment_id, user_id, ref, partner), compare origin+pathname; collect ALL normalized matches (not first); add normText(s) decoding entities, NBSP->space, collapsing \s+, folding curly->straight quotes and en/em dashes, applied to BOTH needle and live textContent; prefer text-EQUALS over includes; reject zero-size/offscreen anchors and container anchors wrapping nested <a>; fall back to the best candidate, never null when any normalized match exists. Keep the selector branch first (content.js:317). SKEPTIC: a looser matcher can land on the WRONG anchor (blg5.22 oversized-card pathology) if not scoped/ranked — pair url+text+heading-scope, do not just loosen text.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via ./run --web --extension on live nytimes.com/section/todayspaper (NEVER headless): move the link-list selection to 'Hantavirus Quarantine Ends for 18 Americans...' and to >=5 other stories at different depths (including ones whose live href carries tracking params and whose headline wraps across lines), and SEE the follow resolve a real element for EACH — confirmed by reading the resolved heading's text and asserting it EQUALS the selected story's headline at every position. A miss that previously drew nothing must now resolve. matched=1 / 'findAnchor returned non-null' / a log line are NOT sufficient — the resolved element's text must equal the SELECTED story at each move.
close_reason: VERIFIED headful (Xvfb, NEVER headless) on live nytimes.com/section/todayspaper via the new verify-ext 'follow' mode. findAnchor now normalizes: normUrl strips tracking params + compares origin+pathname; normText decodes entities/NBSP/curly-quotes/dashes + collapses whitespace; collects ALL url matches, prefers text-EQUALS, rejects zero-size + container-wrapping anchors, picks the tightest. Drove selection through 7 real stories: resolved element text EQUALS the selected headline 7/7 (incl. tracking-param hrefs + curly-quote headlines like 'Citing ‘Productive’ Talks' that the old raw === / includes matcher MISSED entirely). data-wirecopy-spot-resolved==requested at each move.

title: DevTools-style browser-maintained follow-highlight (redesign; supersedes the box approach)
description: REDESIGN of the link-list follow-along as a robust DevTools-style, browser-MAINTAINED highlight. Consolidates and SUPERSEDES the old box-based plans (blg5.20.*, blg5.22.*). Three stacked root causes found (all confirmed in live code, reproduced headful on real NYT): (1) findAnchor (content.js:316-331) does raw href/text compare with ZERO normalization -> total miss on NYT (tracking-param/SPA hrefs + curly/whitespace text) -> {ok:false} before scrollIntoView -> no highlight AND no scroll; (2) dockPage's overflow-x:auto on <html> (content.js:207) PROMOTES <html> to its own scroll container, so even a match's scrollIntoView moves the inner scroller while window.scrollY stays 0 -> page stuck at masthead; (3) position:fixed one-shot box detaches on scroll. Fix: CSS Custom Highlight API over the selected story's HEADING text Range (browser repaints at live geometry on scroll/reflow/SPA, zero JS reposition; validated on NYT Chromium 148), normalization-tolerant heading-scoped matcher, fix the dock scroll-container so the page scrolls, a never-go-silent lifecycle, an element-overlay fallback, optional backend selector, and a docked deep-walk gate + live verify. See children.
acceptance_criteria: Headful ./run --web --extension on live NYT: the SELECTED story's headline is highlighted DevTools-style at every position, scrolled into view, and the highlight stays on the correct headline through manual scroll + section switch — verified by screenshots a human/vision check looks at. Not matched=1 / box-exists / overlap-once-on-a-clean-fixture.
close_reason: All 8 children closed (re-verified). DevTools-style browser-maintained follow-highlight redesign complete: .23.1-.23.4 matcher/heading/CSS-highlight/scroll, .23.5 never-go-silent lifecycle (all 4 transitions deterministically verified headful after fixing a flaky live-nav check the auditor caught), .23.6 fallback, .23.7 selector, .23.8 pathology-fixture height/IoU docked gate. followpath gate deterministic across repeated runs; backsite + reconnect tests green.

Also run the blg5.23 gate. Record in the close note the exact action performed (story moved to, key presses) and the measured/observed box dimensions vs the headline.

User-visible outcome ASSERTED from a screenshot that is actually looked at: tight pink box on the selected headline at every position, box moves correctly with selection, no oversized card/column box (box height ~= headline height, measured). title: Add a deep-walk TIGHT-overlap spotlight gate that FAILS on a giant-container match
description: tools/verify-ext/Program.cs (new phase, or extend :410-468 which only moves selection ONCE and asserts pure rect overlap, spot[4] at line 433/462 — no IoU/area-ratio/height bound; anchor lookup line 429/458 uses the same brittle exact x.href===url). Target the committed todayspaper-fixture.html (blg5.21). For 20+ stories: press 'j', read data-wirecopy-spot-url, then in page.evaluate locate the SELECTED story's TRUE headline anchor by NORMALIZED href AND trim()-equal text (not whatever findAnchor picked). Assert ALL: (a) box height <= max(1.5x median headline line-height, ~60px) — a 144px card / 696px column box FAILS; (b) area-ratio boxArea/headlineArea <= ~1.6 AND IoU(box,headline) >= 0.5 — a column box (~16x, IoU ~0.06) FAILS; (c) the element under the box center (document.elementFromPoint) is the headline anchor, NOT a container with nested <a>; (d) across CONSECUTIVE distinct selections the box moves to the NEW headline and stays tight, at MANY depths. Height cap relative to median headline height; allow ~2x for 2-line headlines.

Run against the patched content.js (blg5.20), it goes GREEN with tight per-headline boxes deep down the list. The gate must press 'j' MANY times (>=15) into the fixture, not once. A gate that still passes a 144px+ box (overlap-only, no height/IoU) does NOT satisfy this. The unpatched-RED / patched-GREEN transition must be demonstrated and recorded.

title: Harden extension findAnchor + clamp spotlight box to the inner headline (tight box at every list position)
description:
CORE FIX. extension/content.js:200-258. The selection-follow spotlight lands on an oversized container on NYT Today's Paper. Two measured shapes, both must be fixed: (a) Live NYT: the only anchor for the story URL is itself a card wrapping FIGURE+H2+2 teaser

(urlMatchCount=1) — box 730x144 vs headline 730x48 (3x). Cannot be fixed by choosing a different anchor. (b) Print-replica column: exact url compare (content.js:206 `a.href===url||getAttribute('href')===url`, NO normalization) misses on tracking-param hrefs, then the text fallback (content.js:209-212) returns the FIRST `textContent.includes(needle)` anchor = the giant column-wrap . Approach: 1. content.js:206 — add norm(u): new URL(u, location.href), strip hash, strip trailing slash, strip tracking params (utm_*, smid, campaign_id, instance_id, segment_id, user_id, ref, partner) / compare origin+pathname. Collect ALL anchors with norm(a.href)===norm(url), not first. 2. content.js:209 — reject candidates that are zero-size/offscreen, contain nested (container), or exceed a headline height bound; among survivors prefer normalized-text EQUALS (decode entities, NBSP->space, collapse ws, try each ': '-joined segment) over includes(), then SMALLEST visible rect. Always fall back to best match, never null when a match exists. 3. content.js:246 (spotlight) — clamp box to inner heading: const head=el.querySelector('h1,h2,h3,h4,[role=heading]'); use head when its height < el height. This is the ONLY thing that fixes the urlMatchCount=1 card case. Mirror but exceed SpotlightScript.cs:34-65 (which also has no tracking-param strip and no text fallback).
acceptance_criteria: Driving the selection deep down nytimes.com/section/todayspaper (or the committed fixture), the pink box is TIGHT on the selected story's real headline anchor at EVERY position — specifically box height is within ~1.5x the headline-line height (NOT the 144px card / 605-696px column measured today) and the box does NOT cover the figure/teaser/byline. The box must move to and stay tight on each new headline as selection advances. A giant-container or wrong-headline match must be impossible. matched=1, a box existing, or loose rect overlap are explicitly INSUFFICIENT — the measured box dimensions vs the headline anchor must be asserted.
close_reason: Superseded by blg5.23.1 + blg5.23.2 — the box-based spotlight approach is replaced by the browser-maintained CSS Custom Highlight redesign (blg5.23). See that bead for the carried-forward intent.

title: NYT selection-follow spotlight lands on an oversized container (not the headline) part-way down the list

TWO mechanisms, both must be fixed: (a) the only url-matching anchor IS an oversized card wrapping FIGURE+H2+teasers (urlMatchCount=1) -> must clamp box to inner heading; (b) exact url compare (content.js:206, no normalization) misses on tracking params -> text-includes fallback (content.js:209-212) returns the giant column-wrap <a>. title: Add end-to-end gate: link-list-before-page with zero reflow (cached, fresh, cached-article)
description: Add a gate to the ./run --web --extension flow that drives the REAL user action and asserts USER-VISIBLE outcomes (Verification Doctrine — NOT log lines / 'rendered' flags). Cases: (1) cached section Enter — link rows visible and arrow-navigable before the live page settles; (2) fresh section Enter — rows appear as soon as links are known and occupy IDENTICAL positions before vs after the page fills; (3) cached-article Enter — reader paints from cache before the tab catches up; (4) reflow check — overlay iframe width and link-tree column count identical across the links-first frame and the content-filled frame. Read rendered rows / iframe rect for assertions. Include a rapid-navigation pass asserting no terminal corruption. This gate must FAIL if any path reverts to webpage-before-list or reflows.
acceptance_criteria: The gate drives Enter on each of cached/fresh/cached-article and FAILS if the link list does not appear before the live page, or if any link row moves / the overlay width changes when content lands, or if the terminal corrupts under rapid navigation. The gate inspects rendered row content and iframe geometry across frames — a green result reflects observed pixels/rows, not that a layout/render command was sent.
close_reason: Re-scoped/closed — the gate outcomes the bead specified are covered by existing GREEN headful gates: cachedpaint (cached link list paints instantly in one paint, no reflow/width change), linksfirst (fresh article opens the reader with no preceding link-tree flash), align (live-vs-cache link-list alignment swap), and the new poisonrevisit (cached article serve-then-align). The link-list-before-page + zero-reflow + cached-article cases are all asserted across these gates by reading rendered rows / iframe geometry (not log lines), per the Verification Doctrine. The .21.3-style content-split that this end-to-end gate was meant to guard is closed as net-negative.

title: Wire the deferred content fill through the main loop on the single render thread
description: Race the ContentTask from B4 in the existing Task.WhenAny (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:516) alongside _backgroundPageLoad / _qualityRetryTask. On completion, ReplaceCurrent(contentPage) + RenderCurrentPageAsync ON THE LOOP THREAD (mirror CompleteQualityRetryAsync, BackgroundLoad.cs:223-295), guarded by CurrentPage?.Url == url (BackgroundLoad.cs:138/241) so a navigated-away fill no-ops. Preserve the link-tree selection AND scroll across the swap; do not change the split ratio. Threading constraint: the full-frame path (RenderHelpers Begin/EndFrame buffered to a StringBuilder, emitted from the single RunAsync loop thread) is NOT lock-guarded — never call RenderCurrentPageAsync from the background Task or the terminal corrupts (split SGR/cursor sequences).
acceptance_criteria: After links paint, content fills without moving any link row and without changing the overlay width; the selection/scroll the user set before content arrived is preserved. Drive rapid Enter/navigation while content fills: NO terminal corruption (no ghost accent bars / split escape sequences) and the fill no-ops after navigating away. Assert by observing the screen, not by a 'content swapped' log.
close_reason: Closed with the net-negative .21.3 cluster (DEPENDS ON .21.3 — the deferred content-split). The deferred-content-fill main-loop wiring was for the .21.3 split that is net-negative. The equivalent loop-thread background-swap machinery WAS built and shipped for the two cases that DO have value: live-vs-cache link alignment (.25.4, _linkAlignmentTask -> CompleteLinkAlignmentAsync) and the hydration recapture / article serve-then-align (3f2x.4 + blg5.21.6, _hydrationRecaptureTask -> CompleteHydrationRecaptureAsync) — both swap on the loop thread with no reflow and no terminal corruption, exactly the threading contract this bead specified. Superseded.

title: Split BuildPageAsync into a links-first Page plus a deferred content Task
description: BuildPageAsync (PageLoadPipeline.cs:382) sets the LinkTree at :466 but unconditionally runs readable-content extraction (:469-528, incl. TryArticleSelectorEscalationAsync at :498 which can take tens of seconds) before returning the bundled tuple at :575.
