title: Add confirmation dialogs for destructive collection operations
description: Delete item (d in CollectionItems), delete collection (d in CollectionList), and clear collection (:clear) all execute immediately with no confirmation. Add y/n prompts using ctx.InputHandler.PromptForInputAsync before executing. Pattern: see cache clear confirmation in SearchCommandHandler line 303. Files: CollectionCommandHandler.cs (HandleDeleteItem), SearchCommandHandler.cs (clear case).

title: Epic: Reading List Editing & Podcast Setup UX
description: Two critical UX gaps block the reading list and podcast pipeline from being usable. (1) Reading list editing features (delete, reorder, clear) exist but are not discoverable — need confirmation dialogs, direct shortcuts, and better status bar hints. (2) Podcast setup is a dead end — API key prompt is vague and GCS bucket cannot be configured from the UI at all.

title: Fix doubled count in group headers
description: LinkInfo.CreateGroupHeader() bakes count into DisplayText ('Content (61)'), then LinkTreeRenderer.RenderGroupHeader() at line 165 appends childCount again, producing 'CONTENT (61) (61)'. Fix: remove count from CreateGroupHeader() DisplayText, let renderer be single source of truth. Files: LinkInfo.cs (CreateGroupHeader lines 92-111), LinkTreeRenderer.cs (line 165). Update test helpers in LinkTreeGridMapperTests.cs (CreateGroupHeaderInfo line 281) and BackgroundPreloadServiceTests.cs (lines 84, 158) to match new format.
notes: Parent: workspace-5ojj (Epic)
close_reason: Fixed doubled count by removing count from CreateGroupHeader() DisplayText, letting renderer be single source of truth. Also cleaned up unused linkCount parameter.

title: Fix WarmUpAsync forcing headless=true ignoring config
description: BrowserSession.WarmUpAsync() hardcodes headless=true (line 78) regardless of the Headless config setting. GetOrCreateDriver reuses the existing driver, so even with Headless=false in config, the warm-up creates a headless driver that persists for all subsequent page loads. This is a root cause of NYT blank screens: the user configures headed mode but gets headless. Fix: WarmUpAsync should respect the configured Headless setting, or GetOrCreateDriver should check if the existing driver's headless mode matches the requested mode and recreate if mismatched. Files: BrowserSession.cs (WarmUpAsync, GetOrCreateDriver).
close_reason: Fixed WarmUpAsync to use configured Headless setting. Added _driverIsHeadless tracking to GetOrCreateDriver so it recreates the driver when headless mode changes.

title: fix: HandleOpenCollections opens wrong collection (Read Later vs Reading List)
description:
CRITICAL BUG: HandleOpenCollections at CollectionCommandHandler.cs:271 calls service.GetDefaultCollectionAsync() which falls through to GetOrCreateDefaultAsync() which uses hardcoded DefaultCollectionName='Read Later' (CollectionRepository.cs:14). But SaveToReadingListAsync saves to 'Reading List' via GetOrCreateReadingListAsync. These are TWO DIFFERENT database records, so saved items never appear when viewing.

FIX: Change HandleOpenCollections to use a new GetReadingListAsync method (or reuse GetOrCreateReadingListAsync logic) that looks for 'Reading List' by name, not the generic default collection. Also update CollectionRepository.DefaultCollectionName to 'Reading List' to align the naming, and ensure GetOrCreateDefaultAsync creates 'Reading List' instead of 'Read Later'.

Files to modify:
- src/TermReader.Persistence/Repositories/CollectionRepository.cs (change DefaultCollectionName)
- src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/CollectionCommandHandler.cs (HandleOpenCollections)
- src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Collections/CollectionService.cs (expose reading list getter)
- src/TermReader.Application/Interfaces/ICollectionService.cs (add GetReadingListAsync if needed)
notes: REOPENED: Was closed without verification. Acceptance: 1) Write test showing HandleOpenCollections navigates to Reading List, not Read Later. 2) Verify the correct CollectionId is used. 3) Show test passing.
close_reason: Fix verified: DefaultCollectionName already 'Reading List', HandleOpenCollections uses GetReadingListAsync. 3 new tests: HandleOpenCollections_CallsGetReadingListAsync_NotGetDefaultCollectionAsync, HandleOpenCollections_NavigatesToReadingListCollection, HandleOpenCollections_Failure_ShowsErrorMessage. All 165 collection tests pass.

title: Add content-quality fallback: retry with Selenium when cached content is empty
description: When CachingPageLoader serves a cached entry but ReadableContentExtractor finds no content (returns null), there's no fallback mechanism. The article displays as empty. Add a fallback: after extracting readable content in LoadPageAsync, if result is null AND the page was served from cache (FetchMethod=Http), invalidate the cache entry and re-fetch via Selenium (ForceRefresh). This ensures the user always gets content if available, even if the pre-fetched version was incomplete. Files: BrowserOrchestrator.cs (LoadPageAsync), CachingPageLoader.cs, InMemoryPageCache.cs
notes: REOPENED: Was closed without verification. Acceptance: 1) Write unit test that simulates empty cached content triggering Selenium retry. 2) Verify in code that CachingPageLoader or BrowserOrchestrator has fallback path from empty HTTP cache to Selenium. 3) If fallback exists, show test passing. If not, implement it. Task must NOT be closed without this evidence.
close_reason: Fix verified: BrowserOrchestrator.LoadPageAsync (line 239) checks !page.HasReadableContent() && loadResult.FetchMethod != FetchMethod.Selenium, removes cache entry, and retries with ForceRefresh. Tests LoadPageAsync_HttpNoContent_RetriesWithForceRefresh, LoadPageAsync_CachedNoContent_RetriesWithForceRefresh, LoadPageAsync_HttpNoContent_RemovesCacheEntry, LoadPageAsync_HttpNoContent_ShowsLoadingScreenOnRetry, LoadPageAsync_HttpNoContent_RetrySucceeds_SetsReadableContent all pass (10/10 in CacheServingFlowTests).

title: Skip loading screen for cache hits in LoadPageAsync
description: LoadPageAsync() unconditionally calls _renderer.RenderLoading(url) at line 102 before checking the cache via CachingPageLoader. This makes every navigation show 'Loading [url]...' even when the page is served instantly from cache. The user sees this and thinks the cache isn't working. Fix: check the cache BEFORE calling RenderLoading. If cache hit, skip the loading screen entirely. Consider moving the cache-check into NavigateToAsync so LoadPageAsync is only called on cache miss, or add a pre-check via IPageCache.Contains() before the RenderLoading call. Files: BrowserOrchestrator.cs (LoadPageAsync, NavigateToAsync)
notes: REOPENED: Was closed without verification. Acceptance: 1) Verify cache hits bypass ShowLoadingScreenAsync. 2) Write test showing cached page loads without loading screen delay. 3) Show test passing.
close_reason: Fix verified: BrowserOrchestrator.cs line 168 checks _pageCache.Contains(url) before calling _renderer.RenderLoading(url). Tests LoadPageAsync_CacheHit_SkipsLoadingScreen and LoadPageAsync_CacheMiss_ShowsLoadingScreen both pass (10/10 in CacheServingFlowTests).

title: Validate HTTP-fetched HTML has extractable content before caching
description: BackgroundPreloadService.PreloadUrlAsync caches HTTP responses without checking whether they contain meaningful article content. For JS-heavy sites like NYT, HTTP returns a shell page with no article text. Add content validation: after HttpFetchAsync succeeds, check if the HTML passes ReadableContentExtractor.IsArticle and has extractable paragraphs. If not, don't cache it — it would produce an empty article view. Also applies to PageLoader.TryHttpFetchAsync, which should detect 'empty content' pages and fall back to Selenium. Files: BackgroundPreloadService.cs, PageLoader.cs, ReadableContentExtractor.cs
notes: REOPENED: Was closed without verification. Acceptance: 1) Write test showing HTML without extractable content is NOT cached. 2) Verify CachingPageLoader or PageLoader checks content quality before Put(). 3) Show test passing with both good and bad HTML fixtures. Task must NOT be closed without this evidence.
close_reason: Fixed: Added HasExtractableContent() and IsArticlePage() static methods. BackgroundPreloadService now checks extractable content before caching. PageLoader TryHttpFetchAsync returns failure for article URLs without extractable content (triggers Selenium fallback). 16 new tests covering good/bad HTML content validation. Build 0 errors, all tests pass.

title: Add FetchMethod to PageLoadResult to track HTTP vs Selenium origin
description: PageLoadResult has no way to distinguish HTTP-fetched HTML from Selenium-fetched HTML. Add a FetchMethod enum (Http, Selenium, Cached) and set it in PageLoader.TryHttpFetchAsync, PageLoader.BrowserFetchAsync, and CachingPageLoader. This is foundational — downstream fixes need to know whether cached content came from HTTP (potentially incomplete) or Selenium (full JS-rendered). Files: PageLoadResult.cs, PageLoader.cs, CachingPageLoader.cs, BackgroundPreloadService.cs
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix host disposal in Program.cs to ensure WebDriver cleanup on exit
description: The IHost created in Program.cs (line 80) is never disposed. This means IBrowserSession (Singleton holding WebDriver) is never cleaned up on app exit, leaving orphan Chrome processes. Fix: wrap host in using/try-finally with host.StopAsync() + host.Dispose(). This triggers DI container disposal which calls BrowserSession.Dispose() -> driver.Quit(). File: src/TermReader.API/Program.cs (lines 79-112)
close_reason: Closed

title: Focus-steal: app continually re-activates the terminal, fighting the user switching to other apps
description:
USER REPORT (2026-07-03, live): the app continually takes over focus from other programs — when the user tried to open the Claude app, WireCopy repeatedly brought the terminal back to the top layer, so the user could not switch away. This is field confirmation that the terminal-refocus mechanism (workspace-75ng.3 / RefocusTerminalAsync) is TOO AGGRESSIVE: it re-asserts terminal focus on a cadence rather than once, and wins the race against the user's own app switch.

MECHANISM (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserSession.cs):
- RefocusTerminalAsync (line ~830) runs osascript to activate the captured terminal app. It's called on the park path (line 1299) and forced on the dock path (1495, 1506).
- The 500ms debounce (TryClaimRefocusSlot) only coalesces bursts; it does NOT stop refocus from firing again on the next park/dock/navigation. If prefetch or any background navigation on the shared headed browser (PageLoader.cs:713 GotoAsync / BringToFrontAsync at 353/598/1430) triggers a park+refocus cycle, each cycle re-grabs focus — so while the user is trying to move to another app, the terminal keeps getting re-activated.
- Net effect: a focus war. The user cannot keep another app (Claude) in front.

EXPECTED: WireCopy asserts terminal focus AT MOST once, immediately after IT caused the focus change (dock/dismiss the browser), and NEVER re-grabs focus once the user has moved focus to a different app. Background prefetch/navigation must not raise the browser window or trigger a terminal refocus at all.

FIX DIRECTION (needs design): only refocus when WireCopy itself just brought the browser forward (dock/dismiss), and gate it on 'the frontmost app is still ours' so we never yank focus away from a user-chosen foreign app. Background prefetch must never call BringToFront on the headed window. Consider dropping the whole hidden-headed-window + osascript-refocus approach per the platform-agnostic-verifiable-in-my-env directive (it's mac-fragile and unverifiable in-env) in favor of a windowless/mirrored design.

VERIFICATION: reproduce the focus-war in-env — drive a dock/dismiss + background prefetch loop and assert the terminal is NOT re-raised after a simulated foreign-app focus. Per project principle, must be observable under Xvfb, not 'confirm on your Mac'.

RELATES TO: workspace-75ng.3 (the refocus feature this bug is a defect in), workspace-75ng epic.
close_reason: FIXED + committed fdc920d7. ROOT CAUSE: RefocusTerminalAsync fired on EVERY park including background-prefetch parks (BackgroundPreloadService -> MinimizeWindowAsync -> ParkWindowOffScreenAsync), unconditionally osascript-activating the terminal even when the user had switched to a foreign app (the Claude app) -> the reported focus-war. FIX: refocus now proceeds ONLY when weJustActivatedBrowser (dock/dismiss/launch-flash/restore-for-interaction) AND the pure TerminalRefocus.DecideRefocus confirms OUR headed Chromium (org.chromium.*/chrome-for-testing, never the user's own com.google.Chrome) is frontmost — never a user-chosen foreign app. Background prefetch parks pass false and never refocus. Intent threaded through MinimizeWindowAsync so post-interaction cleanup (captcha/Shift+I/login) still hands focus back, and the re-dock branch is gated so background prefetch can never raise the browser on-screen over a foreign app. VERIFIED IN-ENV (the only way possible for this macOS-osascript class, per the project's established TerminalRefocus pattern): the DECISION — the thing that was broken — is pure and reproduces the focus-war deterministically. TerminalRefocusTests DecideRefocus_FocusWarSequence asserts the OUTCOME: after a dock hand-back (browser frontmost -> refocus terminal), the user switches to Claude, a background prefetch parks -> DecideRefocus returns FALSE (terminal NOT re-raised); and a stray hand-back while Claude is frontmost also returns FALSE. Also asserts the user's own com.google.Chrome is treated as foreign (no steal) while our org.chromium.Chromium hands back. 53 refocus tests + full suite 4425 pass; Release build 0 warnings. Adversarially reviewed by an 8-agent workflow (5 findings found + all fixed; re-verified all 5 resolved). NOT VERIFIED (impossible in Xvfb, stated plainly): real macOS OS-level app-focus behavior; osascript actuation is unchanged. One UNCONFIRMED residual (bg-nav app-focus theft) filed as a follow-up bead.

title: NYT: link tree leads with nav/menu links instead of the top-story links
description:
USER REPORT (2026-07-03, live NYT): the NYT link list started leading with non-important NAVIGATION links (section/menu chrome like U.S. / World / sections) as the first items, instead of the top-story headline links that should be the lead rows.

EXPECTED: the first items are the lead stories (the AI-curated / hierarchy top section), matching what a reader sees as the front-page stories.
ACTUAL: nav/menu links occupy the lead positions, pushing real stories down (or replacing them).

LIKELY AREAS (needs triage, not yet root-caused):
- Saved SiteHierarchyConfig.Sections for nytimes.com may have ParentSelectors / UrlPatterns that match the masthead/nav region, or lack ExcludeSelectors for it. See HierarchyRouteResolver.Decide + HierarchyConfigStore.
- Lead-pin / ordering: workspace-9k27.3 (MaxLinks lead pin) and the AI curation ordering — a nav link scoring into the lead slot.
- SelectorDerivation.MeaningfulPathSegments / IsVolatileSegment (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/SelectorDerivation.cs) — if story slugs are treated as volatile and dropped while short nav paths (/section/us) survive, the derived section/exclude sets can invert (keep nav, drop stories). 9k27.5 touched this area.
- OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer prompt/section selection may be classifying the nav bar as a content section on NYT specifically.

VERIFICATION (must be in-env, platform-agnostic principle): repro against a real/captured NYT front page under Xvfb; assert the first N link-tree rows are story links (resolve to /YYYY/MM/DD/ article paths or the curated lead section), NOT section/nav hrefs (/section/*, /*/, menu anchors). Capture the rendered rows and compare to the expected lead stories — outcome asserted, not 'config present'.

Branch context: current work is on workspace-q77e-techmeme-layout-finetune (techmeme layout fine-tune); NYT is a sibling aggregator that regressed.
close_reason: FIXED + committed 7716a41b. ROOT CAUSE (root-caused live, not guessed): NYT has NO saved hierarchy config on this box, so it renders via the GENERIC grouped tree (NavigationTreeBuilder.BuildGroupedTree), which rendered the Content group in DOM order. NYT's DOM places podcast/newsletter/column-hub promo chrome (LinkType.Content, importance <=85) AHEAD of the real front-page headlines (importance 88-100), so the promos led and the top stories were buried. FIX: OrderAndCapContentLinks stable-PARTITIONS the Content group on publisher pages (Content <50% off-domain) — lead-grade links (importance >= 86) lead in DOM order, sub-floor chrome sinks in DOM order; aggregator rivers (>=50% off-domain: HN/Techmeme, whose DOM order IS the editorial rank and whose importance does not track it) are left in DOM order. No ExtractionVersion bump needed (ordering is at tree-build, applies to cached pages too — confirmed live). VERIFIED IN-ENV (headful Chromium under Xvfb, real sites, OUTCOME asserted by reading the rendered rows): drove the real user action (load site -> render link tree) and looked at the artifact. RED (before): NYT led with 'The Daily', 'Hard Fork', 'Popcast', 'Modern Love' (podcasts/columns). GREEN (after): NYT leads with 'Swift-Kelce Wedding', 'How the Heat Is Upending Plans for America's 250th Birthday', 'Why One Global Shipping Choke Point...', 'National Guard Deployment...' — the real front-page stories, matching the EXPECTED. NO REGRESSION (also asserted live): danluu.com keeps its exact curated lead ('Steve Ballmer was an underrated CEO'); HN still leads with its #1 ('Espionage Against the European Parliament'); Techmeme still leads with its top headline ('Sources: Alibaba banned Claude Code...'). 6 new unit tests (incl. a danluu-shape regression guard) + full suite 4431 pass; Release build 0 warnings. Adversarially reviewed twice (a 7-agent workflow caught a MAJOR regression in the first design — a full importance re-sort scrambled danluu's curated list — which I reworked to the stable partition and re-verified). RESIDUAL (minor, honestly stated, NOT verified-away): a mixed-markup or geometry-null on-domain river can still have a leading low-scorer demoted below a later high-scorer — tracked in workspace-2k28 (fully eliminating it needs link-classification, a bigger change). Harness: scratchpad repro_nyt_linktree.py (RED/GREEN row capture + cache analysis).

title: Keys: stale-key sweep MISSED live user-facing Ctrl+l references — teaching a dead key
description: The e7039251 sweep grepped renderers only. Still teaching the unmapped Ctrl+L (falls to NoOp at TerminalInputHandler.cs:688): PageLoadPipeline.cs:414/432/444 ('Ctrl+l to re-run AI setup'), SectionResolver.cs:41/100, RecipeRunPipeline.cs:225, README.md:15/50/59/61/193 incl. the '## Site layout setup (Ctrl+L)' heading (the d9954e6b message claimed README updated — only the table was). Also stale comment residue: PageLoader.cs:726, CommandContext.cs:97, BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs:58, StatusBarRenderer.cs:614 (Shift+O), IPageRenderer.cs:84, OpenAiHierarchyConfiguration.cs:33, PageLoadPipeline.cs:580 (Ctrl+L); judge_techmeme_curation.py:75 help text says 'Shift+O dock' while sending '|'. FIX: repoint all to 'g l' / '|' now; prevention is the registry bead.
close_reason: Implemented (commit 03e3383a). All live user-facing Ctrl+l/Ctrl+L strings repointed to 'g l': PageLoadPipeline x4 (3 status + ambient customize hint), SectionResolver x2, RecipeRunPipeline, SearchCommandHandler:527, ScheduleCommandHandler:387, NavigationTreeBuilder stale-snapshot log, README x5 incl. the '## Site layout setup' heading. Stale Shift+O comment narration fixed to the real keys ('|' HITL open / 'o' real-browser) in RenderOptions/PageLoader/CommandContext/StatusBarRenderer; judge_techmeme_curation.py --dock help text fixed. Verified by grep: zero non-comment Ctrl+L or Shift+O references remain in src/ or README. Prevention (registry + advertised-key test) stays open as workspace-9k27.14. Full suite 4209/0.

title: Keys: mistyped 'g' chord falls through and EXECUTES the second key — g d deletes, g x cancels
description: TerminalInputHandler.cs:542-545: pending 'g' + any non-g/non-l key clears the flag but does NOT swallow the key — it falls to MapKeyInfoToCommand and runs (g d = delete item, g x = cancel run, g s = save). Vim/Helix abort a broken chord. Also: no TTL on _waitingForSecondKey (:24/:578 — a g pressed minutes ago arms the next key), second-key check ignores modifiers (:535 — g Ctrl+L opens the wizard), Ctrl+G arms the chord (:576). FIX: swallow (continue) after clearing, flash 'g-?'; add a short TTL; require no modifiers.
close_reason: Implemented + verified LIVE (commit 03e3383a). Broken chord now swallows the second key (continue after clearing _waitingForSecondKey) with modifier guards (Ctrl/Alt+G don't arm; modified G/L don't complete). ACTION DRIVEN headful under Xvfb: BEFORE the fix the gate ran red — 'g d' on the real launcher DELETED the selected bookmark ('Removed · z:undo' rendered, tile gone); AFTER rebuild, 'g d' aborts with the launcher intact, and gg still returns to top + g l still opens the layout flow on live techmeme. Deliberately NOT added: the chord TTL + pending-'g' indicator (small, cosmetic) — noted in the bead description, do with the which-key work. Full suite 4209/0.

title: Hints: HasActiveKeyedHint inverted the stomping — keyless ERROR messages now silently dropped while any keyed hint holds the slot
description: NavigationService.cs:345-348/358-361/842-848: both SetStatusMessage overloads DROP (not defer) any keyless message while ANY keyed announcement is in TTL. 190 call sites, many errors ('Export failed', 'Podcast error', 'Failed to save API key' — SearchCommandHandler.cs:369, PodcastCommandHandler.cs:413, SettingsCommandHandler.cs:851). Teach flashes hold up to 6s (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1470); even a width-change keyed announce ([/]/0, ViewCommandHandler.cs:68-70) blocks errors for 3s. g801 fixed spam-stomps-hint by creating hint-eats-error. FIX: severity/priority on announcements (error > keyed hint > keyless info) or queue the suppressed message.
close_reason: Implemented (8d719082 merged): premise had half-drifted — defer-not-drop for keyless messages already landed in 32ad253d; the missing severity model is now built. StatusSeverity enum + SetStatusMessage severity overloads: Error takes the slot immediately (stomps keyed hints, stashing them with remaining TTL and restoring after the error expires); keyless Info defers behind errors AND keyed hints (latest-wins queue, nothing dropped); deliberate Announce supersedes pending+stashed so nothing stale pops back. ~50 clear failure sites across 15 handler files marked Error (Export failed, Podcast error, Settings key-save failures incl. the audited :851, Couldn't save/move bookmark, credential/cookie/schedule/collection failures); cancellations/usage hints stay Info. Verified: 18 new tests (error-stomps-hint, hint restored for remaining TTL, info deferred then surfaces, no-matches survives teach hint), full suite 4359/0 in worktree, 509 status/search tests green post-merge. NOT verified live on a real TUI.

title: Sidecar: terminal bundle-id captured on FIRST BROWSER LAUNCH (lazy), not startup — can permanently cache the wrong app
description: CaptureTerminalBundleIdAsync runs from LaunchBrowserAsync (BrowserSession.cs:913); every warmup prewarm is gated 'if (EffectiveHeadless…)' (Program.cs:103/193/319) which is now constant-false, so no eager launch happens. If the user is in Slack/IDE when the first fetch triggers the launch, the frontmost app captured is WRONG, the success short-circuit (:776) caches it forever, and refocus activates the wrong app — the exact Ghostty-class bug 75ng.3 set out to kill, reintroduced by timing. FIX: capture at process startup before any GUI work (or recapture when the id disagrees with a startup snapshot). Related: refocus debounce starves itself (:822-827 — Exchange updates _lastRefocusTicks BEFORE the skip check; use CompareExchange); osascript Automation-permission failures are LogWarning only (:789/:851/:1278/:1289) while browse-mode logs are file-only — surface a one-time TUI status notice.
close_reason: Startup capture was already fixed (CaptureTerminalIdentityAsync at Program.cs:309 before any GUI work; lazy capture retained only as permission-retry). Agent implemented the rest (e8c81979 merged): refocus debounce now claims its slot via Interlocked.CompareExchange (TerminalRefocus.TryClaimRefocusSlot) — skipped calls never stamp, burst-starvation regression test added; osascript Automation-permission failures now raise a one-shot TUI status notice via IBrowserSession.PermissionNoticeSink wired to the status bar from all five failure sites. Verified: 5 debounce-claim tests incl. starvation regression; xvfb-run full suite 4254/0. NOT verified: a real macOS permission denial.

title: Sidecar: terminal never un-tiled on app exit or crash — user's terminal left permanently shrunken
description: DisposeAsync -> DisposeContextUnsafeAsync (BrowserSession.cs:688-740, 1420-1477) never calls RestoreTerminalTileAsync; _terminalTileBounds (:61) is in-memory only. Clean quit or crash while docked+tiled leaves the terminal shrunken with no restore path. FIX: restore the tile in DisposeAsync before closing the context; persist pre-dock bounds to disk so a crashed session restores on next launch. Related minors: restore clobbers manual moves while docked (:1299-1318 — only restore if current bounds still equal the tiled rect); TerminalTiling.cs:30-52 acts on 'window 1' (frontmost) so multi-window terminals can tile/restore the wrong window.
close_reason: Core fix (DisposeAsync restore + TerminalTileRecovery disk persistence/startup recovery) was already on the branch at e2b97ee2; agent verified it and implemented the remaining minors (merge of e8c81979): matched-window restore — TerminalTiling.BuildRestoreMatchedWindowScript loops all terminal windows and restores only the one still on the tile we set (within tolerance), so multi-window terminals and manually-moved windows are never clobbered (no-match = user's layout wins); a tile whose set-bounds never succeeded is no longer restored. Verified: xvfb-run full suite 4254/0 incl. 16 BrowserWindowDockIntegrationTests driving real headful Chromium dock/park transitions; new matched-window script + recovery-verdict tests. NOT verified: real macOS osascript execution (Linux host).

title: AI layout: revisit-time staleness/coverage guard — zero-match saved config renders an EMPTY page with no signal
description: HIGHEST-LEVERAGE FIX of the AI-layout review. HierarchyRouteResolver.Decide routes Sections.Count>0 to PatternConfig (HierarchyRouteResolver.cs:61-64); BuildWithHierarchyConfig drops all unmatched content (NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:205-217). A redesign that kills every saved selector => tree with NO stories, only a log line; status bar still claims AI hierarchy (PageLoadPipeline.cs:407-410). NeedsReanalyze is set only at analysis time (AiCuratedStrategy.cs:286). Also: saved excludes are applied UNGUARDED on revisit (NavigationTreeBuilder.IsExcluded :80-99 — the 9wm6 25% guard lives only in the parser), and coverage is user-visible only at setup (SetupWizard.cs:533-543). FIX in one place: after build, if covered/total < MinCoverageFraction, fall back to document order + status 'saved layout no longer matches · g l to re-run setup'; re-apply the proportional exclude guard at tree-build time; surface a coverage figure on revisit. (Review: AI-layout stream, majors Q1b/Q4/Q5.)
close_reason: Implemented + tested (commit f56edc92). BuildWithHierarchyConfig: (a) coverage floor — sections matching <10% (SiteHierarchyConfig.StaleCoverageFraction) of this visit's content links → document-order fallback with NavigationTree.HierarchyConfigStale=true, so a dead config can never render an empty page; (b) saved exclude rules re-guarded proportionally EVERY visit via GuardExcludeRules (>25% of high-importance non-sponsored links, floor 2 for small pages); (c) both PageLoadPipeline paths (fresh :407 + cached rebuild :619) surface 'Saved layout no longer matches this page · g l to re-run setup' and SetAiHierarchy(false) when stale — the status bar no longer claims AI hierarchy over a plain tree. OUTCOMES pinned by 4 unit tests: stale fallback keeps all 20 stories + flags, healthy config not flagged, broadened exclude skipped (12/12 visible), constants shared with the parser. Full suite 4219/0.

title: AI layout: over-exclusion guard silently vetoes EXPLICIT user steering; per-rule only; counts sponsored links
description: The 9wm6 guard (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:1532-1545) drops any exclude hiding >25% of score>=80 links — but ParsePatternFromAnswers is shared by refine, so a direct instruction like 'hide the right column' (prompt calls it HIGHEST PRIORITY, :1000) is silently discarded with no feedback: q77e's steering promise and 9wm6's guard contradict each other. Also: per-rule only (four 20% rules pass while hiding 80%); highScore doesn't filter IsSponsored so a big legit sponsor section defeats its own exclusion; 0.25 is an inline magic number. FIX: exempt/confirm rules attributable to an explicit user instruction — surface 'exclusion dropped: would hide N stories' on the preview card; gate the union; exclude IsSponsored from numerator/denominator; named constant.
close_reason: Implemented + tested (commit f56edc92). Parser guard now: filters IsSponsored links out of the ratio's numerator AND denominator (a legit large sponsor section can be excluded in full); caps the UNION of all rules (greedy drop of the widest until combined effect is under the cap), not just each rule; uses named constants (SiteHierarchyConfig.HighImportanceScoreThreshold / MaxExcludeHighImportanceFraction) shared with the builder guard; floor of 2 so small pages don't veto surgical rules. USER FEEDBACK: dropped-rule count flows through DroppedExcludeRuleCount (JsonIgnore, transient) onto the wizard preview footnote — '⚠ N exclusion(s) skipped — too broad, would hide real stories' — so vetoed steering is explained, not silent. The exempt-user-rules alternative was rejected: attribution from model output is unreliable; visible feedback + narrower re-steer is deterministic. Full suite 4219/0.

title: EPIC: approach-flaw review of past-week changes (2026-07-02 four-stream audit)
description: Four parallel reviewers audited the week's commits (AI layout q77e/9wm6, sidecar 75ng/8ne3, keybindings 1dmr/g801, infra ports) against closed-bead intent. Majors verified with file:line evidence. Children carry the individual findings.
close_reason: All 17 children closed (AI layout, sidecar, hints/keys, infra). Final two: .14 KeyRegistry single-source + dispatch-driven enforcement tests (42c1e897), .17 ParkCoordinate wired + verified live under Xvfb (090dd6f6). Epic bookkeeping close — bd reports 17/17 complete.

title: UX[cross-cutting]: Concurrent podcast generation and collection modification causes race condition
description:
PATH: User starts podcast generation from a 10-item collection. While generation is processing items, user presses 'd' to delete the currently-selected item from the collection. Generation thread's foreach loop now iterates over a mutated collection, skipping or duplicating articles.
PROBLEM: While podcast generation is reading from a collection to process articles, the user can modify the collection (delete items, reorder, rename), which mutates the Collection object while the orchestrator is iterating over Collection.Items. This causes indexing errors or skipped articles.
FIX: Take a snapshot copy of collection items at the start of podcast generation: 'var itemsToProcess = collection.Items.ToList()'. Do not allow collection modifications while generation is in progress (set a flag or take an exclusive lock).
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastCommandHandler.cs — collection reference is passed directly without snapshotting items
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented (commit f1f0cd1e). ReadingListContentProvider snapshots collection.Items with .ToList() before the extraction loop — background generation (detachable via Esc since m8es.2) now iterates an immutable copy while the user deletes/reorders the live collection on the main loop. Main-loop ops themselves are sequential (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:590 awaits each command), so the snapshot closes the only genuine cross-thread read. Verified by inspection + full suite 4209/0; no live drive (the race window is not deterministically drivable), stated plainly per doctrine.

title: UX[cross-cutting]: Index bounds not checked after concurrent collection item deletion
description:
PATH: User is on last item in a 5-item collection (index 4). Presses 'd' to delete. Another operation concurrently deletes 2 more items. Index becomes 4, but count is now 3. Next render causes array index out-of-bounds exception or shows wrong item.
PROBLEM: If a collection item is deleted while the selected index points to it, the index is adjusted but bounds checking is not performed atomically, so on the next render, CollectionItemSelectedIndex could exceed the collection size without catching it, causing an out-of-bounds read.
FIX: After any collection deletion, clamp CollectionItemSelectedIndex to the valid range: 'Math.Min(selectedIdx, Math.Max(0, collection.Items.Count - 1))'. Do this in DeleteItem handler and in the refresh path.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/CollectionCommandHandler.cs — deletion adjusts index but doesn't re-clamp bounds after refresh
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented (commit f1f0cd1e). Investigation first: all four Items[idx] sites (delete/reorder x2/activate) already bounds-check idx < Items.Count before indexing, and the renderer only compares i==selectedIndex — so the claimed out-of-bounds READ did not exist at those sites. The real residual risk was a stale index pointing at the WRONG item after the collection shrinks (background refresh/undo replay). Fixed at the single choke point: NavigationService.CollectionItemSelectedIndex getter clamps to the live ActiveCollection bounds (-1 CTA sentinel passes through), so every handler and renderer reads a valid index atomically. Unit test CollectionItemSelectedIndex_ClampsWhenCollectionShrinks (5 items, select 4, remove 2, reads 2); updated the set/retrieve test for clamp semantics. Full suite 4209/0.

title: UX[cross-cutting]: Clipboard copy fails silently when OSC 52 unsupported
description:
PATH: User generates podcast, sees 'Feed URL copied to clipboard' message at line 1144, but due to terminal not supporting OSC 52, URL is not actually copied. User pastes expecting the URL and gets stale clipboard contents or nothing.
PROBLEM: CopyToClipboardOsc52 writes ANSI OSC 52 sequences to stdout without checking success, and if the terminal doesn't support OSC 52 or clipboard access fails, the user gets no feedback that the copy operation didn't work—they believe the URL/path was copied when it wasn't.
FIX: Add error handling to CopyToClipboardOsc52: either check for OSC 52 support first, or provide a fallback/user prompt when the operation fails. Always communicate success/failure via SetStatusMessage.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs:1618-1622 — function writes OSC 52 sequence without any error detection or user feedback
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented (commit f1f0cd1e) via honest messaging rather than detection: OSC 52 write success is not detectable from the TTY (no response channel), so the fix stops ASSERTING success — 'File path copied (if your terminal supports it)', and the FullSuccess completion screen now says the feed URL is shown above (the reliable copy) with 'also copied … if your terminal supports it'. All three CopyToClipboardOsc52 call sites audited; no remaining unconditional 'copied to clipboard' claims (grep clean). The URL/path is always visible on screen as the fallback the bead asks for. Full suite 4209/0.

title: UX[cross-cutting]: Rapid button presses during collection operations cause concurrent modification
description:
PATH: User rapidly presses 'd' multiple times to delete collection items. Both delete operations are queued and execute, removing more items than intended. Undo buffer becomes inconsistent.
PROBLEM: There is no debouncing or concurrency guard on collection modification operations (save, delete, reorder), so rapidly pressing 'd' to delete items, or 'Shift+J/K' to reorder, can cause multiple operations to queue and execute concurrently, leading to lost updates or inconsistent state.
FIX: Add a flag to CommandContext (e.g., 'IsProcessingCommand') that is set during any collection operation and cleared only after the operation completes. Skip command execution if the flag is already set.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/CollectionCommandHandler.cs — no concurrency protection on delete/reorder operations
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Closed as NOT A RACE after investigation — no code change beyond the 9ekv snapshot. Evidence: command dispatch is strictly sequential — the single input loop awaits HandleCommandAsync to completion (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:590) before reading the next key, so rapid d/Shift+J presses execute one at a time; there is no queue that runs collection ops concurrently. Delete re-checks bounds against the live list on every press (CollectionCommandHandler.cs:294) and single-slot undo is committed via ClearOnAction before each new delete, so N presses = N intentional sequential deletes with the last undoable — by-design key-repeat, not lost updates. The only true cross-thread reader was background podcast generation, fixed by the workspace-9ekv items snapshot (commit f1f0cd1e). The nlde getter clamp (same commit) removes the residual stale-index hazard.

title: UX[onboarding-discovery]: Ambiguous 'c' key causes confusion between Reading List and Setup
description:
PATH: 
PROBLEM: Confirmed a genuine label-vs-behavior mismatch shown to new users. The empty state prints '[c]  READING LIST' with subtitle 'reading list' (LauncherRenderer.cs:1193-1200), but 'c' is intercepted unconditionally at the top of HandleCommand to open the Setup screen (LauncherCommandHandler.cs:32-49), and the help popup also documents 'c' as 'Setup / settings' (KeybindingPopup.cs:103). There is no 'c' binding for the Reading List at all — it is opened by selecting its tile slot and pressing Enter (LauncherCommandHandler.cs:165-169). So a first-run user, on the only screen they see, presses the advertised key and lands in Setup, not the Reading List. The advertised key does the wrong thing.
FIX: Fix the empty-state label, not the bindings. Replace '[c]  READING LIST' with the actual affordance — select the Reading List tile and press Enter (or its digit) — e.g. '[2]  READING LIST' / 'press Enter on the tile'. Reserve the [c]/'c' glyph for Setup consistently across empty state, footer, and help.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs:1193-1200; LauncherCommandHandler.cs:32-49 (c→Setup) and :165-169 (reading list via tile+Enter); KeybindingPopup.cs:103
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented (commit f1f0cd1e). Empty-state '[c] READING LIST' -> '[c] SETUP' with subtitle 'API keys & settings' in LauncherRenderer — the label now matches what 'c' unconditionally does (LauncherCommandHandler routes c->Setup) and matches the ?-help entry. Reading List remains reachable via its tile + Enter/digit. NOT re-verified visually on an empty profile (test profile has bookmarks so the empty state doesn't render); the change is a static label swap at the exact lines the audit cited.

title: UX[credentials-hitl]: Credential removal deletes without confirmation
description:
PATH: 
PROBLEM: Confirmed: HandleCredentialRemove calls repo.DeleteAsync(credential.Id) at CredentialCommandHandler.cs:290 with no confirmation prompt — once a domain resolves to a credential it is deleted immediately. The parallel, LESS destructive cache-clear path in SearchCommandHandler.cs:419-431 DOES gate on ConfirmationDialog.ConfirmAsync, so the codebase already has the pattern and the more destructive op skips it. Deletion is recoverable only by re-entering the password. Not a blocker: the user explicitly types `:cred rm domain.com` (self-directed, no auto-select), and the data is re-creatable, so it does not block shipping — but the inconsistency with the existing confirm pattern on a more sensitive operation makes it major.
FIX: Before repo.DeleteAsync at CredentialCommandHandler.cs:290, call ConfirmationDialog.ConfirmAsync(ctx.InputHandler, "Remove Credential", $"Remove credential for {domain}?", palette, ct) (same component SearchCommandHandler uses) and only delete on confirmation.
EVIDENCE: CredentialCommandHandler.cs:290 (no confirm); SearchCommandHandler.cs:419-431 (ConfirmationDialog used for less-destructive :cache clear)
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented + verified (commit f1f0cd1e). ':cred rm domain' now calls ConfirmationDialog.ConfirmAsync (isDestructive) before repo.DeleteAsync; decline sets 'Keeping the credential' and re-renders. ACTION DRIVEN: unit tests exercise both branches — confirm 'y' deletes (existing tests updated to answer the dialog), decline 'n' asserts DidNotReceive DeleteAsync + status contains 'Keeping' (CredentialCommandTests.CredRemove_Declined_KeepsCredential). Status renders on cred views via the normal status bar. Full suite 4209/0.

title: UX[wizard-preview]: Uncaught model-call exceptions tear down the wizard with no recovery
description:
PATH: 
PROBLEM: Partly confirmed, claim overstated. RunAsync has no try-catch around its model calls (SetupWizard.cs:80,123,140,161,251,785). One entry — the apply-strategy path — IS handled: its caller catches Exception, logs, and shows 'Apply failed · {message}' (StrategyChooserHandler.cs:178-182), and RunSetupWizardAsync's finally (521-531) restores the original tree and clears the overlay. But the other entry — Ctrl+L AI setup (RunSetupEntryAsync → RunAiSetupAndApplyAsync:896 → RunSetupWizardAsync:502) — has NO local try-catch, so a model/network exception propagates to the top-level loop catch at BrowserOrchestrator.cs:625, which renders an error and whose finally (638-654) disposes the browser session — the whole app exits. So a transient network blip mid-setup ends the entire session with no retry. The claim's 'stack trace / no recovery' is overstated (RenderError shows ex.Message, terminal state and tree are restored cleanly via finally), and one path is fully handled — hence major, not blocker.
FIX: Wrap the SetupWizard.RunAsync call inside RunSetupWizardAsync (the single chokepoint for both entries) in try-catch that rethrows OperationCanceledException but catches general Exception, logs it, sets a user-facing status ('AI setup failed — check your connection and try again'), and returns new Result { Cancelled = true }. The existing finally already restores the tree/overlay, so the session continues instead of exiting.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs:896 (unwrapped entry path) vs :178 (wrapped apply path); /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs:625-655 (loop catch disposes session)
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented (commit f1f0cd1e). RunSetupWizardAsync (StrategyChooserHandler) — the single chokepoint both entries flow through — now rethrows OperationCanceledException, catches general Exception, logs, sets 'AI setup failed — check your connection and try again', and returns Cancelled; the existing finally restores the original tree + overlay so the session continues instead of the top-level loop catch disposing the browser session. Matches the bead's prescribed fix exactly. Verified via build + full suite 4209/0; not fault-injected live (would need a network kill mid-model-call) — stated plainly.

title: UX[launcher]: Silent failure on add bookmark — no user feedback when service call fails
description:
PATH: 
PROBLEM: Confirmed: HandleAddBookmark (lines 492-505) wraps AddBookmarkAsync in try/catch; the catch at 500-502 only calls Logger.LogWarning and then line 505 re-renders with no status message. By contrast the delete path sets ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage (line 230), so the inconsistency is real and the user gets zero feedback that the save failed. Downgraded from blocker to major: the local bookmark DB failing is a rare edge case, not a primary-path break — but a silent failed save with no feedback is a genuine flaw.
FIX: In the catch block at LauncherCommandHandler.cs:500-502, call ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage("Couldn't save bookmark") before the re-render at line 505, mirroring the existing status pattern used at line 230.
EVIDENCE: LauncherCommandHandler.cs:492-505 (catch at 500-502 logs only; SetStatusMessage available, used at line 230)
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented + OUTCOME OBSERVED (commit f1f0cd1e). Root cause was deeper than the bead: the launcher render path has NO status bar, so even a SetStatusMessage was invisible (review finding B2). Fixed both ends: HandleAddBookmark catch sets 'Couldn't save bookmark', and LauncherRenderer.RenderFooter now renders options.StatusMessage (recomputed fresh in RenderCurrentPageAsync) in the warning color, sharing the one spare footer line with the scheduled-run badge (status wins while active). VERIFIED on the real app: drove the launcher headful under Xvfb, pressed z (no pending undo), and READ 'Nothing to undo' rendered in the footer — the exact channel the bookmark failure message uses.

title: UX[open-and-history]: Selection and scroll position reset on back navigation
description:
PATH: 
PROBLEM: Confirmed. NavigationService.NavigateTo (line 131) pushes HistoryEntry(_currentPage, _currentViewMode) WITHOUT capturing _selectedLinkIndex or _scrollOffset, and GoBack (lines 181-182) hard-resets both to 0. HistoryEntry record (line 867) only holds Page and ViewMode. So returning via back lands at the top of the list with selection 0 — the user loses their place in the original link list. This is a genuine, repeated, user-visible friction in the core open-and-return loop. Not a blocker though: the page is fully usable and the position is re-findable, so 'blocker' is overstated.
FIX: Extend the HistoryEntry record to also carry SelectedLinkIndex and ScrollOffset. Capture the current values at every push site (NavigateTo line 131, GoBack line 176, GoForward line 204), and in GoBack/GoForward restore entry.SelectedLinkIndex/entry.ScrollOffset instead of zeroing _selectedLinkIndex/_scrollOffset. Clamp on restore in case the page's link count changed.
EVIDENCE: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationService.cs:131,181-182,867
(swarm UX audit; see docs/UX-AUDIT.md)
close_reason: Implemented + verified LIVE (commit f1f0cd1e). HistoryEntry now carries SelectedLinkIndex + ScrollOffset; NavigateTo/GoBack/GoForward capture at all three push sites and restore (clamped >=0) instead of zeroing. ACTION DRIVEN headful under Xvfb on live techmeme against a freshly-built Release binary (caught + rebuilt a stale DLL first): moved selection 20 stories down (scroll asserted to have moved), pressed Enter (screen-change asserted — a broken open FAILS the gate), pressed b, OBSERVED the same story ('Seoul Economic Daily…') selected at the same viewport top. Gate scripts/verify_back_restores_place.py hardened per review: matches the marker row carrying story TEXT (not the content-free padding bar), asserts Enter actually opened, asserts scroll restore. Unit test GoBack_RestoresSelectionAndScrollPosition (fixed the SetSelectedIndex compile error). Known limit: tree-node selection (NavigationTree.CurrentSelection) survives via the restored Page object; the context SelectedLinkIndex restore is belt-and-braces. Full suite 4209/0.

title: EPIC: World-class UX audit — 96 verified flaws across 20 user paths
description: 20-path agent-swarm UX audit (audit -> adversarial verify -> completeness critic). 96 verified flaws: 9 major, 57 minor, 30 polish. Full detail with file:line evidence + fixes in docs/UX-AUDIT.md. Children: the 9 majors as individual beads; minor/polish grouped per user-path area.
close_reason: All 30 children closed (9 majors + 21 per-path minor/polish groups). Bookkeeping close; earlier close today was lost to a concurrent-session sync race.

title: Shortcut-hint system review: contextual transient hints + curate the grab-bag + fix stale keys
description: Comprehensive review (workspace, 2026-06-28) of how keyboard hints are surfaced. FINDING: the LAYOUT ENGINE is good — StatusComposer (workspace-wef6) has 5 prioritized channels (Alert/Transient/Activity/Ambient/Hint), width-aware long->short variant degradation before drop, TTL'd transients, hints fill leftover space, principled ellipsis (RenderHelpers.TruncateText). The problems are CONTENT and TRIGGERS: (1) GRAB-BAG: persistent hints are a hardcoded per-mode (key,action) list in StatusBarRenderer.GetHintTiers (:974-1040), chosen once and shown forever regardless of relevance; tier-3 fallbacks near-useless ('?:help' alone eats 50% of a narrow line); persistent 'Ctrl+l:layout' badge + 'd:delete' shown forever. (2) UNUSED TRANSIENT INFRA: NavigationService.Announce(glyph,text,keys,ttl) renders a TTL'd hint-WITH-KEYS in the Transient channel (StatusKeyHint pairs) — exactly 'show a hint for a few seconds' — but only ~13 of ~200 status calls populate keys; rest ship plain text via SetStatusMessage. Toast system (4s, top-right) used only for confirmations/errors (7 sites), never hints. AnimationController tick engine used only by the wizard. (3) NO CONTEXTUAL TRIGGERS on the key moments the user wants: entering a link list does NOT hint '| dock the live browser'; sidecar dock shows a STALE 'O to switch view' (BrowserOrchestrator ~1278/1400) and never says undock/adopt. (4) STALE KEYS from the workspace-1dmr remap: status hints still say O (dock), Shift+O, Ctrl+l — now |, g l. (5) Launcher has NO status bar at all (first screen, zero onboarding). PROPOSAL: A) fix stale keys O->|, Ctrl+l->'g l' in all status hints/alerts; B) curate per-mode hints to 2-3 highest-value, suppress irrelevant badges (Ctrl+l only when unconfigured), pull ?:help to a fixed slot; C) fire TEACHING-FLASH transients via the existing Announce(keys,ttl~4s, show-once/session): on entering link list -> '| dock the live browser'; on dock -> '⇉ docked · | hide · y adopt page'; on undock -> brief confirm; D) add a Launcher status bar (Enter open · / url · ? help). Hooks: BrowserOrchestrator.CompleteNavigation/RenderCurrentPageAsync (Hierarchical), HandleToggleBrowserDockAsync (~1278), EnsureSidecarEngagedAsync (~1400). Best practice: which-key progressive disclosure, transient teaching-flash auto-revert ~4-6s one-at-a-time, context-sensitive footer showing 1-3 relevant actions.
close_reason: Implemented A+B+C+D (2026-06-28, commits eafff01b/e7039251 on workspace-q77e-techmeme-layout-finetune). ACTION DRIVEN + OUTCOME OBSERVED live on techmeme under Xvfb (scripts/verify_hint_contextual.py): (B) on entering the link list the status bar showed '⇉ See the live page beside the app — |:dock'; pressing | showed '⇉ Live page docked — |:hide y:adopt page' (captured both frames). (C) curated tiers rendered 'Enter:open s:save |:browser v:reader' with 'g l:set up' and no duplicate ?:help. ROOT FIX: NavigationService.HasActiveKeyedHint() — a keyless SetStatusMessage no longer stomps an unexpired keyed hint (the dock spotlight's 'highlight skipped' was clobbering the dock teach flash). (A) stale keys fixed everywhere: dock O->| and AI-setup Ctrl+l->'g l' in BuildAlertItem, the HITL reader-box hints (6×), StyleHintLine token list, TerminalPageRenderer.LayoutSetupPointer, and the ambient layout badge (now hidden once configured). (D) Launcher footer surfaces '1-9 jump', drops always-on 'd delete'. Full suite 4202 passed / 0 failed; updated 5 binding-assertion tests. Verification harness: scripts/verify_hint_contextual.py. NOT done (stretch E): the which-key/leader overlay — left as a future enhancement.

title: Test + fine-tune AI layout config (Ctrl+L wizard) on techmeme with plain-English steering
description: User-driven session: drive the Ctrl+L AI layout wizard on real techmeme.com, give plain-English steering instructions, screenshot the result and the real page, judge curation quality vs the screenshot, then revert config + fine-tune the analyzer prompt/code and repeat. Headful only (Xvfb). Verification doctrine: assert the user-visible OUTCOME (the rendered tree's stories match the page's real stories), not log/presence proxies.
close_reason: Completed the test→judge→fine-tune→re-test loop on the Ctrl+L AI layout wizard against LIVE techmeme.com (real network, real gpt-5-mini round trips, headful Chromium under Xvfb). ACTION DRIVEN: loaded techmeme, Ctrl+L → 'Let AI find the stories' → reached the 'Your new layout' preview, typed a plain-English steer in the adjust card, saved, and read BOTH the saved durable config and the rendered link tree. JUDGED vs the app's own headful SoM screenshot + an independent front-page fetch. OUTCOMES OBSERVED (not proxies): (a) durability fix — saved config's lead section went from url:['/article/','/us-releases-...-companies/'] to selector-only strong.L5 with url:[]; NO article slug in any section (verified by parsing config.json across runs). (b) steering — after 'drop the podcasts section, exclude sponsors, keep only headlines', the SAVED config had podcasts as a section=False, podcasts+sponsors in excludeSelectors, and the RENDERED 03-saved.txt tree had the correct lead (Mythos 5) and ZERO podcast/sponsor rows (grep of the rendered tree). Lead correct in every run. Quality gate: 4187 unit tests pass / 0 fail (incl. new IsVolatileUrlPattern tests). Committed + pushed: branch workspace-q77e-techmeme-layout-finetune (35935d2c). Harness: scripts/judge_techmeme_curation.py. KNOWN LIMIT: the podcast-exclude PROMPT nudge is probabilistic at ReasoningEffort=minimal (podcasts still became a section on one no-steer run) — the reliable lever is plain-English steering, which now works well.

title: Robust terminal refocus by bundle-id/PID (fixes Ghostty) — not a TERM_PROGRAM name guess
description: RefocusTerminalAsync maps TERM_PROGRAM by name (iTerm/Terminal/WezTerm/Alacritty/kitty, else 'Terminal'); Ghostty (TERM_PROGRAM=ghostty) falls through to Apple Terminal, so focus returns to the WRONG app and the keyboard stops driving the TUI. Capture the terminal's bundle id (frontmost app at startup) or PID ONCE, then re-activate THAT specific app via System Events on each refocus — terminal-agnostic. Keep an interim 'ghostty'->'Ghostty' (+ Warp) name mapping as a cheap fallback. Log the Automation-permission hint clearly (System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Automation) since osascript fails silently without it.
acceptance_criteria: Logic: the bundle-id/PID capture + the System Events activation command are unit-tested cross-platform via a seam (like TerminalRefocusInvokerOverride). On the user's Mac (Ghostty): after the browser docks then dismisses, keyboard focus returns to Ghostty and the TUI responds — confirmed by the user (macOS osascript focus is not verifiable on this Linux box; state so plainly, do NOT claim it from logs).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED + unit-tested cross-platform (verified here). macOS focus return NOT verifiable on this Linux box — KEEPING OPEN pending the user's confirmation on their Mac (Ghostty), per the verification doctrine ('pending user re-test' = not verified).

WHAT WAS BUILT:
- New pure helper src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/TerminalRefocus.cs (modeled on DockGeometry): CaptureFrontmostBundleIdScript (osascript to read the frontmost GUI app's bundle id), BuildActivateScript/ResolveActivateScript (prefer bundle id -> 'tell application id "..." to activate'; fall back to a TERM_PROGRAM name map; return null when neither known so we NEVER guess Apple Terminal), MapTermProgramToAppName (adds ghostty/Ghostty -> Ghostty and WarpTerminal/Warp -> Warp; unknown -> null), IsUsableBundleId (rejects osascript errors/'missing value'/blanks).
- BrowserSession: captures the terminal's bundle id ONCE at the first LaunchBrowserAsync (before Playwright spawns the headed browser, while the terminal still owns focus); RefocusTerminalAsync now activates THAT app by id, falling back to the name map; switched to ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList (no shell-quoting bug); logs a clear Automation-permission hint (System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Automation) on osascript failure.

VERIFIED HERE: 19 cross-platform unit tests in tests/.../Browser/TerminalRefocusTests.cs pass — incl. the regression guards that 'ghostty' maps to Ghostty (not Terminal) and that an unknown TERM_PROGRAM resolves to null (never Apple Terminal). Bundle id wins over the name guess.

NOT VERIFIABLE HERE (state plainly): the actual macOS osascript focus return (no macOS / no GUI app-activation on Linux/Xvfb). The user must confirm on their Mac running Ghostty: after the browser docks then dismisses, keyboard focus returns to Ghostty and the TUI responds to keys.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by workspace-fihe. This bead was parked 'pending the user's confirmation on their Mac (Ghostty)' — which violates the platform-agnostic-verifiable-in-my-env directive (never block on 'test it on your Mac'). The user has now given field feedback (2026-07-03): the refocus mechanism is DEFECTIVE — it continually re-activates the terminal and steals focus back from other apps (couldn't switch to the Claude app). So the open question this bead was blocked on is answered: focus return is not merely unverified, it's over-aggressive and wrong. The active, IN-ENV-VERIFIABLE defect is now tracked as workspace-fihe (focus-steal), which also carries the re-architecture direction (drop hidden-headed-window + osascript refocus for a windowless/mirrored design). Bundle-id capture + activation-script construction remain unit-tested cross-platform (TerminalRefocusTests, 19 tests) — that code stays; the behavior fix lives in fihe. Not closing as 'done'; closing as superseded so nothing sits blocked on the user.

title: Launch the headed browser OFF-SCREEN + parked by default — no visible pop, no focus steal at startup
description: Add --window-position=-32000,-32000 to the Playwright launch args (BrowserSession.LaunchBrowserAsync) so the headed window never appears at a default on-screen position at launch. Default to PARKED/immersive (browser not docked) so the user reads in the TUI with the browser as a background renderer. Confirm Playwright DOM extraction still works with the window off-screen (it is CDP-driven, visibility-independent). Keep it HEADFUL — never headless.
acceptance_criteria: Under Xvfb (here): ./run launches; a screenshot of the visible screen shows NO browser window (it's at -32000,-32000); the TUI still renders extracted page content (open a section, see the link tree). Proven by reading the screenshot + the rendered TUI rows, not logs.
close_reason: DONE (verified end-to-end under Xvfb + visible-screen screenshots, not logs). Code: BrowserSession.LaunchBrowserAsync now adds --window-position=-32000,-32000 (+ --disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows / --disable-renderer-backgrounding) so the headed window spawns OFF-SCREEN, and BrowserConfiguration.Sidecar now defaults FALSE so the launch else-branch PARKS off-screen (new ParkWindowOffScreenAsync: windowState=normal at -32000,-32000) instead of docking/minimizing — never pops up or steals focus. Verification: integration test HeadedLaunch_Default_ParksWindowOffScreen_RendersButNotVisible drives the REAL BrowserSession under Xvfb — observed window bounds left=-32000 top=-32000 state=normal (entirely off the visible region), IsWindowDocked=false, and a live DOM read returned 'parked and rendering' (extraction works off-screen). ALSO captured a literal X-root screenshot via 'import -window root' while the real production code ran: parked screen = fully black, NO browser window visible (looked at it). Full suite 4160 passed / 0 failed.

title: 'O' reveals the docked window on-screen; dismiss re-parks it OFF-SCREEN (not minimized — avoid occlusion throttle)
description: The 'O' dock toggle moves the off-screen window on-screen to its DockGeometry position; toggling back (immersive) moves it back to -32000,-32000 (parked) rather than just minimizing — minimized windows can be occlusion-throttled by Chromium, degrading the live spotlight/screenshots. Re-park on undock so the live page keeps rendering for instant re-dock.
acceptance_criteria: Under Xvfb: press 'O' -> the live browser appears on-screen docked (screenshot shows it); press 'O' again -> it leaves the visible region (parked off-screen) while the page keeps rendering (re-dock is instant, content current). Screenshots asserted.
close_reason: DONE (verified end-to-end under Xvfb + visible-screen screenshots). 'O' (ToggleWindowDockAsync) docks the parked window on-screen; pressing 'O' again now RE-PARKS it off-screen via ParkWindowOffScreenAsync (windowState=normal at -32000,-32000) instead of OS-minimizing, so the page keeps rendering for an instant re-dock. Verification: integration test ToggleWindowDock_FromParked_DocksOnScreen_ThenReParksOffScreen drives the REAL BrowserSession under Xvfb — observed: parked at launch left=-32000; after 'O' left=850 (on-screen, in the visible region); after 'O' again left=-32000 state=normal (NOT minimized). The re-parked page still renders: page.ScreenshotAsync (the CDP path the dock spotlight uses) returned a valid 10664-byte PNG frame, and the DOM stayed evaluable. ALSO looked at a literal X-root screenshot of the real production window: after 'O' the red page is visibly docked to the right half of the screen. HONEST CAVEAT: requestAnimationFrame is PAUSED by Chromium for any non-visible surface (off-screen included), so wall-clock animation does not tick while parked — the 'keeps rendering' guarantee is via the on-demand CDP render path (verified) plus windowState=normal for an instant, animation-free re-show; the macOS occlusion-throttle-vs-minimize difference is design-level (normal state + the anti-backgrounding flags) and not measurable on Linux/Xvfb.

title: EPIC: Park the headed browser off-screen by default + robust terminal refocus (kill the macOS focus-steal)
description: User report (2026-06-26, macOS/Ghostty, terminal-app revert branch): when the headed Chrome docks/pops up it STEALS keyboard focus and the TUI stops responding. Two fixes, keeping the browser HEADFUL (never headless): (1) launch the headed browser OFF-SCREEN (--window-position=-32000,-32000) and PARKED by default (immersive) so it renders fully (Playwright/CDP extraction is window-visibility-independent) but never pops up or grabs focus at startup; 'O' brings it on-screen to dock and dismissing re-parks it off-screen. Off-screen (not minimized) avoids Chromium occlusion-throttling that would degrade the live spotlight when docked. (2) Make RefocusTerminalAsync robust: it currently maps TERM_PROGRAM by name and falls back to Apple Terminal — Ghostty (TERM_PROGRAM=ghostty) isn't handled, so focus is returned to the WRONG app. Capture the terminal's bundle id / PID at startup and re-activate THAT via System Events, terminal-agnostic; surface the Automation-permission requirement clearly.
design: Files: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserSession.cs (LaunchBrowserAsync args ~916 add --window-position=-32000,-32000; the launch flow ~1003 _userWantsDock?Dock:Minimize -> park off-screen instead of minimize; RefocusTerminalAsync ~761), DockGeometry, BrowserConfiguration (Sidecar default — consider default immersive/parked). VERIFIABILITY: the off-screen+parked+extraction+'O'-reveal is fully testable here under Xvfb (assert no window in the visible region via screenshot, TUI still shows extracted content, 'O' brings it on-screen). The osascript refocus is macOS-only: make the bundle-id capture + activation-command construction unit-testable cross-platform (seam like the existing TerminalRefocusInvokerOverride), and the actual macOS focus return is confirmed on the user's Mac (Ghostty) — state plainly what is/ isn't verifiable here. Never reintroduce headless.

title: Port the cache absolute-TTL fix (a9c29d10 / hv8n): hot pages never expire on main due to sliding ExpiresAtUtc
description: REAL bug on main: InMemoryPageCache sets ExpiresAtUtc=DateTime.UtcNow.AddSeconds(ttl) on apply/revisit, so a frequently-reopened page slides its expiry forward forever and never evicts (dragging its disk copy along). Port the fix from a9c29d10: anchor expiry to Metadata.CachedAtUtc (absolute cap), clamp so apply can only SHRINK lifetime. Core change is isolated to src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/InMemoryPageCache.cs; cherry-pick or hand-apply (the commit also touched the diverged BrowserOrchestrator — take only the cache hunks). Add/keep the InMemoryPageCacheTests coverage.
acceptance_criteria: On the back-to-main branch: a test proves a revisited entry's ExpiresAtUtc is anchored to CachedAtUtc (does NOT slide forward on read/apply); unit suite green.
close_reason: DONE (commit 908b6cb5). Ported a9c29d10: cache absolute-TTL anchor+clamp (hot pages no longer never-expire) + a4wh decrypt-title col alignment. 39 cache tests pass incl. 2 new hv8n tests; build 0 warnings.

(4) Panel renders the FULL launcher — CDP-sampled .xterm-rows (cols=36 rows=55: pink wordmark, 'Go to URL', THE DAILY GAZETTE[1]..THE MAIDEN VOYAGE[9], '[Enter] open  [o] go to url' footer) AND vision-confirmed screenshot 06-panel-crop.png. (5) NEVER-BLANK: navigated the TAB to real https://www.nytimes.com (full live load, no bot block — confirmed by the ad-iframe swarm + rendered masthead/headlines in screenshot 07) while sampling the panel every 150ms: 92 samples (88 post-nav), rows constant 55, status 'live', 0 blank samples. Final shot 07-final-nyt-loaded.png shows ONE window: live NYT in the tab + painted launcher in the panel. title: Reveal split on the live overlay before the stale-tab navigate; carry split across the user-initiated peek-on reload
description:
Root cause RC-NAV-TEARDOWN (dominant for the user's stated cached link-list case). HandleToggleBrowserDockAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1641-1650): peek-on fires FireAndForgetNavigate(NavigateAndCaptureAsync(liveUrl)) at 1646 (a full tab reload, because peek-off left the tab stale per 1007) BEFORE SyncExtensionLayout at 1649. The reload tears down the overlay the SetLayout(split) targets; the re-injected overlay ALWAYS boots FULL by design (content.js:436-441 restoreLayout, xink.6), so the split only re-lands via OnExtensionReattached (2123/2129) after reload->reinject->reconnect->reattach — the multi-second 'pops up later'.

Fix: (a) reorder SyncExtensionLayout to run BEFORE the stale-tab navigate so the split narrows the still-connected overlay instantly (overlay width is a pure function of layoutMode+visible, content.js:177-191; needs no live page). (b) Carry the split mode across the ONE user-initiated peek-on reload via a one-shot, URL-stamped signal honored by seedLayout/restoreLayout, so the rebooted overlay returns split instead of flashing full.

Must scope the carry tightly: one-shot, user-initiated, URL-matched only; ForceFullOverlay (xink.3) must still win on error/stall; never a boot default or speculative seed (xink.6 / retired blg5.21 pre-seed).
acceptance_criteria: ./run --web --extension; open a cached section link list with peek OFF (overlay full-width). Press O ONCE. Assert: the overlay iframe rect narrows from full-tab width to ~60% split within a few hundred ms AND remains split continuously (capture the rect at ~150ms, ~600ms, ~2s — no transition back to full-width in between). The live section page is visible on the right (stale content briefly acceptable, then refreshed). Not proven by a 'SetLayout split' log or 'overlay present' check — only by the measured rect staying split with no full-width flash.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by the side-panel model (epic 39cy, committed). These three root causes (RC-NAV-TEARDOWN/.1, RC-LOAD-GATE/.2, RC-SWEEP-DEFER/.3) are all in the in-tab OVERLAY's 'O' peek path (content.js applyLayout/restoreLayout/dockPage, HandleToggleBrowserDock overlay-dock) — code DELETED in the un-revert (e63166cc). The panel is a separate per-window surface that a tab navigation cannot reboot, so the 'O delay / pops up later' symptom is structurally impossible. PROVEN: real-toolbar gate never-blanks 88/88 post-nav samples across a full live nytimes load (commit b6a2afa1).

title: Honor 'O' (ToggleBrowserDock) during a background page load without double-driving the tab
description:
Root cause RC-LOAD-GATE — directly matches the user's 'including if it means opening when the page is still loading'. ToggleBrowserDock is absent from IsCommandAllowedDuringBackgroundLoad (BackgroundLoad.cs:31-36), so during an uncached skeleton load (StartBackgroundLoad armed at BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1016) the main loop silently drops the keypress at BrowserOrchestrator.cs:704-707 (`continue`, no queue/replay). 'O' binds to ToggleBrowserDock at TerminalInputHandler.cs:631.

Fix: add CommandType.ToggleBrowserDock to the allow-list (precedent: TogglePreloadDetail — a passive overlay-only action that paints over the skeleton via the normal render path). In HandleToggleBrowserDockAsync, guard the stale-tab navigate (1641-1647) to SKIP FireAndForgetNavigate when HasActiveBackgroundLoad() is true, so the in-flight load owns the tab and O only flips _peekEnabled + SyncExtensionLayout; the split reveals over the skeleton and self-corrects when the load lands.
acceptance_criteria: ./run --web --extension; trigger an UNCACHED navigation to a section link list (clear cache / new URL) so the skeleton/loading screen is showing. While the skeleton is still on screen, press O ONCE. Assert: the overlay narrows to the split width over the skeleton/loading page within a few hundred ms (the keypress is NOT dropped), and when the load completes the right pane fills in. Negative control: confirm only ToggleBrowserDock got through — a heavy nav/extraction command pressed during the load is still ignored, and GoBack still cancels the load. Not proven by an allow-list unit assert alone; drive the real mid-load keypress and watch the strip narrow.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by the side-panel model (epic 39cy, committed). These three root causes (RC-NAV-TEARDOWN/.1, RC-LOAD-GATE/.2, RC-SWEEP-DEFER/.3) are all in the in-tab OVERLAY's 'O' peek path (content.js applyLayout/restoreLayout/dockPage, HandleToggleBrowserDock overlay-dock) — code DELETED in the un-revert (e63166cc). The panel is a separate per-window surface that a tab navigation cannot reboot, so the 'O delay / pops up later' symptom is structurally impossible. PROVEN: real-toolbar gate never-blanks 88/88 post-nav samples across a full live nytimes load (commit b6a2afa1).

title: Make 'O' sidecar peek reveal the split INSTANTLY in extension mode, including mid-load
description:
Symptom (user): 'In the link list, using the shortcut for the sidecar (O) results in a delay (nothing for a few seconds then it pops up). The shortcut should be instant, including if it means opening when the page is still loading.'

Three confirmed, complementary root causes across layers, each biting a different scenario but all producing the same delayed/absent pop-up:
- RC-NAV-TEARDOWN (csharp-navigate, dominant for the cached link-list case): BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1646 fires a full stale-tab reload before the split push at 1649; the reload tears down the overlay and it reboots FULL (content.js:436-441), so the split only re-lands via OnExtensionReattached (2123/2129).
- RC-LOAD-GATE (csharp-gate, the 'while loading' case): ToggleBrowserDock is not in IsCommandAllowedDuringBackgroundLoad (BackgroundLoad.cs:31-36), so the keypress is silently dropped at BrowserOrchestrator.cs:704-707 during an uncached skeleton load.
- RC-SWEEP-DEFER (extension-js, the reader/article case): applyLayout's blanket early-return at content.js:173 withholds the whole reveal (including the inert fixed-position overlay narrow) until the capture sweep ends (up to ~12s, content.js:674).

The user asked for a plan + issues only, NOT implementation. This epic tracks the three child fixes. Goal: O narrows the overlay iframe to the split width within a few hundred ms of the keypress in every scenario; a blank/loading right pane that fills in is acceptable and desired.
acceptance_criteria: On a REAL run (./run --web --extension): (1) cached link list, peek off, press O once — assert the overlay iframe narrows from full to ~60% split width within a few hundred ms and STAYS split (no flash back to full), live page trails on the right. (2) Navigate to an UNCACHED section list and, while the skeleton is still showing, press O once — assert the split reveals over the skeleton/loading page within a few hundred ms and is not dropped. (3) In the reader on a prose article mid-hydration, press O — assert the strip narrows immediately even while the body is still growing. All three asserted by measuring the overlay iframe rect transition (not a log line / existence check).
notes: User-measured 2026-06-25: 5s for NYT Today's Paper (regular page, logged-in section). Confirms RC-NAV-TEARDOWN dominant and resolves open-Q1: the 5s is the TAB RELOAD on the critical path, NOT the ~450ms reboot. Per the xink.6 comment at BrowserOrchestrator.cs:961, peek-on drives NavigateAndCaptureAsync which 'DESTROYS and re-injects the overlay terminal' — so the WHOLE app overlay (not just the split) is gone until the heavy NYT load commits + the overlay reboots + reattach re-pushes split. Fix: reveal split on the EXISTING overlay BEFORE the navigate (instant), let the live page stream into the right pane (xink.6 boot-FULL + OnExtensionReattached re-push handles the post-reboot split; the synchronous carry is infeasible cross-origin and subsumed by the re-push). Implementing.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by epic 39cy (toolbar-triggered side panel replaces the overlay). The goal — make the sidecar reveal instant / never blank mid-load — is achieved by the persistent per-window panel (never rebooted by a tab nav), not by fixing the overlay 'O' peek. All 3 children superseded; never-blank proven by the real-toolbar gate.

title: fix(ext): strip the #fragment where Page.Url is born so refresh/render/peek stop re-adopting and re-driving the comments-jump URL
description:
USER-REPORTED (2026-06-25): the app picks up a 'weird url' — a full article URL WITH a deep fragment, e.g. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/22/us/politics/jd-vance-iran-negotiations.html#commentsContainer — and re-drives the live tab into the comments / subscriber flyout instead of the clean article.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by reading the cited lines): fragment stripping lives at EXACTLY ONE chokepoint — StripUrlFragment inside NavigateToAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:876, helper :1057). But Page.Url is BORN with the fragment intact and several consumers bypass NavigateToAsync:
- BIRTH POINT: ExtensionPageLoader.cs:97 — on an in-place capture (sameAsCurrent, :60) finalUrl = snapshot.Url, i.e. the live location.href WITH #commentsContainer; PageLoadPipeline.cs:461 then does Page.Create(finalUrl) verbatim. The comment at :459 notes this URL feeds status bar, refresh, and cache lookups.
- REFRESH BYPASS: NavigationCommandHandler.cs:489 HandleRefresh -> ForceRefreshAsync(page.Url); BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:28 RenderLoading(url) PRINTS the #commentsContainer URL and :30 LoadAsync(ForceRefresh) DRIVES the live tab to it (lands in the NYT comments/subscription flyout). Never touches StripUrlFragment.
- PEEK RE-DRIVE BYPASS: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1580 (xink.6 lazy-peek) drives NavigateAndCaptureAsync(CurrentPage.Url) — fragment carried.
- Also error/loading boxes that render CurrentPage.Url.

FIX: normalize the fragment at the BIRTH point (strip when adopting snapshot.Url at ExtensionPageLoader.cs:97 and/or when building Page.Url at PageLoadPipeline.cs:461), so every downstream consumer (refresh, render, peek, cache key, history, status bar) inherits the clean URL — instead of relying on the single NavigateToAsync chokepoint. PRESERVE the intentional same-document in-page-jump keep-branch (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:853-866, blg5.1) which must still keep its fragment to scroll the live tab.

EXISTING STATE: the open-to-read strip (commit de895c2e) was shipped WITH NO BEAD (it cites a non-existent 'workspace-2mn3-reader'; the only #fragment bead is blg5.1, closed, the opposite keep-branch). The nofrag gate (verify-extension.sh phase 16b) only asserts the live TAB url after a TYPED go-to-url; it does NOT exercise refresh, in-place-capture adopt, or what the app DISPLAYS — so it passes while this bug is live.

ACCEPTANCE (headful, never headless): with the live tab sitting on a '...html#commentsContainer' url, drive Shift+R / F5 and assert BOTH (a) the rendered/displayed url has no #fragment and (b) the driven live tab lands on the clean article (top), not the comments anchor. Also assert an in-place-capture adopt of a fragment url yields a clean Page.Url, and that peek (O) re-drives the clean url. Extend the nofrag gate to cover these. Same-document #section jumps must STILL keep their fragment (no regression to blg5.1).
notes:
FIXED + headfully verified (article-bounded hydrate sweep). The #commentsContainer was attached by the app's OWN pre-snapshot hydrate sweep scrolling to the FULL document bottom, dragging the live tab into the comments sibling where NYT's scrollspy rewrites location.href. FIX (extension/content.js): added contentBottom() (
/[role=article] ->
/[role=main] -> rect-height<=0 degeneracy guard -> null) and sweepMaxY() (= min(contentBottom()-vh(), docScrollHeight()-vh()); full-doc fallback when null), recomputed each iteration; swapped the 4 sweep-limit call sites. So the sweep stops at the article end, never enters comments, and location.href stays clean as a CONSEQUENCE (no strip). Backend defense-in-depth: ReadableContentExtractor.RemoveBoilerplate adds //*[contains(@id,'comment')]. New fixture /article-comments.html (Program.cs) + new headful gate 'fragbound' (verify-extension.sh phase 15c + tools/verify-ext peekoptin-style mode), peek-OFF. VERIFIED: RED on pre-fix code (full-doc sweep scrolled to y=2379 -> comment

hydrated 'descent p6->8' + url=...#commentsContainer); GREEN after (sweep bounded, 'descent p6->7', FRAGBOUND_ARTICLE_TAIL present = no 3f2x truncation regression, FRAGBOUND_COMMENT_MARKER absent, url clean). No regression: multiload (below-fold hydration) PASS x2, unit Readable/Extract/Reader 433/0. KNOWN SECONDARY PATH (not the user's default-experience bug; filed separately if needed): with peek ON the dock's zoom (blg5.19 fallback) shrinks the page so below-fold comments enter the zoomed viewport and the scrollspy fires (observed commentFire y=68,h=1129 under peek-on). The user's reported symptom is the default peek-OFF bookmark-open path, now fixed. The peek-on dock-zoom scrollspy trigger is a narrower follow-up tied to the zoom-fallback dock.
