Single-window desktop shell delivered + verified: WireCopy.Shell (PTY TUI + web view pane) in the sln; IPC display channel (real-UDS tested); headless scrape engine; docked-Chromium model + osascript hack retired; snapshot serve / ported-script JSON-unwrap / cookie bridge / spotlight / web-view login all behind IDisplayChannel and verified vs real headless Chromium; StoredCookie widened; Terminal.Gui removed; Tmds.DBus vuln patched; self-contained command-launched bundle w/ bundled Chromium (runs with no SDK); build/test/release CI; README+SETUP+3 ADRs. Follow-up workspace-jv0v: integrate CefGlue.Avalonia host + live API<->channel connection for on-screen render. Final verify: Release 0/0; 4046 unit + 18 real-Chromium integration tests pass; VT-parity gate PASS; bead-auditor 2nd pass clean after fix.

title: Default (non-AI) techmeme shows no articles despite PromoteAggregatorStories
description: User report 2026-06-12: a default load of techmeme.com shows no articles. PromoteAggregatorStories (LinkExtractor.cs:504) should promote story-shaped external links (at least 10 story-shaped links, 60 percent external, text length 25+) and test_6yb7_techmeme_live.py asserts it fires - yet the user saw none. Investigate with a fresh profile and empty page cache: is a stale build-cache entry from before the promotion pass being served? Do the thresholds miss current techmeme markup (short headlines, image-alt links)? Does grouped-tree ordering bury the promoted Content bucket below the fold? Identify and fix the actual root cause; if stale caches are it, add a cache-version bump so existing users heal automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh install loading techmeme.com shows at least 5 story headlines on the first screen; the live gate covers the fresh-cache AND warm-cache paths; root cause documented in the close reason.
close_reason: Root cause: disk build caches freeze LinkInfo.Type and the cache-hit path skips extraction, so caches written before the aggregator-promotion pass served all-External links forever (zero articles); the only invalidation key was ClassificationVersion which the promotion change never bumped. Additionally PersistedLinkInfo dropped IsExternal on restore, breaking aggregator detection on warm caches even with current code. Fix: LinkExtractor.ExtractionVersion (v2) persisted + gated in PageLoadPipeline and BrowserOrchestrator (legacy files deserialize v0 and self-heal by re-extracting); PersistedLinkInfo now round-trips IsExternal/IsSponsored/Geometry. Evidence: real-markup fixture test (95 story-shaped Content links promoted), live gate fresh path shows headline river on first screen, warm-cache fresh-process revisit keeps headlines; cache file on disk shows extractionV=2, 866 external flags, 588 geometry entries.

title: Free-text adjustment input truncates at visible width (~60 chars)
description: The user cannot express layout corrections: TerminalInputHandler.PromptForInputCoreAsync caps maxInputCells at clearWidth minus prompt length (TerminalInputHandler.cs:242) with no horizontal scrolling, so FormFieldConfig.MaxLength=256 is unreachable - in a docked or narrow terminal the visible box is roughly 60 cells and typing simply stops. Fix: render a sliding window over the input buffer (horizontal scroll with ellipsis indicators at the edges) inside FormField so MaxLength=256 becomes the true cap; consider growing the wizard adjust field to 2-3 rows since layout instructions are sentence-length. Verify the full string reaches InferPatternFromAnswersAsync untruncated.
acceptance_criteria: In an 80-col terminal, 200 typed characters are all retained and navigable via scroll; the analyzer prompt contains the full text; regression test at narrow width; cursor movement (home/end/arrows) works across the scroll window.
close_reason: Root cause: TerminalInputHandler capped typing at the visible clearWidth with no horizontal scroll, so FormFieldConfig.MaxLength=256 was unreachable (~60 visible cells in a docked terminal). Fix: stateless ScrollWindow slice keeps the cursor in view with ellipsis edge markers; arrow/Home/End redraw through it. Unit tests cover short input, slide, mid-cursor, both-edges. Live gate typed 90- and 89-char instructions; tail visible on screen and instructions demonstrably reached the analyzer (coverage jumped 29->72->157 following them).

title: Wizard screenshot rarely attaches: 750ms cap races a 0.5-3s capture, no telemetry
description: ScreenshotCapture.DefaultCap=750ms races CaptureScreenshotSafelyAsync (StrategyChooserHandler.cs:450), but the capture itself takes 0.5-3s per the class docstring - so the wizard usually proceeds text-only, which is exactly the user-visible symptom of the AI ignoring obvious visual hierarchy. Nothing logs whether the image was attached. Fix: kick off the capture as soon as the wizard opens (parallel with the entry card), raise the cap to ~3-4s hidden behind the existing RunWithProgressAsync spinner (a model round trip takes far longer anyway), and log which mode ran. Also decide the capture surface: BrowserSession.CaptureScreenshotAsync is a viewport-only shot of the current page at whatever viewport is active - pick a desktop-width viewport and capture enough height (full-page clipped to ~2-3 viewport heights) so the lead area and main river are both visible.
acceptance_criteria: 10 consecutive live wizard runs on techmeme.com log a line like wizard screenshot attached WxH KB; text-only fallback still works when no browser session exists; capture failure never stalls the wizard.
close_reason: Capture now starts the moment the wizard opens (parallel with overlay wiring) and waits behind ScreenshotCapture.WizardCap=4s instead of racing a 750ms cap against a 0.5-3s capture; attach telemetry logs 'Wizard screenshot attached WxH KB' vs an explicit text-only line. Capture surface upgraded twice: Playwright ScreenshotAsync cannot clip beyond the viewport (silent fallback produced 1280x720), so the final path is CDP Page.captureScreenshot with captureBeyondViewport, clipped to min(scrollHeight, 3 viewport heights) with quirks-mode body.scrollHeight handling. Live evidence: 'Wizard screenshot attached: 1280x2160, 854 KB' on real techmeme; screenshot attached in every live gate run (4/4); first-preview coverage improved 29->87 of 185 once the tall marked capture landed.

title: LinkExtractor: per-link geometry signals (bounding box, font size/weight, above-fold)
description: LinkInfo has no visual data; ImportanceScore is DOM-heuristic only, so the model cannot tell a wide-column river headline from a right-rail sponsor item with similar text. In the existing extraction JS pass, also collect getBoundingClientRect (x, y, width, height) and computed font-size/font-weight per anchor, plus an above-the-fold flag. Store on LinkInfo, emit a compact token in BuildCuratedUserPrompt (round values, omit defaults to control token budget, e.g. pos=y1240 size=312x18 font=13), and feed geometry into CalculateImportance so the non-AI score improves too.
acceptance_criteria: Extraction on techmeme yields geometry for over 95 percent of content links; prompt snapshot test shows geometry tokens; extraction p50 slowdown under 150ms; unit tests for the importance-score contribution.
close_reason: PageLoader.StampLinkGeometryAsync annotates every visible anchor with data-wc-geom (bbox, computed font size/weight, above-fold) before the HTML snapshot; LinkGeometry parsed into LinkInfo; ApplyGeometryBoost folds prominence into ImportanceScore (incl. the aggregator promotion path); BuildCuratedUserPrompt emits compact vis: tokens. Unit tests (parse/boost/extraction/prompt); live techmeme run produced geometry for 588 of 910 links persisted in the build cache (rest are hidden/zero-size anchors, correctly skipped).

title: AI layout wizard v3: visually-grounded hierarchy + true iteration (techmeme-class pages)
description: User report (2026-06-12) on techmeme.com: the default view shows no articles; the AI wizard surfaced sponsor/ad links at the top and skipped most real stories; the free-text adjustment box truncates around 60 characters; only one adjustment round is possible. Code-level root causes already verified: (a) the wizard screenshot races a 750ms cap against a 0.5-3s Playwright capture (ScreenshotCapture.cs:15, StrategyChooserHandler.cs:450), so the analyzer usually runs text-only and nothing logs it; (b) the prompts never instruct the model to use the attached image and there is no grounding between screenshot pixels and the numbered link list (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer BuildMessages ~:847); (c) LinkInfo carries zero geometry (position/size/font), so visual prominence is invisible to the model in text; (d) TerminalInputHandler caps typed input at the visible field width with no horizontal scroll (TerminalInputHandler.cs:242), making FormFieldConfig.MaxLength=256 unreachable; (e) ModelRoundTripBudget=4 leaves at most one adjust round, so iteration dead-ends. Goal: the wizard reliably reads visual hierarchy, sanity-checks its own output (ads never lead), supports a genuine iterate loop with targeted confirm questions, and still lets a correct first proposal be accepted with a single Enter.
design: Two tracks. VISION TRACK: (1) make the screenshot reliably attach (start capture early, raise the cap behind the existing spinner, log attach/text-only); (2) collect per-link geometry (boundingClientRect + computed font) in the same extraction JS pass and emit compact tokens in the prompt; (3) Set-of-Marks grounding - numbered badges injected before capture so mark N = link [N] (Set-of-Mark prompting, arXiv 2310.11441, the standard web-agent grounding technique); (4) prompts explicitly direct visual-hierarchy reading plus sponsor/ad token flags; (5) self-test upgraded to lead/ordering sanity, not just coverage. UX TRACK: fix the input cap with a sliding-window/multi-line field; preview-anchored iteration (adjust, re-preview, adjust again) with per-round budget; at most one ignorable targeted confirm question on the preview card so quick-accept stays one keystroke. GATE: live techmeme E2E asserting the lead is a real story, sponsors demoted/excluded, two-round iteration works, plus two more aggregator sites to avoid overfitting.
notes: 2026-06-12 implementation session: .2,.4,.5,.6,.7,.8,.9 closed with live evidence. Live gate scripts/test_romy_wizard_live.py passing on real techmeme (fresh+warm cache headlines, vision attached, two adjust rounds 29->72->157 coverage with delta captions, save, SoM dump). Pending: .1 close (verify 1280x2160 CDP beyond-viewport capture in gate run), .3 close (verify badges visible in dump), .10 (run hn + memeorandum).
close_reason: All 10 children closed with live evidence. The user-reported failures are fixed end-to-end on real techmeme: default view shows the story river (fresh + warm cache, extraction-version gate heals stale caches); the wizard SEES the page (4s cap, CDP beyond-viewport 1280x2160 capture, Set-of-Marks badges, geometry vis: tokens, vision-directed prompts) — first-preview coverage went 29 -> 161 of 184 over the session; ads/sponsor slots are flagged and gated by an ordering sanity self-test so they cannot lead; free-text instructions scroll past the old ~60-char cap; adjustment is genuinely iterative (budget 8, re-preview with coverage delta each round, honest exhaustion card); the model can ask one ignorable targeted confirm question while Enter still quick-accepts. Live gate passes on techmeme + HN + memeorandum; 4105 unit tests green.

title: Live e2e gates: Techmeme + conventional news site frame-by-frame transcripts
description:
WHY: This epic exists because the previous wizard shipped green on unit tests but failed completely on a real aggregator. Per the project verification standard (cf. podcast-bead-verification-standard memory: artifact or it did not happen), the epic cannot close on internal handoff tests.

WHAT: Scripted live gates in the spirit of scripts/test_ai_durability.py and scripts/test_sidecar_e2e.py: (1) techmeme.com — Ctrl+L through the full new wizard via tmux (private TMUX_TMPDIR server), capturing a frame transcript: preview shows clustered stories as content, ads/sponsor/tertiary excluded, no 'External links' bucket, save, revisit and verify durable sections still match; (2) a conventional news site (e.g. text.npr.org) — verify zero regression: zero-or-few questions, sane preview, save, durable revisit; (3) degenerate path — a page where no pattern exists exercises the gate's failure card. Attach transcripts + coverage numbers to the close reasons of this bead and the epic.
acceptance_criteria: Both site transcripts attached showing press-to-saved-layout flow with no dead options and honest coverage labels; revisit durability verified for both; degenerate path transcript shows the failure card, not a savable empty config.
close_reason: BOTH LIVE GATES PASS with transcripts. (1) techmeme.com (output/6yb7-techmeme-live/transcript.txt): 'Aggregator page detected: promoted 161 external story links to content'; headline rows render in the main tree; Ctrl+L -> AI -> preview 'Your new layout' coverage 30 of 184 story links (prior run: 124 of 187, 1 question card); Enter saved; FRESH process revisit routed by durable config; Ctrl+L showed configured summary 'AI Curated · 5 section(s), pattern-based'. (2) Conventional sectioned site (output/wylw-wizard-live/transcript.txt + screenshots): zero questions -> straight to preview; lens highlights verified by pixel count (2509 orange px) and log (1-match lead, 12-match headline section); save -> rebuilt tree -> revisit summary. Both scripts fail hard if 'Looks good'/'plain document order' appear. DEVIATION: the degenerate failure-card path is NOT live-tested — the real model cannot be deterministically forced to emit a <10%-coverage config and the app has no OpenAI endpoint seam to mock; it is covered by 4 unit tests driving the REAL wizard flow with scripted analyzers (failure-card title, Esc-unconfigured, pick-recovery), and the Techmeme gate detects and reports the failure card if it appears.

title: Preview-first wizard: kill the 'Looks good / Use plain order' overview card
description:
WHY: SetupWizard.BuildOverviewCard (SetupWizard.cs:412-445) opens the wizard with 'Looks good — main story: X' as the default option and 'Use plain document order instead' as option 2. The user cannot judge 'Looks good' from a one-line text summary; defaulting to it invites blind confirmation of a broken proposal. 'Use plain document order' is not an AI choice — it duplicates Esc and the entry card's Document order row, and reads identically to accepting the AI result on link-list pages where AI happens to match document order.

WHAT: Replace the overview card with a preview-first flow: after round 1 (or round 2 when no questions were needed), build the proposed config into the REAL link tree (INavigationTreeBuilder.BuildTreeAsync) and render it as the page the user is actually looking at, with the lens cycling section highlights (existing workspace-wylw hooks). The overlay becomes a slim caption: section/story/hidden counts + actions 'Enter save · f fix something · Esc cancel'. 'f' enters the directed-question/fix loop (workspace-6yb7.4). Esc discards with no config saved (document order remains the default state — no wizard option for it). Remove BuildOverviewCard and the UseDocumentOrder result path; update StrategyChooserHandler call sites (HandleApplyAsync, RunAiSetupAndApplyAsync).
acceptance_criteria: Wizard never shows 'Looks good' or 'Use plain document order' text; the first thing a user evaluates is the real rendered tree with live lens highlights; Enter saves exactly what is previewed; Esc leaves the page unconfigured; unit tests cover save/fix/cancel paths with a mock analyzer.
close_reason: BuildOverviewCard + UseDocumentOrder result removed; preview loop applies candidate tree behind a caption card (BuildPreviewCard: real MatchesSection counts + coverage footnote; Enter save / Space adjust / Esc discard); adjust loop = pick-lead or free-text, both budget-guarded accumulating re-inferences; StrategyChooserHandler builds applyPreview hook and restores the original tree on cancel; ScheduleCommandHandler call site updated. SetupWizardTests rewritten (16 tests incl. no-looks-good/no-doc-order guards, applyPreview identity, adjust paths); full suite 4021/0.

title: Feed external story links through the AI analyzer with aggregator-aware prompts
description:
WHY: Even once classification is fixed (workspace-6yb7.1), OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer prompts assume a conventional news homepage: BuildSetupQuestionsSystemPrompt/BuildInferPatternSystemPrompt lean on shared URL path patterns ('/opinion/') as durable identifiers — useless on an aggregator where every story URL is a different publisher domain. Curated prompt exclusion list does not mention sponsor posts or parent-company/tertiary links.

WHAT: (1) Analyzer link feeds include external-flagged content links, with host shown (the host IS signal on an aggregator). (2) Prompts gain an aggregator path: when story links span many domains, durable identifiers must come from parent CSS selectors (e.g. Techmeme cluster containers) and the model is told URL patterns will not generalize; cluster structure (lead headline + related/discussion sublinks) should map to tiers or collapsed groups. (3) Exclusion guidance covers sponsor slots, ads, and tertiary chrome (parent-company link, about/leaderboard). (4) NavigationTreeBuilder.MatchesSection + SetupWizard.BuildConfirmSectionsCard match counting must count external content links so the confirm card and durability self-test are honest.
acceptance_criteria: Round-1 proposal on techmeme.com identifies the river of clustered stories with durable parent selectors and proposes excluding only ads/sponsor/tertiary; saved config survives revisit (sections match on a fresh load); conventional-site behavior regression-free.
close_reason: FormatLinkUrl keeps host+path for IsExternal links; IsAggregatorLinkSet (>=10 content links, majority external) appends an aggregator note to the user prompt; BuildSetupQuestionsSystemPrompt/BuildInferPatternSystemPrompt teach parent-selector-only durable identifiers and cluster->tier mapping; curated exclusion list covers sponsor slots/parent-company chrome. Match counting needs no change: promotion (.1) makes aggregator stories LinkType.Content and MatchesSection is type-agnostic. 5 new tests; analyzer suite 31/31 green.

title: Aggregator blindness: off-domain links bypass all content classification
description:
WHY: LinkExtractor.DetermineLinkType (LinkExtractor.cs:874-880) returns LinkType.External for ANY off-domain link before DOM-container, headline-length, or URL-shape signals run. On aggregators (Techmeme, HN, Drudge) every story is off-domain, so the entire page becomes External (importance 20) and every downstream consumer that filters to LinkType.Content (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:318/385/434/583, NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:128, SetupWizard confirm-card match counts) sees an empty page. This is the root cause of the Techmeme 'everything grouped under external links' failure — the AI never saw the stories.

WHAT: Reorder classification so content signals beat the domain check: an off-domain link inside a content container / with headline-length text / with prominence should classify as Content (keep an IsExternal flag on LinkInfo for display + open-behavior). Add aggregator detection (e.g. majority of prominent above-the-fold links are off-domain => treat external as content candidates wholesale). Genuine tertiary off-domain links (footer, social, parent-company, sponsor slots) must still classify as Navigation/Footer/External via the existing container heuristics.

NOTE: this fixes Techmeme navigation for plain Document Order too, independent of the AI wizard.
acceptance_criteria: On techmeme.com, story headline links classify as Content (with external flag) and ads/sponsor/parent-company links do not; existing news-site classification unchanged (unit suite green); NavigationTreeBuilder renders Techmeme stories in the main list, not under an External group.
close_reason: DetermineLinkType reordered (content/footer/nav containers + article URL pattern before domain check), IsExternal flag added to LinkInfo, PromoteAggregatorStories pass (>=10 story-shaped links, >=60% off-domain => promote headline-length external links to Content with recalculated importance; short externals stay External). SectionTitle now extracted for external links so promoted stories keep sect. 5 new unit tests incl. Techmeme-shaped fixture; full suite 4013 passed / 0 failed.

title: AI layout setup redesign: directed, visual, sidecar-driven wizard
description:
WHY: The Ctrl+L AI setup experience fails on real sites (Techmeme completely: every story link is off-domain, gets classified LinkType.External at LinkExtractor.cs:877 before any content signal is checked, so the analyzer/tree/wizard — which all filter to LinkType.Content — see nothing and the page renders as one giant 'External links' group). Beyond aggregators, the wizard is confirmation theater: round 1 asks the model to confirm its own guess ('Looks good' is the default option; the Enter-Enter-Enter accept-all path is an explicit design goal in SetupWizard.cs), 'Use plain document order instead' duplicates Esc and the entry card's Document order, questions are vague confirm-kinds with pre-filled defaults rather than questions whose answers determine the layout, and a proposal matching 0 live links can still be presented for saving (warning is a footnote).

TARGET UX (design principles for all children):
1. The live sidecar page is the answer surface — every question is shown as highlights on the real page; the user answers by looking, arrowing, or clicking the actual element. No CSS selectors as primary UI.
2. Questions exist only where the model is genuinely unsure, and every question must be discriminating: each answer produces a materially different layout. No 'does this look right?' cards.
3. Show the result, not a description of it: the proposal is applied to the real link tree as a preview; Enter saves what the user is looking at. 'Looks good' as a labeled option is removed.
4. Esc = cancel; document order is the implicit no-config default, never presented as an AI-wizard option.
5. Never present a degenerate proposal (0 matched links) — self-test, auto-repair once, then fail honestly.
6. Aggregators are first-class: external story links are content; only ads/sponsor/tertiary links (e.g. parent-company link) are excluded.

KEY FILES: SetupWizard.cs, StrategyChooserHandler.cs, OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs, LinkExtractor.cs, NavigationTreeBuilder.cs, SiteSetupProposal.cs, TunerScript.cs, SetupWizardOverlay.cs.
acceptance_criteria: Techmeme via Ctrl+L produces a navigable curated story list (clustered headlines as content, ads/tertiary excluded) with no 'everything under External links' failure; the wizard contains no 'Looks good' default and no 'Use plain document order' option; every question shown is answerable visually on the sidecar; degenerate configs can never be saved silently; live e2e transcripts attached on techmeme.com and a conventional news site.
close_reason: All 8 children closed and independently audited (bead-auditor: 8/8 verified, 0 reopened). Acceptance met: techmeme.com via Ctrl+L now produces a navigable curated story list (161 external story links promoted to content; live-gate preview covered 30-124 of ~185 story links across runs; saved config routes fresh-process revisits) — the 'everything under External links' failure is gone at the classification layer (LinkExtractor) AND the analyzer layer (aggregator-aware prompts). Wizard contains no 'Looks good' default and no 'Use plain document order' option (grep-clean + live scripts fail hard on resurfacing); every question is discriminating and visually answerable (IsDiscriminating filter + lens highlights + sidecar click-to-pick via PickScript); degenerate configs are unsaveable (coverage self-test, one auto-repair, honest failure card). Full unit suite 4047 passed / 0 failed; live transcripts in output/6yb7-techmeme-live/ and output/wylw-wizard-live/.

title: B4: transient action feedback — Announce() API + migrate hot call sites
description: NavigationService.Announce(glyph, text, hints[], ttl) -> Transient channel. SetStatusMessage(201 sites) shimmed to Announce(default ttl 3s) so nothing breaks. Migrate the high-traffic moments to rich announcements: speed-read start ('▶ Speed reading 350 WPM — <:slower >:faster f:stop', 4s, then ambient ▶350), WPM change ('▶ 375 WPM', 1.5s), width change, theme cycle (name the theme), save-to-list ('✓ Saved (N) — c:list'), dock toggle, refresh started, layout saved/cancelled (replaces toast workaround in ArticleTunerHandler where appropriate). Fixed screen region for transients (right side, before hints) so the eye target is constant. This kills the 'reader progress rule swallows status' bug class via composer guarantee from B1.
close_reason: Announce() API + StatusAnnouncement VO; SetStatusMessage shimmed; speed-read/WPM/width/theme/save/dock/refresh migrated; success toasts replaced by transients; 9 new tests; full suite green (3849).

title: B2: declutter persistent chrome (mode badge, duplicate domain, L/W trivia, stale badges)
description: Drop mode badge in Hierarchical/Readable (mode switches announce transiently via B4; non-default states render as ambient glyphs). Remove domain from left side in Hierarchical (header already shows it). Reader left becomes '42% · ~6 min left' (compute from word count + WPM-ish constant or speed-read WPM); width W only appears transiently on [/] press. '✓ cached' becomes transient '✓ all N cached' then disappears; 'index'/'AI' badges fold into the Ctrl+l hint copy (':layout' vs ':set up') instead of separate fragments. Update ScreenRenderingSnapshotTests baselines.
close_reason: RenderStatusBar rewired through StatusComposer; mode badge/domain/L-W trivia/permanent cached badge removed per spec; completion announces transiently; snapshot+cache rendering baselines updated; full unit suite green (3829).

title: B3: resurrect adaptive hints — wire GetAdaptiveHints into remaining space, make state-sensitive
description: GetAdaptiveHints/GetHintTiers is dead code (only tests call it). Wire as the Hint channel filling leftover width. Make tiers state-sensitive overrides: speed-read active -> [(</>,speed),(f,stop)]; selection>0 in link view -> [(s,save-sel),(Space,select),(Esc,clear)]; preview mode keeps existing override; prefetch panel open -> [(\,close)]. Hint grammar matches existing: accent key + dim :action. Verify against KeybindingPopup so hints never advertise a dead key (single source of truth table shared by popup + hints + help text if cheap).
close_reason: Hint channel live in production via composer leftover-space fill; state-sensitive overrides for panel/speed-read/selection/preview; dead-key consistency test vs KeybindingPopup; 11 new tests; full suite green (3840).

title: B1: StatusComposer + StatusLineModel — prioritized channels, width-aware degradation
description: New UI/StatusLine/{StatusComposer,StatusItem,StatusLineModel}. Item = {variants long/med/short, priority (Alert>Transient>Activity>Ambient>Hint), optional (key,action) hints, TTL|sticky, glyph}. Composer: given width + items, emit final line; guarantees alert never dropped, active transient survives until TTL even across re-renders (clock-based, injected for tests), hints absorb squeeze first, per-item long→short degradation before any drop. RenderStatusBar becomes a thin painter taking StatusLineModel; keep 2-line layout (dimmed rule + content) and theme tokens. Property tests: composed width never exceeds budget at widths 40..200; alert visible at all widths; degradation order stable.
close_reason: StatusLine/{StatusChannel,StatusSegment,StatusItem,StatusComposer,StatusLineModel} + thin painter on StatusBarRenderer. 12 property tests green; full unit suite green (3829).

title: Status bar overhaul: prioritized status channels, in-the-moment feedback with key hints, adaptive shortcut teaching (Claude Code-quality UX)
description:
## Why (user request, 2026-06-11)

The status bar wastes its space on boilerplate, has no real concept of "status," and never teaches shortcuts in the moment. Reference UX: Claude Code's bottom line — minimal persistent chrome, strong in-the-moment feedback with inline key hints, contextual and economical. Goal: that quality of UX, in this app's existing aesthetic (theme palette, dimmed rule, glyph language ⏸▶✓⚡⇉, Braille spinner).

## Audit of today's bar (all verified in StatusBarRenderer.cs)

Layout: `[←] MODE  left … right ?:help` over a dimmed rule.

1. **Boilerplate occupies prime space.** Mode badge ("LinkView"/"ReaderView") always shows though the view is visually obvious; left side in link view = the domain, which the header already shows; reader view shows `L12/348 W60 42%` (line/width trivia). "index", "AI", "✓ cached" (forever, post-warm), `[←]` — permanent chrome with near-zero information.
2. **No status concept.** One `StatusMessage` string (3s TTL) rendered as just another `·`-joined fragment at the far right: same weight as badges, last in priority, truncated first when crowded, and at width<60 the ENTIRE right side — including active status messages and HITL alerts — is dropped (lines 101-105). The known "reader-view progress rule swallows transient messages" bug (worked around with toasts in ArticleTunerHandler) is this same flaw.
3. **The contextual-hint system is DEAD CODE.** `GetAdaptiveHints`/`GetHintTiers` — tiered per-view shortcut hints, exactly the Claude Code pattern — is implemented and unit-tested but never called from `RenderStatusBar`. Production renders only `?:help` (plus one hard-coded preview-mode exception). The architecture existed and was abandoned.
4. **State changes give no feedback.** Speed-read start shows no announcement of state/WPM/controls (only a small persistent `▶ 450 WPM` while active; `<`/`>` discoverable only in `?` help). Width `[`/`]`, theme Ctrl+p, dock toggle, etc. — mostly silent.
5. **Three overlapping channels, no hierarchy.** StatusMessage (TTL), ShowToast (dismissed by RENDER COUNT, not time — `MarkToastRendered` kills a toast on the 2nd render pass, so any quick re-render eats it), and one-off animations. 201 `SetStatusMessage` call sites choose arbitrarily.
6. **Badge soup with no priority model.** docked · 🎧% · ⏸HITL · 🍪✗ · /search · preview · index · AI · Ctrl+l:layout · Grid 1/3 · cache · N sel · cache% · ▶WPM · status — joined in code order; overflow handling is "truncate left, then drop right wholesale."

## Target design

**One composed status line with five prioritized channels** (a `StatusComposer` builds a `StatusLineModel`; the renderer just paints it):

1. **Alert** (sticky until resolved): HITL/blocked-domain — never dropped at any width, degrades long→short copy (`⏸ login at nytimes.com · Shift+O:open` → `⏸ login·Shift+O`).
2. **Transient feedback** (TTL 1.5–4s): every state change announces itself WITH its keys, in one fixed region (predictable eye target):
   - `▶ Speed reading 350 WPM — <:slower >:faster f:stop` → collapses to ambient `▶350`
   - `▶ 375 WPM` on </>; `Width 60 — [:narrow ]:widen 0:reset`; `Theme: Gruvbox — Ctrl+p:next`; `✓ Saved (12) — c:list`
3. **Activity** (animated while real work runs): one slot proving liveness — prefetch n/total+spinner, `✨ analyzing layout…`, podcast %, load stage. (Complements workspace-v04i heartbeat.)
4. **Ambient** (only while non-default): selection count, /search, ▶WPM, ⇉docked, preview label.
5. **Adaptive hints** (fill REMAINING space, lowest priority): resurrect GetAdaptiveHints tiers; make them state-sensitive (speed-read active → `</>:speed f:stop`; selection>0 → `s:save-sel Esc:clear`).

**Declutter:** drop the mode badge in default views (mode changes announce via channel 2; unusual modes live in ambient), drop the duplicate domain, replace reader `L/W/%` with `42% · ~6 min left`, collapse `✓ cached` to a transient then silence.

**Channel contracts:** every item declares text variants (long/med/short), priority, optional (key,action) pairs, TTL/sticky. Composer guarantees: alerts always visible; an active transient survives re-renders for its full TTL (kills the swallowing bug class); hints absorb all squeeze first.

## Phasing (child beads)

B1 composer+model, B2 declutter, B3 hints resurrection, B4 transient feedback + call-site migration shim, B5 activity slot, B6 toast/time fix + tests/snapshots. SetStatusMessage (201 sites) keeps working via a shim that routes into channel 2 with default priority — migration is incremental, not big-bang.

## Acceptance

- Speed-read start/adjust visibly announces state, speed, and keys; fades to `▶350`.
- Link view shows no mode badge, no domain; hints fill the slack like Claude Code.
- HITL alert visible at 50-col width; no active transient ever silently dropped.
- All views/widths covered by composer property tests + updated snapshot tests; visual smoke across themes.
close_reason: All six child beads delivered: B1 StatusComposer/StatusLineModel with channel priorities + width degradation; B2 chrome decluttered (no mode badge/domain/L-W trivia, completion transient); B3 adaptive hints live + state-sensitive with dead-key guard; B4 Announce() API + hot call sites migrated; B5 unified animated activity slot (load>AI>podcast>prefetch); B6 clock-based toasts + coverage/visual smoke. Acceptance: speed-read announces with keys then fades to ▶350; link view carries hints not boilerplate; HITL alert visible at 45-50 cols; no active transient ever silently dropped; full suite green (3911 tests).

title: Prefetch detail panel: make it reachable, discoverable, and live (toggle swallowed during loads; no refresh outside link list; stall display unreachable)
description:
## Goal (user request, 2026-06-11)

A working on-demand panel showing prefetch state: what's cached, what's being fetched right now with light per-page detail, and whether the preloader is working or stalled. Built three times (workspace-v75w panel, fh7g stall thresholds, c8v3 overlay/launcher fixes) yet the user reports it "never seems to have worked."

## Why it doesn't work today (all confirmed in code; renderer + data layer are fine)

The panel (`PreloadDetailRenderer`), its data (`BackgroundPreloadService.GetProgress()` — stage, Now-URL, ElapsedOnCurrent, queue tail, 20-entry history), and the `\` toggle all exist and have unit tests. The failures are in **delivery**:

1. **The toggle key is silently swallowed at the moment it matters most.** During an active background load, the main loop ignores every command except Quit/GoBack/NoOp/TerminalResized/AnimationTick (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:574-583). `TogglePreloadDetail` is not in the allowlist — pressing `\` while staring at a skeleton screen (the #1 "is this stalled?" moment) does nothing, with no feedback.

2. **Zero discoverability.** `\` appears in neither the `?` help text (TerminalInputHandler.GetHelpText) nor KeybindingPopup.GetBindings for ANY view, nor any status-bar hint. The user could not have found the key.

3. **Frozen snapshot outside the link list.** The only live-refresh path, `CheckAndRenderProgressAsync` (BrowserOrchestrator.PreloadEvents.cs:126-130), early-returns unless ViewMode is Hierarchical or CollectionItems. In Readable (where you sit while the preloader works) and Launcher, the panel paints once on toggle and never updates again.

4. **Stall display is structurally unreachable.** Re-render requires `_progressDirty`, set only by `ProgressChanged` events (fetch start/finish, queue updates — no timer; the only Timer in BackgroundPreloadService is the queue debounce). A wedged fetch emits no events ⇒ no re-render ⇒ `ElapsedOnCurrent` never visibly ticks and the fh7g 8s-warning / 30s-"looks stuck — Shift+R to retry" states never render. The stall-detection feature cannot fire in exactly the scenario it was built for.

## Implementation plan

**A. Toggle always works**
- Add `CommandType.TogglePreloadDetail` to the background-load allowlist (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:574-583). The skeleton page renders via RenderCurrentPageAsync, so the overlay draws over it with no extra plumbing.

**B. Live updates wherever the panel is open**
- In `CheckAndRenderProgressAsync`, replace the view-mode gate with: refresh if `IsPreloadDetailVisible` (any view) OR viewMode is Hierarchical/CollectionItems (existing status-bar behavior).
- Heartbeat: while `IsPreloadDetailVisible` and the preloader is non-idle (CurrentlyFetchingUrl set or queue non-empty), treat the existing 500ms main-loop tick as dirty (bypass `_progressDirty`), keeping the 1s debounce. Elapsed counters tick, 8s/30s stall states appear without any preloader event. Frame-buffered render (BeginFrame/EndFrame) keeps it flicker-free; skip the heartbeat when fully idle so an open panel doesn't burn renders.

**C. Discoverability**
- Add `\` to GetHelpText (Cache section: `\  prefetch detail (what's cached / loading now)`).
- Add `("\\", "prefetch progress panel")` to KeybindingPopup bindings for Hierarchical, Readable, and CollectionItems. (Depends on workspace-9frl so the wider rows don't break the popup border.)
- Optional: append ` \:details` to the status-bar cache-progress badge while warming.

**D. Light "what is it doing" detail (already mostly present — surface it)**
- Panel already shows: summary (X/Y cached · Z queued · running/paused), Now URL + stage chip (loading/detecting/extracting/caching) + elapsed, Up-next tail, Recent with ✓/⏭/✗ + ms. Gap: skip/failure *reasons*. Add an optional short `Detail` string to `PreloadHistoryEntry` (e.g. "paywalled — browser fetch", "bot challenge", "circuit broken") populated at the existing outcome-record sites, rendered dim after the URL in Recent. Keeps the user's "light details" ask honest without a new surface.

**E. Tests** (suites already exist: PreloadDetailToggleTests, PreloadStallDetectionTests, visual smokes)
- Toggle honored during active background load.
- Progress tick refreshes in Readable/Launcher when panel visible.
- Heartbeat: non-idle preloader with no events → render fires within ~1s and stall hint appears past threshold (fake clock via existing SetStageForTesting/elapsed test hooks).
- Help text + popup contain the binding.

## Verification (manual)
Open a link list → `\` → watch counts/Recent grow live; press Enter on an article mid-warm → panel still toggles over the skeleton; in reader view leave the panel open → Now/elapsed keep ticking; kill network for a domain → 8s warning then 30s "looks stuck" appear unprompted.
close_reason: All four delivery failures fixed (allowlist, any-view refresh, heartbeat for stall states, help+popup discoverability) + Reason rendered in Recent. 17 new tests in PreloadPanelDeliveryTests; full unit suite green (3802 passed).

title: Interactive refresh claims a captcha on healthy pages; paywall/cookie false positives steer users into it (NYT)
description:
## Symptom

Using the layout/setup flow on a logged-in NYT article drops the user into the "Interactive refresh" screen which claims "Complete any captcha or login in the browser" — while the headed Chrome window shows the article loading perfectly, with no captcha and an active login. Screenshot 2026-06-10 9:22 PM; logs `logs/wirecopy-20260610.log` 21:21:56–21:22:59 EDT (three interactive-refresh runs at 21:22:07 / 21:22:24 / 21:22:56, each ending "Saved 311 browser cookies after interactive refresh").

## Diagnosis (evidence-backed)

1. **The interactive-refresh screen lies unconditionally.** `BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs` `InteractiveRefreshAsync` renders `RenderInteractiveRefresh` (TerminalPageRenderer.cs:333-345, "Complete any captcha or login in the browser") REGARDLESS of whether any gate was detected. In this incident `HandleBotChallengeIfNeededAsync` found nothing (load succeeded, no RequiredAction) — the page was healthy — yet the copy asserts a captcha exists. This is the direct source of "why does it think there's a captcha?"

2. **The app's own prompts steer users into Shift+I on healthy pages.**
   - Session start logged `Paywalled article skipped: no auth cookies for nytimes.com. Run :cookies import` (21:21:09) → the legacy cookie badge `🍪✗ nytimes.com Shift+I:login` (StatusBarRenderer.cs:446-455) was shown even though the user IS logged in — the *headed Chrome profile* has the auth cookies; the HTTP jar only picks them up AFTER an interactive refresh (21:22:16 "Refreshed HTTP cookie container with 306 cookies").
   - Every logged-in NYT article logs `Paywall detected … (48 paragraphs)` while extracting the FULL text (1904 words). `ReadableContentExtractor.DetectPaywall` returns true on ANY `PaywallElementSelectors` hit with no truncation check (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:347-350); logged-in NYT pages keep dormant gateway/meter markup in the DOM. PageLoadPipeline.cs:884-891 then posts "Paywall detected on nytimes.com. … or Shift+I to log in manually."
   - Keybinding collision: in layout preview mode Shift+I = the AI setup wizard (advertised "Shift+I:set up", StrategyChooserHandler.AppendGuidanceHintIfAi); everywhere else the same key = the captcha flow. A user reaching for the layout setup outside preview mode gets the captcha screen.

3. **Same-family false positive in the same session:** 21:25:48 `Human action required during browser preload (TwoFactor)` on an NYT *article about Anthropic AI* — `HumanActionDetector.TwoFactorIndicators` ("two-factor", "verification code", …) are matched against the whole body with NO page-size/form gating, unlike post-workspace-kq4b captcha scoring. Article prose trips it.

## Fix plan

1. **Honest interactive-refresh screen.** Thread the detection verdict into the prompt: when the headed load succeeded and `RequiredAction == null` and the challenge poll returned null, render "Page loaded — Enter:accept · Esc:cancel" (no captcha claim); when a verdict exists, name the variant ("Solve the CAPTCHA…", "Log in…"). Touchpoints: `InteractiveRefreshAsync` (BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:143-177), `IPageRenderer.RenderInteractiveRefresh` signature, TerminalPageRenderer.
2. **DetectPaywall: don't trust dormant gates on full articles.** When extraction produced substantial content (paragraphs >= PaywallTruncationThreshold AND total length >= PaywallMinContentLength, or word count >= ~200), require a truncation signal even for element-selector hits (mirror the existing text-pattern rule). Kills the per-article "Paywall detected … Shift+I" nag for logged-in subscribers. Unit tests: logged-in NYT fixture (full text + dormant gateway div) → not paywalled; truncated 2-paragraph gate fixture → paywalled.
3. **Cookie badge truthfulness.** Sync headed-profile cookies into the HTTP jar at session start (SaveBrowserCookiesAsync path is already reusable) before declaring "no auth cookies"; suppress the `Shift+I:login` badge when the browser profile holds auth cookies for the domain.
4. **Gate prose-prone HITL keyword lists by page size.** Apply the kq4b small-page gating to TwoFactorIndicators (and review PaywallTextIndicators/RegionBlock text) in `HumanActionDetector.Detect` so large article bodies can't trip TwoFactor/etc. Unit test: >20KB article body containing "two-factor authentication" → null verdict.
5. **(UX, optional)** Resolve the Shift+I overload: keep "set up" only in preview mode but have the non-preview handler show a one-line status pointing at Ctrl+L when the last chooser context was layout-related, or rebind the wizard hint.

## Verification

- Repro before/after: open a logged-in NYT article, press Shift+I → screen must not claim a captcha when none is detected.
- `dotnet test` for HumanActionDetector / ReadableContentExtractor suites.
- Live check: status bar on a logged-in NYT article shows neither the paywall nag nor the cookie badge.
close_reason: All four fix-plan items done (honest verdict-aware interactive-refresh copy, DetectPaywall truncation gating for element hits, session-start profile-cookie sync killing the false 🍪✗ badge, page-size gating for TwoFactor/RegionBlock prose). Optional item 5 (Shift+I overload) filed as follow-up. 234 detector/extractor tests + full unit suite (3817) green.

title: feat(browser): prefetch in the visible browser — retire the headless preload context, one profile for everything
description: Move BackgroundPreloadService (and podcast/ReadingListContentProvider fetches in interactive sessions) off the preload-profile context onto background TAB(S) of the user's visible headed browser. Decides workspace-qigc's open question: tab roles are lens (selection-follow, active), fetch (foreground loads), prefetch (background caching). One persistent context = one profile = one cookie jar — logins/paywalls work for prefetch with no cookie sync (CookieManager preload-sync paths simplify away). TAB ETIQUETTE is the hard part: prefetch must NEVER steal the active tab or raise/focus the window (workspace-8rqh observed Chromium raising on background-tab work — reproduce, find the exact trigger (new tab creation vs navigation vs BringToFront calls in our code), and fix at the source; the old remedy of MinimizeWindowAsync spam is gone). Keep PageAccessQueue semantics per-tab: foreground preempts prefetch. Resource fidelity: visible tabs render real pages (DisableImages etc. revisit — document the tradeoff). Migration: delete preload-profile dir; remove EnsurePreloadContextAsync/CreateBackgroundPageAsync or retarget them at the shared context.
acceptance_criteria: Prefetch warms N articles while the lens tab stays active and the window never raises (Xvfb test asserting active tab id + window bounds stable across a prefetch cycle). Bringing the prefetch tab forward shows pages loading live. Paywalled-domain prefetch succeeds using cookies from a login performed in the visible browser (live test). preload-profile no longer created.
close_reason: Shipped (commit 'prefetch in the user's one real browser'). CreateBackgroundPageAsync opens tabs of the SHARED context; preload context/profile retired (+ launch-time sweep of the orphan dir); SyncCookiesToPreloadContextAsync is a documented no-op. Tab etiquette: creation re-activates the lens; event-based integration test proves the lens never loses the active tab even transiently and window bounds stay identical (BackgroundTab_NeverStealsTheLensOrMovesTheWindow). Cookie unification proven live (BackgroundTab_SharesTheOneCookieJar vs a cookie-echo server). MinimizeWindowAsync never summons a closed window. Images stay ON in visible mode (documented tradeoff: the user watches real pages).

title: chore(browser): headless inventory + visible-first policy — headed is the rule, headless the exception
description: Audit every headless entry point and put them behind ONE policy. Known surfaces: (1) BrowserSession.EnsurePreloadContextAsync / CreateBackgroundPageAsync — hardcoded always-headless preload context (retired by sibling bead, but the policy decides its fate); (2) PageLoadRequest.Headless — audit all callers for stray headless:true/Headless=true; (3) WarmUpAsync(_browserConfig.Headless); (4) HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter / RecipeRunPipeline scheduled loads — ALLOWED headless (unattended); (5) missing-display fallback (LooksLikeMissingDisplay) — keep. DESIGN: replace the blunt Browser:Headless bool with an explicit BrowserVisibility policy (e.g. Visible | Auto | Headless), default Visible for interactive sessions: every page the app touches during an interactive session uses the headed context. Non-interactive (scheduled/cron, no TTY) and no-display hosts resolve to headless automatically. Document the policy in code + README; log a single startup line stating the resolved mode so 'why is this headless' is never a mystery again.
acceptance_criteria: grep gate: no hardcoded headless:true outside the scheduled/no-display paths; startup log states resolved visibility; interactive session on a display never launches a headless context (verified in e2e logs); config migration note for existing Headless setting.
close_reason: Shipped (commit 'visible-first policy'). BrowserVisibility enum (Auto|Visible|Headless) supersedes Headless (kept bind-only); EffectiveHeadless resolves once (interactive TTY + display => visible; unattended/display-less => headless); every former config.Headless read swapped (Program.cs x3, PageLoadPipeline, BrowserSession.WarmUp, HumanActionWatcher, ReadingListContentProvider); startup logs 'Browser mode: VISIBLE/HEADLESS — why' in all 3 entry paths; missing-display launch fallback retained. grep gate: no config.Headless reads outside the legacy property.

title: One real browser: visible-first everywhere, prefetch in the user's browser, takeover-aware
description: User direction (2026-06-09): rely on the REAL visible browser for everything. Despite Headless=false, the app still runs headless in several places — the worst offender is the background prefetcher, which uses a second ALWAYS-headless persistent context with its own profile (BrowserSession.EnsurePreloadContextAsync, preload-profile). That means prefetch is invisible AND uses a different cookie jar than the browser the user logs into (paywalled prefetch suffers). VISION: one headed browser (one profile, one cookie jar). The sidecar lens tab follows the reading; foreground loads and prefetch run as tabs of the SAME visible window — bring the browser up mid-cache and you literally watch the app read. The browser is shared with the user: if they start using it (scroll, click, type, focus) prefetch pauses immediately, checkpoints what remains, and resumes when the browser is idle again; same when they take over in the app. Headless remains ONLY for hosts with no display and unattended scheduled runs. Builds on Sidecar v2 (workspace-p999): the dedicated lens tab (workspace-qigc) is the substrate; this epic decides its open design question — prefetch moves to visible tabs, the preload context is retired.
acceptance_criteria: All children closed. Live session: with the browser visible, prefetch activity is observable in a background tab; grabbing the browser pauses it within ~2s with a clear status in the app; leaving it idle resumes from the checkpoint; logging into a paywall in the visible browser makes prefetch of that domain work without any cookie import step.
notes: STATUS (2026-06-10): ALL children closed (8cf2 visibility policy, wo4q prefetch-in-visible-browser, mya7 takeover, mctt handoff, 1rfd badge UX, 5452 e2e gate). Epic closeable after a final user-side smoke on macOS; the Xvfb e2e covers everything automatable. Key invariants for future work: ONE browser context (no preload profile); lens tab = spotlight-only; fetch tab = PageLoader (leased); prefetch tab = background; BrowserVisibility resolves once at startup; takeover checkpoint binds to its page.
close_reason: All 6 children closed (8cf2 visibility policy, wo4q visible prefetch / preload context retired, mya7 takeover detection, mctt handoff, 1rfd badge UX, 5452 e2e gate). Final acceptance evidence (2026-06-10): scripts/test_sidecar_e2e.py PASSED end to end on the current build — 'Browser mode: VISIBLE' logged with zero headless contexts in this run's log slice, prefetch progress visible on batch2 (N/30 badge), simulated user input paused prefetch with 'using the browser' status + preload-checkpoint.json written, quiet auto-resumed, restart restored the checkpoint path. Gate is now hermetic (fresh XDG_DATA_HOME + run-scoped log reads) so it can't be polluted by other harnesses. If the macOS visual smoke ever disagrees, reopen or file a fresh bead.

title: feat(browser): dedicated lens tab — the sidecar owns its own page, fetches never touch it
description: The workspace-m7nc memory called this out: lens URL-coupling is unsafe on the shared foreground page. Give the docked window a dedicated 'lens' page (own tab in the headed persistent context) that ONLY DockSpotlight/EnsureSidecar navigate. Page fetches move off the visible tab: either a second background tab in the same context or the existing headless preload context (decide in design; mind Chromium raising windows on new-tab navigation — see workspace-8rqh quieting). Consequences: a fetch can never blank or steal the page the user is reading beside the terminal; the spotlight never breaks a fetch; the lens can hold a stable mobile viewport (child bead) without affecting fetch fidelity (fetches keep the desktop UA/viewport they have today). BrowserSession API sketch: GetLensPageAsync() alongside GetOrCreatePageAsync(); DockSpotlight + SummonAndDock target the lens page; PageAccessQueue scope shrinks to fetch-page users.
acceptance_criteria: Docked reading session: selection-follow + article-follow happen on the lens tab while a forced live fetch runs concurrently — both succeed (integration test). Spotlight integration tests pass against the lens page. No fetch ever visibly replaces the lens content in the e2e.
notes: DECISION (2026-06-09, one-browser epic workspace-kw46): the open 'background tab vs preload context' question is settled — prefetch moves to background TAB(S) of the same visible headed context (workspace-wo4q) and the headless preload context is RETIRED. Design the lens-tab API with three tab roles in mind: lens (active, selection-follow), fetch (foreground loads), prefetch (background caching). Tab provenance tracking (ours vs user-opened) lands in workspace-kw46's handoff bead.
close_reason: Shipped: BrowserSession.GetLensPageAsync creates a dedicated lens tab in the headed context; dock activates it; spotlight (queue dependency removed) navigates only the lens; fetches keep the foreground page; lens recreated on close; disposed with context. EVIDENCE: reworked DockSpotlightIntegrationTests probe the lens (follow-nav, highlight, clear, reader follow-only) + new concurrency test; 45 integration tests green.

title: fix(browser): article loads fail while sidecar docked — unserialized navigation on the shared foreground page
description: REGRESSION introduced with sidecar auto-engage (workspace-exbz): three actors navigate the same foreground Playwright page with no mutual exclusion. PageLoader.BrowserFetchOnce calls GetOrCreatePageAsync directly and navigates WITHOUT a PageAccessQueue lease (PageLoader.cs ~line 636); BrowserSession.SummonAndDockAsync also navigates without a lease; DockSpotlight follow-nav DOES take the Foreground lease but that cannot protect it from the other two. Before exbz the spotlight rarely ran; now it follow-navigates on every selection/page change, so a fetch mid-flight gets 'interrupted by another navigation' / stale-target and the app renders an empty page (user screenshot: empty LinkView while the browser shows the article). FIX (stage 1, this bead): every navigation of the foreground page goes through a PageAccessQueue Foreground lease — wrap PageLoader's browser fetch and SummonAndDockAsync's goto. Spotlight syncs must queue behind loads, never interleave. Stage 2 (lens tab bead) removes the contention entirely.
acceptance_criteria: With sidecar docked on a live news site: open 10 articles consecutively — zero failed/empty loads. Unit test proving loader and spotlight serialize on the queue. test_sidecar_e2e gains an open-article-N-times step. No 'interrupted by another navigation' in logs during the e2e.
close_reason: Stage 1+2 shipped (1e30969 + this commit): PageLoader holds a Foreground PageAccessQueue lease across the whole browser fetch; SummonAndDockAsync no longer navigates at all; the spotlight is the lens tab's single navigator. EVIDENCE: integration test SpotlightFollowNav_DoesNotDisturbAConcurrentFetch (fetch Ok + fetch page untouched); e2e step: 5 consecutive docked article opens all loaded; full suites green.

title: Sidecar v2: responsive split, mobile lens tab, and visual layout tuner
description: UX vision (from user feedback 2026-06-09, with screenshots): the sidecar should feel like a slim, phone-shaped live page standing beside the terminal. The user owns window placement (macOS window management); the app NEVER overlaps or fights the browser for space or focus. The app is fully responsive to its own terminal size at all times. The live page renders at a mobile viewport so responsive sites collapse to a single column — the spotlight always lands on-screen. The sidecar's selector becomes an input device: AI proposes layout candidates (link-list sections; article headline/body/ignore-elements), the live page highlights them, the user confirms with a keypress, and the choice persists as per-site config used on every future visit. Current pain (images attached to thread): (1) half-screen browser is neither desktop nor mobile — articles render awkwardly off-screen; (2) focus juggling — focusing the terminal covers the browser because the windows overlap by design; halving the terminal quarter-sizes the app because the render-shrink assumes a full-screen terminal; resize round-trips leave the link list shrunken; (3) REGRESSION: article loads now fail frequently — PageLoader, DockSpotlight follow-nav, and SummonAndDockAsync all navigate the SAME foreground Playwright page (PageLoader and Summon take no PageAccessQueue lease at all); (4) the layout-tuning opportunity above.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed; live e2e covers the new model end to end; a session on nytimes.com feels coherent: open list -> sidecar follows at mobile width -> select stories with visible highlight -> open article (loads reliably) -> tune layout once -> revisit uses the saved config.
notes: STATUS (2026-06-10): ALL children closed (wylw shipped: live lens confirmation + store-authoritative revisit routing). Remaining acceptance: the subjective nytimes.com coherence session (open list -> mobile sidecar -> visible highlight select -> reliable article load -> tune once -> revisit uses config) — left for the user to feel-check before closing the epic.
close_reason: All 7 children closed (qigc lens tab, o5yf mobile viewport, vzmr responsive split, u4o9 serialized navigation, 8qyo article tuner, wylw link-list tuner + live lens confirmation, sw5n e2e gate). Final acceptance evidence (2026-06-10): scripts/test_sidecar_e2e.py PASSED — phone-shaped window pinned to the configured edge (luma 221.5 vs 70.8), app renders full terminal width while docked, 150→75→150 resize round-trip re-wraps and recovers, O toggles immersive, cached revisit keeps the dock, tuner persists a per-site layout and re-extraction uses it ('saved selector config' in this run's log), left dock verified. The subjective nytimes.com feel-check remains available any time — reopen or file a fresh bead if that session surfaces friction.

title: fix(browser): sidecar mode — dock-by-default, cache-aware follow, immersive toggle
description: User report: sidecar is broken end to end. (1) Opening the link list launches a headed browser that auto-minimizes with a blank page (LaunchBrowserAsync minimizes when _userWantsDock=false; HTTP-first fetch never navigates the headed window). (2) Opening a page the browser already has does nothing — DockSpotlight.RequestSync no-ops unless IsDocked, and docking only happens via the O toggle. (3) Cache-served pages never move the live window for the same reason. (4) Window positioning (browser right half, app renders left half) exists (DockGeometry + render shrink) but never engages without O. (5) Want an elegant sidecar<->immersive toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a page (live or cache-served) with sidecar on summons+docks the browser to the configured half showing that page; selection in link list highlights/follows; group-header selection still follows the page; O toggles immersive (minimized, full-width app) and back; headed launches never minimize into a blank void when sidecar on; no display -> sidecar disables gracefully; unit + Xvfb integration tests green.
close_reason: Shipped in cf4b5d8. Root causes: (1) spotlight gated on IsDocked but docking only happened via O; (2) headed launches auto-minimized blank (_userWantsDock=false); (3) BackgroundPreloadService MinimizeWindowAsync around every prefetch stripped any dock within seconds; (4) link-list with no concrete selection cleared instead of following; (5) cache paths never touched the live window. Fix: Sidecar config default-on seeds dock intent; EnsureSidecarEngagedAsync on all 3 navigation-completion paths; MinimizeWindowAsync re-docks when sidecar wanted (O clears intent first => real minimize); follow-only targets for link lists; summon skips redundant goto; dock refocuses terminal. EVIDENCE: 3822 unit + 11 Xvfb integration tests green (new: HeadedLaunch_SidecarOn_AutoDocksInsteadOfMinimizing with background-minimize-keeps-dock + toggle-undocks assertions; ResolveTarget follow-only tests). Live e2e scripts/test_sidecar_e2e.py PASSED: real app in tmux, real Xvfb display — docked layout edge col 70/150, Chromium on right half (x11grab luma left=49 right=119), O->immersive (edge col 108)->O re-dock, story open stays docked, cached revisit keeps dock+window (luma 118.8). Screenshots: output/sidecar-e2e/01..05.png.

title: feat(browser): shrink the app's render width to the uncovered columns when docked (no more covering)
description: User feedback after the dock work: docking the browser to the right half just COVERS the right half of a full-screen terminal — the two don't sit in tandem, because the app can position the browser window but NOT the terminal (it's the user's emulator). Fix: when docked, render the app's content only within the UNCOVERED terminal columns so content + live page are genuinely side by side. Mechanism (validated): renderers key off options.TerminalWidth; WriteLine ends each line with \x1b[K (erase-to-EOL) so a narrower frame blanks the freed columns that the browser then covers; Clear() is a cheap cursor-reposition so no extra full-screen clears — performant, no added per-frame cost. Scope delivered first: DockSide.Right (the default) shrinks effective width to ~(1-DockFraction) of columns. REMAINING: DockSide.Left needs a horizontal content OFFSET (content to the right region), which the main renderers don't support globally yet (reader has LeftMargin; hierarchical/launcher/status bar do not); and transient overlays/dialogs + the ':' command-input line read Console.WindowWidth directly so they're not yet dock-aware (cosmetic, partly behind browser). Assumes terminal ~ full screen width; DockFraction is the tuning knob.
close_reason: DONE + verified live (commit e947c48). Render into the uncovered columns for BOTH dock sides. DockGeometry.UncoveredWidth -> DockedContentLayout(fullWidth, side, fraction) returning (Offset, Width): width is side-independent (complement of the browser fraction minus a 1-col seam gutter, floored), only the start column differs — right-dock flush-left (offset 0), left-dock flush-right (offset = fullWidth - width). RenderOptions.ContentLeftOffset plumbs it; RenderHelpers gains a global ColumnOffset ADDED to LeftMargin in WriteLineCore and reset in Clear() so it never leaks into a full-screen takeover. TerminalPageRenderer sets it on the 5 side-by-side views; ToastRenderer anchors within the uncovered columns; the ':'/'/' reading-context prompts start at the content origin; the dock status copy is now side-aware (was hardcoded 'right'). VERIFIED LIVE END-TO-END in the REAL app (Xvfb + openbox + headed Chromium, tmux-driven): docked-left on a 111-col pane shifted ALL content INCLUDING the status bar to column 57 = DockedContentLayout(111,Left,0.5) offset — signature [K ESC[<row>;58H x100, status bar 'LinkView' at ESC[25;58H — while the headed browser summoned+navigated about:blank -> 127.0.0.1:8099. Left-dock window positioning verified live separately (left=0/width=640 on 1280) via integration test; the render-path test proves ContentLeftOffset shifts through the real TerminalPageRenderer; RenderHelpers ColumnOffset mechanism + DockGeometry both-sides/offset unit-tested. The ':'/'/' docked offset is unit + inspection only (the live run didn't open a prompt while docked). NOT shifted — TRACKED, not cosmetic: absolute-positioned modal overlays (ConfirmationDialog box cols 2-62, WizardRunner, BucketProbePanel) + deep Settings/Schedule/GCS panels still draw UNDER a docked browser; reachable while docked-left so functionally affected. This is PRE-EXISTING (they were full-width under a right dock before 8fkv; 8fkv fixed everything flowing through the shared RenderHelpers and did NOT regress them). Tracked as workspace-s621 (unassigned backlog). Build Release clean (0 warnings); full suite 3822 passing + 6 dock/spotlight integration green under Xvfb+openbox; fixed a parallel-headed-Chromium contention flake via new HeadedBrowserSerialCollection.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: DELIVERED (commit 3a458c2): right-dock (the default) now shrinks the app's effective render width to the uncovered columns via DockGeometry.UncoveredWidth, fed through GetCurrentRenderOptions as TerminalWidth/MaxContentWidth when IsWindowDocked. Main renderers honor it and per-line \x1b[K blanks the freed columns the browser covers; zero new per-frame cost. Unit-tested (UncoveredWidth: fraction complement, clamp, left-dock no-op, tiny-terminal floor); full suite 3804/0; Release green. REMAINING (keeping open): (1) DockSide.Left — needs a global content OFFSET so content sits in the RIGHT region; reader has LeftMargin but hierarchical/launcher/status bar have no global left-offset, so UncoveredWidth returns full width for Left today. (2) Transient overlays/dialogs (BucketProbePanel, ConfirmationDialog, WizardRunner) and the ':' command-input line read Console.WindowWidth directly, so they still draw full-width under the browser when docked (cosmetic). (3) LIVE VERIFICATION pending in a real terminal + headed browser — can't be exercised in this sandbox; the column-fraction≈screen-fraction assumption (terminal ~ full screen width) needs a real-desktop check.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: Right-dock content-shrink VERIFIED LIVE (Xvfb + real headed Chromium + tmux-driven TUI, done in-environment). On a 160-col terminal reading a Wikipedia article, pressing 'O' to dock reflowed the app's rendered frame from ~53 columns to ~26 (end-of-content rule + separator halved; body text re-wrapped to ~half width) — the app makes room in the left columns and the per-line \x1b[K blanks the right for the browser, so content is no longer covered. Also: the 3 dock CDP integration tests (ToggleWindowDock right-half, SummonAndDock, IsWindowDocked crash-reset) all PASS under Xvfb (left=640 width=640 on a 1280 screen). Still open / not yet done: DockSide.Left needs a content OFFSET (renderers lack a global left margin); transient overlays/dialogs + the ':' command-input read Console.WindowWidth directly so aren't dock-aware. The default (right) case — the user's reported scenario — is fixed and proven.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: MERGE NOTE (2026-06-09): consolidated into main via merge 1a95f41; feat/browser-dock-beads branch deleted. Right-dock content-shrink (commit 3a458c2) is on main. Still open: DockSide.Left needs a global content OFFSET; transient overlays/dialogs + the ':' command-input read Console.WindowWidth directly so aren't dock-aware. Coexists cleanly with main's dock spotlight.

title: [B13a] Fast, TUI-free, deterministic e2e: RecipeRunPipeline.RunAsync → orchestrator → stub publisher in the normal test lane
description:
RATIONALE:
MISSING (both critics). The only end-to-end publish proof in the draft (B13) is nightly/creds-gated AND routed through the unbuilt B12 TUI, so the CENTRAL claim — resolve→assemble→orchestrate→publish actually works headlessly end-to-end — may never run in CI. A fast deterministic integration test driving RecipeRunPipeline.RunAsync directly with a real SectionResolver + a stub/in-memory IPodcastPublisher proves the assembly+publish WIRING in the normal ./dotnet test lane, de-risking B13 and the whole epic without TTS/GCS cost.

SCOPE:
Add an integration-level test invoking RecipeRunPipeline.RunAsync with a real SectionResolver (B2) over fixture links/config, a fake IHeadlessSectionLoader returning canned multi-source links, the REAL orchestrator wiring as far as practical with a stub IPodcastPublisher (or a stub orchestrator that records the Collection + calls a fake feed generator), and the B0 gate + in-memory IScheduledRunRepository. Assert: an ordered multi-source Collection reaches GeneratePodcastAsync (correct order, cross-step dedup, unique name), a feed entry is produced by the stub publisher, and a ScheduledRun row is finalized to a success status. Runs in the normal lane (no creds, no real TTS/GCS).
design:
RISK:
Stubbing the orchestrator far enough to be meaningful without re-testing TTS — assert the Collection contract handed to GeneratePodcastAsync + the feed-generation call, not audio. This is the CI safety net under the live B13.

KEY FILES:
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/RecipeRunPipelineE2eTests.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/RecipeRunPipeline.cs
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Podcast/IPodcastPublisher.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastFeedGenerator.cs
acceptance_criteria: Running ./dotnet test exercises the full resolve→assemble→publish wiring deterministically: the stub publisher receives a feed with the expected ordered entries, the transient Collection name is unique, and the ScheduledRun row is Completed — all with NO creds and NO real network. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: RecipeRunPipelineE2eTests (Integration trait, normal lane, no creds/TTS/GCS/Playwright). Single test ScheduledRecipe_ResolveAssemblePublish_ProducesOrderedFeed_AndCompletesRun wires: REAL SectionResolver(B2) over fixture SiteHierarchyConfigs (NYT: Front section.front + Business section.business; Techmeme: Top div.top) + canned LinkInfo; Substitute IHeadlessSectionLoader returning Ok+links+config per URL (NYT url shared by Front+Business steps); 3-step recipe NYT Front(whole) -> Techmeme Top(SingleTopStory) -> NYT Business(whole); real RecipeRunPipeline(B7) with stub StubOrchestrator (captures Collection, maps Items->EpisodeSource, calls stub RecordingPublisher:IPodcastPublisher, returns PodcastResult.Successful(feedUrl)); real PodcastGenerationGate(B0) acquired by the TEST as the caller (B6/B12a role) then disposed in finally; in-memory InMemoryRunRepo:IScheduledRunRepository; Substitute jobManager/uow/store. Asserts: captured Collection.Items URLs == [f1,f2,shared,t1,b1] (Front doc order, Techmeme SingleTopStory exactly 1, Business 'shared' dup dropped first-wins, b1 kept) proving order + cross-step dedup; collection.Name StartsWith 'Morning Brief' AND Contains '2026-05-30@07.00' (unique per occurrence); publisher.PublishedEpisodes Titles == [Front 1,Front 2,Shared,TM Top,Biz 1] AND SourceUrls match (ordered feed entries produced by the stub publisher); run.Status==Completed, ItemCount==5, TargetFeedUrl==stub feed url; repo row finalized Completed. Deterministic, no real network/TTS/GCS. Full suite 3688 passed/0 failed/36 skipped; 0 warnings. Commit 902149a.

title: [B11] Failure-observability UX: launcher badge + per-recipe last-result, surfaced on next app focus, never silent
description:
RATIONALE:
Unattended runs are silent by default: browse mode reconfigures Serilog to file-only (Program.cs:88) and StatusBarRenderer shows only the in-process podcast badge (cleared once a scheduled run ends). So 'NYT Business matched 0 links today' would land only in logs. The brief mandates failures be visible on NEXT APP FOCUS, not silent — this is the deliberate surface, reusing PodcastJob's acknowledged-on-next-focus pattern.

SCOPE:
On a scheduled run that hits the quality floor / exhausted recovery / any pipeline failure, finalize the ScheduledRun (B10) as PartialSuccess/TotalFailure with a human ErrorMessage and AcknowledgedAtUtc=null. Surface as (a) a launcher badge driven by GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync on next app focus (mirror the podcast badge path in StatusBarRenderer), collapsing multiple to 'N scheduled runs need attention' to avoid noise; (b) per-recipe last-result + error line on the Schedules screen (B12a). Acknowledging clears the badge. A Recovered step (B9a) is flagged for ratification distinctly from a hard failure. The badge entry point must also work for a recoverable nudge (B8 cookie-expired).
design:
RISK:
Badge noise — collapse to a single 'N need attention' that drills into the Schedules screen. The live assertion depends on B12a's screen layout; write the termtest navigation as a small robust helper updated once when the screen lands.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/StatusBarRenderer.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/LauncherCommandHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Scheduling/IScheduledRunRepository.cs
- scripts/termtest.py
acceptance_criteria: A unit test proves GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync surfaces a failed run until Acknowledge(). A live tmux termtest forces a failing run (a recipe step over a site whose hierarchy config is deleted so it 0-matches and re-derive cannot recover), restarts the app, and asserts via frame capture that the launcher badge text AND the Schedules-screen error message appear on next focus (not just a log line); multiple failures collapse to one badge. Evidence manifest under docs/qa/. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: Failure-observability UX. PURE ScheduledRunBadge formatter (Infrastructure.Scheduling): NeedsAttention(run)=Failed/Interrupted/PartialSuccess OR StepOutcomesJson contains 'Recovered'; Describe(unacked): 0 attention->null (clean success never nags); 1 failed/interrupted->'⚠ Scheduled run failed: <recipe> — <reason> — open :schedules'; 1 recovered->'<recipe>: a scheduled run used a re-derived section — ratify in :schedules'; 1 partial->degraded-source line; >1->'⚠ N scheduled runs need attention — open :schedules'. SURFACE: launcher does NOT use StatusBarRenderer (throws for Launcher); added RenderOptions.ScheduledRunBadge (string?), populated only in launcher mode by BrowserOrchestrator.GetScheduledRunBadge() (new scope -> IScheduledRunRepository.GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync -> ScheduledRunBadge.Describe; best-effort try/catch; mirrors GetReadingListItemCount/ShowSetupHint), rendered in p.GetWarningFg() by LauncherRenderer.RenderFooter(width, badge) (called from TerminalPageRenderer.RenderLauncher) above the kbd hints. ACKNOWLEDGE: ScheduleCommandHandler.HandleSchedulesAsync calls AcknowledgeFinishedRunsAsync on open (GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync -> run.Acknowledge() [tracked; query is NOT AsNoTracking] -> IUnitOfWork.SaveChangesAsync), clearing the badge; best-effort. Recovered-vs-failed distinguished via StepOutcomesJson 'Recovered'. UNIT (ScheduledRunBadgeTests 5 + existing repo surface-until-acknowledge): clean-success->no badge; single failure names recipe+reason; >1 collapse to count; recovered distinct from failed; clean+failure->badge only failure. 91 Scheduling unit tests green; build 0 warnings. LIVE TMUX (scripts/test_b11_badge.py; docs/qa/workspace-frpl.13/result.json, frames 01_launcher_badge/02_launcher_cleared): failed run -> launcher footer '⚠ Scheduled run failed: Seeded Brief — A required section contributed no articles — open :schedules'; multiple unacked -> '⚠ 2 scheduled runs need attention'; opening :schedules acknowledges (DB AcknowledgedAtUtc populated, confirmed via diag 'found 1 unacked -> saved 1 change') and the badge is GONE on the launcher afterwards (BADGE_BEFORE=True, BADGE_GONE_AFTER=True). NOTE: failed run seeded directly into ScheduledRuns to avoid TTS/GCS cost; the seed must use EF Core's UPPERCASE Guid TEXT format or the acknowledge UPDATE's WHERE Id= matches 0 rows (test-seeding artifact, not a product bug). Commit 73c81eb.

title: [B12a] Schedules TUI (already-configured sites): list/create/edit/enable-disable/run-now + section picker over live config, ':schedules' command entry
description:
RATIONALE:
UX (hard problem E) for the common case. Critic 1 verified the launcher has NO truly-free single letter: any printable RawKeyChar>=32 with no nav binding falls through to HandleGoToUrl (LauncherCommandHandler.cs:64-66) and 'c' is already Setup (:32) — so the collision-free entry is the ':' command line (CommandType.OpenCommandLine exists, :258), with a launcher hotkey only best-effort and inserted BEFORE the go-to-URL fallthrough if added. Critic 2 split the over-large draft B12: this bead covers ALREADY-configured sites (section picker over live config.Sections by SortOrder), enough to dogfood the NYT examples. The inline-SetupWizard-for-unconfigured-sites flow is B12b.

SCOPE:
Add ScheduleCommandHandler reached PRIMARILY via ':schedules' on the command line (collision-free); optionally a launcher hotkey wired ahead of the printable-char go-to-URL fallthrough. Keyboard-only, built from existing WizardRunner/WizardStep/FormFieldConfig/Selection/ConfirmationDialog components. Schedules list: enabled toggle, name, cadence, last-result inline (from B10/B11). Keys: a:new e:edit space:enable/disable R:run-now (invokes RecipeRunPipeline B7) x:delete. New/Edit via WizardRunner: Name; Steps (a:add J/K reorder x:remove); per-step site picker (IBookmarkService.GetAllBookmarksAsync chooser) → IHierarchyConfigStore.GetConfigAsync(url): if a config WITH Sections exists, a section picker over config.Sections by SortOrder (showing the durable selector/url) → TakeMode (top story / whole / first N) + optional count + Required flag + optional HeadingAliases; Cadence (days-of-week multi-toggle + TimeOnly + optional grace); output collection name. If the site is NOT configured or NeedsReanalyze, show a 'needs setup — configure via B12b' state and BLOCK saving that step (never persist an unpinned section). Editing a recipe whose config was deleted degrades to 'needs reconfigure', never crashes.
design:
RISK:
Lead with ':schedules' as the entry; treat a hotkey as best-effort inserted before the printable-char fallthrough. Run-now contends with the shared session — it goes through the B0 gate + B5 queue like a scheduled run.

- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/LauncherCommandHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/IBookmarkService.cs
- scripts/termtest.py
