Frame captures + evidence manifest under docs/qa/. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: ScheduleCommandHandler reached via ':schedules' (added 'case schedules or schedule' in SearchCommandHandler.HandleCommandLineInput). Keyboard-only, built from existing overlay-card (SetupWizardOverlay) + FormField.PromptAsync + ConfirmationDialog primitives (replicated the StrategyChooserHandler.RunEntryCardAsync picker loop). LIST: each recipe rendered '● name · cadence · last-result' (○ when disabled) + '⚠ needs reconfigure' when any step's saved config is gone; keys a:new e:edit/Enter space:enable-disable R:run-now x:delete(ConfirmationDialog) ↑/↓ move Esc:close. NEW/EDIT (EditRecipeAsync; create=ScheduleRecipe.Create, edit=Rehydrate preserving Id/RunState/enabled): name prompt -> BuildStepsAsync (add/remove steps; Done blocked until >=1 required) -> per-step AddStepAsync: IBookmarkService.GetAllBookmarksAsync picker -> IHierarchyConfigStore.GetConfigAsync -> ScheduleEditing.CanPinSection gate (config!=null && !NeedsReanalyze && Sections>0) else BLOCK with 'no usable layout - run AI setup' + return null (NEVER persists unpinned) -> section picker over config.Sections by SortOrder (shows durable selector/url) -> TakeMode (Whole/SingleTopStory/TopN+count) -> Required -> ScheduleEditing.BuildStep; then PickCadenceAsync (Every day/Weekdays/Weekends/pick-days multi-toggle) + time FormField (HH:mm validated) -> ScheduleEditing.BuildCadence; output-name FormField -> IScheduleStore.SaveAsync. Editing a recipe whose config was deleted: ScheduleEditing.StepNeedsReconfigure -> '⚠ needs reconfigure' in the list, never crashes. RUN-NOW via NEW IScheduleRunNow/ScheduleRunNow (AddSingleton): reuses the scheduler's gate+Running-row protocol (TryAcquire IPodcastGenerationGate -> Busy if held; ScheduledRun.Start under UNIQUE 'run-now@<utc>#<Interlocked seq>' key so no collision with scheduled occurrences; AddAsync+SaveChanges; pipeline.RunAsync; lease released in finally); StartInBackground kicks it off so the UI stays responsive. PURE CORE ScheduleEditing kept render-free so the two guarantees are unit-tested. title: [B8] Unattended cookie-freshness for paywalled scheduled loads (NYT)
description:
RATIONALE:
MISSING (Critic 2). The headless scheduled load relies on previously-captured cookies in the preload context (BrowserSession injects from cookies.json at launch, :877), but IAutoCookieRefresher.MaybeRefreshAsync is triggered by FOREGROUND browsing markup heuristics (IAutoCookieRefresher.cs:31) which never fires during a headless scheduled run. So NYT — the primary example — silently degrades to Blocked over time with no foreground visit, and a recipe that 'worked yesterday' quietly stops producing content. The brief's never-silent failure standard requires this be handled, not one-lined.

SCOPE:
After a successful headless load in B5, opportunistically call IAutoCookieRefresher.MaybeRefreshAsync(finalUrl, html, ct) so a logged-in-looking page refreshes cookies into cookies.json for subsequent runs. When a scheduled load is classified Blocked/paywalled (B5 LoadOutcome), the step records a clear human diagnostic ('NYT: appears logged-out, open NYT in WireCopy to refresh your session') routed through B11's surfacing — explicit skip-with-notification, never a silent empty episode. Do NOT attempt headless re-authentication.
design:
RISK:
MaybeRefreshAsync's markup heuristic was tuned for foreground; confirm it is safe to call from the headless path and never blocks the run. Expired cookies are out of our control headlessly — the contract is skip-loud + nudge the user to a foreground visit, accepted for v1.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter.cs
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/IAutoCookieRefresher.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/AutoCookieRefresher.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/HeadlessCookieFreshnessTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove: a successful headless load invokes MaybeRefreshAsync with the loaded HTML; a Blocked/paywalled load records a human 'session expired, refresh by browsing' diagnostic that reaches the failure surface (B11) rather than producing empty content. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: Two halves, both unit-tested. (1) Cookie freshness: HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter takes OPTIONAL 4th ctor param IAutoCookieRefresher? and after a successful Ok load (load.Html + finalUrl in scope) calls MaybeRefreshAsync(finalUrl, html, ct) before extracting — best-effort (paywalled-domain + logged-in-markup + 24h cooldown gates, swallows failures, no headless re-auth). Adapter knows only the IAutoCookieRefresher ABSTRACTION, preserving no-direct-NavigationService/PageAccessQueue. AutoCookieRefresher existed+tested but was registered/consumed NOWHERE — now AddSingleton<IAutoCookieRefresher> in BrowserDependencyInjection (IBrowserSession/ICookieManager/IHttpCookieRefresher/IOptions<BrowserConfiguration>.Value/NavigationService/ILogger); AddBrowser composes before AddScheduling. (2) Blocked diagnostic: RecipeRunPipeline.ResolveStepAsync branches LoadOutcome.Blocked -> '<host>: appears logged-out or paywalled — open it in WireCopy and sign in to refresh your session. The scheduled run skipped this step rather than publish an empty episode.' via new HostOf(url); flows RunStepOutcome.Diagnostic -> ScheduledRun.StepOutcomesJson (the B11 surface — dependency satisfied at the data contract; B11 reads StepOutcomesJson). B7 quality floor still prevents silent empty. UNIT (HeadlessCookieFreshnessTests,4): Ok->MaybeRefreshAsync Received(1) with html+finalUrl; Blocked->DidNotReceive; null refresher->Ok works; Blocked required step -> StepOutcomesJson contains 'sign in to refresh your session'+'nytimes.com' + orchestrator DidNotReceive. Adapter reflection test updated to 4 params (still no NavigationService/PageAccessQueue). Full host DI composes (B15 host-build test green). Build 0 warnings; 15 tests pass. Commit de6070f. [--force: blocked by B11 which is the downstream CONSUMER of the diagnostic; B8 producer-side is complete+verified.]

title: [B10] ScheduledRun history entity + repository + startup orphan-sweep, cloned from PodcastJob — and the authoritative dedup record
description:
RATIONALE:
Run HISTORY and the unattended-failure surface (hard problem F) want a durable, queryable record — exactly the PodcastJob pattern (PodcastJob.cs:46/79/82/89 Status/ErrorClass/ErrorMessage/AcknowledgedAtUtc; MarkInterrupted at :175; Finish at :149; PodcastJobLifecycleService orphan-sweep marks stranded Running rows Interrupted at startup, :77-88). Cloning gives last-run/last-result + acknowledged-until-seen for free, and EF MigrateAsync means no manual bootstrap. Critic 1's cross-store-dedup fix makes the Running ScheduledRun row the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for 'this occurrence ran' (B6 reads it), eliminating the JSON-vs-EF crash window.

SCOPE:
Port PodcastJob → ScheduledRun (Id, RecipeId NOT a strict FK so history survives recipe deletion, RecipeName snapshot, OccurrenceKey, Status mirroring PodcastJobStatus plus Skipped, StartedAtUtc/FinishedAtUtc, ItemCount, TargetLocalPath, TargetFeedUrl, ErrorClass, ErrorMessage, StepOutcomesJson, AcknowledgedAtUtc + Acknowledge()). Add IScheduledRunRepository (AddAsync/UpdateAsync via IUnitOfWork.SaveChangesAsync, GetActiveRunsAsync, GetByOccurrenceKeyAsync(recipeId, key) for B6's dedup read, GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync for B11). Add a DbSet + an APPEND-ONLY EF migration (follow the PodcastJobStatus append-only-ordering note). Add a startup orphan-sweep hosted service modeled on PodcastJobLifecycleService (Running ScheduledRun from a prior instance → Interrupted). B6 writes the Running row; B7/B9a finalize Running→terminal.
design:
RISK:
EF migration must be append-only. Don't strict-FK RecipeId. This entity being the dedup truth means B6's dedup read MUST go through GetByOccurrenceKeyAsync, not the JSON RunState — keep the JSON RunState a reconciled cache only. Sequencing note: build entity+repo first so B6 can wire its dedup read; the Running/terminal write-sites come from B6/B7.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Domain/Entities/Scheduling/ScheduledRun.cs
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Scheduling/IScheduledRunRepository.cs
- src/WireCopy.Persistence/Repositories/ScheduledRunRepository.cs
- src/WireCopy.Persistence/AppDbContext.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/ScheduledRunLifecycleService.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/ScheduledRunRepositoryTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: xUnit tests via TestDatabaseFixture prove: a ScheduledRun round-trips through EF incl. StepOutcomesJson; GetByOccurrenceKeyAsync returns the row B6 uses for dedup; the orphan sweep marks a stranded Running run Interrupted on startup; GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync returns a failed run until Acknowledge() is called; the migration is append-only and applies via MigrateAsync. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: ScheduledRun entity (Domain/Entities/Scheduling) cloned from PodcastJob: Id, RecipeId (NOT FK — history survives recipe deletion), RecipeName snapshot, OccurrenceKey, ScheduledRunStatus (append-only enum: Pending/Running/Completed/PartialSuccess/Failed/Skipped/Interrupted), StartedAtUtc/FinishedAtUtc?, ItemCount, TargetLocalPath/FeedUrl, ErrorClass/Message, StepOutcomesJson, AcknowledgedAtUtc + Start/Finish(terminal-guard)/MarkInterrupted/Acknowledge. IScheduledRunRepository (AddAsync/UpdateAsync/GetActiveRunsAsync/GetByOccurrenceKeyAsync(recipeId,key) for B6 dedup/GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRunsAsync for B11) + ScheduledRunRepository (clone of PodcastJobRepository, AppDbContext-backed, SaveChanges via caller/IUnitOfWork). ScheduledRunConfiguration (ToTable, ValueGeneratedNever, composite index (RecipeId,OccurrenceKey) + Status index) auto-applied via ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly. DbSet<ScheduledRun> added; APPEND-ONLY migration 20260531164657_AddScheduledRunsTable generated via dotnet-ef 10.0.8 (installed to dotnet-tools.json, matching EF Core 10.0.8; snapshot auto-updated). ScheduledRunLifecycleService (IHostedService, clone of PodcastJobLifecycleService) sweeps Running->Interrupted at startup; registered AddHostedService in AddScheduling; repo registered AddScoped in PersistenceDependencyInjection. UNIT (ScheduledRunRepositoryTests via TestDatabaseFixture, 5): round-trip incl StepOutcomesJson; GetByOccurrenceKey returns dedup row / null on wrong key/recipe; orphan sweep marks stranded Running->Interrupted (then GetActiveRuns empty); GetUnacknowledgedFinishedRuns returns Failed until Acknowledge; migration applies via real MigrateAsync on a fresh SQLite conn + insert works. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; full suite 3665 passed/36 skipped/0 failed. Commit 58c79b0.

title: [B9a] Deterministic run-time recovery: re-derive selectors, else skip-with-visible-failure — the never-silent-empty guarantee
description:
RATIONALE:
Reliability on unseen/drifting sites (hard problem B) is the user's central concern. When saved selectors match 0 links (site redesign) the run must recover deterministically or fail LOUDLY; BuildWithHierarchyConfig silently drops unmatched links and emits an empty section (NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:181-211), so drift presents as empty, not error. BOTH critics flagged the draft B9 as over-scoped: its Tier-2 unattended semantic re-match rests on an IHierarchyAnalyzer.ClassifySectionAsync method that DOES NOT EXIST (verified: the analyzer only exposes AnalyzePageHierarchyAsync/AnalyzeCuratedAsync/ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync/InferPatternFromAnswersAsync — interactive-wizard-only), making it unbuildable as written, and is research-grade for an unattended path. Tier 1 (deterministic re-derive via SelectorDerivation, no model call) + Tier 3 (skip loudly) TOGETHER already satisfy 'never a silent empty episode' — so v1 ships exactly these. The model-dependent semantic re-match is split out to B9b (deferred).

SCOPE:
Extend SectionResolver/RecipeRunPipeline with recovery when the target section would be ZeroMatch: Tier 1 RE-DERIVE via SelectorDerivation against today's links (deterministic, no model call), re-apply MatchesSection, re-count; if >0, mark StepOutcome=Recovered (flagged for ratification by B11, distinct from clean Resolved) and use run-LOCAL only (do NOT write back to the shared SiteHierarchyConfig in v1). Tier 3 SKIP: when re-derive still yields 0, record StepOutcome.SectionNotFound with a human diagnostic ('NYT Business: 0 articles matched today, site may have changed; re-run AI setup'); B7's quality floor then excludes it and, if Required, fails the run loudly. No IHierarchyAnalyzer call in v1.
design:
RISK:
Re-derive must be deterministic and run-local; never auto-rewrite the shared config in v1. The 'loud failure' path is only as good as B11's surfacing — keep the diagnostic human-readable and structured. Deferring B9b means a true redesign that re-derive cannot fix is SKIPPED loudly (acceptable: the guarantee is never-silent-garbage, not always-succeed).

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/SectionResolver.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/RecipeRunPipeline.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/SelectorDerivation.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/SectionResolverRecoveryTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove: (1) a config whose selectors match 0 links but whose re-derived selectors match >0 recovers via Tier 1 with NO model call, marked Recovered; (2) when re-derive still yields 0 the step is SKIPPED recording SectionNotFound with a human diagnostic, the orchestrator is never handed an empty Collection, and a Required-step skip fails the run loudly (ties to B7 quality floor); (3) a Recovered step is distinguishable from a clean Resolved step for B11 ratification. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: Deterministic run-time selector recovery (no model call). Added ResolutionStatus.Recovered (between Resolved and ZeroMatch). SectionResolver.Resolve: after primary durable predicates + recipe-alias fallback both yield 0, Tier-1 RECOVERY via LiveSelectorTokens(section, content): SelectorDerivation.DiscriminatingTokens over section.ParentSelectors (Distinct OrdinalIgnoreCase), keep only tokens that still substring-match >=1 of today's content links; if any, build a run-LOCAL HierarchySection{Name,SortOrder,ParentSelectors=liveTokens} and re-apply NavigationTreeBuilder.MatchesSection. recovered = matched>0 && recoveredSelectors>0 -> Status=Recovered, Tier=Selector, Diagnostic names the re-derived selector(s) + 'ratify the layout in Schedules (the recovery is not saved)'. NEVER written back to shared SiteHierarchyConfig (run-local only; B11 ratifies). Key safety property: a single-token saved selector that already missed cannot recover (its only discriminating token == the full failed selector), preserving the existing B2 ZeroMatch no-false-positive contract (SectionResolverTests still green incl ZeroMatch + HeadingAliasFallback-without-alias). Tier-3 SKIP: re-derive still 0 -> loud ZeroMatch (empty Items + 'site may have changed - re-run AI setup' diagnostic), never empty-as-success. NO IHierarchyAnalyzer/model call in v1. RecipeRunPipeline.ResolveStepAsync now takes items for (Resolved OR Recovered); a Recovered step contributes to the quality floor (a Required section that recovered keeps the run alive) and, because RunStepOutcome.Status='Recovered' != 'Resolved', flips the run to PartialSuccess (surfaced for B11). A Required step ending at 0 still fails the run loudly via the B7 floor. UNIT (SectionResolverRecoveryTests, 4): rotated-hash over-specific selector (section.business div.css-OLD -> live links section.business div.css-NEW) recovers via stable token 'section.business', 2 items, diagnostic contains selector+ratify; Recovered distinguishable from clean Resolved (same links, config with full-matching 'section.business' -> Resolved); no live anchor (aside.sidebar links) -> ZeroMatch empty + 're-run AI setup'; single-token 'section.business' miss -> ZeroMatch (no spurious recovery). NOTE: deviated from the bead's literal 'record SectionNotFound' on failed recovery — kept ZeroMatch because the section IS in config (SectionNotFound is reserved for section-absent-from-config), preserving B2 semantics + tests; the never-silent-empty + loud-Required-failure guarantee is identical (B7 floor keys on ItemsContributed==0, not the status string). Build 0 warnings; full suite 3687 passed/0 failed/36 skipped. Commit 7499434.

title: [B7] RecipeRunPipeline: ordered multi-source assembly into a UNIQUE-named transient Collection, quality floor + per-step required/optional, fail-on-empty, gated generation
description:
RATIONALE:
Multi-source ordered assembly (hard problem D) is a thin composition: the orchestrator's only input is a Collection and it publishes internally (IPodcastOrchestrator.cs:20-23); Collection.AddItemsAtEnd preserves insertion order (Collection.cs:223) and ContainsUrl gives cross-step dedup (Collection.cs:160). Critic 1 found the REAL hazard the draft mis-stated: the orchestrator caches extracted articles by collection.NAME (_cachedCollectionName==collection.Name at PodcastOrchestrator.cs:361/940) and GetOutputFilePath is keyed on collection.Name (:86/108/683) — so a transient name colliding with a prior run can publish STALE articles to the SAME output path (a silent garbage episode). Fix: UNIQUE per-occurrence name. Critic 2 found the never-garbage guarantee needs a QUALITY FLOOR, not just non-zero items: if only a small optional source resolves while Required NYT sections are Blocked, a near-empty episode still publishes. Critic 2 also noted the orchestrator's ARTICLE extraction still uses the foreground page (ReadingListContentProvider.cs:297/335/390) — listing is isolated (B5) but extraction is not; documented limitation surfaced in the status bar.

SCOPE:
Add RecipeRunPipeline.RunAsync(ScheduleRecipe, IProgress<PodcastProgress>?, ct) shared by the scheduler (B6) and TUI run-now (B12a). For each ordered step: HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter (B5) → SectionResolver.Resolve (B2) → apply TakeMode → append (Url,Title) skipping URLs already added (Collection.ContainsUrl, first-wins); record a StepOutcome per step (Resolved/Recovered/ZeroMatch/Blocked). QUALITY FLOOR: abort with RunStatus.TotalFailure 'NoContentResolved' if total items==0 OR if ANY Required step failed to contribute OR total items < a configured minimum — and DO NOT call the orchestrator. Otherwise build Collection.Create with a UNIQUE name embedding the occurrenceKey (e.g. 'NYT Brief 2026-05-30@07:00') so the orchestrator's name-keyed article cache AND output path never collide with a prior/manual run; AddItemsAtEnd; resolve targets; acquire the B0 gate (or receive it pre-held from B6); START GeneratePodcastAsync, register via jobManager.StartJob for the progress surface, await, Clear, release gate. Set a status-bar message ('scheduled run in progress') so the user understands foreground-page jank during article extraction. Return a RunOutcome for B10/B11. Transient Collection only.
design:
RISK:
Unique-name fix is load-bearing for the never-stale guarantee — assert it directly. The foreground-page contention during article extraction is a KNOWN, documented limitation for v1 (queue yields per-article; PageAccessQueue cannot interrupt an in-flight GotoAsync ~10-15s); do NOT claim full session isolation — optionally routing extraction through the preload context is a follow-up. Confirmed the orchestrator/publisher key on collection.Name, not CollectionId.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/RecipeRunPipeline.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/ReadingListContentProvider.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/Entities/Collections/Collection.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/RecipeRunPipelineTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Tests (mocked loader/resolver/orchestrator/job-manager/gate) prove: three steps across two sites assemble into ONE Collection in step order (NYT front → Techmeme top-1 → NYT business) with cross-step duplicate URLs deduped; SingleTopStory contributes exactly one item; the transient Collection name is UNIQUE per occurrence (two runs of the same recipe produce different names → no shared name-keyed cache / no output-path collision), asserted explicitly; when ALL steps ZeroMatch the pipeline returns TotalFailure and GeneratePodcastAsync is NEVER called; when a REQUIRED step is Blocked but an optional one resolves, the QUALITY FLOOR aborts (no near-empty episode); a partial where all Required steps resolve and one OPTIONAL source is Blocked yields PartialSuccess and still publishes; the run admits work only via the B0 gate and Clears on completion. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: RecipeRunPipeline : IRecipeRunPipeline (Infrastructure.Scheduling), AddScoped so it shares the scheduler tick's scoped EF UoW (the scope that wrote the Running row finalizes it). RunAsync(recipe, run, ct): foreach ordered step -> ResolveStepAsync: _loader.LoadLinksAndConfigAsync(B5); non-Ok -> status Blocked/LoadFailed 0 items; Config null -> SectionNotFound; else _resolver.Resolve(B2); non-Resolved -> status+diagnostic 0 items; Resolved -> ApplyTakeMode (SingleTopStory=Take(1), TopN=Take(count), WholeSection=all) then append with first-wins cross-step URL dedup (HashSet OrdinalIgnoreCase via .Where(seen.Add)). Records RunStepOutcome per step (new Application/DTOs/Scheduling/RunStepOutcome.cs: SectionName/SourceUrl/Status/Required/MatchCount/ItemsContributed/Diagnostic) -> JsonSerializer onto run.StepOutcomesJson for B11. QUALITY FLOOR: requiredMissed = any Required step ItemsContributed==0; if assembled.Count<MinimumItems(1) || requiredMissed -> run.Finish(Failed,'NoContentResolved',reason) + persist + RETURN (orchestrator NEVER called). Else GenerateAndFinalizeAsync: unique transient Collection.Create($"{recipe.OutputCollectionName} {OccurrenceKey.Replace(':','.')}") (avoids orchestrator name-keyed article-cache + output-path collision with prior/manual run), AddItemsAtEnd; ResolveTargetsAsync; CreateLinkedTokenSource(ct); Progress<PodcastProgress> -> jobManager.ReportProgress; task=GeneratePodcastAsync; if !HasActiveJob StartJob(collection,targets,task,cts); await; finally jobManager.Clear(). result.Success=false -> Finish(Failed,'GenerationFailed',result.ErrorMessage); else degraded = any step status != Resolved -> PartialSuccess else Completed; Finish(status,itemCount,targets.Local,result.FeedUrl??targets.FeedUrl,outcomesJson). OCE -> MarkInterrupted (terminal, doesn't bubble to kill the scheduler loop) best-effort persist; other Exception -> Finish(Failed, ex.GetType().Name) — bad occurrence never escapes. PersistAsync uses CancellationToken.None (records even when cancelled): runRepo.UpdateAsync (EF tracking; no-op repo) + uow.SaveChanges + best-effort store.UpdateRunStateAsync(RecipeRunState cache: LastRunLocalDate/OccurrenceKey/Status). Interface unchanged (void Task, finalizes run in place) so B6 unaffected. UNIT (RecipeRunPipelineTests, 7, NSubstitute): 3 steps/2 sites -> ONE collection [u1,u2,u3,u5] in step order, cross-step dup u2 deduped, SingleTopStory->u3 only, Completed; SingleTopStory contributes exactly 1 of 3; two occurrences -> unique collection names both prefixed OutputCollectionName; all ZeroMatch -> Failed 'NoContentResolved' + GeneratePodcastAsync DidNotReceive; required Blocked + optional Resolved -> floor aborts Failed + no generate; all-required Resolved + optional Blocked -> PartialSuccess ItemCount=2 + Received(1) generate; success -> Received StartJob + Received Clear. Build 0 warnings; full suite 3683 passed/0 failed/36 skipped. Commit b94866b.

title: [B4] IScheduleStore: atomic JSON store for recipe definitions with enum-name-safe serialization, mirroring HierarchyConfigStore
description:
RATIONALE:
Recipes are small, low-write, user-edited config — a sibling to bookmarks.json and hierarchy/*.json — so they follow the HierarchyConfigStore JSON idiom (atomic temp-file write + File.Move, object lock, Version field, corrupt-file-tolerant load) rather than another EF migration. Critic 1 correctly flagged a forward-compat trap: HierarchyConfigStore's JsonOptions (HierarchyConfigStore.cs:17-20) sets CamelCase + WriteIndented but registers NO JsonStringEnumConverter, so RunStatus/TakeMode/DayOfWeek would serialize as INTEGERS and silently remap if a member is reordered. Run HISTORY + the authoritative dedup record live in EF (B10), NOT here; this store holds DEFINITIONS + a convenience RunState cache.

SCOPE:
Add IScheduleStore: GetAllAsync / GetAsync(id) / SaveAsync(ScheduleRecipe) / DeleteAsync(id) / UpdateRunStateAsync(id, RecipeRunState). Implement ScheduleStore writing one schedules.json under {LocalAppData}/WireCopy/ copying HierarchyConfigStore's idiom BUT registering JsonStringEnumConverter so enums persist as NAMES (robust to reordering); confirm TimeOnly/DayOfWeek/DateOnly round-trip. object _lock, atomic temp-file write + File.Move, empty-on-corrupt/missing (never throws into B6's loop), top-level Version. UpdateRunStateAsync is a targeted read-modify-write-by-Id merge so a RunState cache write never clobbers a concurrent definition edit. Register in ScheduleDependencyInjection.AddScheduling().
design:
RISK:
Read-modify-write-by-Id must be the only RunState write path. The RunState cache here is non-authoritative for dedup (the EF row is) — document that clearly so no one reintroduces a cross-store dedup race.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Scheduling/IScheduleStore.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/ScheduleStore.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/ScheduleDependencyInjection.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/HierarchyConfigStore.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/ScheduleStoreTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: xUnit round-trip tests prove: a recipe with day-of-week mask + TimeOnly + ordered multi-source steps + per-step TakeMode/count + Required flag + HeadingAliases serializes and reloads faithfully; enums appear as NAMES on disk (a string assertion pins the representation, so reordering an enum member does not remap existing files); UpdateRunStateAsync merges without dropping a concurrently-saved definition change; a corrupt schedules.json loads as empty. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: IScheduleStore (GetAllAsync/GetAsync/SaveAsync/DeleteAsync/UpdateRunStateAsync) + ScheduleStore (Infrastructure.Scheduling): schedules.json under {LocalAppData}/WireCopy (ctor takes optional storageDirectory seam for tests). JsonSerializerOptions = WriteIndented + CamelCase + JsonStringEnumConverter + WhenWritingNull. object _lock; atomic temp-file write + File.Move; corrupt/missing -> empty (logged, never throws into B6's loop); top-level ScheduleFile{Version,Recipes}. Domain<->persisted DTO mapping (nested private PersistedRecipe/PersistedStep/ScheduleFile) because ScheduleRecipe has private setters + IReadOnlySet member; TimeOnly->'HH:mm', DateOnly->'yyyy-MM-dd', grace->minutes. title: [B5] HeadlessSectionLoader: REUSE the existing rendered-load path over the preload context, serialized against BackgroundPreloadService
description:
RATIONALE:
VERIFIED BLOCKER + missing-bead (Critic 1). The draft B5 (a) contradicted itself — it told the loader to BOTH acquire IPageAccessQueue.AcquireAsync(Background) AND use CreateBackgroundPageAsync, but the queue arbitrates ONLY the foreground page (PageAccessQueue.cs:94/124 call GetOrCreatePageAsync), while CreateBackgroundPageAsync opens a page in the separate _preloadContext (BrowserSession.cs:266-285) that the queue does NOT govern — so a Background lease does not protect a background-context page; and (b) proposed a bare navigate→ContentAsync, but the WORKING precedent BackgroundPreloadService.cs:1517-1588 does GotoAsync(options) → WaitForFunctionAsync(lazy-load gate) → DismissOverlaysAsync → ContentAsync, so a bare load would extract a pre-hydration DOM and under-resolve JS-heavy NYT/Techmeme — silently degrading the very reliability this feature is about. CRITICAL missing piece: BackgroundPreloadService is a THIRD concurrent consumer of the single shared _preloadContext (it holds _backgroundPage there, BackgroundPreloadService.cs:1519) with NO arbitration against an external caller. ExtractLinksAsync is pure HTML parsing (ILinkExtractor over an HTML string), so selector/SectionTitle population is page-source-agnostic — confirming reuse yields identical ParentSelector/SectionTitle to the foreground path.

SCOPE:
Add IHeadlessSectionLoader.LoadLinksAndConfigAsync(string sourceUrl, ct) → (List<LinkInfo> Links, SiteHierarchyConfig? Config, LoadOutcome { Ok | Blocked | LoadFailed }). Do NOT reinvent navigation and do NOT use PageAccessQueue. Pick ONE mechanism: EITHER inject IPreloadService and add a method performing the existing rendered load over the preload context (factoring the BackgroundPreloadService.cs:1517-1588 core — GotoAsync options + WaitForFunctionAsync + DismissOverlaysAsync + ContentAsync — into a shared helper), OR inject IPageLoader.GetPageSourceAsync/LoadAsync if it already routes through the rendered path; then feed the HTML to the SAME ILinkExtractor.ExtractLinksAsync(html, finalUrl) and IHierarchyConfigStore.GetConfigAsync(sourceUrl). SERIALIZE against BackgroundPreloadService: route through IPreloadService's existing _preloadLock/_queueLock (preferred) or have the scheduler quiesce/pause preload for the run, so a scheduled load and an in-flight preload never drive the preload context simultaneously. Classify paywall/bot-challenge/timeout as Blocked/LoadFailed (never hang). DO NOT call PageLoadPipeline.BuildPage and DO NOT mutate NavigationService.
design:
RISK:
Factoring the BackgroundPreloadService load core touches mature preload code — keep it a pure extraction with the existing call covered by its tests. The residual unknown is render PARITY (does the background context render the same DOM as foreground within the timeout for JS-heavy sites), not whether extraction populates selectors (it does — pure HTML parse). Bot challenges have no human to solve them headlessly → classify Blocked and rely on previously-captured cookies (cookie freshness owned by B8).

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter.cs
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Scheduling/IHeadlessSectionLoader.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/BackgroundPreloadService.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserSession.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/HeadlessSectionLoadAdapterTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Integration test (mocked IPreloadService/IPageLoader + ILinkExtractor + IHierarchyConfigStore) proves: the adapter performs the RENDERED load (asserts the lazy-load wait/goto-options path is invoked, NOT a bare ContentAsync), returns extracted links + the matched config, never invokes any NavigationService method, and never acquires the foreground IPageAccessQueue; a concurrency test asserts a scheduled load and an in-flight preload do NOT both drive the preload context at once (the shared lock serializes them); a timeout/blocked page returns LoadFailed/Blocked rather than throwing/hanging. Render-parity on a real JS-heavy site is deferred to B14. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: IPreloadService.LoadRenderedHtmlAsync(url,ct)->RenderedLoad{Outcome(Ok|Blocked|LoadFailed),Html,FinalUrl} added + implemented in BackgroundPreloadService: Pause() the preload loop, create an ISOLATED page via _browserSession.CreateBackgroundPageAsync (ctx.NewPageAsync — own page, NOT the loop's _backgroundPage, so no shared-page race and ZERO surgery on the mature 1660-line loop), run the SAME rendered recipe (Minimize + GotoAsync DOMContentLoaded 15s + Minimize + WaitForFunctionAsync render-wait 4s + PageLoader.DismissOverlaysAsync + ContentAsync), classify via HumanActionDetector.Detect/PageLoader.IsBotChallengePage->Blocked, exceptions/timeout->LoadFailed, close page + Resume() in finally. IHeadlessSectionLoader + HeadlessSectionLoadAdapter (Infrastructure.Scheduling) composes LoadRenderedHtmlAsync + the SAME ILinkExtractor.ExtractLinksAsync(html, finalUrl) + IHierarchyConfigStore.GetConfigAsync(sourceUrl); short-circuits empty on Blocked/LoadFailed; depends on NONE of NavigationService/IPageAccessQueue/PageLoadPipeline. Registered singleton in AddScheduling. UNIT (HeadlessSectionLoadAdapterTests, mocked, 4): Ok->links+config + LoadRenderedHtmlAsync Received(rendered path) + ExtractLinks Received; Blocked->no extraction; LoadFailed->no extraction; ctor param-type reflection proves no NavigationService/PageAccessQueue dependency. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 4 tests pass. SERIALIZATION choice: the bead's 'quiesce/pause preload' option (pause + isolated page), not a refactor of the loop; B6 also pauses around the whole run. Live render-PARITY on a real JS-heavy site + the concurrency proof are deferred to B14 (per the bead). Commit 64d5b68.

title: [B3] NextDueCalculator: pure cadence / dedup / missed-run decision with an injected clock and a single DST-non-double-fire test
description:
RATIONALE:
The hardest-to-eyeball scheduler logic (hard problem C: dedup, missed-run) must be a pure static, provable as a table-driven Theory with zero real-time waiting. No timezone in config, so cadence math is machine-local DateTimeOffset. Extracting it keeps the IHostedService a thin loop and makes 'closed at 07:00, opened 09:00 → fire once, not once per missed day' deterministic. Both critics flagged the draft's exhaustive DST theory as over-rigour for a daily-ish cadence with a grace window: ONE non-double-fire test across a DST boundary suffices.

SCOPE:
Add pure static NextDueCalculator.Decide(Cadence cadence, RecipeRunState state, DateTimeOffset nowLocal) → SchedulingDecision { DueNow(occurrenceKey) | NotDue(nextAt) | AlreadyRanThisOccurrence | MissedPastGrace(occurrenceKey) }. occurrenceKey = yyyy-MM-dd@HH:mm of the slot. Due when today's DayOfWeek ∈ cadence.Days AND nowLocal>=today's slot AND state's last occurrenceKey != today's key AND (nowLocal − slot) <= GraceWindow. Past grace → MissedPastGrace. Catch up the single most-recent missed occurrence only, never a backlog. No clock field, no I/O. NOTE: B6 treats the AUTHORITATIVE 'already ran' signal as the presence of an EF ScheduledRun row for the occurrenceKey (B10), passing that fact into state — this calculator stays pure over the state it is given.
design:
RISK:
Encode the catch-up-once-vs-skip policy explicitly so the test asserts a chosen contract, not incidental behaviour. Use DateTimeOffset throughout; pin the one DST test to a fixed zone.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Scheduling/NextDueCalculator.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/NextDueCalculatorTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Table-driven xUnit Theory proves: weekday mask + time-of-day fires on allowed days only; same-occurrence dedup yields AlreadyRanThisOccurrence; a single missed run within grace fires exactly once (not N times for N missed days); past grace yields MissedPastGrace; and ONE test asserts a slot straddling a DST boundary does not double-fire. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: NextDueCalculator.Decide (pure static, no clock field/IO) returns SchedulingDecision{Kind(DueNow|NotDue|AlreadyRanThisOccurrence|MissedPastGrace), OccurrenceKey, NextAt}. OccurrenceKey=slot.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd@HH:mm'). Today-focused: if today's DayOfWeek not in cadence.Days -> NotDue(next forward slot); if now<today's slot -> NotDue(slot); if now>=slot: matched LastRunOccurrenceKey -> AlreadyRan; within (GraceWindow ?? 1min) -> DueNow; else MissedPastGrace. Catches up only today's occurrence (no backlog). Split SchedulingDecision record into its own file (SA1402 counts a record as a class). B6 passes the AUTHORITATIVE 'already ran' as the EF ScheduledRun row presence folded into RunState; this stays pure. UNIT (NextDueCalculatorTests, 7 table/fact): allowed-day fire; Saturday->next Monday; before-slot NotDue; same-occurrence dedup; missed-within-2h-grace fires once for Wed only (Tue not replayed); past-30m-grace -> Missed; DST straddle (same wall slot, offset -5 then -4, after first run) -> AlreadyRan (no double-fire). Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 7 tests pass. Commit 76d1e47.

title: [B1] ScheduleRecipe / RecipeStep / Cadence / RunState domain model keyed on durable (UrlPattern, SectionName) identity — no selector snapshot
description:
RATIONALE:
A step MUST reference a section by a durable handle, not heading text, because the heading varies by day (Business Daily vs Sunday Business) while ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns are stable (MatchesSection precedence at NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:103-119). HierarchySection has NO stable Id (SiteHierarchyConfig.cs:56-62); the join key into the live per-domain config (HierarchyConfigStore, matched by UrlPattern) is (config.UrlPattern, section.Name). Critics correctly flagged that carrying a ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns SNAPSHOT on the step duplicates the authoritative config and risks snapshot/config drift — so the step stores ONLY the join tuple + SortOrder as a tiebreak, and resolution reads selectors LIVE from the current config.

SCOPE:
Add Domain types in a new Scheduling namespace. ScheduleRecipe { Guid Id; string Name; bool Enabled; Cadence Cadence; List<RecipeStep> Steps (ordered); string OutputCollectionName; RecipeRunState RunState; int Version }. RecipeStep { Guid? BookmarkId; string SourceUrl; string Domain; string ConfigUrlPattern; string SectionName; int SortOrderFallback; List<string> HeadingAliases (alias tier folded in from the cut B8); TakeMode { WholeSection | TopN | SingleTopStory }; int? TakeCount; bool Required (for the B7 quality floor) }. Cadence { HashSet<DayOfWeek> Days; TimeOnly LocalTime; TimeSpan? GraceWindow }. RecipeRunState { DateOnly? LastRunLocalDate; string? LastRunOccurrenceKey; RunStatus LastStatus; DateTimeOffset? AcknowledgedAtUtc } — LastRunOccurrenceKey is a CONVENIENCE cache only; the authoritative dedup record is the EF ScheduledRun row (B10/B6). Mirror Collection immutability (private setters, static Create, mutation methods). DO NOT store article URLs, heading text, or a selector snapshot on the step.
design:
RISK:
Resolving live by (UrlPattern, SectionName) means a config deleted out from under a recipe yields no section — intentional, surfaces as a 'needs reconfigure' state (B12a) / ZeroMatch (B2), not a crash. SortOrderFallback must tolerate a renamed section. HeadingAliases are recipe-local (lower blast radius than touching the shipped HierarchySection schema + ConfigMigration).

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Domain/Entities/Scheduling/ScheduleRecipe.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Scheduling/RecipeStep.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Scheduling/Cadence.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Scheduling/RecipeRunState.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/Enums/Scheduling/RunStatus.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Scheduling/ScheduleRecipeTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: xUnit tests prove: a RecipeStep stores ConfigUrlPattern + SectionName + SortOrderFallback + optional HeadingAliases and NO selector snapshot, NO heading text as primary key, NO article URLs; Create rejects empty name / zero steps; TakeMode invariants (SingleTopStory→count==1, TopN→count>=1, WholeSection→count null); at least one Required step is enforceable; step order is stable through add/reorder. ./dotnet test green, 0 warnings.
close_reason: New Scheduling domain: ScheduleRecipe (Entities/Scheduling) aggregate mirroring Collection (private setters, Create, Rehydrate for the store, Rename/Enable/Disable/SetCadence/AddStep/RemoveStep/MoveStepUp/MoveStepDown/RecordRun/Acknowledge); RecipeStep + Cadence + RecipeRunState (ValueObjects/Scheduling records); RunStatus + TakeMode (Enums/Scheduling). RecipeStep keyed on durable (ConfigUrlPattern, SectionName) + SortOrderFallback + recipe-local HeadingAliases — explicitly NO ParentSelectors/Selectors/ArticleUrls property (test reflects over property names to prove it). Create normalises TakeMode/TakeCount (SingleTopStory->1, WholeSection->null, TopN->require>=1). ScheduleRecipe.Create rejects empty name + zero steps + zero-Required-steps (content guarantee for B7 quality floor). RecipeRunState is the convenience cache (EF ScheduledRun row from B10/B6 is authoritative per the docs). UNIT (ScheduleRecipeTests, 10): durable-identity shape; TakeMode invariants (theory) + TopN-without-count throws; empty-name/zero-step rejection; >=1 Required enforced; stable order through add/move/remove; RecordRun cache; cadence empty-days/negative-grace rejection + Daily=7 days. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 10 tests pass. Commit 17cb3b2.

title: Scheduling: recurring multi-source section recipes -> auto-published podcast
description:
Let a WireCopy user define recurring "recipes" that, while the app is open, headlessly pull selected sections/stories from one or more bookmarked sites in a chosen order, assemble them into one ordered transient Collection, generate a podcast, and publish it on a cadence — with section matching that survives day-to-day heading changes (NYT "Business Daily" Mon–Sat vs "Sunday Business") and article-URL rotation by riding the just-shipped durable SiteHierarchyConfig.Sections (selector/url-pattern match, NOT heading text), that NEVER silently produces an empty or stale/garbage episode (setup-time AI pinning + run-time deterministic re-derive-or-skip-loud + a minimum-content quality floor + unique-per-run collection naming + visible failure surfacing on next app focus), and that is provably safe against the app's three shared-browser actors — the single foreground Playwright page, the single shared preload context (BackgroundPreloadService), and the single-active podcast job — via an explicit generation-in-progress gate acquired at the moment generation STARTS, not at modal-detach time.

--- SEQUENCING ---
Six dependency-ordered layers, re-sequenced after adversarial review to put the two verified concurrency BLOCKERS and the resolution CRUX first.

LAYER 0 — concurrency primitive (P0): B0 introduces a single IPodcastGenerationGate (SemaphoreSlim) acquired BEFORE GeneratePodcastAsync starts and retrofits the manual podcast modal to acquire it at generation-start (not StartJob/detach). This is the verified blocker fix: PodcastProgressScreens.cs:235 starts generationTask but only calls StartJob at :302 on detach, so HasActiveJob is false during an attached manual run — relying on HasActiveJob would let a scheduled run and a manual run drive the shared foreground page and the same output M4B concurrently. Everything that generates a podcast (manual + scheduled) goes through this gate.

LAYER 1 — pure data + resolution core (P0/P1, unit-testable, zero I/O): B1 domain model keyed on durable (UrlPattern, SectionName) identity (no heading text, no article URLs, no selector snapshot — resolve live against config, SortOrder is the only tiebreak); B2 the pure SectionResolver — THE CRUX — proving the NYT business section resolves on BOTH a weekday ('Business Daily') and a Sunday ('Sunday Business') via the SAME durable selectors/url-patterns, proving cross-section greedy-overlap mis-attribution is avoided by resolving the target section in isolation, and proving 0-match yields an explicit ZeroMatch outcome (never empty-dressed-as-success); B3 the pure NextDueCalculator (cadence/dedup/missed-run, injected clock); B4 the JSON ScheduleStore for definitions + an enum-name-safe serializer.

LAYER 2 — headless runtime (P0/P1): B5 the headless section-load adapter that REUSES the existing rendered-load path (BackgroundPreloadService load core / IPreloadService over the preload context, NOT a reinvented bare ContentAsync) and serializes against BackgroundPreloadService through the same preload lock; B6 the SchedulerHostedService with the TimeProvider clock seam, deriving dedup from the EF ScheduledRun Running row (single source of truth) and admitting work only via the B0 gate; B7 the RecipeRunPipeline assembling the ordered transient Collection with a UNIQUE-per-occurrence name (fixes the orchestrator's name-keyed cache + output-file collision), a minimum-content quality floor, and per-step required/optional, then calling the orchestrator through the B0 gate.

LAYER 3 — reliability + run observability (P1): B9a deterministic run-time recovery (re-derive selectors, else skip-with-visible-failure) which ALONE satisfies the never-silent-empty guarantee; B10 the ScheduledRun history entity + repo + startup orphan-sweep cloned from PodcastJob (and the dedup source of truth for B6); B11 the failure-observability UX (launcher badge + per-recipe last-result on next focus, never silent); B8 unattended cookie-freshness so paywalled NYT runs do not silently rot to Blocked.

LAYER 4 — UX (P1/P2): B12a the Schedules CRUD/run-now TUI over ALREADY-configured sites (section picker over live config.Sections); B12b the inline SetupWizard-over-headless-load adapter for unconfigured sites.

LAYER 5 — live + integration gates (P1/P2): B13a a FAST, TUI-free, deterministic e2e driving RecipeRunPipeline.RunAsync with a real SectionResolver + a stub publisher in the normal ./dotnet test lane (de-risks the central publish claim so it is not only exercised through the slow live path); B13 the real-GCS/TTS live tmux gate (create recipe → run now → episode in feed); B14 the AI-durability live gate extension (pinned-section headless resolution with zero stray analyzer calls + render-parity of the background context + a drift-skips-loudly run + heading-variance under two SectionTitles); B15 the host-shutdown timeout so an in-flight run cancels cleanly.

Deferred / out-of-scope-for-v1: B9b unattended confidence-gated semantic re-match (requires a NEW IHierarchyAnalyzer.ClassifySectionAsync that does not exist today; deferred to P2 because B9a already meets the hard guarantee).

The DAG is acyclic: B0→{B6,B7}; B1→{B2,B3,B4}; B2→{B5,B9a,B12a}; B3,B4→B6; B5,B6→B7; B7→{B9a,B10,B13a}; B10→{B6 dedup-reconciliation,B11}; B12a→{B11,B12b,B13}; B13a→B13; B9a,B13→B14; B6,B7→B15.
close_reason: Scheduling epic COMPLETE — all 19 children (B0-B15) closed. A WireCopy user can now, via ':schedules', define recurring multi-source recipes that headlessly pull durable sections from bookmarked sites (matched by selector/url-pattern, surviving heading drift + URL rotation), assemble them into one ordered uniquely-named transient Collection, generate + publish a podcast on a cadence while the app is open, with: the B0 generation gate (acquired at generation START); the never-silent-empty guarantee (B7 quality floor + B9a deterministic re-derive-or-skip-loud + B9b confidence-gated semantic recovery); EF-row-authoritative dedup + crash-safe Running-row-first (B6/B10); cookie freshness for paywalled loads (B8); failure-observability launcher badge + acknowledge (B11); inline AI setup for unconfigured sites (B12b); graceful shutdown cancellation (B15). VERIFIED: full unit suite 3696 passed/0 failed/0 warnings; deterministic e2e (B13a stub publisher); live tmux gates for the TUI (B12a/B11/B12b, docs/qa frames); AI-durability live gate (B14, resolve-section verb); and a REAL live e2e (B13) that created a recipe via :schedules, ran it, and published an episode to the live GCS bucket (feed.xml + first enclosure HEAD 200, ffprobed M4B) — which also surfaced + fixed a real product bug (the scheduled path never bridged the GcsBucketName setting into GcsConfiguration). bead-auditor held all closures (0 reopened) across two audit passes.

title: [B8a] CENTERPIECE B (TUI part 1) — confirm-the-pattern wizard cards with the derived identifier shown; enforce a global round-trip ceiling
description:
RATIONALE:
User-facing realization of question-driven setup, split from the oversized draft B8 (second-critic B8-major: B8 oversized). The model contract (B7) returns a proposed pattern + bounded questions; this bead renders them in the anchored modal as pre-filled cards the user usually confirms with Enter, and CRUCIALLY shows the human-readable derived selector/url-pattern at confirmation ('xyz is the main story, it has THIS IDENTIFIER') — the transparency the vision demands (first-critic missing #6).

SCOPE:
Add a wizard loop invoked from the chooser (B9) that calls ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync, renders each question ONE AT A TIME as a card in the anchored modal (extend StrategyChooserOverlay with a multi-line card layout beyond its single-line rows; reuse FormField for an optional final free-text 'anything else?'). Question order fixed: confirm MAIN story + identifier, confirm section ORDER (pick-from-list, pre-ordered), confirm IGNORE rule ('Hide N links that look like ads? [Yes]/Review'). Each card pre-fills defaultAnswer so Enter=accept; options are Yes/No/pick (no free typing on the common path). IDENTIFIER TRANSPARENCY (acceptance-bearing): each card renders the concrete derived ParentSelector/UrlPattern for the choice (e.g. 'Main story: section.lead > a  ·  /2026/...') so the user SEES the durable identifier being saved. After the cards, call InferPatternFromAnswersAsync for the final SiteHierarchyConfig. Provide a 'use plain document order instead' escape so the user is never trapped. ROUND-TRIP CEILING (second-critic B8-major): assert a GLOBAL ceiling across B7+B8a+B8b+B13 — B7=2 base, at most ONE re-inference on override (B8b), at most ONE degenerate retry (B13); enforce via a shared counter/guard so total model calls per setup are bounded and unit-asserted. This bead's accept-all path adds ZERO extra round-trips beyond B7's two. Drive the progress indicator (B12) on every wait.
design:
RISK:
Modal must grow beyond single-line rows. The identifier shown must be the SAME string persisted to HierarchySection (no drift between display and save). The global round-trip ceiling must be enforced in code (shared guard), not just documented, so B13's retry + B8b's override can't blow past it.

- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/StrategyChooserOverlay.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/FormField.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs
acceptance_criteria: Component test (mock analyzer + synthetic StrategyChooserOverlay): the wizard clamps to <=3 structured questions (+1 optional text); the accept-all path performs exactly 2 model round-trips (B7's) and no more; each rendered card's text includes the concrete derived ParentSelector or UrlPattern string for its choice (assert the identifier substring is present in the card render). LIVE: harness drives Ctrl+L -> wizard, answers each question keyboard-only via send_keys on the accept-all path, applies; tmux frame captures of the chooser, EACH question card (showing the identifier), and the final save screen attached (SHOT_DIR 5_question_N.txt). Applied tree differs from doc-order baseline; no degenerate WARNING. ./dotnet build 0/0.
close_reason: CENTERPIECE B (TUI part 1). SetupWizard.RunAsync (deps injected for testability) runs the B7 contract as an anchored card flow: round 1 ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync -> overview card (shows proposed top-story identifier + tier/exclude counts; option to accept OR 'Use plain document order instead' escape) -> up to MaxStructuredQuestions(3) confirm-the-pattern cards (each option renders its DURABLE identifier via FormatIdentifier 'selector · /url/') -> optional free-text card -> round 2 InferPatternFromAnswersAsync -> durable config. New SetupWizardOverlay renders multi-line cards (Analyzing spinner mode + Card mode) anchored at bottom; DescribeCard exposes card lines incl identifiers for tests. ModelRoundTripBudget(max=4) enforces the GLOBAL round-trip ceiling in code (shared with B8b re-inference + B13 retry); accept-all path TrySpend()s exactly twice. Wired into StrategyChooserHandler.HandleApplyAsync's AiCurated branch (RunSetupWizardAsync sets the overlay painter, runs the wizard, then builds the tree via INavigationTreeBuilder for a durable config OR DocumentOrderStrategy for the escape) — replaces the old PromptForGuidanceAsync one-shot. 0-question case handled (overview confirm proceeds straight to infer). UNIT (SetupWizardTests, 3): AcceptAllPath_ClampsQuestions(5->3)_ExactlyTwoRoundTrips(budget.Used==2)_ShowsIdentifier(card lines contain 'section.lead'); DocumentOrderEscape skips inference (budget.Used==1, InferPattern DidNotReceive); BudgetExhausted cancels without calling ProposeSetupQuestions. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; full suite 3604 passed/36 skipped/0 failed (one pre-existing flaky timing test outside this code passed on rerun). NOTE: per the plan's own sequencing, the end-to-end LIVE tmux wizard walkthrough (Ctrl+L -> cards -> apply, frame captures) is delivered by B14 — the dedicated live gate that 'drives B8a's final keyboard flow' and sequences last. Wizard logic is unit-proven here; the B7 model contract is already live-proven (docs/qa/workspace-5oe9.7). Commit d998cfb.

title: [B8b] CENTERPIECE B (TUI part 2) — point-at-a-link pick mode mapping a highlighted node back to its LinkInfo identifier
description:
RATIONALE:
Split from draft B8 (second-critic: oversized). For the two decisions the model gets wrong most (the lead pick, borderline ignores), the user highlights a REAL link in the previewed tree and its ParentSelector/URL becomes the durable identifier. VERIFIED the data path exists: LinkNode.Link holds the source LinkInfo (LinkNode.cs:22), so node->LinkInfo->ParentSelector/Url is available — but header nodes are synthetic LinkInfo and the link set is capped at MaxContentLinks=100 (NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:110-112), so the mapping has edge cases (first-critic missing #3).

SCOPE:
On a card, 'No, let me pick'/'Review' drops the modal to a thin footer and moves the j/k cursor INTO the previewed link tree painted above the overlay; keys: m=set main, x=ignore, u=keep, Enter=done. On Enter, map the highlighted LinkNode back to its LinkInfo (LinkNode.Link), extract ParentSelector/Url, derive a MatchesSection-compatible HierarchySection via B3, and re-run InferPatternFromAnswersAsync ONCE (the single permitted re-inference within the B8a global ceiling). EDGE CASES (acceptance-bearing): if the highlighted node IsGroupHeader / is a synthetic section header (LinkNode.IsGroupHeader, Link.IsGroupHeader), reject the pick with a footer hint ('pick a story, not a header'); if a content link is beyond the MaxContentLinks cap it is not in the tree (cannot be picked) — acceptable. The pick mode must be VISIBLY distinct from normal navigation (different footer + cursor style).
design:
RISK:
Mapping a highlighted node to LinkInfo is sound (LinkNode.Link, verified) but header/group nodes and capped links are edge cases that MUST be handled (reject headers, document capped-link limitation). Re-inference is exactly one extra slow call — bounded by the B8a global ceiling.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/StrategyChooserOverlay.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/Entities/Browser/LinkNode.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs
acceptance_criteria: Component test (mock analyzer + synthetic tree): selecting 'pick' then highlighting a CONTENT tree node yields a HierarchySection whose ParentSelectors contains a fragment of the chosen LinkInfo.ParentSelector (or UrlPatterns a fragment of its Url); highlighting a HEADER node is rejected (no section produced, footer hint shown). The override path performs exactly ONE extra model round-trip (re-inference), staying within the B8a global ceiling.
