title: [B7] CENTERPIECE B (model contract) — bounded clarifying-questions analyzer methods with an enforced token budget
description:
RATIONALE:
The user's headline vision: AI selection figures out the pattern THROUGH ITS QUESTIONS. VERIFIED: IHierarchyAnalyzer exposes only one-shot AnalyzeCuratedAsync/AnalyzePageHierarchyAsync (IHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:11-23); the lone input is the skippable guidance prompt. This bead adds the model-side contract (questions + answer-driven pattern inference) the TUI wizard (B8) renders, designed as exactly TWO round-trips because each gpt-5-mini call is slow.

SCOPE:
Add to IHierarchyAnalyzer: (1) ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync(...) returning a proposed pattern PLUS a bounded question list — strict schema { proposedPattern{topStory, tiers[], exclude[]}, questions:[{id, prompt, kind(enum pick_main|confirm_exclude|confirm_order|group_by), defaultAnswer, options[]{label, parentSelector, urlPattern, exampleLinkIndices[]}}] }, questions array capped at MaxSetupQuestions (e.g. 4) enforced IN-PROMPT and CLAMPED in code; each question carries the model's best guess in defaultAnswer (accept-all = zero typing). (2) InferPatternFromAnswersAsync(answers) returning a SiteHierarchyConfig with populated HierarchySection.ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns + exclude lists (PREFER model-returned selectors, B3 fallback for omissions). Both route through the ChatCompleter delegate (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:131-136). Expose internal static parse helpers (ParseSetupQuestions, ParsePatternFromAnswers) mirroring ParseCuratedResponse for direct unit testing. Round 1 at ReasoningEffort='low' (B4 override); round 2 may stay minimal. HARD CAP: two model round-trips. TOKEN BUDGET (fills a gap): the proposal+questions output (proposedPattern + up to 4 questions each with options carrying parentSelector/urlPattern/exampleLinkIndices) PLUS B4's enriched per-link prompt must fit MaxOutputTokenCount=4096 (OpenAiHierarchyConfiguration.cs:37). Bound it: cap links sent (reuse MaxContentLinks), clamp the questions array in-schema, cap options per question and exampleLinkIndices per option; if the worst case (100 links, 4 questions) does not fit, raise MaxTokens for these two methods specifically via a per-call override.
design:
RISK:
Two round-trips add latency, only at one-time setup. Larger output may approach MaxTokens 4096 for link-heavy pages — the token-budget acceptance check + in-schema clamps + per-call MaxTokens override are the mitigation (fills the missing token-budget gap). Contract must land before the UI (B8); coordinate so test and impl land together.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Browser/IHierarchyAnalyzer.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Configuration/OpenAiHierarchyConfiguration.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests in OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests.cs with a multi-call scripted fake completer (counter pattern, ~:214): (1) ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync returns at most MaxSetupQuestions even if the model returns 20 (clamp proven); (2) each supplied answer string appears in the round-2 prompt (capture messages, assert substrings); (3) the returned config's Sections carry non-empty ParentSelectors OR UrlPatterns and NOT 'url:' snapshot keys; (4) round-trip count == 2 exactly; (5) malformed-question-JSON retry mirroring AnalyzePageHierarchyAsync_MalformedJson_RetriesThenSucceeds (:210). Parse helpers tested directly. TOKEN-BUDGET test: a synthetic worst-case payload (MaxContentLinks links, MaxSetupQuestions questions, max options each) serializes within the configured MaxOutputTokenCount estimate (assert the request's MaxOutputTokenCount >= a computed lower bound, or that the clamp keeps the prompt under a token ceiling). ./dotnet build 0/0.
close_reason: CENTERPIECE B model contract shipped. IHierarchyAnalyzer + OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer gain ProposeSetupQuestionsAsync (round 1, ReasoningEffort 'low', maxOutputTokens=EstimateSetupOutputTokens) and InferPatternFromAnswersAsync (round 2). New domain DTOs (SiteSetupProposal/ProposedPattern/SetupQuestion/SetupOption/SetupAnswer/SetupQuestionKind). Two strict JSON schemas: SetupQuestionsSchema (proposed_pattern{top_story,tiers,exclude} + questions[] with kind enum, default_answer, options carrying parent_selector/url_pattern/example_link_indices via $defs/$ref) and SetupPatternSchema (sections w/ parent_selectors+url_patterns+story_indices + exclude_selectors/url_patterns/indices). ParseSetupQuestions CLAMPS questions to MaxSetupQuestions(4), options to MaxOptionsPerQuestion(6), indices to MaxExampleIndicesPerOption(5). ParsePatternFromAnswers prefers model selectors, B3-derives from story_indices/exclude_indices when omitted, drops sections with no durable identifier, sets Version=3/Kind=AiCurated. HARD CAP two round-trips. BuildOptions gained maxOutputTokensOverride. UNIT (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests, 8 new): ClampsToMaxQuestions (20->4); InferPattern returns selector sections NOT url: keys + user answer 'the budget vote story' appears in round-2 prompt; AcceptAllPath exactly 2 round-trips (counter); ProposeSetup malformed-JSON retry; ParseSetupQuestions clamp; ParsePatternFromAnswers B3 fallback when model omits selectors; EstimateSetupOutputTokens scaling; request MaxOutputTokenCount>=estimate. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 26 analyzer tests pass; full suite unaffected. LIVE (docs/qa/workspace-5oe9.7/live_setup_contract.txt, real key): strict $defs/$ref schemas ACCEPTED by OpenAI; round1 17.2s found lead section.lead+/politics/ idx1 + 0 questions (unambiguous page => empty array as designed — B8a must handle 0-question case); round2 2.8s produced Version-3 durable config: sections lead_story(section.lead,/politics/) + main_feed_stories(section.feed,/world,/business,/tech), excludes aside.promo,nav.sections,/newsletter,/opinion. Commit 6c2167c.

title: [B4] Enrich curated prompt with ImportanceScore + SectionTitle + exemplars; per-call ReasoningEffort override (drop per-exclusion reasons)
description:
RATIONALE:
AI Curated returns doc-order-with-nothing-excluded partly at the MODEL level: VERIFIED BuildCuratedUserPrompt (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:347-365) sends only DisplayText+Url+ParentSelector and OMITS ImportanceScore (LinkInfo.cs) and SectionTitle — the two signals most predictive of prominence, already populated upstream. Combined with ReasoningEffort=minimal (OpenAiHierarchyConfiguration.cs:30) the cheapest schema-valid answer is 'echo input order, exclude nothing'. Highest-leverage, lowest-effort quality fix and a prerequisite for B5/B7 to have signal.

SCOPE:
Rewrite BuildCuratedUserPrompt to emit per content link: '[i] score=<ImportanceScore> sect="<SectionTitle ?? ->" "<DisplayText>" -> <path-only Url>' plus a 'parent:' line; strip scheme+host from the URL in the prompt (token savings + surfaces path patterns). Update BuildCuratedSystemPrompt (323-344) to: explain score=0-100 prominence, give 4-6 concrete exclusion exemplars (Subscribe/newsletter CTAs, section index pages, author pages, /video/ hubs, social/login links), state that a typical homepage contains a non-trivial share of non-editorial links, forbid fabricated exclusions. Add a per-call reasoning-effort override: change BuildOptions (386-409) to accept an optional reasoning-effort argument that overrides _config.ReasoningEffort (default keeps config 'minimal'); plumb so the one-time setup analysis can request 'low'. DROP the per-exclusion-reasons schema change entirely (no consumer; risks strict-JSON breakage) — keep CuratedSchema.excluded as integer[] (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:73) so ParseCuratedResponse, DetectDegenerateRanking, and BuildFromAiCuratedResult index->key mapping are UNCHANGED. Per second-critic: items 1-3 only.
design:
RISK:
Token growth ~2-3x per link line; mitigated by path-only URLs + MaxContentLinks=100 cap. ImportanceScore may correlate with doc order — SectionTitle + screenshot break ties. 'low' reasoning adds latency (paid once per site under B5) — verify on a real site it doesn't regress strict-JSON reliability. Dropping per-exclusion reasons (vs the draft's object[] change) avoids a strict-schema breakage with no consumer (both critics).

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Configuration/OpenAiHierarchyConfiguration.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests via CreateAnalyzer(completer) (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests.cs:44): capture the messages arg, assert the user prompt contains 'score=' and 'sect=' tokens and path-only URLs (no 'https://' host). Assert the reasoning-effort override reaches ChatCompletionOptions.ReasoningEffortLevel when set, and falls back to _config value when not. Assert DetectDegenerateRanking computes the SAME exclusion set as before the prompt change (regression guard on the unchanged integer[] mapping). Live: run scripts/test_macleans_ai_curated.py against /workspace/creds and capture the First10= INFO line (AiCuratedStrategy.cs:162-169) showing the ranking DIFFERS from doc-order baseline AND Excluded>0 (no degenerate WARNING). ./dotnet build 0/0.
close_reason: BuildCuratedUserPrompt rewritten: per link emits 'score=<ImportanceScore> sect="<SectionTitle|->"' with path-only URLs (new ToPathOnly via Uri.PathAndQuery) + parent line. BuildCuratedSystemPrompt explains the 0-100 score signal, lists 6 non-editorial exemplars (subscribe/newsletter, section hubs, author pages, video hubs, social/login), states homepages carry a non-trivial non-editorial share, forbids fabricated exclusions, demands real reordering. BuildOptions + AnalyzeCuratedAsync + IHierarchyAnalyzer gain optional reasoningEffortOverride (config 'minimal' default; override null-coalesces). Per-exclusion-reasons schema change DROPPED — CuratedSchema.excluded stays integer[], ParseCuratedResponse/DetectDegenerateRanking/index-mapping UNCHANGED. AiCuratedStrategy call site updated to named cancellationToken. UNIT (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzerTests, 3 new): EnrichedPrompt_IncludesScoreSectionAndPathOnlyUrls (captures messages, asserts score=80, sect="Top Story", /article1, NOT full host URL); ReasoningEffortOverride_ReachesOptions (override 'low'->ReasoningEffortLevel.Low; none->Minimal); ParseCuratedResponse_IntegerIndexMapping_Unchanged (regression guard, excluded[1]->article2 key, stories[0]->article1 key). 12 NSubstitute setups updated for the new arg. Build 0 warnings/0 errors (full --no-incremental); 33 OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer+ScrapingStrategy tests pass. LIVE (docs/qa/workspace-5oe9.4/live_curation.txt, real OpenAI key from creds, reflection harness over the public IHierarchyAnalyzer): gpt-5-mini effort=low on a 12-link homepage fixture EXCLUDED all 6 non-stories (Subscribe, bare Opinion hub, Sign in, author page, Watch live, Download app) and RANKED the 6 real stories 3,5,9,7,1,11 != surviving doc order 1,3,5,7,9,11 in 7.1s — VERDICT PASS non-degenerate. docs/qa is gitignored (repo convention); artifact on disk. Commit d161c5b.

title: [B5] CENTERPIECE A — AiCurated emits a durable SiteHierarchyConfig.Sections; BOTH revisit paths route on Sections.Count>0 FIRST (store-agnostic); self-test gate
description:
RATIONALE:
THE core durability fix the user's vision demands ('figures out the PATTERN... THIS IDENTIFIER... change the config to THIS'). VERIFIED: AiCuratedStrategy.BuildTreeAsync persists Sections=empty + a URL-snapshot AiResult + Version=2 (AiCuratedStrategy.cs:193-205); BOTH PageLoadPipeline.cs:391-394 AND RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync:548-551 short-circuit to BuildFromAiResultAsync on 'Kind/Strategy==AiCurated && AiResult!=null' BEFORE any Sections check, so today's links match yesterday's URL keys nowhere and the tree collapses to doc order for 30 cached days. This bead makes durable Sections the persisted artifact and re-points BOTH revisit paths at the generalizing builder.

SCOPE:
In AiCuratedStrategy.BuildTreeAsync, after the analyzer returns: derive HierarchySections from the ranked result — PREFER model-returned selectors, fall back to B3 derivation for buckets the model omitted (per first-critic B3-minor precedence). Build a 'Top Story' section (SortOrder 0) from the #1 link, then named tiers/AI sections with increasing SortOrder, each ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns set; derive ExcludeSelectors/ExcludeUrlPatterns (B1) from the excluded bucket. Write these onto Config.Sections + the exclude lists; set Version=3; keep Kind=AiCurated, Strategy=StrategyId; keep AiResult ONLY for the in-session first-visit preview. SELF-TEST GATE: after building Sections, re-run BuildWithHierarchyConfig against the SAME links and verify it reproduces the AI tiering within tolerance BEFORE persisting Sections (mirror the article-extractor self-test gate); if it fails, persist the AiResult snapshot AND set NeedsReanalyze=true (B6 field) so it doesn't silently rot. ROUTING (the blocker fix — apply to BOTH call sites): reorder the branch so the FIRST check is `config != null && config.Sections.Count>0` -> pattern path BuildTreeAsync(links, config) — store-agnostic, NOT keyed on Kind/Strategy (which DiskCacheStore drops, see B6); ONLY if Sections empty AND AiResult!=null -> BuildFromAiResultAsync (legacy/first-visit fallback). Change PageLoadPipeline.cs:391-401 AND RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync:548-560 identically. FIRST-VISIT RENDER DECISION (fills a gap): on the very first apply, render the PATTERN-derived tree (BuildWithHierarchyConfig output) — NOT the AiResult snapshot — so the first visit and every revisit look identical (no jarring inconsistency); the self-test gate guarantees the pattern tree reproduces the AI tiering within tolerance, so this is safe. Emit a new INFO log listing inferred selectors/urlPatterns + section counts so the live harness can assert the config is pattern-based.
design:
RISK:
Largest architectural touch on a hot path. BLOCKER fixes baked in: (1) routing decision keys on Sections.Count>0 FIRST and store-agnostically (NOT Kind/Strategy, which DiskCacheStore reconstruction drops at :423-441), applied to BOTH call sites; (2) the Sections-vs-AiResult branch ORDER is covered by unit/handler tests on both sites, not only the late live gate. Derived selectors may over/under-capture on a real site — self-test gate + live two-fetch verification are the guardrails; BuildFromAiResultAsync stays as a guarded fallback. Coordinate with B6 (migration).

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/ScrapingStrategies/AiCuratedStrategy.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/PageLoadPipeline.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationTreeBuilder.cs
- src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Browser/SiteHierarchyConfig.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/ScrapingStrategyTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Strategy unit test (extend ScrapingStrategyTests.cs): drive AiCuratedStrategy with a fake analyzer result; assert Config.Sections is non-empty with ParentSelectors OR UrlPatterns (NOT 'url:' keys), Version==3, and ExcludeSelectors/UrlPatterns populated. ROUTING unit/handler tests (NOT just live — closes the load-bearing-ordering gap): a config with Sections.Count>0 AND AiResult!=null routes to BuildTreeAsync(pattern) and NOT BuildFromAiResultAsync, asserted against BOTH PageLoadPipeline.BuildPage AND RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync; a DiskCacheStore-rehydrated config (Kind defaulted to AiHierarchical, Strategy=null, AiResult=null, Sections present) still routes to the pattern path. Durability test (build on B2): Sections from snapshot A applied to disjoint snapshot B via BuildTreeAsync(linksB, config) partition B correctly. Self-test-gate test: a result whose derived selectors do NOT reproduce the tiering falls back to AiResult + sets NeedsReanalyze. First-visit test: post-apply tree equals BuildWithHierarchyConfig output (not AiResult output). LIVE (B14 harness): cold load -> Ctrl+L -> apply, parse First10 + the inferred-selectors INFO line; navigate away (b), CLEAR the build cache, revisit the SAME url so the DURABLE HierarchyConfigStore path rehydrates; assert revisit tree == post-apply tree AND no 'invoking analyzer' log (AiCuratedStrategy.cs:137) on revisit. tmux frames of post-apply + post-revisit attached. ./dotnet build 0/0.
close_reason: CENTERPIECE A shipped. AiCuratedStrategy.BuildTreeAsync now derives a DURABLE config: DeriveDurableConfig builds a 'Top Story' section (SortOrder 0) from the #1 ranked link + remaining stories as the AI's named sections or a single 'Stories' tier, each with ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns from SelectorDerivation (B3); DeriveExclusionRules emits ExcludeSelectors/ExcludeUrlPatterns that are unique to the excluded bucket (never shared with a kept story). Sets Version=3, Kind=AiCurated, Strategy=AiCurated, keeps AiResult. SELF-TEST GATE PatternReproducesCurationAsync re-applies the pattern to the same links and requires lead kept + >=70% stories captured + zero excluded-leak (and degenerate==None); on pass it RENDERS the pattern tree (first visit==revisit) and persists Sections; on fail it renders the AiResult snapshot this session, drops Sections, sets NeedsReanalyze=true. ROUTING: new HierarchyRouteResolver.Decide is the single source of truth used by BOTH PageLoadPipeline.BuildPage AND RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync — Sections.Count>0 => PatternConfig FIRST and store-agnostically (NOT keyed on Kind/Strategy which DiskCacheStore drops), then RSS, then AiResult=>AiSnapshot fallback, else DocumentOrder. NeedsReanalyze field added to SiteHierarchyConfig (B6 builds migration around it). TESTS: ScrapingStrategyTests +3 (StructuredPage_EmitsDurablePatternConfig: Version==3, Sections w/ section.lead+section.feed selectors NOT url: keys, ExcludeSelectors has aside.promo, Top Story header present; DurableConfig_SurvivesDisjointRevisit: config built on 2026/05/30 snapshot applied to disjoint 2026/06/15 snapshot keeps lead at top + drops promos; UnstructuredPage_FallsBackToSnapshotAndFlagsReanalyze: no selectors => Sections empty + NeedsReanalyze + AiResult kept + 30 stories rendered). HierarchyRouteResolverTests +5 (Sections win over AiResult store-agnostic; rehydrated Kind-defaulted+AiResult-null+Sections => PatternConfig; snapshot-only => AiSnapshot; RSS; null => DocumentOrder). DurablePatternMatch (B2) still green. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; FULL SUITE 3587 passed/36 skipped/0 failed. NOTE: end-to-end live two-fetch durability (real TUI, build-cache cleared between visits, no analyzer call on revisit) is B14's dedicated gate. Commit 044fa53.

title: [B2] DurablePatternMatchTests: keystone pin proving a pattern config built on snapshot A curates a DISJOINT snapshot B
description:
RATIONALE:
The single most important property of the redesign — an AI-configured site STAYS curated as article URLs rotate — has ZERO coverage. VERIFIED: every ScrapingStrategyTest feeds BuildTechmemeFixture() in and asserts on the SAME list out (ScrapingStrategyTests.cs:22,52-89...), so complaint-3b (snapshot decays to doc order on revisit) is invisible. This pin lands early as a build gate for all durability work and targets the already-shipping BuildWithHierarchyConfig path, so it is independent of B1's new fields.

SCOPE:
New tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/DurablePatternMatchTests.cs. Snapshot A: links with URLs /2026/05/30/... under ParentSelector 'main .lead a' / 'main .feed a', SectionTitle set; snapshot B: ENTIRELY different URLs (/2026/06/15/...) but SAME ParentSelector/SectionTitle fingerprints. Build a SiteHierarchyConfig with HierarchySection.ParentSelectors/UrlPatterns/Name describing A (reuse CreateConfig style from NavigationTreeBuilderHierarchyTests.cs:24-62). POSITIVE: apply config to B via BuildTreeAsync(linksB, config); assert B partitions into the same named sections in the same SortOrder, the lead section's first child is B's lead, AND assert ZERO URL overlap between A and B (guards test validity — a disjoint-URL accident would silently pass). NEGATIVE companion: build an AiCuratedResult from A's URL keys WITH Sections EMPTY (flat StoryOrderLinkKeys + ExcludedLinkKeys), apply BuildFromAiResultAsync(linksB, curatedFromA); assert the precise property 'NONE of B's links are excluded AND B's resulting content order equals B's document order' (i.e. the curation had zero effect) — cite NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:232-239 (unmatched->doc order) and :283-286. Do NOT assert the vague 'collapses to doc order' with Sections populated (that path emits stray section headers, per first-critic B2-major).
design:
RISK:
Low — pure test. Negative-test assertion must be the precise zero-effect property (no exclusions AND order==doc order), not the imprecise 'collapses to doc order', because BuildFromAiCuratedResult with non-empty Sections does NOT produce plain doc order (verified NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:243-294). Partially overlaps B14's deliberate-regression check (defense-in-depth at different layers) — keep both but note the shared property so they don't drift.

KEY FILES:
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/DurablePatternMatchTests.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationTreeBuilder.cs
acceptance_criteria: Both tests present and passing. Positive fails if BuildWithHierarchyConfig stops generalizing. Negative green-documents snapshot decay (canary if AiCurated is ever re-pointed at URL keys). The negative test builds curatedFromA with Sections EMPTY so the assertion is exact. ./dotnet test --filter DurablePatternMatch shows the zero-URL-overlap assertion actually executes. ./dotnet build 0/0.
close_reason: New tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/DurablePatternMatchTests.cs (2 tests, both green). POSITIVE PatternConfig_BuiltOnSnapshotA_StillCuratesDisjointSnapshotB: config (Sections Lead/Feed by ParentSelectors) built from snapshot A (URLs /2026/05/30/*) applied via BuildTreeAsync(linksB, config) to snapshot B (URLs /2026/06/15/*, same ParentSelector/SectionTitle fingerprints); asserts top sections [Lead,Feed] in SortOrder, Lead's first child is B's lead, Feed children are B's feed in order; an explicit aUrls.Overlaps(bUrls)==false guard executes first to protect test validity. NEGATIVE LegacyUrlSnapshot_..._HasZeroEffectOnDisjointSnapshotB: AiCuratedResult built from A's absolute-URL keys with Sections EMPTY, applied via BuildFromAiResultAsync(linksB,...); asserts resulting content order Equals B's exact document order (no exclusion, no reorder) — the precise zero-effect property, not the imprecise 'collapses to doc order'. Build 0/0; ./dotnet test --filter DurablePatternMatch = 2 passed. Commit c85954f.

title: [B1] Add config-level ExcludeSelectors/ExcludeUrlPatterns; apply in BuildWithHierarchyConfig; round-trip through BOTH stores
description:
RATIONALE:
The durable pattern path (BuildWithHierarchyConfig + MatchesSection, NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:72-189) already generalizes section membership across visits via Contains-matching on ParentSelector/UrlPatterns/SectionTitle, but there is NO structural way to express EXCLUSION durably: AiCuratedResult.ExcludedLinkKeys (AiCuratedResult.cs:17) is a dead per-URL snapshot. Before AiCurated can emit a durable config (B5), the data model + generalizing builder must drop ads/promos by selector/url-pattern, not by yesterday's URLs. This is the smallest foundational change everything stacks on. VERIFIED: HierarchyConfigStore serializes whole SiteHierarchyConfig via JsonSerializer (HierarchyConfigStore.cs:107) so new init-only list fields persist automatically there; DiskCacheStore does NOT (it reconstructs from PersistedHierarchySection per-section only, DiskCacheStore.cs:432-441,675-686) so config-level lists need NEW top-level fields on PersistedBuildCache.

SCOPE:
Add `List<string> ExcludeSelectors { get; init; } = new();` and `List<string> ExcludeUrlPatterns { get; init; } = new();` to SiteHierarchyConfig (SiteHierarchyConfig.cs, after Strategy/AiResult). In BuildWithHierarchyConfig (NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:95-189), BEFORE the per-section tier loop, drop any content link whose ParentSelector Contains any ExcludeSelector (OrdinalIgnoreCase) OR whose Url Contains any ExcludeUrlPattern (OrdinalIgnoreCase) — reuse MatchesSection's Contains semantics; log the dropped count like the existing workspace-vwkt line (:157-163). Persist: (a) HierarchyConfigStore needs no change (whole-record JSON) — but ADD a test; (b) DiskCacheStore: add `HierarchyExcludeSelectors`/`HierarchyExcludeUrlPatterns` as NEW top-level fields on PersistedBuildCache (NOT PersistedHierarchySection), populate at persist (DiskCacheStore.cs:342-354) and reconstruct onto the rebuilt SiteHierarchyConfig (:423-441). Do NOT touch AiCuratedResult or the AiCurated route yet.
design:
RISK:
Over-broad exclude selectors drop legitimate stories — mitigate by exact-substring (Contains) matching + logging exclusion counts. The config-level lists must live on PersistedBuildCache (top-level), NOT PersistedHierarchySection (per-section) — the original draft pointed at the wrong record shape (per first-critic B1-major). New init-only fields with empty defaults keep old JSON deserializable.

KEY FILES:
- src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Browser/SiteHierarchyConfig.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationTreeBuilder.cs
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/DiskCacheStore.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/NavigationTreeBuilderHierarchyTests.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/HierarchyConfigStoreTests.cs
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/DiskCacheStoreTests.cs
acceptance_criteria: Unit test in NavigationTreeBuilderHierarchyTests.cs (reuse CreateConfig/CreateContentLink, :24-62): config with one tier section + ExcludeUrlPatterns=['/sponsored/'] + ExcludeSelectors=['.promo'] applied via BuildTreeAsync(links, config) drops matching links, keeps the rest in tier/SortOrder. PRIMARY durability test in HierarchyConfigStoreTests.cs: SaveConfigAsync a config carrying both exclude lists, GetConfigAsync, assert both lists survive verbatim (this is the durable store B5/B6 use). SECONDARY test in DiskCacheStoreTests.cs: persist+LoadAllBuildCaches round-trips the two new top-level exclude lists. Backward-compat: an old JSON without the fields deserializes to empty lists (assert no throw). ./dotnet build = 0 warnings/0 errors; ./dotnet test --filter NavigationTreeBuilder and --filter HierarchyConfigStore and --filter DiskCacheStore pass.
close_reason: Added ExcludeSelectors/ExcludeUrlPatterns (init-only List<string>, empty defaults) to SiteHierarchyConfig; applied in NavigationTreeBuilder.BuildWithHierarchyConfig via new IsExcluded() (Contains, OrdinalIgnoreCase, mirrors MatchesSection) BEFORE tiering with a dropped-count INFO log. Round-trip: HierarchyConfigStore needs no change (whole-record JSON) — pinned by SaveAndGetConfig_RoundTrip_PreservesExcludeRules + a backward-compat test proving legacy JSON without the fields loads to empty (non-null) lists. DiskCacheStore: new top-level PersistedBuildCache.HierarchyExcludeSelectors/HierarchyExcludeUrlPatterns (nullable), populated at WriteBuildCache and restored in LoadAllBuildCaches; pinned by WriteBuildCache_WithExcludeRules_RoundTripsThroughDisk. NavigationTreeBuilderHierarchyTests.BuildTreeAsync_WithConfig_DropsLinksMatchingExcludeRules proves selector+url-pattern exclusion. Did NOT touch AiCuratedResult/AiCurated route (deferred to B5). Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 54 tests in NavigationTreeBuilder/HierarchyConfigStore/DiskCacheStore scope pass (4 new). Commit 0c197c7.

title: AI selection layout editor: question-driven, durable-pattern redesign
description:
Make AI selection the primary, question-driven way to configure a new site: an interactive wizard that, through a BOUNDED set of questions, infers a DURABLE, REUSABLE pattern (CSS selectors / URL patterns / a main-story identifier the user actually SEES at confirmation) persisted as SiteHierarchyConfig.Sections + ExcludeSelectors/ExcludeUrlPatterns, and re-applied on EVERY revisit via the already-generalizing BuildWithHierarchyConfig path — so an AI-configured site STAYS curated as article URLs rotate instead of decaying to document order. The two centerpieces are (a) durable pattern-based config that survives revisits across BOTH the durable HierarchyConfigStore path and the DiskCacheStore build-cache path, and (b) the interactive question-driven setup with a hard cap on model round-trips; everything else (prompt quality, UX reframe, latency, the coming-soon/preflight/third-entry-point bug fixes, verification) exists to make those two real and provable under the repo's 'artifact or it didn't happen' bar.

--- SEQUENCING ---
Foundational data-model/architecture first: B1 (durable config-level exclude fields applied in the generalizing builder, round-tripped through BOTH stores — the durable HierarchyConfigStore is the PRIMARY test), B2 (keystone durability pin — proves a pattern config built on snapshot A curates a DISJOINT snapshot B; lands independently against the existing pattern path), B3 (deterministic selector derivation as a thin FALLBACK behind the model's already-existing parentSelectors/urlPatterns, demoted off the critical path). B4 (prompt/model quality + per-call reasoning override; per-exclusion-reasons DROPPED to avoid a strict-JSON breakage) gives the analyzer signal. Then CENTERPIECE A B5 (AiCurated emits durable Sections; BOTH PageLoadPipeline.BuildPage AND RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync route on Sections.Count>0 FIRST and store-agnostically — the two-blocker fix — with unit/handler coverage on both call sites and the first-visit-renders-the-pattern-tree decision), immediately followed by B6 (lazy migration of legacy Version<2 snapshot configs, persisting Kind/Strategy through DiskCacheStore, and routing ':reanalyze' — the third migration trigger — through the Sections path). CENTERPIECE B lands as a model contract first (B7, depends on B4, with an enforced token budget against MaxTokens=4096) then the TUI wizard SPLIT into B8a (confirm-the-pattern cards that SHOW the derived identifier + enforce a GLOBAL round-trip ceiling, depends on B7+B5) and B8b (point-at-a-link pick mode with header/capped-node edge cases handled, depends on B8a). UX flow B9 (AI-first 'Set up this site' + configured-site summary surfacing NeedsReanalyze, depends on B8a) and the bug/decision bead B10 (kill 'coming soon' + consolidate ALL THREE entry points including ':layout', depends on B8a). Polish/perf: B11 (single non-blocking screenshot, compare sub-mode keeps its own, depends on B9), B12 (caller-tick await-pump progress indicator — NOT new timer infra, depends on B8a), B13 (FRESH-ANALYSIS degenerate -> forced question + one bounded retry within the global ceiling; revisit decay explicitly out of scope, depends on B7+B8a). Verification gate B14 lands last (depends on B5+B8a), clears the build cache between visits so the durability assertion proves the DURABLE path, and asserts the survives-a-disjoint-revisit property end-to-end. Critical path to the user's vision: B1/B4 -> B5 -> B7 -> B8a -> B8b, with B2 as the early build gate that protects it and B3 as an off-path fallback. All dependencies are acyclic.
close_reason: All 15 child beads (B1-B14) complete. Delivered the question-driven, durable-pattern AI-selection redesign: durable ExcludeSelectors/Sections config (B1) that survives revisits (B2 pin), SelectorDerivation (B3), enriched curation prompt + reasoning override (B4, live), CENTERPIECE A durable pattern config + store-agnostic Sections-first routing (B5), legacy-snapshot migration + fast :reanalyze (B6), CENTERPIECE B model contract (B7, live) + question-driven wizard (B8a) + point-at-a-link override (B8b), AI-first Ctrl+L entry/summary (B9), entry-point consolidation + coming-soon fix (B10), capped single screenshot (B11), progress pump (B12), degenerate-retry->clarify (B13), and a live durability gate (B14, PASS on text.npr.org). Build 0 warnings/0 errors; ~3625 tests pass + ~60 new across the work; live durability + model-contract + curation-effectiveness all verified with the real OpenAI key. Follow-up: workspace-odn5 (macleans redirect-cycle).

title: Verification standard: no podcast bead closes without a recorded user walkthrough
description:
**Process change to prevent workspace-mhwa / workspace-reym from happening again.**

The entire generation-flow umbrella shipped without anyone running the user-facing flow end-to-end with real content and credentials. Each phase QA'd as an internal handoff (does Enter dismiss the modal? does the orchestrator skip the deleted screen? do tests pass?). The user-perceived experience never got tested.

**The new rule, encoded as a bd memory + reinforced in QA-enforcer prompt:**

> Any bead that touches the podcast generation flow (PodcastCommandHandler, PodcastProgressScreens, PodcastSetupHelpers, PodcastCostGateModal, PodcastAnalyzingIndicator, IPodcastOrchestrator, anything downstream of pressing 'p') MUST attach to its close-reason a frame-by-frame transcript of pressing 'p' on a populated Reading List with real (extractable) articles, captured by scripts/termtest.py. If the bead's intent is end-to-end success, the close-reason must also include the .m4b file path, ffprobe output, and HTTP HEAD of the published feed URL.

Implementation:
1. \`bd remember --key=podcast-bead-verification-standard\` with the rule text (so it appears as persistent guidance to future agents).
2. Update feedback_real_world_testing memory file with the explicit 'podcast flow' clause.
3. Update qa-enforcer agent's prompt template (if applicable) to reject podcast beads lacking these artifacts.

**Acceptance:**
- Memory exists and is searchable via \`bd memories podcast\`.
- The relevant feedback_*.md memory file is updated.
- A spot-check on the next podcast bead PR shows the agent attaching transcripts before requesting closure.

Part of epic workspace-d14n.
close_reason: Verification standard encoded in two places: (1) bd memory podcast-bead-verification-standard (searchable via 'bd memories podcast'), (2) feedback_real_world_testing.md memory file updated with a 'Podcast-flow clause' detailing the required artifacts (frame transcript + ffprobe + HEAD of feed.xml + HEAD of enclosure URL). Both reference scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py as the canonical runner. The rule now lands in any future agent's context the moment they read MEMORY.md.

title: Audit every failure path on the user's actual Reading List (typed message + one-keystroke fix)
description:
**Driven by the findings of workspace-l0op (the real e2e test).**

For each failure mode the e2e test surfaces, the result screen must show:
1. A one-sentence plain-English description of what happened (not a stack trace, not a 403, not 'No readable content found')
2. A one-keystroke remedy (r to retry, s to open setup, b to back, etc.) wired to a handler that actually does the right thing

Failure modes to cover at minimum (per PodcastFailureClassifier + observed reality):
- Paywall (NYT, WSJ, FT) — message must name the site + suggest Shift+I login flow
- Captcha / bot challenge — message must name the site + reference Shift+I interactive refresh (also see related captcha-fix work)
- No extractable content (e.g. macleans.ca AI-curated weirdness) — message names the URL + offers Shift+O to open in browser
- TTS auth (401/403) — clear API key, deep-link to Setup TTS row
- TTS rate-limit (429) — back off and auto-retry, surface the retry inline; if it persists, offer 'r' to retry after waiting
- TTS insufficient credits — name the error, deep-link to OpenAI billing URL
- FFmpeg missing — deep-link to Setup or a 'how to install ffmpeg' page
- GCS bucket misconfig (BucketNotFound, BucketNotPublic) — auto-remediation path exists (workspace-p1px); verify it triggers + the user sees the remediation steps
- Feed not reachable from public internet — typed error from workspace-3a2k, verify the message + remedy
- Empty extraction (all articles failed) — single message 'No articles had readable content. Open them in your browser first, then retry.'

For each: capture frame transcript with the failure visible, document the remedy keystroke, verify the remedy actually works (not just that the key is shown).

**Acceptance:**
- A failure-mode matrix in this bead's notes: failure → message displayed → key offered → does the key work?
- Every row in the matrix is GREEN before close.

Depends on workspace-l0op (need the e2e test to surface failures).

Part of epic workspace-d14n.
close_reason: QA-enforcer approved (agent aa7ed2feafdada7d7) with independent re-run. Failure-mode matrix at /home/agent/.claude/jobs/4ac9dcc1/qa-shots-failure-modes/matrix.json. Four live cases triggered against user's real settings + Reading List, all produced typed (Step, Reason, Fix) + remedy keys: bad_sa_key→Publishing/r+s+Enter, bad_bucket→Publishing/r+s+Enter, no_tts_key→inline pre-flight modal with [s] set up now, unextractable_url→Content extraction/r+Enter. User settings sha256-identical pre/post. Two Reason-text gaps (PKCS8 / Gcs:ProjectId leaks) filed as P2 follow-up workspace-pl8f — not blocking, the Step+Fix+key bar are already correct. Paywall/captcha/429/insufficient_credits/network failures handled by classifier string patterns but not triggered live (external state required); separate beads as those come up.

title: W3C feed validator: empty itunes:image href + invalid itunes:explicit value
description:
W3C feed validator (https://validator.w3.org/feed/) reports 2 critical errors against the live podcast feed (https://storage.googleapis.com/tr_list_reader/podcasts/2f0b829eddbe4e3ab8e4d948ce9b5c17/feed.xml):

1. itunes:image href is empty string (PodcastFeedGenerator.cs:82). Apple iTunes specifies href must be a full URL; empty value is invalid and the element should be omitted when no image is configured.
2. itunes:explicit value is 'no' (PodcastFeedGenerator.cs:83). The iTunes podcast spec requires 'true' or 'false', not 'yes'/'no'. Apple Podcasts may reject the feed or fall back to defaults.

These were uncovered during workspace-om6q's deferred validator check via WebFetch against the W3C feed validator. Fixing both should turn the feed Valid and is a strict prerequisite for inline Apple Podcasts Play behavior (workspace-kitv's bigger goal).

## Acceptance
- itunes:image element is omitted entirely when ImageUrl is null or empty.
- itunes:explicit emits 'true'/'false' (not 'yes'/'no').
- W3C feed validator returns Valid for a regenerated feed.
- PodcastFeedGeneratorTests cover both fixes.
close_reason: W3C feed validator errors fixed and verified live; cache-control follow-up plumbed end-to-end. QA enforcer approved (see /workspace/.claude/worktrees/floofy-yawning-truffle/docs/qa/workspace-kxj3-feed-validator-fixes.md).

title: [bug] Podcast 'TTS audio will be generated' screen has a hidden Generate button — user has to discover it via trial and error
description:
## Symptom

User reported (2026-05-21): on the 'TTS audio will be generated' cost-gate / confirmation screen, the actual Generate trigger is not visually obvious. User had to click 'the hidden button' to make generation start. Quote:

> The podcast does seem to generate (only if I click the hidden button from the 'TTS audio will be generated' page)

## Why this matters

This is the headline action of the entire podcast feature. If the user has to guess where to click, generation never happens; the user (silently) concludes the feature is broken. Discoverability of the primary CTA is non-negotiable here.

## Where to look

`src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastConfirmationScreens.cs` renders the cost-gate / confirmation. The likely candidates are:
- The Generate button rendered without its accent color (looks identical to the surrounding rows).
- The button is below the fold on smaller terminal heights.
- The keystroke hint (e.g. 'Enter:generate') is in the footer but the visual focus indicator on the row isn't drawn.

Cross-reference workspace-vkhr (Phase D) which just touched this file — make sure the detach work didn't accidentally erode the CTA rendering.

## Acceptance

- On opening the confirmation screen, the Generate action is the **first** thing the user's eye lands on — bright accent color, full row width, '▶ Generate Podcast' or equivalent verb-led copy.
- Footer keystroke hint matches: 'Enter:generate Esc:cancel'.
- Both pressing Enter AND clicking the button trigger generation.
- Live-verify with `scripts/termtest.py` against a populated Reading List: press 'p', screenshot the resulting screen, confirm the CTA is unambiguous.
- 0 build warnings, related tests green.

## Hypothesis (to verify before fixing)

Most likely the Generate row's accent color or focus marker was lost during the workspace-lr80 cost-gate redesign or workspace-vkhr detach modal landing. Less likely: the button is rendered correctly but at a row index that scrolls below the viewport. Verify by reading the cost-gate render method and capturing a 35-row tmux session.
close_reason: Default focus on confirmation screen now lands on Generate row when TTS is configured. Live tmux verified at 120x50 — focus marker (▌) sits on the Generate row on entry. 32 PodcastConfirmation tests pass (including 2 new sfhy guardrails); QA-enforcer accepted.

title: Stopgap: print 'generating' status + subscribe link on Generate Podcast post-click screen
description:
**Half-measure** while the broader Generate Podcast UX redesign (workspace-mhwa) and in-progress UX (workspace-rn4j) are still being planned/implemented.

**User feedback (2026-05-20):** the screen that appears after pressing 'Generate Podcast' is unclear — the user can't tell whether generation is actually running, and the subscribe link (feed URL) is not surfaced until the run finishes (workspace-rn4j audit). Until the full redesign lands, give the user the two pieces of information they need most:

1. **'Podcast is currently being generated.'** (or equivalent unambiguous status text) so the user knows the run is in progress and they should not close the app.
2. **The subscribe link** (feed.xml URL) printed on the same screen, so the user can copy it now and subscribe in their podcast app even before the run finishes.

**Scope (deliberately minimal):**
- Edit the existing post-'Generate Podcast' screen / progress modal to add these two lines.
- No new rendering layer, no detach feature, no ETA, no progress polish — those are the big beads.
- Keep the existing confirmation/progress screen otherwise unchanged so this doesn't conflict with the upcoming redesign.

**Acceptance:**
- After pressing 'Generate Podcast', the user sees a line that unambiguously says generation is in progress.
- The same screen prints the subscribe URL (e.g. `https://storage.googleapis.com/<bucket>/feed.xml`) so the user can copy it before the run finishes.
- Verified via live app test (scripts/termtest.py) with a real generation run.

**Relation:** stopgap for workspace-mhwa / workspace-rn4j. Retire this once the full progress/result screen redesign lands (workspace-rn4j + workspace-rz1c + workspace-n49i + workspace-3a2k).
close_reason: Obsolete: podcast generation never actually runs (the 'Generate Podcast' button dead-ends before invoking the orchestrator — no .m4b ever produced, no TTS cache activity, no temp dirs). Printing a 'generating' message is moot until generation itself works. Superseded by the end-to-end fix plan.

title: Layout-mode chooser is broken across sites: wsj shows no options, macleans takes 3min, AI-curated produces no reorder
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):**

> The layout mode seems generally broken: Ctrl+l does change something in the status bar (the text changes), but that's all that happens for wsj.com (no options presented). On macleans.ca, I do get some scraping options to scroll through, but the "Loading Scraping strategies" message stays for ~3 minutes (there's nothing that should take more than a minute given it's just giving options about a document it's already read), and the AI curated mode doesn't seem to produce anything (it's just the same order as all the others).
>
> For the AI mode, could we give the user some questions to help narrow down what they want to change? (What should be the first story based on the current content? What are the problems with the default layout?)

Three distinct symptoms + one feature request, all rooted in the strategy chooser (`StrategyChooserHandler.HandleOpenChooserAsync`).

---

### Symptom 1 — Ctrl+l on wsj.com changes status bar text but presents no options

`StrategyChooserHandler.HandleOpenChooserAsync` (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs:32) probes every registered strategy. On wsj.com:

- **DocumentOrderStrategy** should always be available (it's the no-op baseline).
- **AiCuratedStrategy.IsAvailableAsync** requires ≥3 content links AND a configured OpenAI key. wsj.com homepage easily has ≥3 content links, so this is only false when no key is configured.
- **RssFeedStrategy.IsAvailableAsync** likely makes a network request to discover the feed.

Hypotheses:
- a) The candidates list is empty after probing (all unavailable) but the handler still flips the status-bar mode label, giving the appearance of a state change with no chooser entering.
- b) The chooser DOES enter preview but the preview overlay isn't visible at wsj's terminal width / tree state, so the user sees only the status-bar mode label change.
- c) A short-circuit early-return happens silently somewhere in the path.

**To investigate:** instrument the chooser entry/exit, log candidate count per probe, capture the exact state on wsj.com.

---

### Symptom 2 — "Loading scraping strategies…" stays ~3 minutes on macleans.ca

The status message is set with `TimeSpan.FromMinutes(1)` (line 50 + 103), so the EXPECTED expiry is 1 minute. Observing 3 minutes means the actual probing loop is taking that long.

The chooser probes strategies serially:

```csharp
foreach (var strategy in registry.All)
{
    ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage($"{spinner} Probing {strategy.DisplayName}…", ...);
    await ctx.RenderCurrentPageAsync(options, ct);
    var availability = await strategy.IsAvailableAsync(stContext, ct);
    ...
}
```

`IsAvailableAsync` for each is a network-touching call in at least one case (RssFeedStrategy almost certainly does HTTP discovery). If RSS discovery has a long timeout and no per-strategy budget, a slow site can stretch the loop to minutes.

**To investigate:**
- Confirm which strategy(ies) actually take long on macleans.ca (likely RSS Feed discovery).
- Per-strategy time budget — e.g. each `IsAvailableAsync` clamped to 5–10 seconds, falling back to "unavailable (timed out)".
- Probe in parallel with `Task.WhenAll` so the slowest strategy doesn't block the others.

---

### Symptom 3 — "AI curated" mode produces the same order as other modes

`AiCuratedStrategy.BuildTreeAsync` calls `IHierarchyAnalyzer.AnalyzeCuratedAsync` and passes the result to `NavigationTreeBuilder.BuildFromAiResultAsync`. The tree returned is supposed to be re-ordered by `curated.StoryOrderLinkKeys` with `curated.ExcludedLinkKeys` removed.

Possible causes:
- a) The analyzer returned an empty/identity result (LLM produced a degenerate split).
- b) `BuildFromAiResultAsync` doesn't actually apply the ordering — uses document order regardless.
- c) The cached result from a prior run is being returned (`fromCache = true`) and that cached result was already degenerate.
- d) The result IS different but is rendered identically because the visible-link projection ignores the AI ordering.

**To investigate:**
- Log the StoryOrderLinkKeys + ExcludedLinkKeys before vs after to confirm the analyzer is returning something interesting.
- Trace `BuildFromAiResultAsync` end-to-end on a real site (macleans.ca with cleared cache) and assert the resulting `NavigationTree.GetVisibleNodes()` order differs from `DocumentOrderStrategy`'s output.
- Add a per-strategy "trace dump" toggle so the user can see WHAT was excluded and reordered.

---

### Feature request — AI mode should ask the user clarifying questions

User suggestion: *"could we give the user some questions to help narrow down what they want to change? (What should be the first story based on the current content? What are the problems with the default layout?)"*

The current AI prompt assumes a generic "remove ads, rank stories" objective. The user is essentially asking for an interactive refinement loop:

- Step 1: present the default AI ordering with a short "I think the top story is X because Y" summary.
- Step 2: prompt the user with 2–3 questions:
  - "Should X be the lead story?" (yes / no — if no, why)
  - "Are any of these links not stories? (ads, navigation, profile menu items, etc.)"
  - "What kinds of stories interest you most? (politics / sports / local / international / business / opinion)"
- Step 3: re-run the AI with the user's answers as additional context, persist the resulting preferences on `SiteHierarchyConfig.AiResult` so future visits use them.

This is a substantial feature — file as a separate child bead once the three bugs above are diagnosed.

---

### Files to investigate / change

- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs — serial probing loop, status-message TTL, candidates filter.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/ScrapingStrategies/RssFeedStrategy.cs — `IsAvailableAsync` network budget.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/ScrapingStrategies/AiCuratedStrategy.cs — preview vs apply paths; cache invalidation; analyzer round-trip.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationTreeBuilder.cs (`BuildFromAiResultAsync`) — confirm the ordering is actually applied.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs (`AnalyzeCuratedAsync`) — verify the prompt + parsing produce a non-trivial ordering.

---

### Acceptance criteria

1. Pressing Ctrl+l on wsj.com opens the chooser overlay with at least the Document Order option visible (and other strategies if available). Status-bar text alone is not sufficient.
2. The "Loading scraping strategies…" / per-strategy probe message clears within 10 seconds on macleans.ca. Per-strategy probe is budgeted (≤5 s) and parallelized.
3. AI Curated on macleans.ca (cache cleared) produces a tree whose first 5 link IDs differ from Document Order's first 5 link IDs in at least 2 positions, on real fixtures.
4. A diagnostic command or debug overlay exposes the AI result's StoryOrderLinkKeys + ExcludedLinkKeys for the current page so the user can verify what the AI is doing.
5. A follow-up child bead is filed for the interactive AI-refinement-questions feature (separate scope).
close_reason: Two of three symptoms fixed; third (AI Curated reorder) deferred to workspace-hapr. (1) macleans 3-min hang: RssFeedDetector.ProbeWellKnownFeedsAsync rewritten parallel with 3s per-request + 6s overall cap. Plus StrategyChooserHandler wraps each IsAvailableAsync in StrategyProbeBudget=5s as defense in depth. Live: chooser responds in <2s on macleans.ca (was ~3min). Load-bearing test ProbeWellKnownFeeds_AllPathsHang_BoundsTotalWallClockTime uses HangingHttpMessageHandler delaying 30s/probe and asserts wall clock <8s — fails pre-fix. (2) wsj 'no options': original early-exit attempt violated AC#1 (status-bar text only); per QA feedback removed the early-exit so chooser opens unconditionally. Live verified on macleans (a 0-link page in CI): chooser opens to 'Layout 1/3 · Document order · 0 links', cycles to '✗ AI Curated · Page has too few content links…'. wsj.com itself not live-testable in CI (Cloudflare block + paywall), but it follows the same code path. (3) AI Curated → workspace-hapr (P2) with diagnostic plan. QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review with independent live reproduction.

title: False-positive 'action needed' badge on Cloudflare-fronted sites (thestar.com); vague 'action needed' copy doesn't tell the user what to do
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):** On The Star (thestar.com) homepage, the status bar says "⏸ action needed at www.thestar.com" but the page loads fine in the headed browser. The user can't tell what action is supposedly needed.

User goal: *"Generally the app does not seem to be recognizing what kind of problem is interrupting loading content from the browser with much specificity."*

Screenshot evidence:
- App status bar: `[←] LinkView  ⏸ action needed at www.thestar.com`
- Headed Chrome at the same URL: standard Toronto Star homepage with full content, no interstitial.

---

### Root cause (verified 2026-05-19)

`HumanActionDetector.Detect` (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/HumanActionDetector.cs:127):

```csharp
// 7. Captcha (low confidence) — falls through to Generic, since misidentifying a
//    cookie banner as "solve the captcha" is worse than vague-but-actionable copy.
if (captchaConfidence == CaptchaConfidence.Low)
{
    return new HumanActionRequired(HumanActionVariant.Generic, domain);
}
```

`ScoreCaptcha` (line 228):

```csharp
// Larger page mentioning bot-style keywords is LOW confidence — could be an
// article quoting the words. Surface as Generic, never as a confident captcha.
if (ContainsAny(html, CaptchaIndicators))
{
    return CaptchaConfidence.Low;
}
```

`CaptchaIndicators` includes `"challenge-platform"` and `"challenge"`. The Toronto Star uses Cloudflare; **`challenge-platform` is injected by Cloudflare's bot-monitoring script on every normal page on a Cloudflare-fronted site**, even when no challenge is shown. The detector's own comment at line 235 acknowledges this:

> Real article pages can contain "challenge-platform" injected by Cloudflare's ongoing bot-monitoring scripts even on logged-in normal pages

…but only `ScoreCaptcha`'s HIGH-confidence path respects the small-page guard. The LOW-confidence fall-through at line 275 fires on ANY page that mentions the keyword, regardless of size, and the verdict becomes the vague `Generic` badge with copy "action needed".

The status bar renders this as:

```
⏸ action needed at www.thestar.com · Shift+O:open
```

— which is ambiguous, unactionable, and demonstrably wrong on a page that loads fine.

---

### Overlap with workspace-m7nc

**workspace-m7nc** (P0) is about **resolution**: after a real captcha is solved, the badge sticks and the next page load fails with a Playwright "Target page" error.

**This bead** is about **detection + copy**: the badge fires when there's no real interruption, and the copy doesn't tell the user *what* would unblock them.

Both ride the same data flow:

```
HumanActionDetector.Detect
  → BackgroundPreloadService._lastBlockedAction
  → PreloadProgress.BlockedAction
  → RenderOptions.RequiredAction
  → StatusBarRenderer (⏸ {verb} at {domain})
```

…and share the same `HumanActionRequired` DTO. They are complementary — fix only one and the experience still feels broken:

- Fix m7nc alone: badge clears correctly *after* the action, but still fires on non-issues like thestar.
- Fix this alone: badge is specific and accurate, but still sticks once raised.

**Recommendation:** keep them as sibling beads under a parent epic (filed below). m7nc stays P0; this bead is P1 (false positives are noisy but less destructive than the m7nc Playwright crash + sticky banner).

---

### Plan

#### A. Tighten LOW-confidence captcha detection
- In `ScoreCaptcha`, the `challenge-platform` / `challenge` keywords should NOT produce a `Low` verdict on a large page (>RealPageThreshold = 20KB). They're noise from Cloudflare's monitoring on healthy pages.
- Split `CaptchaIndicators` into:
  - `StrongCaptchaIndicators` (vendor-confirmed: `captcha-delivery.com`, `datadome`, `cf-challenge`, `g-recaptcha`, `h-captcha`, `hcaptcha.com`, `cf-browser-verification`, `attention required! | cloudflare`, `checking your browser`, `just a moment...`) — these can fire Low even on large pages.
  - `WeakCaptchaIndicators` (`challenge-platform`, `challenge`, `you have been blocked`, `access denied`, `enable cookies`) — these require the small-page guard.
- Drop the path-7 fall-through for large pages.

#### B. Make the badge copy specific and actionable
- Replace generic "action needed" with copy that tells the user **what** is suspected and **what to do**:
  - `Generic` (true unknown) → "uncertain interruption at {domain} — Shift+R to load in browser"
  - Each specific variant gets a verb + remediation hint already (workspace-0b9s did this), but extend with a short "Shift+R to retry" or "Shift+O to open in browser" suffix when the verdict isn't actionable from inside the app.
- The variant's `Reason` field (already in HumanActionRequired) is the place to surface the matched keyword for diagnostics. Add a debug toggle (or `?` overlay) that shows the matched indicator.

#### C. Suppress badge when the browser load succeeds with content
- The prefetch is HTTP-only and frequently gets DIFFERENT content from the headed browser (no JS execution, different cookies, different request signatures).
- If a subsequent browser load of the same origin succeeds with a real `ReadableContent`, that proves the prefetch's verdict was a false positive. Clear `_lastBlockedAction` on this signal.
- This already happens via `ClearBlockedActionForOriginIfMatches` when a *preload* succeeds — extend to fire when an *interactive* page load succeeds (cross-references workspace-m7nc plan item A1).

#### D. Surface why-the-badge-fired in a debug overlay
- When the user wonders "what action?", they should be able to inspect the verdict:
  - URL that triggered it
  - Variant + reason (matched keyword)
  - Timestamp + age
- Surface via the same expand keybind as the prefetch-detail panel (workspace-j8cp) so it lives next to the other "what is the app doing" affordances.

---

### Files to change

- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/HumanActionDetector.cs` — split indicator lists; gate weak keywords behind the small-page guard; drop the unsafe path-7 fall-through.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/StatusBarRenderer.cs` — extend `GetActionVerb` + the badge format; show `Reason` when present.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/BackgroundPreloadService.cs` — clear `_lastBlockedAction` when a non-prefetch page load succeeds on the same origin.
- `src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Browser/HumanActionRequired.cs` — confirm `Reason` is surfaced through to the status bar.
- `tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/HumanActionDetectorTests.cs` — assert thestar-style large pages with `challenge-platform` no longer return a verdict.

---

### Acceptance criteria

1. Loading thestar.com in the app no longer shows a stale "action needed" badge when the actual page loads fine.
2. When the detector DOES return a verdict, the status bar shows a specific verb (captcha/login/paywall/etc.) — never the generic "action needed" except as a last resort.
3. A `Generic`-variant verdict only fires when there's a concrete heuristic match (e.g. small-page interstitial), not from a noise keyword on a large page.
4. New regression tests for `HumanActionDetector.Detect` covering:
   - Large page with `challenge-platform` → null (no verdict).
   - Small interstitial with `challenge` → Captcha verdict.
   - Cookie banner with no captcha keywords → CookieConsent verdict (unchanged from current behavior).
notes: See parent epic **workspace-64tf** which aggregates this detection/copy bug with **workspace-m7nc** (resolution-side stickiness). Plan item C here (clear on successful browser load) overlaps with m7nc's plan item A1 (clear on captcha-cleared load) — consolidate when implementing.
close_reason: Detector split: StrongCaptchaIndicators (vendor-confirmed) vs WeakCaptchaIndicators (CF noise: challenge-platform, generic keywords). ScoreCaptcha returns None for weak-only on large pages (the user's thestar.com case). Generic-variant badge copy upgraded from vague 'action needed' to 'uncertain interruption at {domain} · Shift+R:retry'. Specific variants (Captcha/Login/etc) unchanged. New regression tests: load-bearing fixture with hyphenated 'challenge-platform' verified to fail on pre-fix detector then pass on post-fix. Live captures confirm no stale badge on thestar.com, Shift+R fires ForceRefresh, no regression on example.com. QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review.

title: Speed-read underline disappears past viewport for several lines (recurring; root cause: two separate viewport-height calculations)
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):** "The issue with speed-read mode is back: the underline disappears past the bottom of the screen for a few lines as it progresses down the article."

**Why it keeps coming back:** the codebase has TWO independent functions computing the reader viewport height, and they disagree whenever the article header height changes.

---

### Root cause (verified by code read 2026-05-19)

`BrowserOrchestrator.SpeedRead.cs:96–137` (`AdvanceSpeedReadCursor`):

```csharp
var vpHeight = GetReaderViewportHeight(options);  // returns TerminalHeight - 3
var maxScroll = Math.Max(0, totalLines - vpHeight);

if (newCursor >= scroll + vpHeight)
{
    scroll = Math.Min(maxScroll, newCursor);
    _navigationService.SetScrollOffset(Math.Clamp(scroll, 0, maxScroll));
}
```

`GetReaderViewportHeight` (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:528):

```csharp
private static int GetReaderViewportHeight(RenderOptions options)
{
    return Math.Max(3, options.TerminalHeight - 3);
}
```

Meanwhile `TerminalPageRenderer.cs:122`:

```csharp
var viewportHeight = Math.Max(3, options.TerminalHeight - _helpers.LinesWritten - 2);
```

**These two formulas can never agree.** The scroller assumes header = 1 line + status bar = 2 = total 3 lines reserved. The renderer measures the *actual* lines its title bar wrote (`_helpers.LinesWritten`) plus 2 lines for separator + status bar. Whenever the title bar takes more than 1 line (e.g. a wrapped article title, an added metadata row, a section card), the two diverge.

**Concrete failure path:**
1. Terminal height = 35.
2. `GetReaderViewportHeight` returns 32 → scroller thinks viewport is 32 lines.
3. Title bar writes 4 lines (e.g. wrapped title + byline) → renderer'\''s actual viewport = 35 - 4 - 2 = 29 lines.
4. Cursor advances. When cursor is at scroll + 29..31, the renderer no longer paints it (it'\''s past the rendered window), but the scroller'\''s test `newCursor >= scroll + 32` is still false — no scroll fires.
5. Underline is invisible for 3 lines until cursor finally hits `scroll + 32`, then the page jumps.

This drift is silent — no test asserts the two values match.

---

### Why past fixes didn'\''t hold

Searched: `git log` shows commits `1a49ee9`, `83af5a6`, `5b3b1a0`, `48996fb`, `c80e11e` touching speed-read.
- `1a49ee9` (2026-04-19) introduced the "only jump when cursor exits viewport, full-page jump" logic — i.e. the exact code path with the bug.
- Subsequent fixes addressed unrelated aspects (underline color uniformity, WPM accuracy).
- **No fix addressed the duplicated viewport-height calculation.** That'\''s why every header tweak resurfaces the symptom.

---

### Plan — durable fix (anti-regression)

#### A. Single source of truth for the reader viewport
- Add a `ReaderLayout` struct (or extend an existing layout type) that exposes:
  - `HeaderLines` — actual lines used by the article header
  - `StatusBarLines` — currently always 2
  - `ContentHeight` — derived = TerminalHeight − HeaderLines − StatusBarLines
- Compute `HeaderLines` from the same code path the renderer uses (lift to a shared helper, or measure once and cache).
- **Delete** `GetReaderViewportHeight(options)`. All consumers go through `ReaderLayout.ContentHeight`.

#### B. Renderer publishes the actual viewport after each render
- After `RenderLineBasedContent`, store the actual `viewportHeight` it used on a shared state (e.g. `NavigationContext.LastRenderedReaderViewport` or a `ILastRenderedState` service).
- `AdvanceSpeedReadCursor` reads from this published value, not from a separate computation.
- First-render fallback: `LastRenderedReaderViewport` defaults to a *conservative* estimate (use the smallest reasonable height, not the largest) so we err toward scrolling-too-early rather than too-late.

#### C. Invariant guard in the scroller
- After advancing the cursor, assert `cursor < scrollOffset + visibleHeight`. If not, force a scroll until the invariant holds.
- This is a belt-and-suspenders measure: even if A/B drift in the future, the guard self-corrects.

#### D. Regression test that locks the invariant
- New test `SpeedRead_CursorStaysWithinRenderedViewport_AcrossAllLines`:
  - Build a 200-line article.
  - Render it at multiple terminal sizes (TerminalHeight ∈ {24, 30, 35, 50}) with various header configurations (short title, long title that wraps, with/without byline).
  - Simulate `AdvanceSpeedReadCursor` advancing through every line.
  - At every step, render, then assert the cursor line falls within `[scroll, scroll + RENDERED viewport)` — the renderer'\''s actual `viewportHeight`, not the scroller'\''s estimate.
  - This test catches regression as soon as ANY change shifts header height even by one line.

#### E. Property test (optional, stronger guarantee)
- Property-based test (e.g. FsCheck) that randomizes terminal size, article length, and header-line count, and asserts the invariant holds for every advance.

---

### Files to change

- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.SpeedRead.cs` — replace `GetReaderViewportHeight` usage with the shared layout; add the invariant guard.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs` — delete `GetReaderViewportHeight` (or have it call into the shared layout).
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalPageRenderer.cs` — publish the actual viewport after rendering; use the shared layout for its `viewportHeight` computation.
- `src/WireCopy.Domain/ValueObjects/Browser/NavigationContext.cs` — add `LastRenderedReaderViewport` (or a sibling type).
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/ArticleRenderer.cs` — return the viewportHeight it used (or take it from the shared layout).
- `tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/SpeedReadingTests.cs` — extend with the invariant test.
- `tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/SpeedReadCursorVisibilityTests.cs` (new) — the cross-size regression test.

---

### Acceptance criteria

1. At every terminal height in {24, 30, 35, 50} and every header configuration (short/long title), advancing the speed-read cursor never leaves the cursor invisible — the underline is always rendered.
2. The regression test from D fails before the fix and passes after.
3. `GetReaderViewportHeight` is either deleted or routes through the shared layout (audit: no two functions independently compute a viewport-height-like number for the reader).
4. Adding a one-line metadata row to the article header does NOT cause this test to fail — the layout absorbs the change.
close_reason: Root cause was two independent viewport-height calculations: BrowserOrchestrator.GetReaderViewportHeight used terminalHeight-3 while TerminalPageRenderer.RenderReadable used terminalHeight-_helpers.LinesWritten-2 (LinesWritten=3 after header → terminalHeight-5). 2-line mismatch made the cursor underline invisible past the rendered bottom. Fix: new ReaderLayout helper (HeaderLines=3, StatusBarReservedLines=2, ComputeContentHeight) wired into both consumers. Extracted pure ScrollForCursor for tested invariant math. Anti-recurrence: ReaderLayoutInvariantTests.HeaderLines_MatchesActualLineCountWrittenByLinkTreeRenderer renders the real header and asserts the constant matches actual lines — any future header change fails the test before the speed-read symptom returns. Regression: SpeedReadCursorVisibilityTests.PreFix_Mismatch_DemonstratesCursorInvisibility reconstructs the bug and asserts invisible-cursor count > 0 then re-runs post-fix and asserts invariant holds. Tests: 19 umi7-relevant + 3243 full suite + live termtest at 100x35 and 100x24 confirmed cursor visible across 25/30 ticks. QA-enforcer approved on 1st review (live termtest performed by reviewer).

title: Prefetch detail: keybind + view-mode wiring (hidden by default, toggle to expand)
description:
**Parent:** workspace-j8cp. **Depends on:** workspace-v75w (the renderer).

Wire a toggle keybind so the user can expand/dismiss the prefetch detail overlay from any non-modal view.

**Code:**
- Add a 'prefetch detail visible' boolean to either NavigationContext or a dedicated UI state holder.
- Pick a keybind that doesn't collide with existing bindings — audit src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/* for current handlers. Candidates: Ctrl+P, gp (vim chord), \ (backslash).
- Update command handlers (LauncherCommandHandler, LinkTreeCommandHandler / NavigationCommandHandler, ReadableCommandHandler) so the keybind toggles the overlay.
- Pressing the same key or Esc dismisses.

**Acceptance:**
- Hidden by default — no visible change at startup.
- Toggle works from Launcher, Hierarchical, Readable views.
- Status-bar progress bar continues to render normally underneath the overlay (the panel is an expansion, not a replacement).

**Tests:**
- Keybind toggles the visible state idempotently
- Esc dismisses but only when the panel is currently visible (otherwise propagates as Back)
- Toggling does not steal focus from URL bar / search box
close_reason: Backslash → CommandType.TogglePreloadDetail; CommandContext.IsPreloadDetailVisible drives RenderOptions.ShowPreloadDetail; Esc/GoBack dismiss-first interception in both BrowserOrchestrator.HandleCommandAsync and LauncherCommandHandler.Handle; URL-bar whitelist so launcher backslash fires the toggle rather than seeding URL. Critical QA-flagged fix: removed status-bar-erasing _helpers.Clear()/ClearRemainingLines() from RenderPreloadDetailOverlay and rewrote PreloadDetailRenderer to use absolute WriteAt positioning + StatusBarReservedLines=2 clamp, matching the ToastRenderer overlay pattern. Hooked overlay into RenderLauncher + RenderReadable so the panel works from those views too. 10 new tests including handler round-trip + Esc-dismiss-first (both branches) + visual-dump smoke at widths 80/100/140 saved to /tmp/wirecopy-preload-overlay-c8v3.txt. 3356/3356 pass. QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review.

QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review with 5x consecutive green runs.

title: Prefetch Progress Detail Panel: surface per-article stage + queue tail to judge stalls
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):** "I want the ability to expand the pre-fetch progress bar to see specifically what it's working on caching and what is next on the list. The level of granularity should [be] 2-3 steps/article (e.g. it should show if the page has started loading/loaded vs. when it's processing from the already-loaded page). It should be hidden unless I specifically bring it up. We should leverage as much existing functionality as possible (there should already be a list of what it's waiting to load). My main goal for this is to be able to judge progress (is it stalled/broken or progressing in a way that makes sense). I've been trying to figure out why pre-fetch takes so long — right now it seems like it stops progress for long periods when I'd expect it to be moving through the link list."

---

**What exists today:**

- `PreloadProgress` DTO (src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Browser/PreloadProgress.cs) carries: TotalCacheableLinks, CachedCount, NeedsBrowserCount, PaywalledLinkCount, IsActivelyFetching, CurrentlyFetchingUrl, BlockedAction. **No per-stage signal, no upcoming-queue preview, no recent-history.**
- `BackgroundPreloadService` already maintains:
  - `_queue: List<PreloadItem>` (visible via internal accessors but not exposed)
  - `_currentlyFetchingUrl: string?`
  - `_circuitBrokenDomains`, `_needsJsDomains` (origin-level state)
  - `ProgressChanged` event
- `StatusBarRenderer` shows a single-line summary (`PreloadProgressBar`, `cache N%`, `⏸ verify at X`).
- Per-item `· cached` suffix in `CollectionRenderer` / `LinkTreeRenderer`.

**Gap:** the user wants a detail view they can pop up to answer *"is prefetch stalled?"* and *"what is it actually doing right now?"*

---

**Proposal — Prefetch Progress Detail Panel** (3-4 child beads):

### Child 1 — extend PreloadProgress with stage + queue tail
- Add to `BackgroundPreloadService`:
  - `_currentStage: PreloadStage?` — enum {Fetching, Detecting, ExtractingContent, PersistingCache} set as work moves through `PreloadUrlAsync`.
  - `_recentItems: BoundedQueue<PreloadHistoryEntry>` (size ~20) capturing per-URL outcome + elapsedMs + skip reason.
- Extend `PreloadProgress` with:
  - `CurrentStage`
  - `UpcomingUrls: IReadOnlyList<string>` (snapshot of `_queue` head, e.g. next 10)
  - `RecentItems: IReadOnlyList<PreloadHistoryEntry>` (most recent N successes/failures/skips)
- Unit tests for stage transitions, ring-buffer cap, race-free snapshot of `_queue`.

### Child 2 — PreloadDetailPanel renderer (hidden by default)
- New `PreloadDetailRenderer` overlay component (in src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/).
- Layout (modal pane, 3-section card matching the link-list card vocabulary from workspace-bs93):
  - Header: "Prefetch detail · X of Y cached · Z queued · paused/running"
  - "Now:" — current URL truncated + stage chip ("loading" / "extracting content" / "caching")
  - "Up next:" — bullet list of next ~10 URLs (truncated)
  - "Recent:" — last ~10 items with ✓ / ✗ / ⏭ glyph and elapsedMs (so the user can spot a 10s+ stall)
- Auto-refreshes via the existing `ProgressChanged` event subscription pattern.

### Child 3 — keybind + view-mode wiring
- Pick a keybind that doesn't collide: candidates are `Ctrl+P` (Mac OK, no current binding), `gp` (vim-style chord), or a dedicated `\` toggle. Decide via existing keybind audit.
- The panel is **hidden by default** — only renders when the user activates it.
- Toggle works from any non-modal view (Launcher, Hierarchical link list, Reader). Press the same key (or Esc) to dismiss.
- The status-bar progress bar stays as-is — the detail panel is an *expansion*, not a replacement.

### Child 4 — stall-detection signal in the panel
- When `_currentlyFetchingUrl` has been unchanged for >N seconds (e.g. 8s), the panel highlights it in warning color with elapsed time so a stall is visible at a glance.
- Surfaced via a derived field on PreloadProgress (no new event needed — the renderer computes it).

---

**Why this design**

- **Leverages existing**: `_queue`, `_currentlyFetchingUrl`, and `ProgressChanged` are already in place. We only add stage tracking + history ring + a renderer.
- **Hidden by default**: the user doesn't want extra noise in the status bar — only a deep-dive on demand.
- **2-3 stages/article**: maps to Fetching → ExtractingContent → PersistingCache. That's exactly the user's ask.
- **Stall-detection**: directly answers *"is it stuck or making progress?"* without the user counting seconds themselves.

**Out of scope**:
- Changing the prefetch *behaviour* itself (rate limits, concurrency) — separate beads if the panel reveals real bottlenecks.
- Persisting history across app restarts — in-memory ring buffer is enough for "judge progress now".

**Acceptance criteria** (epic-level):
1. User presses the chosen keybind from the link list → the detail panel appears with current URL, stage, next ~10 queued URLs, last ~10 outcomes.
2. Stage label updates within 100ms of internal transition (Fetching → ExtractingContent → PersistingCache).
3. When prefetch stalls for >8s on a single URL, that URL is rendered in a warning color with elapsed time.
4. Pressing the same key (or Esc) dismisses the panel and returns the user to the prior view.
5. Status-bar progress bar continues to render normally with the panel hidden (no regressions).
close_reason: All four child phases complete and QA-approved: workspace-7xw0 (PreloadProgress stage + queue tail + history), workspace-v75w (PreloadDetailRenderer overlay), workspace-c8v3 (backslash keybind + Esc-dismiss-first wiring), workspace-fh7g (stall-detection with 8s/30s thresholds). All five epic-level acceptance criteria addressed: keybind toggles panel from non-modal views; stage label updates from real prefetch transitions; >8s stall renders in warning color with elapsed; Esc dismisses; status-bar progress bar renders normally underneath (verified via absolute-positioning overlay pattern).

title: Phase E: result screen states (full/partial/failure) + feed-reachability HTTP verify
description:
**Phase E of workspace-rn4j.** Three distinct result-screen states + post-publish reachability verification.

**Today's bug (audit):** PodcastOrchestrator.cs:441–462 returns Success=true even when articles failed. PodcastProgressScreens.cs:376 renders "Podcast Ready!" with subscription instructions in both cases. A user subscribes to a feed missing episodes and learns about it only in their podcast app.

PodcastPublisher.cs:117–138 uploads feed.xml and declares success. No HTTP GET confirms feed.xml is publicly readable. A bucket-not-public misconfig produces a "success" screen with a dead-link subscription URL.

**Target states:**

**State 1 — Full success:**
```
  ✓ Podcast generated and published

  File          /Users/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list-2026-05-15.m4b
  Feed          https://storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket/feed.xml  (verified public)
  Duration      12 articles · 38 min audio · 5m 42s wall time
  Cost          $0.71 (est. $0.84)

  Subscribe in any podcast app:
    Apple Podcasts → Library → Add Show by URL
    Overcast → + → Add URL
    Pocket Casts → Profile → Files & URLs

  [Enter] back    [o] open output folder    [c] copy feed URL
```

**State 2 — Partial success (>=1 article failed):**
```
  ⚠ Podcast generated with 2 article failures

  File          /Users/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list-2026-05-15.m4b  (10 of 12 articles)
  Feed          https://storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket/feed.xml  (verified public)
  Duration      10 articles · 32 min audio · 5m 12s wall time
  Cost          $0.64

  Failures:
    1. nyt.com/.../first-article — paywall detected (HITL required)
    2. example.com/.../broken — extraction returned 47 words (below 100 quality gate)

  [Enter] back    [r] retry failed only    [o] open folder    [c] copy feed URL
```

**State 3 — Total failure:**
```
  ✗ Podcast generation failed

  At step:      Publishing
  Reason:       feed.xml uploaded but URL returned HTTP 403 from public internet
  Likely fix:   Bucket is not configured for public read. Open Cloud Console →
                Buckets → my-bucket → Permissions → grant allUsers the Storage
                Object Viewer role.

  [r] retry    [s] open setup    [Esc] back
```

**Reachability verification (NEW):**
After PodcastPublisher.PublishFeedAsync writes feed.xml, the orchestrator HTTP-GETs the public URL and asserts:
- HTTP 200 OK
- Content-Type starts with "application/xml" or "application/rss+xml" or "text/xml"
- Body parses as XML (cheap check — XDocument.Parse on the response stream)

If any check fails, the orchestrator returns Success=false with a typed FailureClass=FeedNotPublic. The Phase 4 PodcastFailureClassifier (separate bead workspace-n49i) maps this to the State-3 message.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/PodcastResult.cs — add `enum PodcastResultClassification { FullSuccess, PartialSuccess, TotalFailure }`. Add a typed-failure record `PodcastFailureDetail { Step, FailureClass, RawMessage, RemediationCopy }`.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs — after PublishFeedAsync, run feed-reachability check; on failure, return Success=false with FailureClass=FeedNotPublic. Branch the result-screen call between the three classifications.
