title: UnitOfWork.CommitTransactionAsync can double-dispose transaction on rollback failure
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Repositories call SaveChangesAsync directly, bypassing UnitOfWork pattern
description:
Both BookmarkRepository and CollectionRepository call _context.SaveChangesAsync() directly inside every repository method (AddAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, SeedDefaultsAsync). This completely bypasses the UnitOfWork pattern that is registered in DI (IUnitOfWork -> UnitOfWork).

Consequences:
1. It is impossible to group multiple repository operations into a single transaction. For example, CollectionService.SaveToCollectionByNameAsync creates a collection then updates it -- each calls SaveChangesAsync independently, so a failure after the first save leaves partial data.
2. The UnitOfWork class and IUnitOfWork interface are dead code -- never used by any consumer in the codebase (verified: no usages outside registration and definition).
3. The repositories' UpdateAsync methods just call SaveChangesAsync() relying on change tracking, which means calling UpdateAsync on an entity loaded from a DIFFERENT scope (different DbContext instance) will silently do nothing.

Fix: Remove SaveChangesAsync calls from repositories, use IUnitOfWork.SaveChangesAsync() in the service layer instead, and wrap multi-step operations in transactions.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: CollectionRepository._lastUsedCollectionId lost on app restart
description: CollectionRepository stores the last-used collection ID in an in-memory field (_lastUsedCollectionId, line 20 in src/TermReader.Persistence/Repositories/CollectionRepository.cs). Since CollectionRepository is registered as Scoped (new instance per scope), this value is lost not only across app restarts but also across DI scopes within the same session. Each time CollectionCommandHandler creates a new scope for a collection operation, it gets a fresh CollectionRepository with _lastUsedCollectionId = null. The default collection preference set by the user is effectively never persisted. This should be stored in the database (e.g., a Settings table) or in a singleton state holder.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: CollectionRepository._lastUsedCollectionId lost between scoped resolutions
description:
CollectionRepository stores _lastUsedCollectionId as an in-memory field with a comment saying 'Persists across the application lifetime (singleton scope)'. However, CollectionRepository is registered as Scoped (AddScoped<ICollectionRepository, CollectionRepository>). Since BrowserOrchestrator correctly creates a new scope for each database operation via IServiceScopeFactory, each scope gets a fresh CollectionRepository instance, and the _lastUsedCollectionId is lost.

Impact: SetDefaultCollectionAsync stores the ID in one scope's instance, but GetDefaultCollectionAsync in a subsequent scope gets a new instance with _lastUsedCollectionId=null, so the default collection preference never persists across operations.

Fix: Either persist _lastUsedCollectionId to the database (add a settings/preferences table), or extract this state into a separate Singleton service that tracks the last-used ID.
close_reason: Duplicate of workspace-9w4 (same _lastUsedCollectionId bug)

title: Collection item reorder not persisted to database
description: Collection.MoveItemUp/MoveItemDown swap items in the in-memory _items List, but the CollectionItem entity has no SortOrder or Position property persisted by EF Core. The CollectionItemConfiguration (src/TermReader.Persistence/Configurations/CollectionItemConfiguration.cs) has an index on (CollectionId, SavedAt), meaning items are implicitly ordered by SavedAt timestamp. When re-loaded from the database, EF Core will return items in SavedAt order, not the user's reordered order, silently losing any reordering the user performed. Either add a SortOrder column to CollectionItem, or persist order through the relationship configuration.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Launcher scroll calculation mismatch between renderer and AdjustLauncherScroll
description: LauncherRenderer.RenderLauncher computes visibleRows and rowHeight using a dynamic formula based on totalRows and availableHeight (lines 61-65), while LauncherCommandHandler.AdjustLauncherScroll uses a fixed formula with hardcoded divisor of 6 (tileHeight = availableHeight / 6) and different header/footer line counts (headerLines=5, statusBarLines=3) vs the renderer (headerLines=LinesWritten which is 2, footerLines=2). This means the scroll handler thinks a different number of rows are visible than what the renderer actually shows. When scrolling near the bottom of a long bookmark list, the selection can scroll off-screen or leave empty space because the two calculations disagree on how many rows fit. Fix: extract the visible-rows calculation into a shared method used by both the renderer and the scroll handler.
close_reason: Already fixed. AdjustLauncherScroll uses LauncherRenderer.ComputeLayout() - the shared method. Both renderer and scroll handler use identical calculations via the LauncherLayout record.

title: '?' (ShowHelp) keybinding never fires due to Shift modifier interception
description: In TerminalInputHandler.MapKeyToCommand, the '?' key is mapped as ConsoleKey.Oem2 at line 187. However, '?' requires Shift+/ on standard keyboards, which means the Shift modifier is set. The Shift modifier check on line 131 intercepts ALL Shift-modified keys first and falls through to the default case (MoveDown) for any key not explicitly listed (G, J, K). Since Oem2 is not in the Shift switch, pressing '?' always produces MoveDown instead of ShowHelp. Fix: add ConsoleKey.Oem2 to the Shift modifier switch case, or handle it as a character-based check (keyInfo.KeyChar == '?') before the MapKeyToCommand call, consistent with how ':', '/', 'N', etc. are handled.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Duplicate persistence layer: TermReader.Persistence and Infrastructure/Persistence
description: The entire persistence layer (AppDbContext, UnitOfWork, Repository, BookmarkRepository, CollectionRepository, entity configurations, AppDbContextFactory) exists in two places: src/TermReader.Persistence/ (namespace TermReader.Persistence) and src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Persistence/ (namespace TermReader.Infrastructure.Persistence). These are nearly identical copies. The DI registers from TermReader.Persistence, but the Infrastructure copy creates ambiguity and maintenance risk. The stale Infrastructure/Persistence/ folder should be deleted entirely since TermReader.Infrastructure.csproj already has a ProjectReference to TermReader.Persistence.

title: Infra: LinkListTtl 1h -> 16h is a news-freshness regression that nulls the section-page TTL feature
description: CacheConfiguration.cs:31 (908b6cb5 port): LinkListTtlSeconds=57600 == DefaultTtlSeconds, so ApplyLinkListTtl (clamp-only) is a no-op with defaults — the 'section pages change frequently' design is silently retired; techmeme headlines can serve 16h stale. In extension mode this widens live-vs-cache skew (CachingPageLoader.cs:43-72 serves the stale tree while the docked browser shows the current page) — the exact misalignment class blg5.25 fought. FIX: keep the absolute-cap (correct), restore a short link-list TTL or stale-while-revalidate, or force-refresh section pages when extension mode docks. Minors, same stream: InMemoryPageCacheTests.cs:481-489/511-514 use Thread.Sleep(1000) inside a 2s TTL (wall-clock flake — inject a clock); run:52-66 stderr blocklist swallows real geckodriver/console.error failures (narrow patterns or tee to a log).
close_reason: Resolved with evidence — the headline claim is INVALID, minors fixed. The 16h LinkListTtl is the USER'S explicit choice, not a regression: workspace-hv8n (closed 2026-06-25) records 'the user expects cached items (including sections) to expire on a ~16h absolute clock' and 'LinkListTtl set to 16h per the user'. Extension-mode live-vs-cache skew is separately covered by workspace-blg5.25.4's alignment check (background re-fetch + evict on mismatch for reader + link list). MINORS FIXED: (1) wall-clock cache tests replaced — internal GetExpiresAtUtc test hook added to InMemoryPageCache; both hv8n tests now assert the absolute-cap/shrink-only behavior on expiry TIMESTAMPS (no Thread.Sleep race, cache suite 39/39); (2) run script: unfiltered stderr now tee'd to logs/stderr-<ts>.log before the noise blocklist, with the path announced — a geckodriver crash or content-script console.error can no longer vanish (bash -n clean).

title: Keys: no single source of truth key<->hint (140+ literals, 12+ files) and the KeyChar-before-Shift dispatch trap survives
description: Root cause of the recurring stale-hint bug class: key labels are free string literals disjoint from the binding switch — 23 StatusKeyHint sites in 4 files, ~30 tier literals in StatusBarRenderer.cs:1004-1035, ~69 tuples in KeybindingPopup.cs:24-116, TerminalPageRenderer HITL variants + StyleHintLine, docs/KEYBINDINGS.md is a third hand-synced copy, StatusComposerTests fixtures hard-code Shift+O so tests stay green with stale keys, and 7 hard-coded chord drives across 5 live scripts (no shared keys.py). AND the structure that killed Shift+L is intact: the KeyChar switch (capitals N,S,A,E) runs before the Shift-modifier switch (TerminalInputHandler.cs:610-649 vs 654-674) with nothing enforcing disjointness — the next capital added can silently kill a binding. FIX: KeyFor(CommandType) registry that renderers/hints interpolate; a static test that every advertised key maps to a non-NoOp command and no Shift-switch char appears in the KeyChar switch; scripts/keys.py constants.
close_reason:
Implemented in commit 42c1e897 (branch workspace-q77e-techmeme-layout-finetune).

WHAT: KeyRegistry.cs is now the single source of truth (CommandType -> canonical key label). TerminalInputHandler exposes MapKeyInfoToCommand/MapKeyToCommandStatic as internal and the goto-chord second-key mapping is extracted into pure ResolveGotoChord (behaviour unchanged) so the chords are checkable. Migrated to interpolate from the registry: KeybindingPopup (atomic + safe-composite rows, all 6 mode tables), StatusBarRenderer hint tiers (per-view + speed/selection/prefetch state tiers), and the StatusKeyHint sites in ViewCommandHandler/BrowserOrchestrator/CollectionCommandHandler. Deliberately-abbreviated forms (b / B, R, I, X, J / K, q / Ctrl+C, Esc, 1-9, :new/:rename) stay literal but are still enforced. StatusComposerTests' hard-coded "Shift+O" alert fixtures now derive from the registry. scripts/keys.py added; the 7 hard-coded 'g l' drives across 5 live scripts (judge_techmeme_curation, test_6yb7_techmeme_live, test_ai_durability, test_romy_wizard_live, test_wylw_wizard_live) call choose_layout(). docs/KEYBINDINGS.md header names KeyRegistry as the label source of truth. No key bindings changed.

ENFORCEMENT TESTS (KeyRegistryEnforcementTests.cs — drive the REAL dispatch, no source parsing):
- KeyCharSwitch_DoesNotShadowAnyShiftBinding: any capital claimed by the KeyChar switch must not also be claimed by the Shift-modifier switch — makes the Shift+L trap a hard failure.
- EveryRegistryLabel_ResolvesToItsOwnCommand: each registry label dispatches back to its command.
- EveryAdvertisedPopupKeystroke_ResolvesToNonNoOp (5 modes) + EveryAdvertisedHintKeystroke_ResolvesToNonNoOp: every popup + hint-tier key resolves to a live non-NoOp binding.

EXACT VERIFICATION PERFORMED:
1. Build: 0 warnings / 0 errors.
2. Full ./dotnet test: 4406 passed / 0 failed / 56 env-skipped (+8 new).
3. Mutation-proved both guards actually bite (not tautological): (a) re-added capital 'B' to the KeyChar switch (collides with Shift+B=GoForward) -> KeyCharSwitch_DoesNotShadowAnyShiftBinding FAILED as expected; (b) pointed ForceRefresh at a bogus "Shift+Z" -> EveryRegistryLabel_ResolvesToItsOwnCommand FAILED with "found NoOp". Both mutations reverted; tree reconfirmed clean; rebuild 0/0.
4. REAL-APP CHECK: launched the live TUI (Debug build) in tmux and pressed '?'. Verified the rendered Launcher popup (Enter, 1-9, o, a, d, J / K, :rename, c, Ctrl+P, q / Ctrl+C) and the Hierarchical popup (Enter, Space, s, A, j / k, h / l, gg / G, v, r / t, g l, /, n / N, R, I, \, o, |, y, b / B, Esc, :) render the registry-sourced labels correctly and aligned within the box border. Read the captured panes directly. (The link page itself did not load — browser unavailable/no network in this env — but the '?' popup paints over it and is the same GetBindings data.)

STALE ADVERTISEMENTS FOUND/FIXED: none in production — every advertised popup/hint key already resolved. The only stale copy was in tests: StatusComposerTests' "Shift+O" fixtures (no such binding); now registry-derived.

NOT VERIFIED LIVE: the status-bar hint tiers in Hierarchical/Readable views (the page did not load without a headful browser/network), and the '1-9' launcher-digit token is resolved in the enforcement test by declaration rather than by driving dispatch (its handler is stateful — launcher-mode + no count-prefix gated — not a pure function; not a collision candidate). Hint-tier labels are covered byte-identically by the green StatusBarHintTests (exact-string assertions like "Enter:open", "s:save", "</>:speed").

title: Infra: NuGetAuditMode=direct globally silences ALL transitive advisories — use advisory-specific suppression
description: Directory.Build.props:16 (00bafc2f) fixed the NU1903 Release failure (SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 transitive HIGH via EF Core Sqlite) by turning off transitive auditing for every project forever. Documented tradeoff but far broader than the problem. FIX: NuGetAuditSuppress Include=<advisory-url> (supported by the pinned .NET 10 SDK) or pin the patched transitive package; restore NuGetAuditMode=all.
close_reason: Implemented + verified (this session). Directory.Build.props: NuGetAuditMode=all restored; the single known-acceptable advisory suppressed BY URL via NuGetAuditSuppress Include=https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2m69-gcr7-jv3q (SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 2.1.11, NU1903, transitive via EF Core Sqlite — fix only in ABI-incompatible 3.x). VERIFIED: dotnet restore --force emits zero NU1903/vulnerability lines and Release build (TreatWarningsAsErrors) passes 0 errors — so future transitive advisories WILL fail Release while this one stays suppressed. Advisory identified live via dotnet list package --vulnerable --include-transitive.

title: Sidecar/headless: delete the dead headless plumbing instead of deadening it (armed-if-revived)
description: Never-headless enforcement is correctly single-choke-point (BrowserSession.cs:1019-1021 hardcoded Headless=false; fallback rethrows :250-263; run:25-35 errors). BUT EffectiveHeadless reads like a resolution while being constant-false, and dead logic keyed on it remains armed: PageLoadRequest.Headless defaults TRUE (PageLoadRequest.cs:20) threading into GetOrCreatePageAsync(bool headless) whose switch (:219) can never fire; 'headless challenge -> retry headed' branches (PageLoadPipeline.cs:782/884); three warmup gates (Program.cs:103/193/319 — which also cause the lazy bundle-id capture bug). A future edit restoring logic to EffectiveHeadless re-arms all of them at once. FIX: delete the headless parameter and dead branches. Also: -32000 parking is verified only under WM-less Xvfb — a real Linux WM may clamp/refuse the move (visible window), Windows treats -32000 as the legacy minimize coordinate; and --disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows keeps a hidden window painting (undocumented battery cost) — document + make park coords/settle delays tunable (Task.Delay(180) at :1392, 60ms x 6 SettleAsync are guessed race constants).
close_reason: Implemented by worktree agent, merged (3fa5c670 -> merge 9e6f7f75), then HARDENED for the headful law in 6f2ea6b1 after user review. Direction of the change: headless is now UNEXPRESSIBLE — EffectiveHeadless, PageLoadRequest.Headless, GetOrCreatePageAsync(bool)/AcquireAsync(bool) params, both dead challenge-retry branches, always-false warmup gates, HumanActionWatcher dead branch, and _pageIsHeadless all DELETED; the single Playwright launch chokepoint hardcodes Headless=false; 8 BrowserHeadlessPolicyTests pin via reflection that no headless knob exists. 6f2ea6b1 additionally purged every misleading 'headless' NAME repo-wide: IHeadlessSectionLoader->IUnattendedSectionLoader (+adapter/DTO — these always used the headful browser), 'Headless load failed'->'Background load failed', BrowserVisibility docs rewritten as legacy-ignored, PickScriptIntegrationTests now launches HEADFUL Chromium under fixture Xvfb (5/5 pass), scripts/screenshot.py headful under its own Xvfb (verified with a real shot, no ambient DISPLAY). Zero headless launches remain in src/tests/scripts. Full suite 4219/0.

title: Sidecar: dock hard-pinned to the primary display — tiling yanks the terminal across monitors
description: SidecarDocker.cs:33 anchors the parked window at global (0,0) (always primary) before measuring; BrowserSession.cs:1379 computes the terminal tile from that display. Terminal on a secondary monitor => browser docks on primary AND the terminal is resized/moved into the primary work area. FIX: anchor to the display the terminal occupies (System Events can report the terminal window's bounds before the anchor move). Note plausibility checks (SidecarGeometry.cs:94-95) can't catch plausible-but-wrong-display reads.
close_reason: Implemented + unit-tested (commit f56edc92). SidecarDocker.PlaceAsync takes an optional anchor: BrowserSession probes the TERMINAL window's position via osascript (macOS + captured bundle-id) and anchors the pre-read move there instead of the global origin — the work-area read resolves to the terminal's display, so the dock and terminal tile stay on the user's monitor. Null anchor preserves the old behavior. Tests: Place_AnchorsAtTerminalPosition_WhenKnown (dock flush inside the SECONDARY display's work area) + Place_AnchorsAtGlobalOrigin_WhenTerminalUnknown. Real multi-monitor macOS behavior not verifiable on this Linux box — flagged for the user's Mac session with 75ng.3.

title: Sidecar: _isDocked=true even when placement was skipped — window left at (0,0) 800x600 over the terminal
description: When SidecarDocker.PlaceAsync returns null the window has already been normalized+moved to (0,0), yet DockWindowAsync still sets _isDocked=true/_userWantsDock=true and returns Docked (BrowserSession.cs:1386) — state lies about the outcome (the exact wired-not-correct pattern the Verification Doctrine bans). FIX: on null placement re-park the window off-screen and return null.
close_reason: Already fixed on branch at e2b97ee2: on null SidecarDocker.PlaceAsync, DockWindowAsync logs, re-parks off-screen (clearing _isDocked), force-refocuses the terminal and returns null; _isDocked/_userWantsDock set only on non-null placement (BrowserSession.cs ~1437-1447). Null path covered by SidecarDockerTests.Place_NoPlausibleDisplay_ReturnsNull_PlacementSkipped; dock integration tests green under xvfb (headful). Bead predates the fix landing.

title: AI layout: incremental refine does not survive wizard exit/re-entry; confirm/repair paths discard accepted refines; save failure is silent success
description: SetupWizard.RunAsync takes no existing config (SetupWizard.cs:55-68): re-entering on a configured site regenerates from scratch — the exact 'small tweak throws away an almost-perfect layout' defect 7d322315 fixed, reintroduced at the session boundary. FIX: seed the preview loop from the saved config; route the first adjustment through RefineLayoutAsync. Also: the confirm-question (:250-257) and degenerate-repair (:774-781) paths call InferPatternFromAnswersAsync with the ORIGINAL proposal, discarding accepted refines, and undoConfig is not updated there. And HierarchyConfigStore.SaveConfigAsync swallows all exceptions (:118-122) while the caller shows '✔ Site set up' (StrategyChooserHandler.cs:932) — full disk looks saved, config vanishes on restart.
close_reason: Implemented + LIVE-VERIFIED (commits f56edc92 + 9e6f7f75). THREE fixes: (1) the refine path was DEAD CODE — 'shape ??=' ran before the repair-vs-refine branch check, so every normal adjustment regenerated from scratch and RefineLayoutAsync was never called; fixed with isRepair captured pre-coalesce, tests updated to pin refine semantics. (2) Wizard RE-ENTRY seeds the preview loop from the SAVED config (existingConfig param + extracted RunPreviewLoopAsync). ACTION DRIVEN headful under Xvfb on live memeorandum.com: g l -> 'Reconfigure with AI' -> 'Your new layout' appeared at the FIRST 0.5s poll with the 'Reading the page' round-1 spinner never shown — zero model calls to reach the preview. (3) confirm-answer regeneration sets undoConfig ('z' restores); SaveConfigAsync returns bool and a failed write shows '⚠ Layout applied, but saving FAILED' instead of '✔ Site set up'. Full suite green.

title: AI layout: MaxLinks=1 lead pin re-verified only at save time — redesign can silently drop N-1 stories
description: The pin's safety condition (every overflow link claimed by a later section) is evaluated once at parse time (OpenAiHierarchyAnalyzer.cs:1552-1569). On revisit the capped section stops at 1 (NavigationTreeBuilder.cs:174-177) and unmatched content is destructively dropped (:205-217): if the downstream selector breaks while the lead selector survives, the lead swallows 1 and drops N-1 — the exact failure 0a7635d6's 'no story is ever lost' claims impossible. FIX: honor MaxLinks at build time only when the overflow actually matches a later section on THIS visit, else lift the cap. Also serialize the cap into BuildRefineUserPrompt (:1062-1078) so refines can't silently drop/add it.
close_reason: Implemented + tested (commit f56edc92). The MaxLinks cap is re-verified at BUILD time on every visit: the builder collects the capped section's full match set, checks every overflow link still matches a LATER section NOW, and lifts the cap (with a warning log) when the downstream claim broke — a redesign can no longer make the pinned lead swallow 1 story and drop N-1. Unit test BuildTreeAsync_CappedLead_LiftsCap_WhenOverflowNoLongerClaimedDownstream: dead downstream selector → lead keeps all 3, zero stories lost; the original 9wm6 cap/re-offer test still green. The refine-prompt serialization sub-item is subsumed: the cap re-derives per parse and is now safe under build-time re-verification either way. Full suite 4219/0.

title: UX/sidecar: 3 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'sidecar' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Auto-engage silently disables after 2 failures with no notification
  fix: Replace the misleading default-case copy when a summon actually failed (vs. no summonable URL) and emit one user-visible line for the no-display branch (1396). A Shift+| retry is unnecessary — | already retries.
  ev: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1421-1427, 1392-1398, 1262/1303-1304; BrowserDockCommandHandler.cs:27-56

[minor] Spotlight miss feedback throttled to once per page
  fix: Keep the throttle but re-arm it on a state change rather than once per page lifetime — e.g. reset _lastHintPageUrl after a subsequent SUCCESSFUL sync so the next genuine miss is reported again, instead of removing throttling outright.
  ev: DockSpotlight.cs:641-650, 516, 532

[minor] Generic 'Couldn't open the live page' message lacks actionable guidance
  fix: Log the swallowed exception in SummonAndDockAsync at Warning (BrowserSession.cs:432) for troubleshooting, and optionally enrich the line 1420 copy with the broad failure class. Low priority.
  ev: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1420, 1392-1399; BrowserSession.cs:430-434
close_reason: All 3 implemented (e8c81979 merged): (1) auto-engage failure copy distinguishes summon-failed vs no-page, the no-display branch emits a visible status line, and the 2-failure auto-disable announces 'auto-open paused for this session (| retries…)' instead of going silent; (2) DockSpotlight miss hint re-arms after a successful sync (_lastHintPageUrl reset on both success paths) — test drives miss→hint, success, miss→hint again; (3) SummonAndDockAsync swallowed exception upgraded to LogWarning and copy names logs/wirecopy-*.log. Verified: xvfb-run full suite 4254/0 incl. DockSpotlightIntegrationTests through real headful Chromium. NOT verified: live broken-display mid-session trigger.

title: UX/speed-read: 2 minor + 1 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'speed-read' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Silent consumption of keypress stops speed-read with no announcement
  fix: Add a brief _navigationService.Announce()/SetStatusMessage('Speed reading stopped') in the consume path at line 570-572 so the stop is explicitly confirmed. Optionally re-dispatch the key as a fresh command after stopping so the user's intended action (e.g. j) isn't silently dropped.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs:565-574

[minor] Space toggles speed-read but is missing from help, and appears broken for pausing
  fix: Add ('Space', 'speed read on/off') to the Readable bindings in KeybindingPopup.cs (~line 63). No loop change is needed — Space already starts and stops speed-read correctly; the proposed special-casing of ToggleSelection in the loop is redundant.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/KeybindingPopup.cs:52-73; BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1033-1044, 565-574

[polish] End of article auto-stop has no announcement
  fix: At BrowserOrchestrator.cs:541-545, before StopSpeedRead(), call _navigationService.Announce with a short-TTL 'Finished reading' message to explicitly confirm the auto-stop, complementing the existing end-of-article footer.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs:541-545; ArticleRenderer.cs:95-101
close_reason: All 3 implemented (4c1b908b merged): keypress-stop announces '⏸ Speed reading stopped' (2s TTL) — re-dispatch deliberately rejected with in-code rationale (Space would restart what it stopped; q would quit from a reflex press); ('Space','speed read on/off') added to Readable keybinding popup; end-of-article auto-stop announces '✓ Finished reading' (2.5s). Verified: popup tests, full suite 4381/0 post-merge. NOT verified live: the two RunAsync-loop announcements verified by code path only — no harness reaches that loop; not eyeballed on a real terminal.

title: UX/search: 6 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'search' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Silent failure when search yields no matches
  fix: In ScrollToSearchMatch, when matches.Count == 0 (in the active view branch), call _navigationService.SetStatusMessage($"No matches for \"{searchQuery}\"") before returning so HandleSearch's render shows it.
  ev: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1714-1726; SearchCommandHandler.cs:35-41

[minor] No match count or position indicator in status bar
  fix: Add SearchMatchCount to NavigationContext and a backing field/setter in NavigationService; set it in all ScrollToSearchMatch branches (matches.Count). In StatusBarRenderer.cs:476 render `/{query} {index+1}/{count} (n/N)` when count>0, `/{query} 0 matches` when 0.
  ev: NavigationContext.cs:64-72; StatusBarRenderer.cs:466-482; BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1705-1766

[minor] Search query persists across page navigation without clearing
  fix: In NavigateTo (after line 145) add `_searchQuery = null; _searchMatchIndex = 0;`. GoBack/GoForward restore prior pages, so clearing there is a judgment call; at minimum clear on NavigateTo (new-page case) to stop the stale status display.
  ev: NavigationService.cs:127-150, 166-220, 601-604

[minor] Search query persists when switching views (Reader <-> Link)
  fix: If clearing is desired, add `_searchQuery = null; _searchMatchIndex = 0;` in SetViewMode (NavigationService.cs:588). Consider that persisting across views may be intentional — confirm desired UX before changing.
  ev: NavigationService.cs:586-596

[minor] No visual match highlighting in hierarchical (link tree) view
  fix: In RenderLinkNode, when context.SearchQuery is set, highlight the matching substring within displayText. Note the node line is composed inline with prefix/indent/color and Reset, so WriteLineWithHighlight (whole-line) won't fit directly — pass the SearchQuery into the renderer and apply substring highlighting only to the displayText segment, preserving the selected-row background.
  ev: LinkTreeRenderer.cs:110-111; ArticleRenderer.cs:84; RenderHelpers.cs:412,735

[minor] No feedback when pressing n/N on zero-match search
  fix: Fix once at the source: have ScrollToSearchMatch set a 'No matches' status message when matches.Count == 0. That covers both HandleSearch and HandleSearchNext/Previous without duplicating logic in the handlers.
  ev: SearchCommandHandler.cs:44-72; BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1714-1726
close_reason: All 6 implemented (8d719082 merged): ReportSearchMatchOutcome sets 'No matches for "q"' (Error severity so teach hints can't swallow it) from every ScrollToSearchMatch branch incl. n/N; SearchMatchCount in NavigationContext + status bar renders '/query 2/14 (n/N)' and '/query 0 matches'; NavigateTo clears search state (GoBack/GoForward keep it per bead); SetViewMode AND ToggleViewMode clear (toggle is the real Reader↔Link path); link-tree substring highlighting via HighlightSegment (visible-length-preserving, restores selected-row background) wired through the REAL render path BuildCardLine — bead's cited RenderLinkNode has zero callers (premise corrected with evidence), wired there too for completeness. Verified: highlight render tests incl. background restoration + layout-unchanged, ComposeStatusLine indicator tests, clearing tests, full suite 4359/0. NOT verified live: /-search on a real terminal not driven.

title: UX/scheduling: 4 minor + 4 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'scheduling' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Silent no-op when selecting existing step for editing
  fix: Either drop 'Enter select' from the source-row hint (line 317) so existing rows clearly only support 'x remove', or on selecting an existing row show a one-line status like 'To reorder, remove (x) and re-add'. Best: open a small per-step menu (change TakeMode / required / remove).
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:331-333 (no-op continue) and :317 (hint advertises 'Enter select')

[minor] Unexplained 'needs reconfigure' warning symbol
  fix: Expand the label to name affected steps (e.g. '⚠ needs reconfigure: News, Top 5'), or when the user edits a flagged recipe, surface a status listing the broken steps and how to fix (re-add the section). AnyStepNeedsReconfigureAsync already enumerates steps (line 232-244) — return the offending step names instead of a bool.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:179 and AnyStepNeedsReconfigureAsync at :232-244

[minor] Ephemeral run-now feedback; 'launcher badge' jargon
  fix: Replace 'watch the launcher badge' with plain language ('Running in the background — results will appear here'), and after 'R' (or on a timer/return) reload recipes so the row's last-result updates in place. Optionally have StartInBackground report completion back to refresh the status line with '✓/✗'.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:99-103 (message), :193-200 (last-result line), :44 + :202-230 (badge ack)

[minor] Misleading 'Done' button state in step builder
  fix: Make the Done label reflect the SAME condition the validation checks: show '✓ Done (needs ≥1 required step)' whenever `steps.Count == 0 || !steps.Any(s => s.Required)`, not just when count is zero. Consider rendering it visually disabled until satisfied.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:315 (label keyed only on Count) vs :348 (validation also requires a Required step)

[polish] Verbose 'needs OpenAI key' message assumes launcher knowledge
  fix: Reword to name the destination plainly, e.g. 'Auto-setup needs an OpenAI API key (set it in Config / AI settings), or open this site (Ctrl+L) and run the Setup Wizard.' Avoid bare 'the launcher'.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:386-388

[polish] Time-of-day input format / timezone unclear
  fix: Append 'local time' to the prompt label. Validation already accepts both 24h and AM/PM via TimeOnly.TryParse, so no error-message change is needed for the AM case; just clarify the timezone.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:552-553

[polish] No confirmation or feedback when removing a step
  fix: Show a transient status 'Removed step: {SectionName}' after RemoveAt (:326). A full confirm dialog is unnecessary given nothing is persisted yet.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:324-327 (silent RemoveAt) vs delete-recipe confirm at :110-112

[polish] Empty-state prompt vague about what 'sections' are
  fix: Add a brief gloss, e.g. 'pulls saved sections (like "Top Stories") from your bookmarked sites…' and note sections are defined per-site via the Ctrl+L setup wizard.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ScheduleCommandHandler.cs:162
close_reason: All 8 items implemented (merge 6e21e234, agent commit c6bc6d3c): per-step edit menu (take mode/required/remove) replaces silent no-op Enter; StepsNeedingReconfigureAsync names offending steps in the ⚠ label; run-now message plain-language + list re-read on keypress so last-run updates in place; Done label mirrors the real validation (needs ≥1 required step); OpenAI-key copy names real destinations (merged with newer 'g l' wizard binding from HEAD, not stale Ctrl+L); 'local time' in time prompt; 'Removed step: X' status; empty-state glosses sections. Verified: full suite 4132 passed/0 failed in agent worktree; 48 schedule tests re-run green post-merge. NOT verified live: interactive overlay flows not driven on a real TUI screen (no harness for the schedule overlay input loop).

title: UX/reader-view: 2 minor + 3 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'reader-view' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] No visual feedback while typing numeric count prefix
  fix: In WaitForInputCoreAsync after accumulating _numericPrefix (around line 566), call _navigationService.Announce/SetStatusMessage with the pending count (e.g. show '10' or '10x' transiently), and clear it when the motion key resolves at line 582-583.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalInputHandler.cs:564-568

[polish] Width adjustment hints always shown, even when at bounds
  fix: Capture the pre-clamp width; if unchanged, append '(maximum)'/'(minimum)' to the announcement text (e.g. 'Width 120 (maximum)'). Optionally omit the now-inert hint.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ViewCommandHandler.cs:59-93

[polish] No feedback when scroll reaches document bounds
  fix: Optional: in MoveReaderCursor detect when newCursor==0 or ==totalLines-1 and the cursor did not move, then Announce a transient 'Top'/'Bottom of article'. Low priority given the existing progress indicator.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/NavigationCommandHandler.cs:814-838

[minor] End-of-article footer only appears for short articles
  fix: Append the footer lines (— end —, word count, read time) to the cached line list itself so they scroll into view as real content at the bottom, rather than only painting them when spare viewport rows exist. Drive a long article to the bottom and assert the '— end —' marker renders.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/ArticleRenderer.cs:92-102

[polish] Width announcement shows new value only, not change delta
  fix: Capture `current` before clamping (already available at lines 61/79) and include it: e.g. $"Width {current} → {ctx.ContentWidthOverride}".
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ViewCommandHandler.cs:65
close_reason: All 5 implemented (4c1b908b merged): vim-style pending-count indicator '10×' painted bottom-right per digit, cleared on motion/chord resolution or abort (guarded by IsInteractive; FormatPendingCount unit-tested — the direct Console paint itself is untestable headlessly); width announcements show 'Width 60 → 70' and '(maximum)/(minimum)' at bounds via shared AnnounceWidthChange; 'Top/Bottom of article' announced when cursor can't move (covers j/k and Ctrl+D/U); end-of-article footer (— end —, word count, read time) appended to the cached line list by LineCacheManager.BuildEndOfArticleFooterLines so it scrolls in as real content on ANY article length, with ContentLineCount boundary so speed-read never reads the footer. Verified: LineCacheEndFooterTests (60-paragraph article, exact 4-line shape, rewrap keeps footer), scroll-bounds tests (announced at bounds, NOT mid-document), width tests, full suite 4381/0. NOT verified live on a real terminal.

title: UX/save-collections: 2 minor + 6 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'save-collections' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] SaveToSpecific prompt shows no existing collections or guidance
  fix: Show existing collection names inline in or before the prompt (e.g. 'Save to collection [Reading List, Favorites]: ') or use an autocomplete selector so users reuse rather than accidentally create collections.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:197; CollectionService.cs:35-41 (creates new collection on unmatched name)

[polish] Inconsistent feedback: SaveToSpecific uses plain message, SaveToCollection uses Announce
  fix: Have SaveToSpecific call Announce('✓', $"Saved to {collectionName}: {title}", new[]{new StatusKeyHint("c","list")}) to match the success pattern.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:90-94 vs :212-213/:221-222; NavigationService.cs:343-351

[minor] Confusing dual behavior of 's' across views
  fix: Document the context-sensitive behavior in help text, and/or rebind 'set default' to a dedicated key (e.g. 'd'/Enter) in CollectionList to remove the overload.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:33-141 (save) vs :142-158 (set default)

[polish] 'Already in collection' message for SaveToSpecific lacks success indicator
  fix: Use Announce('ℹ' or '✓', $"Already in {collectionName}") to mark it as benign/informational and match the success styling.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:221-222

[polish] SaveToSpecific provides no row flash animation
  fix: Capture flash position before the prompt and play SaveFlashAnimation.PlayRow on the non-duplicate save path in HandleSaveToSpecific, mirroring HandleSaveToCollection.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:79-81/166-185 vs :188-235

[polish] SaveToCollection message omits which collection received the item
  fix: Include the destination name in the Announce text, e.g. $"Saved to {defaultName}: {title}", resolving the default collection name before announcing.
  ev: CollectionCommandHandler.cs:89-94

[polish] Help text doesn't clarify 's' dual behavior
  fix: Amend the help line to note the context-sensitive behavior, e.g. 's  Save to Reading List / (in Collections) set as default'.
  ev: TerminalInputHandler.cs:123

[polish] Default collection star not visible outside CollectionList view
  fix: Add a status-bar indicator (e.g. 'Default: Reading List') in Hierarchical/Readable views so the save target for 's' is visible without navigating to Collections.
  ev: CollectionRenderer.cs:76-78
close_reason: 7/8 implemented, 1 skipped with broken premise (7258b408 merged). Prompt lists existing collections ('Save to collection [Reading List, Favorites]: ', width-budgeted); SaveToSpecific uses Announce ✓ with c:list hint; 's' dual behavior documented in help/popup/status tier + set-default now announces '★ Default collection: {name}' (was silent; rebind rejected — d/Enter collide with delete/open); duplicate announces 'ℹ Already in {name}'; row flash on non-duplicate SaveToSpecific; save message names real destination 'Saved to Reading List: {title}' (bead's 'resolve default name' was FALSE — s calls SaveToReadingListAsync hardcoded to Reading List); help line covers dual behavior + fixed stale 'c Reading list' launcher help line. Item 8 (Default: X status-bar badge) SKIPPED: the starred default does not govern the s save target (SaveToDefaultCollectionAsync has zero production callers) — the badge would advertise a save target the code ignores. Verified: prompt string captured from real PromptForInputAsync, announce glyph/keys asserted, full suite 4335/0 post-merge. NOT live-driven; flash animation suppressed under redirected output.

title: UX/podcast: 3 minor + 1 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'podcast' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Cancellation confirmation uses bare text prompt instead of a visual modal
  fix: Replace the PromptForInputAsync call with a small 3-row bordered confirm box mirroring PodcastCostGateModal.RenderBox (summary 'Cancel podcast generation?' + hint '[y] yes  [n] no'), reading a single keystroke via WaitForInputAsync, so the cancel-confirm matches the visual language of the other gates.
  ev: PodcastProgressScreens.cs:776-783

[minor] No 'Cancelling…' feedback while the generation task unwinds after confirm
  fix: Before awaiting generationTask in both the detached (line 369) and attached (line 706) cancel branches, paint a single status line / spinner row ('Cancelling in-flight articles…') so the user sees the cancel was accepted while the task drains.
  ev: PodcastProgressScreens.cs:364-374, 700-714

[minor] Esc means 'cancel' on the cost-gate but 'detach (keeps generating)' on the progress screen
  fix: Optional consistency improvement: add an explicit '[d] detach' key on the progress screen and keep '[x] cancel', so detach is a discoverable verb rather than an overloaded Esc; or accept the current labelled behaviour, which already disambiguates.
  ev: PodcastCostGateModal.cs:76-78 vs PodcastProgressScreens.cs:335-343, 1514-1516

[polish] Invalid keystrokes on modals re-paint silently with no feedback
  fix: Optional: on an unhandled key, briefly flash the box border in the warning colour (palette.GetWarningFg) for one render frame before restoring, giving a subtle 'key ignored' cue. Low priority.
  ev: PodcastCostGateModal.cs:110-112; PodcastMissingKeyModal.cs:87-89
close_reason: All 4 implemented (44186972 merged): cancel-confirm is a bordered single-keystroke modal via shared PodcastInlineBox (y confirms, n/Esc declines), 'Cancelling in-flight articles…' painted before awaiting the draining task in both cancel branches, explicit 'd:detach (keeps generating)' key on the progress screen alongside x:cancel, invalid keys flash the box border in the warning colour for one frame (wired into cost-gate, missing-key and cancel-confirm modals; duplicate RenderBox code deduplicated). Verified: CancellationConfirmModalTests (y/n/Esc/invalid-flash, prompt never used), detach-hint + detach-while-running tests, full suite 4129/0 in worktree; 932 targeted tests green post-merge. NOT verified live: flash perceptibility/box placement not watched on a real terminal; detached-modal 'd' path shares the attached path's condition but wasn't separately driven.

title: UX/open-and-history: 1 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'open-and-history' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] No feedback when forward navigation (Shift+B) has no history
  fix: In HandleGoForward, when GoForward() returns null, call ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage("No forward history") (or a short toast) so the keypress is acknowledged.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/NavigationCommandHandler.cs:309-317
close_reason: Implemented (4c1b908b merged): HandleGoForward null branch sets 'No forward history' and re-renders. Verified: tests assert message + render when GoForward() null, no message when forward history exists and URL changes; full suite 4381/0.

title: UX/onboarding-discovery: 2 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'onboarding-discovery' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Confusing and premature API key setup hint on first launch
  fix: Tighten the copy so it reads as optional and self-explanatory, e.g. '→ Optional: add API keys for podcasts & AI layout · press c', making clear browsing already works. Do not block or over-promote it.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs:800-803,867-869; ShowSetupHint trigger at BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs:86 and SettingsCommandHandler.cs:97-104

[minor] Podcast generation feature is hidden until the Collections view
  fix: When the Reading List/collection has saved items, add a one-line discoverability nudge — e.g. surface 'p: make a podcast from your reading list' in the launcher footer or as a transient announcement the first time a non-empty collection is opened. Keep it conditional on items existing so it never advertises a non-actionable feature.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/KeybindingPopup.cs:88,94-106; StatusBarRenderer.cs:1034-1035; LauncherRenderer.cs:111-132
close_reason: Both implemented (7258b408 merged): setup hint reads '→ Optional: add API keys for podcasts & AI layout · press c'; podcast discoverability via one-shot-per-session transient '🎧 p: make a podcast from this list' (8s TTL) on first NON-EMPTY collection open (footer variant rejected — p is dead at the launcher; empty opens don't consume the one-shot). Verified: nudge state-transition tests (first-open/empty/repeat/empty-then-non-empty), hint copy render test, full suite 4335/0. NOT live-driven.

title: UX/link-tree-nav: 1 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'link-tree-nav' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Help text incorrectly documents Space key behavior in Link Tree View
  fix: Change TerminalInputHandler.cs:84 from 'Space           Toggle expand/collapse' to 'Space           Select / deselect item', matching the actual behavior already documented at line 122. (Optionally also map CommandType.ToggleNode to a key if a collapse-toggle shortcut is desired, since the command is currently dead code.)
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalInputHandler.cs:84 (wrong text) and :702 (Space->ToggleSelection); NavigationCommandHandler.cs:381 + NavigationTree.cs:387-390 (multi-select); CommandType.ToggleNode unmapped (no key binding found)
close_reason: Implemented (4c1b908b merged): TerminalInputHandler help 'Space  Select / deselect item' + same falsehood fixed in Hierarchical KeybindingPopup entry and 2 rows of docs/KEYBINDINGS.md (verified against HandleToggleSelection→ToggleCurrentNodeSelection). ToggleNode key mapping skipped per bead's 'only if trivial and conflict-free' — no conflict-free key available in Hierarchical. Verified: popup/help-text tests assert new text present and 'Toggle expand/collapse' absent; full suite 4381/0.

title: UX/global-consistency: 3 minor + 3 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'global-consistency' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Ctrl+P (cycle theme) keybinding undiscoverable in main views
  fix: Add ('Ctrl+P','cycle theme') to the Hierarchical, Readable, CollectionList, and CollectionItems arrays in KeybindingPopup.cs:28-93. (Adding to the default case is pointless — it is unreachable.)
  ev: KeybindingPopup.cs:104 (Launcher only) vs 28-93; default 107-115 unreachable (ViewMode.cs has 5 values); TerminalInputHandler.cs:683

[minor] Esc (go back) keybinding completely absent from help popup
  fix: Optionally add ('Esc','go back') next to the existing 'b' binding in the modes where go-back applies (KeybindingPopup.cs lines 48,71,79,91) so the Esc alias is discoverable. Adding to the default case is ineffective (unreachable).
  ev: KeybindingPopup.cs:48,71,79,91 ('b' go back present); TerminalInputHandler.cs:711 (Esc=>GoBack); default 107-115 unreachable

[polish] Ctrl+C (quit) keybinding undiscoverable in main views
  fix: If desired, change the 'q' entries to 'q / Ctrl+C' in the five reachable mode arrays (lines 50,72,80,92,105). Low value given 'q' already covers quit discoverability.
  ev: KeybindingPopup.cs:50,72,80,92,105 ('q' only); line 113 default unreachable; TerminalInputHandler.cs:687

[polish] Inconsistent capitalization and phrasing in modal dismissal prompts
  fix: At minimum capitalize KeybindingPopup.cs:203 to 'Press any key to dismiss' for voice consistency. Leave the context-specific verbs (close/back/return) as-is or align only the leading 'Press any key to' stem and drop the ellipsis at SettingsCommandHandler.cs:525.
  ev: KeybindingPopup.cs:203; GcsHelpOverlays.cs:91,155; SettingsCommandHandler.cs:525; PodcastGcsWizard.cs:778

[minor] Inconsistent semantic labels for Esc key in modals
  fix: Relabel PodcastMissingKeyModal.cs:57-58 '[Esc] back' to '[Esc] cancel' to match PodcastCostGateModal and the Outcome.Cancel semantics. Keep ':cancel' in StatusBarRenderer preview mode (already consistent).
  ev: PodcastCostGateModal.cs:77-78; PodcastMissingKeyModal.cs:38,57-58 (Outcome.Cancel = 'abort the generation flow'); StatusBarRenderer.cs:939-940

[polish] Case inconsistency in Ctrl+P key representation across UI
  fix: Change the StatusKeyHint in ViewCommandHandler.HandleCycleTheme from 'Ctrl+p' to 'Ctrl+P' to match the KeybindingPopup convention (standard keyboard notation).
  ev: ViewCommandHandler.cs:124 (StatusKeyHint('Ctrl+p','next')); KeybindingPopup.cs:104; TerminalInputHandler.cs:141
close_reason: All 6 implemented (eacb70ab): Ctrl+P cycle-theme in all 4 main-view popup arrays; ('Esc','go back') beside 'b' in the 4 back-capable modes; 'q / Ctrl+C' quit rows in all 5 reachable modes; 'Press any key to dismiss' capitalized + Settings ellipsis dropped (Gcs overlays already conformed); PodcastMissingKeyModal '[Esc] cancel'; StatusKeyHint 'Ctrl+P'. All verified against actual TerminalInputHandler bindings. Verified: KeybindingPopupTests extended for every added row, full suite 4398/0. NOT verified live on a terminal.

title: UX/launcher: 2 minor + 2 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'launcher' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Silent no-op when trying to delete Reading List slot
  fix: Add an else branch when IsReadingListSlot(idx, bookmarkCount) is true that calls ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage("Reading List can't be deleted — clear it from the collection view") before the re-render at line 234.
  ev: LauncherCommandHandler.cs:195-234 (guard at 197 skips block; re-render at 234 with no message)

[minor] No feedback on successful or failed reorder
  fix: In the catch blocks at LauncherCommandHandler.cs:529-532 and 560-562, call ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage("Couldn't move bookmark") before falling through to the re-render. No success message is needed — the grid already reorders visibly.
  ev: LauncherCommandHandler.cs:508-567 (success re-selects moved item at 522-527/552-558; failure catches at 529-532/560-562 log only)

[polish] Launcher footer hides critical features from first-time discovery
  fix: Optional polish: change the footer hint label at LauncherRenderer.cs:129 from '[?] help' to '[?] all keys' so it reads as a gateway to the full list. No functional change needed.
  ev: LauncherRenderer.cs:122-129 (footer hints); KeybindingPopup.cs:94-106 (full launcher binding list behind '?')

[polish] No status feedback when reorder reaches grid boundary
  fix: Optional: in HandleReorderUp/Down, detect idx already at 0 / Bookmarks.Count-1 and SetStatusMessage("Already at top"/"Already at bottom"). Low priority.
  ev: LauncherCommandHandler.cs:538-567 (no boundary check before MoveBookmarkDownAsync)
close_reason: All 4 implemented (7258b408 merged) + 2 latent bugs the feedback exposed: Reading List delete no-op now explains itself; reorder failures announce 'Couldn't move bookmark'; footer '[?] all keys' (merged with g801's curated hint set — kept 1-9 jump, d stays in ?-help); 'Already at top/bottom' boundary messages. Latent bugs fixed: reorder handlers indexed bookmarks with the raw virtual index (Reading List = virtual slot 1) so reordering past it moved the WRONG bookmark — now mapped via BookmarkIndexFromVirtual/VirtualIndexFromBookmark; launcher footer paints transient status (merge kept HEAD 511u status-over-badge priority; agent's inverted test rewritten). Follow-up worth filing: launcher :rename still uses raw virtual index. Verified: rendered-frame assertions (footer text, boundary messages), right-bookmark-moved service assertions, full suite 4335/0 post-merge. NOT live-driven.

title: UX/cross-cutting: 9 minor + 0 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'cross-cutting' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Terminal resize during modal animation corrupts display without re-render
  fix: Wrap CelebrationAnimation.Play in a try-catch that checks for TerminalResized, and either skip the animation on resize or restart it with new dimensions. Alternatively, make the animation cancellation-aware and exit early if a resize is detected.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs:800-806 — animation is not resize-aware

[minor] View switching during page load leaves LineCacheManager in stale state
  fix: Add a 'loading' flag to CommandContext and prevent view switches while loading. Alternatively, store per-view cache state so line counts and widths are scoped to the view mode rather than shared globally.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/ViewCommandHandler.cs:20-31 — InvalidateLineCache is called immediately without checking if page load is in progress

[minor] URL with special characters (%, &, #) not validated before navigation
  fix: Validate URLs using Uri.TryCreate() before navigation, and either reject invalid URLs with a clear error message or encode/escape problematic characters. Add validation in the NavigateToAsync path.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/NavigationCommandHandler.cs:235-240 — URLs are passed directly without escaping or validation

[minor] Search query persists in prompt across view switches without clearing
  fix: Clear search state in NavigationService.SetViewMode() or SearchCommandHandler when the view mode changes. Store search state per-view so each view has its own active search.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs — search query is global and not view-scoped

[minor] PromptForInputAsync doesn't handle terminal resize mid-typing
  fix: In PromptForInputCoreAsync, check for TerminalResized commands in the same loop as key input, and if resize is detected, exit the prompt gracefully with current input, re-show the prompt on the new terminal dimensions, or display a clear error message.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalInputHandler.cs:253-359 — while loop only reads from _keyChannel and does not check for resize events

[minor] Sidecar disconnection during page load shows generic error without recovery hint
  fix: When sidecar errors occur, append actionable recovery hints: 'Try pressing Shift+I (interactive refresh) to re-attempt, or check that your browser process is still running.' Log the specific disconnection reason.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs — sidecar summon failures disable auto-engage but provide no recovery hint

[minor] Modal dismissal doesn't drain pending input tasks, causing key ghosting
  fix: Call DrainBufferedInput() before exiting any modal handler, or ensure all pending input tasks are properly cancelled/completed before returning from modal handlers.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalInputHandler.cs:386-397 — DrainBufferedInput exists but is not called consistently by all modal dismissal paths

[minor] Very narrow terminal prevents proper rendering of help text in modals
  fix: Check terminal width before rendering modals. If width < 40, either show a 'Terminal too narrow' message and prompt to resize, or reflow help text to multiple lines with better wrapping. Test all modals at 40-column width.
  ev: /workspace/src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/FormField.cs and WizardRunner.cs — no minimum width checks before rendering labels and help text

[minor] Modal cancellation via Escape doesn't restore previous render state
  fix: Before showing a modal, capture a snapshot of the current render state (NavigationContext, scroll offset, selection index). After modal dismissal, explicitly restore this
close_reason: 6 implemented, 3 skipped stale with evidence (eacb70ab): (1) CelebrationAnimation bails on mid-animation dimension change; (3) NavigationUrl.TryNormalize (Uri.TryCreate, http/https, non-empty host) wired into :open, command-line URL fallback and launcher 'o' bar — malformed input rejected with message, complements (not duplicates) LooksLikeUrl; (5) PromptForInputCoreAsync races the resize channel, repaints preserving buffer+cursor, no keystroke lost; (6) transient sidecar summon failure adds 'press | to retry'; (7) DrainBufferedInput after help-popup and settings-panel dismiss (deliberately not on modals handing off to prompts — would eat type-ahead); (8) WizardRunner <40-col 'Terminal too narrow' notice, FormField clamps to viewport width. SKIPPED stale: (2) view-switch already gated during background loads (IsCommandAllowedDuringBackgroundLoad, workspace-v04i) + cache re-invalidated on completion; (4) SetViewMode/ToggleViewMode already ClearSearchState (6z3a); (9) modal-cancel paths re-render via RenderCurrentPageAsync and modals don't mutate scroll/selection. Verified: NavigationUrlTests, malformed-:open test, full suite 4398/0. NOT verified live: resize bail, prompt-resize reflow, narrow-terminal handling not driven on an interactive TTY (Console.SetCursorPosition throws when redirected).

title: UX/errors-loading: 2 minor + 1 polish gaps
description:

[minor] Force refresh silently converts HITL gates to generic errors
  fix: In ForceRefreshAsync, before throwing at line 36, do `if (loadResult.RequiredAction != null) throw new HumanActionRequiredException(loadResult.RequiredAction, loadResult.ErrorMessage);` and add a `catch (HumanActionRequiredException hitl)` above the generic catch that calls _renderer.RenderHumanAction(hitl.RequiredAction, url) and StartHumanActionWatcher(url, hitl.RequiredAction, cancellationToken), mirroring CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync:174-195.

  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:225 vs :50

[minor] Quality retry succeeds silently in background – page changes without awareness

close_reason: All 3 implemented (44186972 merged): ForceRefreshAsync re-throws HITL gates as HumanActionRequiredException and renders the human-action box + watcher instead of a generic error (orchestrator loop test asserts RenderHumanAction received AND RenderError NOT received); interactive refresh announces '✓ Content loaded — manual refresh complete' only when no gate remains; quality-retry improvement branch announces '⟳ Found better content' + PlayDecryptRevealAnimation for parity. title: UX/command-line: 4 minor + 1 polish gaps
description:
Grouped UX gaps for the 'command-line' path (swarm audit; full detail docs/UX-AUDIT.md).

[minor] Unknown/mistyped commands silently treated as URL navigation
  fix: In the default case, only treat the input as a URL when it actually looks like one (already has a scheme, or contains a '.'/':port'/'localhost', and is a single token). Otherwise call ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage($"Unknown command: {command}. Type :help for commands") and re-render instead of navigating.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs:319-322 (default case); NormalizeUrl at 569-578

[minor] Commands with required arguments fail silently when the argument is omitted
  fix: Add a usage status message in the missing-arg branch of each: e.g. ctx.NavigationService.SetStatusMessage("Usage: :open <url>") at 132-134, "Usage: :new <name>" before the 193 re-render, and "Usage: :rename <name>" before the 237 re-render.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs:127-135 (:open), 176-194 (:new), 196-238 (:rename)

[polish] Command-line prompt shows only ':' with no hint about available commands
  fix: Optional polish: append a faint hint to the prompt or status line, e.g. prompt with a dim ' (:help)' suffix, when the command line opens. Not required for correctness.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs:79-80; src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalInputHandler.cs:228-231

[minor] ':help' shows the keybinding popup, not a command-line reference
  fix: Either branch HandleShowHelp on the invocation source (':help' vs '?') to render a command reference (verb, args, one-line description), or add a 'Command line' section to the popup listing the available ':' commands for the current mode.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs:122-124; ViewCommandHandler.cs:142-176; KeybindingPopup.cs:24-116

[minor] Typo'd command with a space becomes a nonsensical URL (e.g. 'https://opne http://...')
  fix: Covered by the claim-1 fix: when the first token is not a known command and the input contains a space (or otherwise doesn't look like a URL), show 'Unknown command: {command}' rather than navigating. A space in a single typed token is a strong signal it is a mistyped command, not a URL.
  ev: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SearchCommandHandler.cs:94-95 (split) and 319-322 (default uses full `input`, not parts[1])
close_reason: All 5 items implemented (merge 6e21e234): LooksLikeUrl heuristic gates the default case — unknown/mistyped commands (incl. space-containing typos) get 'Unknown command: X. Type :help for commands' instead of URL navigation; usage messages for :open/:new/:rename missing args; dim ' (:help)' ghost hint on the ':' prompt; :help now renders a 22-entry command-line reference popup while '?' keeps keybindings. Verified: 18-case LooksLikeUrl theory + exact-status/no-navigation tests + popup content tests, full suite 4132/0. NOT verified live: ghost-hint visual dimness and popup fit not driven on a real terminal.

title: Keybinding review: fix lone-capital/Ctrl-convention bindings + dead Shift+L + help gaps
description: Comprehensive keyboard-shortcut review (2026-06-28). Bindings are hardcoded in TerminalInputHandler.cs (MapKeyInfoToCommand KeyChar switch ~599; MapKeyToCommandStatic Shift/Ctrl branches ~639); NO user-remap config exists. CONCRETE BUGS (taste-independent): (1) Shift+L 'go forward' is DEAD CODE — 'L' is captured as TuneArticleLayout in the KeyChar switch (line 628) before the Shift branch's ConsoleKey.L=>GoForward (651) is reached, so forward-history is unreachable; README still lists Shift+L=forward. (2) Help popup (KeybindingPopup.cs) omits universal bindings gg/G/:/Ctrl+P that the README says are everywhere. (3) 'c' overloaded: Launcher=Setup (LauncherCommandHandler ~32) vs Collections elsewhere (TerminalInputHandler 614). LONE CAPITALS (user dislikes capitals unless paired w/ related lowercase): O=dock (o=open-in-browser unrelated; 'O reads as open' everywhere — USER's main complaint), L=tune-layout (l=expand), E=regen-layout (no e), D=dump-html (d=delete). Mutually-paired/OK: n/N,s/S,Shift+J/K (vs j/k),Shift+P (vs p),Shift+G/gg; borderline Shift+R/Shift+I (refresh pair), A=save-all (save family), Shift+X=clear. CTRL CONVENTION CONFLICTS: Ctrl+L=AI-layout-wizard collides with universal clear/redraw; Ctrl+P=cycle-theme vs previous/print. HELIX (target overlap): already match j/k/h/l, gg/G, Ctrl+d/u, /, n/N. Helix tension: Helix uses Ctrl-o/Ctrl-i for history back/forward and x=select-line — conflicts with user's 'Ctrl+O should mean open'. PROPOSED (letters negotiable): dock O->| (vsplit mnemonic); forward Shift+L(dead)->B (pairs b=back); tune/regen -> e/E pair (e=regenerate, E=tune, both 'fix article layout'); dump-html D-> :dumphtml command; AI wizard Ctrl+L-> off clear-screen key (g-chord or rely on :layout). Consider adding a user-remappable keybindings config.
close_reason: Keybinding remap implemented + verified this session (commit d9954e6b + status-bar fixes in e7039251): dock O->|, dead Shift+L->Shift+B forward, e/E regenerate/tune pair, dump-html off-key (:dump), AI wizard Ctrl+L->'g l' chord. Help popup now shows the universals (gg/G/:/Ctrl+P/1-9). README + new exhaustive docs/KEYBINDINGS.md. Status-bar/HITL stale keys swept (O->|, Ctrl+l->g l). Live-gate scripts updated to the new keys. Unit tests updated; full suite green. NOT done (optional, deliberately deferred): a user-remappable keybindings CONFIG file (bindings remain hardcoded in TerminalInputHandler) and the 'c' launcher/collections overload + Ctrl+P theme — left per the review as separate future items.

title: AI layout: aggregator selector-precision (coverage variance + broad lead selector)
description:
Exposed while testing world-class UX on techmeme (workspace-q77e, 2026-06-28, after techmeme's page shrank 681->271 links overnight). After the cleanliness/durability/steering wins, the remaining defect is SELECTOR PRECISION on aggregators:
1. COVERAGE VARIANCE: across runs the model picks section parentSelectors that match anywhere from 6 to 17 of ~32 content links. Low outliers (e.g. medium-2: 6/32) come from (a) the model over-excluding a real story container (it put 'div.itc1 > div.ifsp' in exclude_selectors) and (b) a headline-container selector that doesn't match most promoted EXTERNAL story links (whose ParentSelector differs from the techmeme headline 'div.ii > strong'). Consistent ~50% (16/32) across minimal/low/medium and demotion on/off — so it's structural, not a regression.
2. BROAD LEAD SELECTOR: the lead/first section often uses 'div.itc1 > div.itc2 > div.item > div.ii > strong' which matches EVERY main headline, so 'Lead' shows 5 stories instead of the single top story. Only 1/3 medium runs used the precise 'div.item:first-child > ... > strong'. World-class lead = exactly one story.
DIRECTIONS: (a) understand the actual ParentSelector of promoted external story links (instrument LinkExtractor or dump links) so derivation/prompt can target the story HREFS, not just headline containers; (b) deterministically tighten a lead section that matches >1 link to its first match (:first-child or position) ; (c) guard against the model excluding a high-coverage story container (sanity-gate exclude_selectors that would drop >X% of high-score links); (d) consider lowering default SetupReasoningEffort to 'low' (measured equally clean + faster — demotion carries cleanliness). Harness: scripts/measure_layout_consistency.py, scripts/judge_techmeme_curation.py.
close_reason: Implemented + verified the two user-visible defects (commit 0a7635d6): (b) single-story lead via HierarchySection.MaxLinks (pinned to 1 only when overflow is claimed downstream, so no story lost — verified live: lead=strong.L5, precise) and (c) over-exclusion guard dropping any exclude rule hiding >25% of high-importance links (kills the 6/32 coverage-collapse outlier — verified live: 26-47/67, no collapse). 4 new unit tests + 2 live techmeme runs. Also fixed all live-gate scripts broken by the keybind remap (C-l->g l, O->|, L->E). REMAINING (lower-value, not blocking): (a) deeper 'target the promoted story HREFs not just headline containers' to lift the ~50% denominator (most uncovered links are related-coverage sublinks, arguably fine) and (d) whether to lower default SetupReasoningEffort to 'low' (measured equally clean+faster; left at medium for lead/structure quality). File a fresh bead if (a)/(d) become priorities.

title: Re-acknowledge main's known browser caveats (separate OS window on macOS; headless no-display fallback) and decide defer vs address
description: Going back to main re-opens the exact issues that motivated the abandoned work: (a) the docked Chromium is a SEPARATE OS window (the single-window concern on macOS — a stray window beside the terminal), and (b) EffectiveHeadless falls back to headless with no DISPLAY (headful under a real display/Xvfb, so fine for normal use, but violates the hard no-headless rule in a display-less env). Decide consciously: accept as-is for now, or open targeted follow-ups (e.g., force-headful always; off-screen/again-embed the window) — WITHOUT reintroducing the extension. This is a decision/triage task, not a commitment to build.
acceptance_criteria: A written decision per caveat (accept / defer / new bead), so the revert is informed and we don't silently hit the same square-peg.
close_reason: DECISION recorded. (1) HEADLESS no-display fallback: ADDRESSED — never-headless is now enforced in code (commit fc7c0879): EffectiveHeadless=false always, single Playwright launch hardcoded headful, the missing-display->headless fallback removed (fails loud), and the run script auto-provides Xvfb. No longer a caveat. (2) Docked browser is a SEPARATE OS WINDOW on macOS (single-window concern): DEFERRED / accepted as-is. The user chose the terminal app knowing this; the prior attempts to solve single-window (image-mirror, native embed, CEF, the extension) are exactly what we're abandoning. Re-opening it now would re-enter the square-peg territory. Accept the separate docked window for the terminal-app baseline; revisit only if the user re-prioritizes single-window — without reintroducing the extension.

title: Port the low-risk isolated improvements: de-flake test (68c4d2f0) + frj2 startup/shutdown perf (e845e45c, d8a6873b), each individually verified
description: Re-apply, ONE AT A TIME with its own build+test: 68c4d2f0 (isolate process-global CWD mutation to de-flake the scheduler-shutdown test — main has ViewCommandHandlerTests); e845e45c (frj2 warmup fire-and-forget + R2R probe on Program.cs/BrowserWarmupStarter); d8a6873b (frj2.9 refocus-skip + frj2.2 bounded-teardown on BrowserSession). Skip any that conflict heavily or depend on branch-only types — these are nice-to-haves, not worth dragging in scaffolding. Document what was ported vs skipped.
acceptance_criteria: Each ported commit applies cleanly (or is hand-applied), builds, and the unit suite stays green; a note records exactly what was ported and what was skipped and why.
close_reason: DONE (commit 523161d0). Ported de-flake 68c4d2f0 (ProcessGlobalStateCollection); SKIPPED frj2 e845e45c (branch-only BrowserWarmupStarter) + d8a6873b (conflicts with no-headless BrowserSession, branch-only teardown tests). ViewCommandHandler 32 tests pass.

title: Toolbar-trigger UX + placeholder copy: browser acts normal until the button is clicked; tab placeholder points to the Wire Copy toolbar button (not 'load unpacked / waiting for overlay')
description: In the spike the tab placeholder still showed overlay-centric copy ('Waiting for the Wire Copy overlay to load', 'Load unpacked'). a94aed0f (reverted) made the placeholder side-panel-aware via data-wc-extid — confirm it restores and reads correctly for the toolbar-trigger model: when the extension is present, tell the user to click the Wire Copy toolbar button to open the app; the app is NOT auto-present. Polish the open UX (title/tooltip on the action).
acceptance_criteria: Fresh launch: normal tab + a placeholder that, with the extension installed, instructs clicking the Wire Copy toolbar button (no 'load unpacked'/'waiting for overlay' copy); clicking opens the panel. Screenshot read.
close_reason: DONE (commit 5e397c99). The restored side-panel-aware placeholder (a94aed0f) already shows, when the extension is present (data-wc-extid), 'Wire Copy runs in the side panel — click the Wire Copy icon in the toolbar'. FIXED the one inaccuracy: it told users to press Alt+Shift+W, but Chrome does NOT auto-bind that command (verified chrome.commands.getAll -> shortcut:''). Now: lead with the toolbar icon (pin from the puzzle-piece menu for one-click), and note the shortcut must be assigned at chrome://extensions/shortcuts. Onboarding step updated to 'click the Wire Copy toolbar icon' instead of the unbound shortcut. The browser-acts-normal-until-clicked model is satisfied (no auto-present; content.js mounts no overlay; the panel opens only on the toolbar gesture). Build green (placeholder rebuilt in the gate run).

title: Re-triage superseded beads once the panel ships: retire overlay-only 06zy(+children) & blg5.28 FACET B; re-scope xink for the panel model
description: With the overlay deleted, several open beads become moot: 06zy (make O sidecar peek instant) + 06zy.1/.2/.3 are overlay-specific and SOLVED by the never-blank panel; blg5.28 FACET B (overlay blanks during load) no longer exists; xink (app-primary OVERLAY hardening) + xink.3/.5 need re-scoping (the panel is app-primary by construction, the tab is the secondary surface). Audit each against the shipped panel and close/delete/rewrite as appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Each of 06zy(+.1/.2/.3), blg5.28, xink(+.3/.5) reviewed against the shipped panel and resolved (closed-as-superseded / deleted / re-scoped) with a grounded reason.
close_reason: DONE. Re-triaged every overlay-era bead against the shipped panel model and closed 8 as superseded: 06zy + 06zy.1/.2/.3 (overlay 'O' peek-instant — solved structurally; panel never reboots on tab nav), blg5.28 FACET B (overlay blank-during-load — impossible; panel is a separate surface, never-blank proven 88/88), xink + xink.3 + xink.5 (app-primary OVERLAY hardening — now structural: panel is always the app surface, ForceFullOverlay preserved as the tab-content directive). Kept OPEN (panel model doesn't supersede them): blg5 epic + 4y80/14yw (extension packaging/permissions/docs + legacy-shell deprecation — still valid; NOTE docs/extension.md should be refreshed for the panel model under 14yw). Verified remaining tracker is clean: only 39cy, blg5(+4y80,14yw), and the unrelated deferred xott remain.

title: Retarget legacy verify-ext gates to the side-panel page model
description: g3y8.8 retired the in-tab overlay and flipped the side-panel default ON. The legacy overlay-coupled verify-ext gates (section, reader, navigate, linkenter/N, hijack, multiload, boot, fullcols, hostresize, navwidth, journey, twophase, cachedpaint, linksfirst, mobiledock, followpath, backsite, stallfull, nofrag, peekoptin, fragbound, reader-recapture, poisonrevisit, crashfull) drive+assert the deleted overlay iframe, so they are OFF by default (VERIFY_LEGACY_GATES guard in scripts/verify-extension.sh) — the default sweep is noext + the 4 sidepanel gates, GREEN. Retarget each to the panel model: open the panel via context.NewPageAsync()+sidepanel.html (helper BringUpSidePanelAsync already exists), type keystrokes into panel.Keyboard (not page.Keyboard), read rows via SidePanelRowsAsync (not the overlay frame), KEEP the page-side outcomes (tab URL, data-wirecopy-spot-*, data-wirecopy-drives, scrollWidth). DELETE the purely-overlay-geometry gates (fullcols/vpdock/mobiledock-geometry/hostresize/navwidth/journey/twophase full-width) — meaningless without an iframe. Verify each by running VERIFY_LEGACY_GATES=1 VERIFY_ONLY_MODE=<mode>. Capture/nav BEHAVIOUR is unchanged by g3y8.8 (only the overlay UI was deleted), so this is mechanical retargeting, not new logic.
notes: MOOT — g3y8.8 was reverted (overlay restored), so the legacy overlay verify gates apply as-is again; no retarget to a panel model is needed. The default verify-extension.sh sweep is back to the legacy overlay gates.

title: Separate overlay reveal from page dock in applyLayout so the strip narrows during a capture sweep
description:
Root cause RC-SWEEP-DEFER (reader/article case; does NOT bite >40-link lists since content.js:603 skips the sweep). applyLayout's blanket early-return at content.js:173 (`if (_captureSweeping) { _layoutDeferredDuringCapture = true; return; }`) returns before the fixed-position overlay width write at 187, so when O drives a navigate+capture of a prose article the ENTIRE reveal is withheld until the hydration sweep ends (up to ~12s, deadline content.js:674; re-applied only at 746-751). The overlay/splitter writes are position:fixed and inert to the page's scroll/IntersectionObserver geometry; only dockPage (191) mutates <html>/<body> and genuinely conflicts with the sweep (the sweep already neutralizes the dock at 645-647 for its whole duration).

Fix: run the fixed-position reveal (overlay width + splitter) ALWAYS; guard ONLY dockPage/undockPage behind _captureSweeping, still setting _layoutDeferredDuringCapture so the post-sweep re-apply (746-751) docks the page. Adjust dedup arming at 199 so the dock-deferred early reveal does not arm _lastApplied and suppress the post-sweep dock.
acceptance_criteria: ./run --web --extension; open the reader on a long prose article (e.g. a 40-paragraph NYT-style page) with peek OFF, while it is still hydrating press O. Assert TWO things: (1) the overlay iframe narrows to the split width immediately (within a few hundred ms), even though the body is still growing; (2) the captured article body still reaches its full paragraph count (no regression of workspace-3f2x.1/.6 — verify the data-wirecopy-captureh paragraph count is the full ~40, not 24-30, proving dockPage stayed deferred). Both must hold; instant reveal alone is not done if the lazy-load capture regresses.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by the side-panel model (epic 39cy, committed). These three root causes (RC-NAV-TEARDOWN/.1, RC-LOAD-GATE/.2, RC-SWEEP-DEFER/.3) are all in the in-tab OVERLAY's 'O' peek path (content.js applyLayout/restoreLayout/dockPage, HandleToggleBrowserDock overlay-dock) — code DELETED in the un-revert (e63166cc). The panel is a separate per-window surface that a tab navigation cannot reboot, so the 'O delay / pops up later' symptom is structurally impossible. PROVEN: real-toolbar gate never-blanks 88/88 post-nav samples across a full live nytimes load (commit b6a2afa1).

title: Sidecar: reveal once on the FIRST link list of a session (soften opt-in), then stay opt-in
description:
USER DIRECTIVE (2026-06-25): the reduced sidecar interjection (xink.6 opt-in peek) is GOOD, but the sidecar should APPEAR THE FIRST TIME a user loads a link list in a session (that auto-appearance stopped when we made it opt-in). Keep everything else opt-in.

CURRENT (verified): BrowserOrchestrator._peekEnabled defaults false; SyncExtensionLayout (:2163-2164) docks split only when _peekEnabled && DecideOverlayLayout==Live; 'O' (:1557) and WIRECOPY_EXT_PEEK_DEFAULT=1 (:209) are the only ways peek turns on. So no link list ever auto-reveals.

PLAN (workflow, adversarially verified):
A. New instance field _firstLinkListRevealed (after :84). Instance = API-child = one session; never reset on nav/reattach/'O'; a new child re-arms.
B. Ctor (:209): when WIRECOPY_EXT_PEEK_DEFAULT==1, also set _firstLinkListRevealed=true (pre-armed gates don't double-count).
C.
