Helper MaybeRevealOnFirstLinkList(page): if (_firstLinkListRevealed || _peekEnabled){_firstLinkListRevealed=true; return false;} if bridge null return; if page.Classification!=LinkList || (LinkTree?.TotalLinks??0)==0 return; _peekEnabled=true; _firstLinkListRevealed=true; return true. CRITICAL HOOK LOCATION: call it BEFORE the tab-drive decision at NavigateToAsync:933 (cached cross-document path, after RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync :932) AND in CompleteNavigation (~:1119, non-cached path). It must NOT be done inside SyncExtensionLayout (:2164): on a cached link list with peek off, the tab is NOT driven (the else branch at :951 skips NavigateAndCaptureAsync), so flipping peek at render time would dock split over a STALE tab (launcher/prev page) — the exact 'docked over the wrong page' failure xink + the lazy-peek logic exist to prevent. Flipping BEFORE :933 makes the existing if(_peekEnabled) branch drive the tab + align, so the revealed pane shows the right page.
D. SyncExtensionLayout UNCHANGED (reads the already-flipped sticky _peekEnabled).
E. Reveal is STICKY (_peekEnabled=true), so 'O' hides it like any peek (no confusing 3rd transient state).

EDGE CASES: first thing opened is an ARTICLE -> one-shot WAITS for the first LinkList (gate on Classification==LinkList && TotalLinks>0, not 'first live content'). User pressed 'O' first -> guard consumes the one-shot, no later re-reveal. Back-to-launcher then 2nd link list -> does NOT re-trigger. Stall/error/crash -> never fires (not LinkList+tree); a stall before any link list leaves the one-shot armed for the first real list after.

INVARIANTS: app-panel-primary (single deliberate full->split SNAP on confirmed content = blessed; no pre-seed); xink.2 (no speculative pre-seed — we flip a flag, render-driven dock still does the work); xink.3 ForceFullOverlay unaffected; cached-paint decoupling preserved (flip before :933 so the tab IS driven for the revealed pane); reattach must NOT reset the flag.

VERIFY (headful; rewrite peekoptin phase, tools/verify-ext Program.cs ~:2444, verify-extension.sh:303 peek-off): STEP 1 INVERT — first link list of a fresh session REVEALS split WITHOUT 'O' (Split>0, Min<=0.75) [RED today, GREEN after]. STEP 1b NEW — press 'O' to hide, open a SECOND different link list -> stays FULL (Split==0, Min>=0.88) [one-shot did not re-fire; user's hide stuck]; 'O' re-shows (sticky). Keep STEP 4 boot-flash + STEP 5 lazy-peek (sequence so peek is OFF before them). launcher/stall/crash stay full (no live content) unchanged. Per Doctrine: assert the fullness OUTCOME across the state CHANGE, screenshot each step, write the exact action+outcome.

On ship: update workspace-xink.5 (gate) + memory ext-sidecar-opt-in-peek.
close_reason: Implemented + verified headful RED->GREEN (peekoptin gate, rewritten). One-shot _firstLinkListRevealed + MaybeRevealOnFirstLinkList flips _peekEnabled on the first page that renders as a link tree (gated on 'renders as a list' incl. Unknown-classified indexes, NOT just LinkList — the linklist-fixture classifies Unknown). Hooked on the cached cross-doc path (before the tab-drive decision) + CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync (uncached first list, the loader already drove the tab) so the revealed split shows the right page. STEP 1 INVERT: first list reveals split (split 29/36, no 'O') [RED on old xink.6 all-full, GREEN now]. STEP 1b: hide->second different list STAYS full (one-shot did not re-fire)->'O' re-shows (sticky). Boot-flash + lazy-peek still green. cachedpaint updated (hide peek after the now-revealing fresh visit) so xink.6 instant cached paint still asserts (sample 1<=2). All gate assertions read overlay-iframe geometry across the state change per the Doctrine. Edge cases hold: article-first waits for the first real list; 'O'-first consumes the one-shot; reattach/back-to-launcher-then-2nd-list does not re-trigger.

title: Shift+I (Interactive refresh) parks forever in extension mode — reads as a stuck Loading page
description:
USER-REPORTED (2026-06-25): the 'I' shortcut doesn't work — it brings up a 'Loading' page that never goes away. CONFIRMED (workflow, every cited line re-read).

ROOT CAUSE: capital I (Shift+I) IS mapped (not a missing binding) — TerminalInputHandler.cs:654 ConsoleKey.I => CommandType.InteractiveRefresh (advertised in help :142). It is an 'interactive refresh': open a HEADED server-side browser so the user can solve a captcha / log in, then re-render. In EXTENSION mode that server-side browser is retired (ExtensionBrowserSession.IsBrowserAvailable==false, ExtensionBrowserSession.cs:38; ExtensionPageLoader.LoadAsync ignores ForceBrowser/Headless, ExtensionPageLoader.cs:39). InteractiveRefreshAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:148-239) has NO extension-mode guard: it renders Loading (:154), requests a headed browser (Headless=false, ForceBrowser=true, :158-160), then UNCONDITIONALLY renders the RenderInteractiveRefresh modal (:191) and BLOCKS on WaitForInputAsync (:193) until Enter (accept, :213-227) or Esc (cancel, :202). No window ever opens, the modal's 'interact with the browser first' instruction points at nothing, and the user — expecting an auto-refresh — never presses Enter, so it sits. Esc clears it but isn't obvious. Dispatch: BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1381-1382 -> NavigationCommandHandler.HandleInteractiveRefresh :496-503 -> ctx.InteractiveRefreshAsync (wired :259).

CORRECTION to the 'Loading' wording: the parked screen is most likely the 'Interactive refresh' modal (TerminalPageRenderer.cs:344-372: 'Page loaded — accept it, or interact with the browser first.' + 'Enter:accept Esc:cancel'), a centered box that looks like a wait screen, NOT literally RenderLoading. The momentary Loading frame (:154) precedes it.

REPRO: extension mode (run --web --extension), open any article (NON-preview reader/link-list), press Shift+I -> the modal appears and sits. CAVEAT to confirm with reporter: on an AI-Curated PREVIEW row, Shift+I instead launches the layout wizard (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1283-1287) — a DIFFERENT path; this report is the normal-screen path.

EXPECTED: in extension mode, I should auto force-refresh the current page (re-capture via the extension + re-render), equivalent to Shift+R, with no manual modal — since there is no server-side browser to interact with.

FIX PLAN: guard InteractiveRefreshAsync at the top (Refresh.cs:148): when no server-side browser is available (_browserSession is not IBrowserSession s || !s.IsBrowserAvailable), do the NON-interactive path — render Loading, LoadAsync(ForceRefresh=true), then go straight to the accept/repaint block (:213-227) WITHOUT rendering the modal (:191) or calling WaitForInputAsync (:193); prefer delegating to the same code HandleForceRefresh/HandleRefresh uses (NavigationCommandHandler.cs:493-494) to avoid duplicating build/repaint. Safety net: never show the modal when IsBrowserAvailable==false. Help copy :142 ('headed browser for captcha/login') is misleading in extension mode — clarify (lower priority). VERIFY (headful, never headless): open a real article in extension mode, press Shift+I — RED today (modal appears + never advances without input), GREEN after (screen clears to refreshed content, no modal); confirm Esc still cancels and a same-screen Shift+R parity.
close_reason: Fixed + verified headful RED->GREEN (multiload gate STEP 4b, Shift+I on an open article). Root cause: InteractiveRefreshAsync rendered the server-browser interaction modal + blocked on WaitForInputAsync even in extension mode where ExtensionBrowserSession.IsBrowserAvailable==false (no window to interact with) — so it parked. Fix: top-of-method guard delegates to ForceRefreshAsync (Shift+R parity) when no server browser is available. RED (guard stashed): sawModal=True, nonce frozen 2->-1, reader replaced by the 295-char modal. GREEN (guard in): sawModal=False, nonce 2->3 (real re-capture), full multiload gate passes. Esc-cancel parity is N/A in extension mode (no modal to cancel); the server-browser interactive path is unchanged.

title: Cache entries never expire — link-list/section TTL slides forward on every revisit (should be an absolute cap)
description:
USER-REPORTED (2026-06-25): cached items are supposed to expire (~16h) but stay indefinitely. CONFIRMED (workflow, every cited line re-read; static analysis, not a live repro).

ROOT CAUSE — sliding/refresh-on-access TTL (not a missing read check, not a bad constant, not missing disk expiry). InMemoryPageCache.UpdateTtl recomputes expiry from NOW on every access instead of anchoring to the original cache time:
  src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/InMemoryPageCache.cs:300-303
    ExpiresAtUtc = DateTime.UtcNow.AddSeconds(ttlSeconds);   // BUG: slides from now, not CachedAtUtc-anchored
ApplyLinkListTtl (InMemoryPageCache.cs:286-288, LinkListTtlSeconds) calls UpdateTtl on every cached READ/revisit of a link-list page:
  - PageLoadPipeline.cs:259 (build-cache HIT branch)
  - BrowserOrchestrator.cs:922 (extension cross-document cached-build paint)
  - BrowserOrchestrator.cs:978 (synchronous fast-path build-cache HIT)
So each revisit pushes ExpiresAtUtc to now+TTL. IsExpired => UtcNow>=ExpiresAtUtc (CacheEntryMetadata.cs:48), so any page reopened within its TTL NEVER expires. The attached build cache rides the same HTML entry (gated only by !IsExpired — TryGetBuildCache InMemoryPageCache.cs:219-229; TryGetValidBuildCache checks it FIRST, BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1068-1070), so the extracted link-tree is kept alive too, and the true fixed-age 16h gates (standalone build dict :244-245, disk build files) are bypassed. UpdateTtl re-persists to disk (:307), so the slid lifetime survives restarts. The eviction sweep (:397) only deletes already-past-expiry entries, which a frequently-visited section page never reaches.

EXPECTED vs ACTUAL config nuance: there are TWO TTLs (CacheConfiguration.cs, no appsettings Cache override): DefaultTtlSeconds=57600 (16h, articles) and LinkListTtlSeconds=3600 (1h, link-lists/sections). Expiry IS correctly enforced on read (TryGet rejects stale, InMemoryPageCache.cs:67-72) and plain article entries / standalone dict / article cache do NOT slide (TryGet only bumps LastAccessedAtUtc for LRU, :75). So the symptom is specifically: the pages the user opens most (home/section/link-list) live forever. NOTE: predates the xink.6 cache-paint work (ApplyLinkListTtl on the revisit path was already there); my recent edits did not introduce it.

REPRO: open a section/home page (classified LinkList); within <1h reopen it; repeat. ExpiresAtUtc advances to now+1h each open; the entry + attached build cache are never evicted, even past 16h and across restarts.

SUGGESTED FIX: make the TTL an ABSOLUTE cap, not read-sliding. Preferred: anchor in UpdateTtl to ExpiresAtUtc = existing.Metadata.CachedAtUtc.AddSeconds(ttlSeconds) and clamp so it can only shrink, never extend. Alternative: stop calling ApplyLinkListTtl on read-time HITs (PageLoadPipeline.cs:259, BrowserOrchestrator.cs:922/978) and apply the link-list TTL only at STORE time (PageLoadPipeline.cs:1055/1058). VERIFY: a test that repeatedly revisiting an entry does NOT push ExpiresAtUtc past CachedAtUtc+TTL, and that the attached build cache + disk copy expire on the same absolute clock. Also reconsider whether link-lists should be 1h or 16h (user said 16h — confirm intended value).
close_reason: Fixed + unit-verified: UpdateTtl now anchors ExpiresAtUtc to CachedAtUtc (absolute cap) with a shrink-only clamp, so repeated revisits no longer slide expiry forward; LinkListTtl set to 16h per the user. New tests ApplyLinkListTtl_IsAbsoluteCap_DoesNotSlideForwardOnRevisit + UpdateTtl_OnlyShrinks_NeverExtendsBeyondCachedAtCap pass; cache suite 39/39.

title: Background re-capture when the live page grows (lazy hydration completes)
description: Defense-in-depth + composes with blg5.21/blg5.25.4. Even with .1's scroll-hydrate, a very slow or interaction-gated body may finish hydrating after the first capture. Add a bounded background task (model on the two-phase swap _qualityRetryTask -> CompleteQualityRetryAsync -> ReplaceCurrent, BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs) that re-captures the live DOM once the live page's scrollHeight/paragraph-count grows materially past what was extracted, re-runs ReadableContentExtractor, and ReplaceCurrent's the reader page with NO scroll/selection reflow when the new extraction is longer. Skip when the bridge is disconnected. Reuse blg5.25.4's alignment machinery if it lands first.
acceptance_criteria: Headful: open an article whose body hydrates late; assert the reader VISIBLY fills in the later paragraphs via a background swap with no scroll jump, and a fully-loaded article shows NO swap (negative case).
close_reason: Implemented + verified headful RED->GREEN (reader-recapture gate). New RunHydrationRecaptureAsync (3rd background swap task, modeled on blg5.25.4's link alignment) re-captures the live DOM in place (ForceRefresh=false => CaptureDomAsync, no reload) up to 3 bounded times; swaps the reader when the re-extracted article grew materially (WordCount >1.15x+20 AND more paragraphs). CompleteHydrationRecaptureAsync preserves the cursor (ReplaceCurrent doesn't reset it; body appends below) so no scroll reflow. POSITIVE (reader-latehydrate.html, body appends ~6s post-capture on a timer): RED with recapture disabled (gainedOmega=False/swapLog=False, reader stuck at intro), GREEN with it (reader gains OMEGA via the swap — child-log validity 'live article grew 286->564 words / 5->10 paragraphs — swapping' confirms it came via recapture not first capture; jumpedToTop=False). NEGATIVE (article.html fully-loaded): no swap (no 'live article grew' log, content stable 2517->2517). 106 pipeline/orchestrator unit tests green. Note: re-capture is bounded (3 attempts) and invisible with peek off (the default — live tab hidden).

title: STOPGAP: teach evalWidthMedia CSS range-syntax so applyPaneMedia stops half-converting (only if blg5.19 viewport fix can't land this cycle)
description:
STOPGAP ONLY — ship only if the structural blg5.19 viewport fix cannot land this cycle; otherwise SKIP and go straight to blg5.22.3 which retires applyPaneMedia for the live page.

In extension/content.js, evalWidthMedia (content.js:160-176) detects width media ONLY via the colon-syntax regex at content.js:165 /\(\s*(max|min)-width\s*:\s*(\d+...)(px|em|rem)\s*\)/g. Verified empirically that it returns widthBased=false for every CSS Media Queries Level 4 RANGE form: (width >= 1024px), (width <= 1040px), (width > N), (width < N), (N <= width <= M), (width: 600px). So applyPaneMedia (content.js:177-192) skips 1639 of NYT's 2949 @media rules (content.js:187 if(!info.widthBased) continue), leaving them keyed to the real 1440px viewport — the half-conversion that collides the masthead.

EXTEND evalWidthMedia to ALSO parse range syntax and the two-sided form, mapping >=,<=,>,< and (width:N) to the same w<px / w>px match logic, and extend the 'rest' cleanup (content.js:174) to strip width/>=/<=/>/< so a pure range query yields rest==='' and widthBased=true. Result: applyPaneMedia converts ALL width breakpoints to paneW consistently (no half-conversion).

KNOWN LIMITS (state in the close note): does NOT fix the box-clamp/positioning overlap that persists with media OFF; recurring CSS-parser arms race (container queries, calc()). This is why it is a stopgap, not the close of blg5.22.
acceptance_criteria: Headful screenshot of docked nytimes.com/section/todayspaper masthead shows the logo and 'Today's paper' label NO LONGER overlapping (their on-screen rects do not horizontally intersect) AND login is reachable (elementFromPoint at the login control returns it) — LOOK at the screenshot crop, do NOT assert 'N rules now widthBased' or 'media converted'. If the header still overlaps from the box clamp, this stopgap is INSUFFICIENT and the bead stays open pointing at blg5.22.3.
close_reason: SKIPPED as designed: this stopgap was conditional ('ship ONLY if the structural blg5.19 viewport fix cannot land this cycle; otherwise SKIP'). The structural fix landed (.19.2 zoom viewport dock + .19.8 applyPaneMedia retirement), verified headful on NYT+theverge — so the half-conversion path it patched is GONE (applyPaneMedia no longer called for the live page). No range-syntax regex work needed.

title: Centralize the Article->Reader switch in navigation-completion; remove the dead handler-level switch and add a non-navigable-selection hint
description:
After r1 makes reader-open a property of navigation completion, remove the now-dead/stale-state reader switch in NavigationCommandHandler.cs: the post-NavigateToAsync HasReadableContent()->SetViewMode(Readable) block at :460-465 (link-tree) and the mirror at :437-443 (collection-items) inspect the PRE-nav CurrentPage in extension mode and cannot open the reader. Reader-open should live ONLY in CompleteNavigation (:909-911) / the deferred fill, gated on the NEWLY loaded page's classification.

SKEPTICAL CONSTRAINT: confirm the NATIVE (non-extension) path still auto-enters reader purely via :909-911 BEFORE deleting the duplicate (it does).

ALSO (separate latent gap): the link-tree branch's `else if(!IsNullOrEmpty(selectedNode.Link.Url))` at :456 has no trailing else, so a story row with empty/whitespace Url silently no-ops = its own 'Enter does nothing'. Add the trailing else to set a status hint ('No link for this story') via the status sink (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:192). Keep group-header expand/collapse (:451-455) unchanged.

Files: NavigationCommandHandler.cs:437-443, :456-466; BrowserOrchestrator.cs:192, :909-911.
acceptance_criteria: Verified HERE: (1) DEAD-CODE REMOVAL — the post-NavigateToAsync HasReadableContent()->SetViewMode(Readable) duplicates in HandleActivateLink (link-tree + collection-items) are removed; reader-open is now solely CompleteNavigation (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1023-1027), gated on the NEWLY loaded page's Article classification. Headful gates confirm both paths still auto-open the reader: VERIFY_MODE=reader (native, scrollTo drive + 'has readable') and VERIFY_MODE=linkentern (extension, story-id=5 reader content) both GREEN. (2) EMPTY-URL HINT — EmptyUrlStoryHintTests (2 passing): Enter on an empty-Url story sets CurrentContext.StatusMessage='No link for this story' and does NOT navigate; Enter on a real-Url story navigates and sets no hint. Group-header expand/collapse path untouched.
notes: PARTIAL: the empty-Url hint is implemented — a story node with empty/whitespace Url now hits a trailing else that sets 'No link for this story' status + renders (NavigationCommandHandler link-tree branch). Builds clean; golden path unaffected. NOT done: removing the dead post-NavigateToAsync HasReadableContent()->Readable switch (:460-465) + the CollectionItems mirror — those depend on blg5.26.1 centralizing reader-open in CompleteNavigation, which is login-gated/not landed. NOT verified: an empty-Url story fixture to SEE the hint. Keep open.
close_reason: Both parts done + verified. Removed the two duplicate reader-switch blocks in NavigationCommandHandler.HandleActivateLink (link-tree :471-476 + collection-items :449-454) that inspected the stale pre-nav CurrentPage (and in extension mode never opened the reader); reader-open now lives only in CompleteNavigation, gated on the newly-loaded page's Article classification. ACTION+OUTCOME: native reader gate (auto-reader via completion seam) GREEN, extension linkenterN gate (reader shows story id=5's STORYMARKER_5 via completion seam) GREEN — no regression from the removal. Empty-Url hint verified by EmptyUrlStoryHintTests (2 passing): empty-Url story → 'No link for this story' status + no nav; real-Url story → navigates, no hint. Build + full reader gates green.

title: Optional backend determinism: send a precise selector / raw on-page href to drive resolution
description: Determinism layer (follow-fix-1 fixes the bug alone; this removes the heuristic on duplicate-href pages). Extend SpotlightTarget (SpotlightTarget.cs:16, readonly record struct) with an optional Selector/RawHref captured at extraction time (LinkInfo.ParentSelector exists, LinkInfo.cs); thread it through DockSpotlight.ResolveTarget (DockSpotlight.cs:164) and HighlightAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1856 currently always selector:null) so content.js's selector branch (content.js:317, implemented but never fed) resolves the exact anchor, then follow-fix-2 still scopes to its heading. url/text remain fallbacks. MUST keep the now-fixed url/text fallback so a stale selector never regresses to no highlight. SKEPTIC: 'selector sent'/'command wired' is not proof — the resolved element must be the headline on a duplicate-href page, and removing the selector must still yield a correct highlight via fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Headful on the committed fixture with a story that has a duplicate-href thumbnail: with the selector plumbed, the highlight lands on the headline anchor's heading, NOT the thumbnail or a container — confirmed by reading the resolved heading text. Then remove the selector hint and SEE a correct highlight still appear via the url/text fallback. selector-sent logs are insufficient — the resolved element must be the headline in both cases.
close_reason: SpotlightTarget.Selector added; DockSpotlight.ResolveTarget threads selected.Link.ParentSelector; SyncExtensionSpotlight/DriveHighlight pass selector:t.Selector. content.js resolves the selector to the SPECIFIC unambiguous anchor INSIDE the container (url AND exact-text required), never the container itself — caught and fixed a real regression where the class-path selector matched the first card and stuck the highlight on story 1 (the gate went red, then green after the fix). VERIFIED headful: with ParentSelector threaded, follow gate GREEN on live macleans 7/7 on-target; the url/text matcher remains authoritative so a stale/over-broad selector never regresses.

title: Element-overlay fallback (document-coord, browser-glued) for engines without the Highlight API or with no text node
description: Fallback when typeof Highlight is undefined or the resolved target has no usable Text node (pure link lists, oddly-wrapped headlines). Port the proven server-side pattern SpotlightScript.cs:91-109: a translucent DevTools-blue box (rgba(106,168,221,.33) + border #1a73e8) appended to document.documentElement (survives body{pointer-events:none}), positioned in DOCUMENT coords (left=rect.left+scrollX-4, top=rect.top+scrollY-4) by a placeSpotlight() re-reading the live rect; capture-phase passive window 'scroll' + 'resize' listeners installed once (guarded flag), rAF-coalesced; ResizeObserver + IntersectionObserver on the target for lazy-load reflow that fires no scroll event; nulled in clearSpotlight so listeners become no-ops. This is the ONLY hand-repositioned path — blg5.20.1's box logic survives here, not as the primary. SKEPTIC: a document-coord box still detaches if the wrong scroller moves (see follow-fix-4) — verify it stays glued under the dock, not just in a bare browser; presence of the box node is not glue.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via ./run --web --extension with the Highlight API force-disabled (feature-flag the fallback on): select a story and SEE a translucent box on the selected headline, then SCROLL the live page up and down and SEE the box stay GLUED to the headline (moves WITH the content, does not freeze on screen) — confirmed by re-reading the anchor's live rect after scroll and the box still overlapping it, plus a screenshot. The box must re-align after a splitter resize. Box-exists / a 'fallback active' log are insufficient — the box must visibly track the element through scroll.
close_reason: Replaced the position:fixed fallback with a document-coord box the browser keeps glued (rect+scroll coords, capture-phase passive scroll/resize listeners rAF-coalesced, ResizeObserver+IntersectionObserver; clearSpotlight nulls the target). forceFallback flag + __wc_force_fb localStorage hook make it triggerable on Chromium. VERIFIED headful on live macleans.ca with the Highlight API forced OFF: the fallback box appears on the selected headline AND shifts EXACTLY 350px for a 350px scroll of the docked page (a frozen box shifts 0) — proving it is GLUED to the content under the dock, not detaching. Screenshot fb-before-scroll.png shows the box on a live headline.

title: Send a precise spotlight target (stable selector / raw on-page href) from the backend
description: OPTIONAL determinism layer (blg5.20 fixes the bug alone; this makes the match deterministic instead of heuristic). BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1761 & :1808 always call HighlightAsync(selector: null, url: t.LinkUrl, text: t.DisplayText). Extend SpotlightTarget (SpotlightTarget.cs:16, a readonly record struct) with an optional Selector or RawHref captured at extraction time (LinkInfo.ParentSelector already exists, LinkInfo.cs:44); thread it through DockSpotlight.ResolveTarget (DockSpotlight.cs:164) and HighlightAsync so content.js's selector branch (content.js:201-203, already implemented but never fed) resolves the exact anchor. url/text remain fallbacks. MUST keep the (now-fixed) url/text fallback so a stale selector never regresses to no box.
acceptance_criteria: With the selector/raw-href plumbed, the box lands on the ONE correct headline anchor even when several anchors share a path or the live href differs from the canonical url — verified by driving a story that has a duplicate-href thumbnail (on the committed fixture) and asserting the box is on the headline anchor, not the thumbnail or a container. A stale/missing selector must still produce a tight box via the blg5.20 fallback (verified by removing the selector hint and re-checking). selector-sent / command-wired logs are INSUFFICIENT — the resolved element must be the headline.
close_reason: Superseded by blg5.23.7 — the box-based spotlight approach is replaced by the browser-maintained CSS Custom Highlight redesign (blg5.23). See that bead for the carried-forward intent.

title: Generalize the skeleton seam into a real-links-first frame (cache rebuild + first fresh extraction)
description: ShowSkeletonPage (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:928-958) proves a links-first frame is renderable but paints an EMPTY tree (:940) and only on full cache miss; cached/extension paths bypass it. Feed the skeleton/first-frame path the real link tree the moment it exists (RebuildFromBuildCacheAsync sets it; B4's links-first phase for fresh loads) so the user gets populated rows in one paint at final layout, then content fills via B6. Must NOT introduce a flash where the synchronous cached path previously painted in one shot (NavigateToAsync comment at :847-848): a cached link list still appears in a single paint at final layout, and an article still lands in the reader with no link-list flash.
acceptance_criteria: Cached link list still appears in exactly one paint at final layout (no empty-then-full flash, no width change). Fresh section shows real rows on first paint (not an empty tree that then fills). Article lands in reader with no intervening link-list flash. Assert by watching the first frame on each path, not by counting render calls.
close_reason: Closed with the net-negative .21.3 cluster (DEPENDS ON .21.3/.21.5). Feeding the skeleton seam a real link tree was part of the net-negative content-split. The outcome it wanted — a cached link list painting in exactly one paint at final layout with no empty-then-full flash, and an article landing in the reader with no link-list flash — is already GREEN: cachedpaint (instant one-paint cached list) + linksfirst (article reader, no preceding link-tree flash). Superseded.

title: PART 1: recover an in-flight highlight dropped on a transient /ws/ext disconnect
description: On a brief /ws/ext drop (service-worker sleep/wake, network blip) the bridge swaps _ready and FailAllPending faults any in-flight command with 'Extension disconnected.' (ExtensionBridge.cs:388-394). A faulted HighlightAsync is swallowed by FireAndForget (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1808/1836-1840) with no retry, while _lastExtensionSpotlight was already set (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:1806). When the socket reconnects WITHOUT a fresh 'ready', the dedup suppresses the re-send (OnExtensionReattached re-pushes only on a 'ready', ExtensionBridge.cs:466) so the follow hangs until the next selection move. Fix: building on result-dedup's continuation, when a highlight faults with the disconnect/timeout exception, clear _lastExtensionSpotlight so the next render re-drives, OR queue a single re-drive once IsConnected flips true — re-deriving the CURRENT target at retry time (never replaying the captured stale one) so a selection that changed during the outage isn't replayed.
acceptance_criteria: Headful via ./run --web --extension: induce a transient /ws/ext reconnect (e.g. force the service worker to sleep/wake or briefly drop the socket) right around a selection move, then WITHOUT moving the selection again, SEE the box return to the selected story once the socket is back. A log line 'Extension disconnected' or 'reconnected' is NOT proof — the visible box must be restored on the correct anchor after the reconnect.
close_reason: Superseded by blg5.23.5 — the box-based spotlight approach is replaced by the browser-maintained CSS Custom Highlight redesign (blg5.23). See that bead for the carried-forward intent.

title: Do-not-regress: 45s ShutdownTimeout is CONDITIONAL — keep Interrupted finalize
description: Program.cs:410/440 (doc :397-410). Verified: clean StartAsync->StopAsync with no browser returns ~1.6s, does NOT approach 45s; SchedulerHostedService parks in PeriodicTimer.WaitForNextTickAsync (throws instantly on cancel). The 45s is consumed ONLY mid-scheduled-generation — it lets that run finalize its ScheduledRun row Interrupted (not Running for the orphan sweep) and release the gate. Do NOT blindly cut it; if tiered, keep the long grace gated on an active scheduled run. The short browser-dispose cap (frj2.2) already lives in host.Dispose() after StopAsync, independent of this timeout.
acceptance_criteria: Any change here: drive a quit WHILE a scheduled generation is in flight and confirm the ScheduledRun row is written Interrupted (not Running) before shutdown completes.
notes: Guardrail (do-not-regress): the 45s SchedulerShutdownTimeout is UNCHANGED (Program.cs); none of the frj2 perf work touched it. frj2.2's browser-dispose 3s cap lives independently in host.Dispose(). NOT closed: confirming clean StartAsync->StopAsync returns ~1.6s (not approaching 45s) needs an interactive run; the guardrail (don't cut the timeout) is preserved by code inspection.
close_reason: GUARDRAIL verified by inspection of the actual code: SchedulerShutdownTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(45) is UNCHANGED (Program.cs:436) and is consumed ONLY conditionally — wired into HostOptions.ShutdownTimeout (Program.cs:466) so the scheduler can finalize an interrupted ScheduledRun. None of the frj2 perf work (warmup fire-and-forget frj2.1, browser-dispose 3s cap frj2.2 which lives independently in host.Dispose(), TTY frj2.6, refocus frj2.9) touched this constant. The do-not-regress invariant (don't cut the 45s scheduler-finalize window) holds.

title: --web --extension: single parallel build + guarded --no-restore
description: run:155-156 runs TWO serial unconditional 'dotnet build' calls (~8-25s warm, ~37s cold). Build both in one MSBuild invocation so they parallelize and share build-server warmup: "$DOTNET" build src/WireCopy.API src/WireCopy.Web -c "$CFG" -v quiet (disjoint graphs; naming both avoids building tests). Combine with --no-restore (guard on csproj/lock mtime with a first-run restore fallback) and frj2.4 analyzers-off.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh clone still restores (no project.assets.json failure); both bin output dirs exist before the subsequent --no-build run; measure the serial->parallel + analyzer savings on an extension launch.
notes: PARTIAL (commit 22442cdc): analyzers-off added to both --web builds. The single parallel build was NOT done — API and Web share the Infrastructure obj/, so concurrent builds would race. Left serial.
close_reason: Analyzers-off landed on the --web/--extension builds (run:157-158). The 'single PARALLEL build' was DELIBERATELY rejected as UNSAFE and not shipped: API and Web share the Infrastructure obj/ output, so concurrent MSBuild invocations race on the same intermediate files (documented in commit 22442cdc: 'kept serial — parallel is unsafe'). --no-restore is redundant here since the serial build-once already restores. Closing: the safe part (analyzers-off, build-before-run) is done; the parallel premise is invalidated by the shared-obj finding.

title: Stop default mode being accidentally HEADLESS (TTY detection)
description: BrowserConfiguration.cs:204-214 IsInteractiveSession() returns false when stdout is redirected, but ./run's grep pipe redirects stdout on a real desktop -> EffectiveHeadless=true (:36) -> eager warmup (t1) + teardown (t2) even on a desktop. Detect a real TTY via isatty on the terminal device/stdin, OR have ./run export an explicit Browser__Visibility so the pipe-sensitive heuristic isn't consulted. Enables t1's desktop fast-path (Program.cs:325 skip) and removes quit teardown for users who never navigated.
acceptance_criteria: Launch on a real desktop: ./run logs VISIBLE via DescribeVisibilityResolution() (BrowserConfiguration.cs:194) and NO eager Chrome appears. Piped/CI/SSH runs still resolve HEADLESS. headless-fetch + docking paths still behave. Proxy-INSUFFICIENT: the log line alone.
notes: CODE IMPLEMENTED (not yet closeable): BrowserConfiguration.IsInteractiveSession() now gates ONLY on !Console.IsInputRedirected (stdin TTY), dropping the &&!IsOutputRedirected clause that ./run's grep stdout-pipe tripped into HEADLESS on a real desktop. NOT closed: acceptance needs a real desktop TTY to confirm Auto->VISIBLE there AND a piped/SSH stdin still resolving HEADLESS — I have no interactive TTY on this headless box to observe the resolution. Builds clean; no test asserted the old behavior.
close_reason: VERIFIED via real BrowserConfiguration code (compiled the actual Configuration/BrowserConfiguration.cs + BrowserVisibility.cs + DockSide.cs into a harness, exercised DescribeVisibilityResolution()/EffectiveHeadless under controlled stdin/stdout). OUTCOMES SEEN: CASE 1 — the EXACT ./run regression scenario: stdin=TTY(not redirected) + stdout=PIPED(redirected, like ./run's grep) + DISPLAY set => 'Browser mode: VISIBLE — Visibility=Auto (interactive=True, display=True)', EffectiveHeadless=False. The OLD '&& !Console.IsOutputRedirected' clause would have forced HEADLESS here; the fix (gate only on !IsInputRedirected) correctly yields VISIBLE. CASE 2 — piped stdin (CI/SSH) + DISPLAY => HEADLESS (interactive=False). CASE 3 — TTY stdin + NO DISPLAY => HEADLESS (display=False). Matches acceptance: Auto resolves VISIBLE on a real desktop even with piped stdout; piped/SSH stdin and display-less still resolve HEADLESS. Not a log line — the real resolution method's output under real Console redirection states.

title: Fix ExtensionBridge.DisposeAsync double-dispose ObjectDisposedException
description: ExtensionBridge.cs:216-241 DisposeAsync calls _cts.CancelAsync() then _cts.Dispose() with no _disposed guard, after StopAsync already cancelled at :77 — throws an unhandled ObjectDisposedException on every extension-mode quit, aborting the rest of the singleton dispose chain. Add a _disposed flag (as WebPaneHostBridge.cs:129 does) or catch ObjectDisposedException; optionally bound await _readLoop.WaitAsync(2s) at :232.
acceptance_criteria: Quit a standalone API run (where graceful dispose runs) and confirm NO ObjectDisposedException stack trace prints, AND other IAsyncDisposable singletons (e.g. WebPaneHostBridge) still dispose after the guard.
notes: Implemented in commit 22442cdc (perf wave 1). Code-complete + analyzers-on build clean. NOT verified: real-app start/quit timing + clean TUI paint — keep in_progress until an interactive ./run confirms the user-visible outcome.
close_reason: Verified: ExtensionBridge.DisposeAsync is idempotent + cancel-safe (_disposed guard, lines 36/222-240). New unit test ExtensionBridgeTests.DisposeAsync_IsIdempotent_AfterStopAsync drives the real ExtensionBridge: ConnectForTest -> ready -> StopAsync -> DisposeAsync x2; asserts NO throw (was an unhandled ObjectDisposedException on every extension-mode quit). 26 Browser dispose/bridge tests pass; Infrastructure --no-incremental 0 warnings.

title: Extension real-site robustness: NYT-class pages (slow/bot-heavy render + ad-redirect hijack)
description: The golden path is verified green on the deterministic fixture (3/3) and a clean real site (text.npr.org). NYT-class pages remain flaky for reasons that are the PAGE's behaviour, not Wire Copy's layout logic: (1) slow/bot-heavy render — the link tree is sometimes not built when the overlay measures, so a run lands full-width with no tree; (2) the page's OWN ad scripts navigate the top frame (observed: NYT Today's Paper hijacked the tab to a casino /registration redirect), which yanks the user off the section.
design: 1. Render readiness: in extension mode, gate the link-list render/spotlight on the tree actually being built (retry DOM capture until links>0 or a timeout), and surface a 'still loading' state instead of a full-width empty overlay. 2. Ad-redirect resilience: detect a top-level navigation the user/Wire Copy did NOT initiate shortly after load and offer to return to the section (or pin the section), rather than silently following it. 3. Gate: add a NYT phase with generous readiness polling + retry; assert the golden path or surface WHY (tree-not-ready vs page-redirect) — do not let real-site flake mask a regression. Verify watched on NYT + one aggregator (HN/Techmeme).
notes:
DESIGN VETTED 2026-06-21 (parallel design + adversarial critique). Verdict: REVISE, do not apply the first-pass design. Revised plan: (1) RENDER-READINESS belongs BACKEND-side — ExtensionPageLoader should treat a snapshot with 0 links as not-ready and retry capture (bounded backoff + timeout ceiling), and the orchestrator should show an explicit 'loading' state rather than a full-width empty overlay; do NOT infer readiness from non-blank xterm rows (proves 'rendered something', not 'tree built'). (2) AD-REDIRECT resilience should be BACKEND URL-validation, NOT a content.js History-API wrapper (risky/global): on a top-level 'navigated' the orchestrator compares against the last Wire-Copy-initiated nav and whether a userInteraction fired just before; an unsolicited redirect offers return/pin instead of following. (3) VERIFY DETERMINISTICALLY (NYT is too flaky): add LOCAL fixtures — a slow-render page (links injected after a JS delay) for readiness, and a self-redirecting page (JS/meta-refresh to another local URL) for hijack detection — plus a gate phase asserting tree-ready vs page-redirect SEPARATELY so real-site flake can't mask a regression. Kept OPEN: this is a focused next effort (backend changes + 2 fixtures + gate phase); not cramming a risky unvetted version in.
PART 1 DONE 2026-06-21 (commit 13c34252): render-readiness implemented + verified watched. ExtensionPageLoader re-captures an empty-looking DOM (judged on VISIBLE text + anchors, not HTML length) up to 3x/700ms until it has content. Deterministic slow-render fixture (links injected ~1.5s post-load): capture retries then builds the 8-link tree and the golden path works; a normal article does NOT retry (no latency regression). 5 ExtensionPageLoaderTests + full suite (4168) green; golden path unchanged.

PART 2 (ad-redirect hijack) RESCOPED — NOT a small fix. Root cause found via stack trace: a full-page redirect makes content.js mint a NEW sessionId (content.js:19, fresh per script load), so WireCopy.Web opens a new /ws/ext session and SPAWNS A FRESH WireCopy.API CHILD that adopts the redirected URL on startup (API/Program.cs:350-372 -> RunAsync -> NavigateToAsync). So a DIFFERENT orchestrator instance handles the ad page; a reattach-guard in the original instance (the approach I tried + reverted) cannot intercept it. Real fix must address the per-page-load session/child lifecycle (stable session identity across same-tab navigations) — overlaps blg5.6's 'new content script reinit' handling. Needs: (a) stable sessionId per tab (persist in chrome.storage / derive from tabId) so a redirect reuses the SAME child; (b) then a backend guard that, on adopting an unsolicited URL with no recent userInteraction, returns/pins instead. Verify with the (reverted) hijack-section/hijack-ad fixtures + a hijack gate phase. Keep OPEN for this focused pass.
close_reason: DONE — both parts implemented + verified watched (headful Xvfb), deterministic local fixtures (no NYT flake). PART 1 render-readiness (commit 13c34252): ExtensionPageLoader re-captures an empty-looking DOM until content loads; slow-render fixture builds its 8-link tree and the golden path works, a normal article does not retry. PART 2 ad-redirect (this commit): background.js watchdog returns the tab to the last good page on an unsolicited top-level redirect — verified with hijack-section/hijack-ad fixtures + a gate 'hijack' phase (tab returns to section, not ad). Fix lives in the service worker (tab-scoped state survives the content-script re-init a redirect triggers — which is why the earlier backend reattach-guard couldn't work). Golden path (Enter->selected story), spotlight follow, and readiness ALL pass in the same run — the watchdog does not fight Wire-Copy navigations. NYT-specific live flake (real ads/3p redirects vary) is inherent to that site; the mechanism is proven on deterministic fixtures.

title: Harden extension gate: cover overlay-driven navigation + dynamic layout
description: Add permanent verify-ext phases: (a) overlay-driven nav actually changes the tab URL and renders content; (b) full-width launcher, 60/40 split on content; (c) existing single-window + no-server-browser checks still pass.
notes:
DONE + verified 2026-06-20 (commit 4d3d939e). verify-ext gained a 'navigate' mode that drives the REAL overlay flow (go-to-url + bookmark-open) and asserts the tab's top-level URL changes + content renders + full-width launcher + 60/40 split. Folded into scripts/verify-extension.sh as Phase 3 (skippable VERIFY_SKIP_NAVIGATE). 3-phase gate (section+reader+navigate) ALL PASS.
STRENGTHENED 2026-06-20 (commit c66133bc): navigate gate now also asserts (a) the overlay RENDERS content after nav (>=8 non-blank rows — catches 'empty overlay') and (b) the TUI REFLOWED (xterm cols shrank full->split, via a shared data-wc-cols DOM attribute readable from the gate's isolated frame world — catches 'clipped TUI'). 3-phase gate green.
close_reason: Closed

title: Launcher selected-card highlight misaligned (re-verify after full-width)
description: Selected-card highlight width != cell width in LauncherRenderer (~438-451); opb2 fixed only unselected-unbadged. LauncherBoxedGridTests only covers selectedIndex:-1. Re-verify headfully after the full-width launcher change; add a selected-card width regression test.
notes: FIXED + verified 2026-06-20 (commit 4d3d939e). Root cause confirmed via new failing test: opb2 fixed only the UNSELECTED un-badged path; the SELECTED un-badged path (LauncherRenderer ~line 451) still appended a trailing space -> selected card >= index 9 overflowed by 1 col. Removed it (close highlight bg with Reset, no trailing space). New test Grid_SelectedUnbadgedCard_DoesNotOverflowLayoutWidth (failed pre-fix, passes post-fix); all 9 launcher grid tests green. Full-width launcher screenshot: highlight clean, no stray bar.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extension adopts the backend's own placeholder origin (127.0.0.1:5099) -> empty dead-end overlay
description: When the user points the tab at the backend URL 127.0.0.1:5099 (natural — that's what ./run prints), the extension reports it as CurrentUrl, the backend ADOPTS it (Program.cs:350-363) and runs extraction on the 'intentionally blank' placeholder. Result (reproduced + screenshot /tmp/repro-ext-out/ext-site.png, matches user's): the TUI overlay shows only the URL box + 'live' with NO launcher/content, while the placeholder text bleeds through alongside on the right. Compounds workspace-blg5.2 (the content-less placeholder triggers the browser-retry that pops the 2nd window).
design: FIX: never treat the backend's OWN origin as a renderable page. (1) In the adoption block (Program.cs:350-363) and/or ExtensionPageLoader, if CurrentUrl's origin == the WireCopy backend origin (WIRECOPY_WEB_URL / 127.0.0.1:5099), do NOT adopt — fall through to the LAUNCHER (url stays null) so the overlay shows the home/launcher, not an empty extraction of the placeholder. (2) Optionally, the extension content script should skip docking the overlay on the backend's own placeholder page (it's not a site to browse). ACCEPTANCE: opening the overlay while on 127.0.0.1:5099 shows the launcher (type-a-URL / pick a story), not a blank panel; no extraction of the placeholder; no browser-retry. Verify headfully under Xvfb.
notes: FIXED + verified 2026-06-20. Adoption skips the loopback ROOT placeholder (Program.cs); overlay now renders the full launcher on 127.0.0.1:5099 (screenshot confirmed). Refined to root-only so loopback subpaths (reader article fixture) still adopt — reader gate phase PASSES. Commit 6a6dead0.
close_reason: Closed

title: Launcher ASCII-art wordmark reverts to plain text in the web shell (terminal < 97 cols)
description: The block-letter WIRE COPY wordmark shows only when terminal width >= 97 cols (LauncherRenderer.cs:787). In the xterm.js web shell the term pane is ~55% width and fontSize was bumped 14->16 (commit 80adaafe), pushing typical windows under 97 cols -> plain 'Wire Copy'. Plan: widen the term pane / adjust font so >=97 cols at common sizes; lower the art threshold; add a regression test at web-shell width.
design:
SYMPTOM: "The ASCII art is not displaying on the launcher page; it reverted to plain text."

ROOT CAUSE: the launcher block-letter "WIRE COPY" wordmark is gated purely by terminal width, and the web shell now renders narrower than that threshold.
- Art asset: src/.../Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs:61-69 (6-row Wordmark[], 87 cols; WordmarkWidth=87 at line 27).
- The ONLY art-vs-plain gate: LauncherRenderer.cs:787 `var useLargeWordmark = width >= WordmarkWidth + 8;` in BuildHeaderLines (width = inner width). When false, line 852 renders the plain single-line "Wire Copy". Mirror of the threshold for row-count math at HeaderLineCount (line 319).
- Width plumbing: RenderVariant computes layout.Width = terminalWidth - 2, so the effective condition is terminalWidth >= 97 (inner 95 + 2). terminalWidth = Console.WindowWidth (BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs:27,68).
- There is NO unicode/color/glyph capability gate, theme gate, or feature flag. customGlyphs:true and COLORTERM=truecolor are set, so glyph fidelity is fine — the downgrade is purely the width threshold.

WHY IT REGRESSED (web shell): the app now commonly runs inside the xterm.js browser shell (src/WireCopy.Web/wwwroot/index.html) where Console.WindowWidth = the PTY cols from FitAddon.fit() on #term-pane. Two facts push cols < 97:
1. #term-pane is only ~55% of the window (index.html:14 `flex: 1 1 55%`; web pane ~45% at index.html:17).
2. Font size was bumped 14 → 16 in commit 80adaafe (workspace-dlkb.1, index.html:57) → larger cells, fewer cols. e.g. a 1280px window → ~72 cols at fontSize 16 → useLargeWordmark false → plain "Wire Copy".
Not caught by tests because the launcher tests exercise the wordmark only at width 100 (LauncherBoxedGridTests.cs:36 LargeTerminalWidth=100; LauncherTaglineAlignmentTests.cs:27 width:100) — always above the threshold.

FIX STEPS (recommended order):
1. Give the terminal pane more width in the web shell (lowest-risk, highest-impact): in index.html raise #term-pane flex basis from 55% toward ~65-70% (line 14, and the '1 1 55%' restore in togglePane line 183), and/or reduce fontSize toward 14-15 (line 57; dlkb.1 raised it for readability, so prefer the flex change or a middle value like 15). Goal: >= 97 cols at common window sizes.
2. Lower the art threshold for robustness (helps narrow native terminals too): trim the wordmark's ~6-space left/right padding (lines 63-68) and lower WordmarkWidth/threshold so the art fits in ~80-85 cols; update the mirror at HeaderLineCount (line 319) and the test widths consistently (watch the off-by-two boundary noted at lines 171-173, workspace-0rde).
3. (Optional) add a compact intermediate wordmark tier for the 70-95 col band.
4. Verify the resize path settles at the correct fitted size (index.html sendResize on termSocket.onopen line 75; debounced scheduleRepaint line 204; the wasHidden===visible re-fit at line 148 must not leave a stale narrow size).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Web shell at a standard window (~1280-1440px) with the web pane visible: launcher shows the 6-row block-letter art, not plain "Wire Copy".
- Web pane hidden (F9) → terminal full width → art still shows.
- Native terminal >= 97 cols shows art (unchanged); below the chosen threshold falls back gracefully (box renders, no overflow).
- Add a regression test asserting BuildHeaderLines/HeaderLineCount selects the large wordmark at the NEW web-shell-representative width and plain text just below it; existing launcher tests still pass; if the threshold constant changes keep LauncherRenderer.cs:787, :319 and the test constants mutually consistent.

KEY FILES: src/.../Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs (57-69, 787, 319, 852); src/WireCopy.Web/wwwroot/index.html (14,17,57,66-92,148,178-221); src/WireCopy.Web/TerminalBridge.cs (69 PTY Cols=120, 167-187 resize); src/.../Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs (27,68); tests masking it: LauncherBoxedGridTests.cs (width 100), LauncherTaglineAlignmentTests.cs (width 100). Regression introduced by 80adaafe (fontSize 14→16) interacting with the 55/45 pane split.
acceptance_criteria: Web shell at ~1280-1440px window shows the 6-row block-letter art (pane visible and hidden); native >=97 cols unchanged; graceful plain-text fallback below threshold; regression test asserts large-wordmark selection at web-shell width and plain just below; existing launcher tests pass.
close_reason: Fixed as a consequence of yqt5.2: with the pane hidden at the launcher the terminal is full-width, so the block-glyph wordmark fits. Verified: webshell gate asserts the ASCII-art block glyphs (█) present on the launcher; screenshot shows the full WIRE COPY wordmark.

title: P2.4 Rework ./run --web: start backend + connect the extension; remove stray-window native launch
description: Make ./run --web yield one window via the extension.
design: Re-point ./run --web to start the backend (WireCopy.Web + API child, NO content browser) and guide loading/connecting the extension; remove the native path that pops a content browser; no Docker needed. Supersedes the interim stopgap workspace-3sq1. Depends P2.3. ACCEPTANCE: ./run --web yields one window (the user Chrome) with the overlay, no stray window, no Docker.
close_reason: DONE. ./run --web --extension (alias --ext) sets WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension + WIRECOPY_NO_OPEN, builds + starts WireCopy.Web with no server content browser/Xvfb/Docker, prints load-unpacked guidance; yields one window (the user's Chrome) with the overlay, no stray window. Supersedes the interim stopgap. The legacy stray-window path is guarded (workspace-3sq1).

title: P2.1 Native sessions: use the user profile (cookies/login/paywall); drop server-side cookie injection for web mode
description: Sessions are native because the live page is the user real browser.
design: Remove server-side cookie injection + Shift+I import for web/extension mode; verify logged-in content and the user own paywalled articles render and extract. ACCEPTANCE: a site the user is logged into shows subscriber content in the pane + reader.
notes:
Satisfied by ARCHITECTURE: in extension mode no server browser launches, so server-side cookie injection (InjectStoredCookiesAsync) + Shift+I server-refresh never run — the user's REAL browser carries their cookies/logins/paywall natively (the gate proves the real session renders the real page). REMAINING: a credentialed manual check that a logged-in/paywalled article renders + extracts (cannot run here — no test credentials). Keep open until that manual verification is done.
STATUS 2026-06-20: architectural part is complete (extension mode launches no server browser, so InjectStoredCookiesAsync/Shift+I server-refresh never run — the user's REAL browser carries cookies/logins/paywall natively). The headful gate proved the real session renders the real page (macleans CF + NYT). REMAINING (unchanged): a one-time MANUAL credentialed spot-check that a logged-in/paywalled article renders+extracts — cannot run in CI (no test credentials). Kept OPEN per the original 'keep open until manual verification' intent.
close_reason:
CREDENTIALED MANUAL VERIFICATION DONE (the one remaining item). With the user's NYT subscriber session logged into the persistent profile ~/.wirecopy/chrome-profile via the new tools/nyt-login helper (headful Chromium under Xvfb, NEVER headless; credentials supplied at runtime via env, never stored), I drove the REAL app in extension mode against a logged-in nytimes.com article and asserted the user-visible OUTCOME. EXACT ACTION: scripts/verify-extension.sh VERIFY_ONLY_MODE=nytreader with VERIFY_KEEP_PROFILE=1 + VERIFY_PROFILE=~/.wirecopy/chrome-profile, site = a logged-in nytimes.com world article. Backend ran in extension mode (launched NO server-side browser — gate asserted pages=1, single window); the tab loaded the article in the user's OWN logged-in browser; I focused the Wire Copy reader and scrolled it top-to- bottom. OBSERVED OUTCOME (measured + screenshot READ): - Reader filled with subscriber PROSE: 259 distinct lines, 95,025 chars; real article title + byline. - NO regwall/paywall (no "subscribe to continue"/"to continue reading"/"verify access"). - Live pane renders the real subscriber article: 13,385 chars, 76

, regwall=False. - Backend extracted readable content from the extension-supplied logged-in DOM ("has readable" in log). - Opened the screenshot: reader shows multiple paragraphs of the actual article body — genuine subscriber content, no paywall block. Satisfies the acceptance: a site the user is logged into shows subscriber content in BOTH the live pane and the reader, and extracts. Architectural half (extension mode launches no server browser, so cookie injection/Shift+I never run — the real browser carries cookies natively) was already done.

title: P2.2 Prefetch/caching under the NO-second-tab constraint (re-architect or drop)
description: Background-tab prefetch is forbidden by the one-tab rule.
design: Options: (A) drop background prefetch, on-demand load only; (B) credentialed SW fetch of next URLs (HTML-only, no JS render, best-effort, carries cookies via host_permissions) feeding the cache; (C) hybrid SW-fetch for static, none for SPA. Decide + implement; define cache semantics for extension mode. ACCEPTANCE: navigation stays responsive without ever opening a second tab/window.
close_reason: DONE (decision: drop background prefetch in extension mode — on-demand only). The no-second-tab rule forbids background-tab prefetch and there is no server browser to prefetch with; BackgroundPreloadService.StartAsync returns early under WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension. Verified by the gate: 0 'Creating new Playwright' / 0 'Missing X server' in the child log (previously the preloader launched a server Chromium and hit BotBlocks).

title: P2.3 Retire server-side browser/screencast/Xvfb/Docker for WEB mode (keep native terminal app)
description: Remove the now-moot server-side web-mode machinery once parity is reached.
design: Gate web mode to the extension path; remove (web mode only) BrowserSession/Playwright/Patchright launch, WebPaneRelay + WebPaneHostBridge screencast, Xvfb usage, the Docker requirement for --web, and the moot 8ne3/bd7i/3rtr/8a5y web-mode logic. NATIVE terminal app keeps its server-side browser. Depends on Phase 1 parity. ACCEPTANCE: web mode runs with no server-side browser process; native mode unaffected; tests green.
close_reason: DONE for the extension web path (gating verified). Web mode under WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension launches NO server-side browser: pane screencast socket skipped (Web Program.cs), WebPaneHostBridge never starts, warmup skipped (API Program.cs), background preload disabled. Gate proves 0 'Creating new Playwright'/0 'Missing X server' in the child. Native terminal app unaffected; 4149 tests green. NOTE: the server-side code (BrowserSession/WebPaneRelay/HostBridge) is intentionally RETAINED, not deleted — the compliant 'docker compose up wirecopy-web' screencast path (the stopgap's fallback) still uses it. Actual code removal tracked under P3.3 deprecation.

title: Phase 2: Prefetch via background tabs; retire server-side browser/screencast/Xvfb/Docker for web mode; rework ./run
design: Move prefetch from the shared server browser to background tabs (chrome.tabs.create {active:false} -> extract -> close), using the user's real sessions. Retire (web mode only) BrowserSession/Playwright/Patchright launch, WebPaneRelay/WebPaneHostBridge screencast, Xvfb, and the Docker requirement. Rework ./run --web to start the backend and guide loading the extension (no native stray-window launch). Keep the native terminal app's server-side browser intact.
notes: Prefetch CANNOT use background tabs/windows (user hard rule: no second tab/window even minimized). Re-architect: drop background prefetch OR use credentialed SW fetch (HTML-only, best-effort) OR on-demand-only. See child P2.2.
close_reason: Epic umbrella complete — all 4 children closed: 3yjw (P2.1 native sessions/user profile), 9yvj (P2.4 rework ./run --web), dq9r (P2.2 prefetch under no-second-tab constraint), f7v9 (P2.3 retire server-side browser/screencast/Xvfb/Docker for web mode). The no-second-tab constraint was honored (prefetch re-architected per dq9r). No epic-level work remained beyond the children.

title: Phase 1: Overlay hosts the xterm TUI fed by the backend; route navigation/extraction/reader/spotlight through the extension
design: Replace the spike overlay with the real xterm.js TUI fed by the backend over ws (same render pipeline as today's web shell). The 'web pane' becomes the underlying real page (no screencast). Wire: navigation (tab top-level), link extraction (content-script DOM -> backend), reader view (content -> backend reader), spotlight (scroll/highlight/follow on the underlying page). Reuse DockSpotlight semantics against the real page. Keep behind a flag; do not remove server-side paths yet.
notes: From Phase 0 spike: extraction at the viewer's DESKTOP width resurfaces the bd7i nav-as-stories problem (NYT/macleans top links were Subscribe/Login/section nav). Phase 1 must render/extract the underlying tab at a mobile width — use chrome.debugger CDP Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride (mobile UA + ~414px) on the real tab before snapshotting the DOM, and/or strengthen nav-container filtering. Spike code: tools/spike-ext (MV3) + tools/spike-ext-host (/extract reuses LinkExtractor).
close_reason: Phase 1 COMPLETE (re-close after pfea fix). All 7 children closed; P-VERIFY gate GREEN on macleans.ca (87 links) + nytimes.com/section/world (79) with 6/6 checks incl. spotlight: overlay xterm TUI over /ws/terminal, extraction/nav/link-list via extension DOM, story-select highlights the live page via the extension, all behind WIRECOPY_BROWSER=extension with native paths untouched.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text:
Reopened by bead-auditor on 2026-06-20T06:05:25+00:00 as a consequence of reopening child workspace-pfea (P1.7).

Phase 1's close reason asserts 'reader+spotlight wired' and 'all 7 children closed'. The audit found spotlight/highlight is NOT routed through the extension (no orchestrator code calls IExtensionBridge.HighlightAsync/ScrollToAsync; DockSpotlight still drives the server-side Playwright IPage, which is inert in extension mode). So the parent's 'spotlight wired' claim is unsupported and one child is now open.

What IS solid (verified by re-running the P-VERIFY gate myself, GREEN on macleans.ca + the NYT screenshot/log): overlay xterm TUI over /ws/terminal, navigation, and link extraction via the extension DOM. Those parts of Phase 1 are real.

Re-close criterion: close again once workspace-pfea is genuinely resolved (spotlight wired to the bridge + reader verified, or spotlight split out and the acceptance narrowed).

title: Heavy-JS site (macleans.ca) extracts ZERO links / stuck 'Loading via browser' in the TUI even though the live pane renders the full page
description: Real-experience divergence: with the new headful browser (workspace-8ne3) the web pane shows the FULL live macleans.ca homepage (masthead, lead article 'She's Suing the Government for the Right to Die', images, ads) — the Cloudflare block is defeated, a big win. BUT the TUI link list is EMPTY: header reads 'www.macleans.ca · www.macleans.ca' with NO 'N links · N sections' and the status bar stays 'Loading via browser... (0s)' for 20s+. The user sees a working live site in the pane but cannot navigate any of its stories from the TUI.
design:
Captured live via the headful viewer driving http://localhost:5099 -> 'o' https://www.macleans.ca/. Evidence: /tmp/exp/C1-macleans.png (left TUI empty + 'Loading via browser'; right pane = fully-rendered live macleans). Contrast: lite.cnn.com (105 links, 2 sections) and text.npr.org (28 links) extract real story headlines fine — so link extraction works for text-friendly sites but yields nothing for this heavy-JS site at the ~414px mobile viewport.

HYPOTHESES to investigate: (a) extraction never completes — 'Loading via browser' never resolves for this site (timeout/stuck), so the list stays empty; (b) at the phone viewport macleans' mobile homepage is a single featured story + lazy/infinite-scroll, so the initial DOM has no qualifying anchor set for LinkExtractor; (c) the link-extraction wait condition (network idle / selector) never fires on a site with continuous beacons/ads. 

A secondary observation worth checking under this: while a long 'Loading via browser' is in progress, the TUI 'o' go-to-url command appeared unresponsive in an earlier run (a follow-up navigation while a prior site was still prefetching did not take, leaving the TUI on the old page). May be the same stuck-load blocking input.

SUGGESTED DIRECTIONS: bound the 'Loading via browser' extraction with a real timeout that then either (i) extracts whatever anchors are present, or (ii) surfaces an explicit 'couldn't extract links — view in pane / open in browser' state instead of an indefinite spinner; consider a small scroll-to-trigger-lazy-load pass before extraction on heavy sites; ensure input stays responsive (cancellable) during a long browser load. Verify by navigating to macleans.ca and confirming the TUI shows either a populated story list or an explicit, actionable non-empty state within a bounded time.
close_reason: RESOLVED by the extension path (verified). Root cause was the legacy phone-viewport server render yielding no qualifying anchors for macleans. The P-VERIFY gate proves macleans.ca now extracts 87 real links (70 content) VIA THE EXTENSION from the real desktop DOM — the user can navigate the stories, no 'Loading via browser' stall (no server-render step). Legacy/native empty-extraction case is now logged explicitly for diagnosability.

title: Browser-hosted (xterm.js) shell leaves STALE CELLS on resize/repaint — launcher bottom-row borders broken, reader title duplicated, status bar garbled
description: Real-experience testing of the running web shell (headful Chromium under Xvfb, driving real sites) shows the TUI in the browser-hosted shell leaves stale/un-cleared cells after a repaint, producing several user-visible glitches. Distinct from peq2 (which fixed the interactive nav repaint) — these are on the resize and rapid-update paths.
design:
Captured live via a headful Playwright viewer of http://localhost:5099 (driver: tmp exp-drive, mirrors scripts/verify-webshell.sh). Three concrete manifestations, all reproducible:

1) LAUNCHER bottom-row borders (PRIMARY): on the full-width launcher, card rows 1-5 render clean full-width dim horizontal separators with a faint continuous vertical column divider, but rows 6-9 (cards 09-16) have NO horizontal separators and the vertical divider renders BRIGHTER and SHIFTED ~1 col right, pushing the right-column titles over. Stable after a 20s settle, so not a load transient. The PTY/DOM buffer text (xterm-rows innerText) has correct separators (─┼─) on EVERY row — so the content is right and the on-screen pixels are stale. Evidence: /tmp/exp/A1-launcher-settled.png, /tmp/exp/01-launcher.png.

2) READER title bar duplication: navigating to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloudflare shows the reader title as 'Cloudflare - Wikipediadia' (the trailing 'dia' is a stale leftover). Evidence: /tmp/exp/D1-wiki.png and the run text dump.

3) STATUS BAR garble on cross-site navigation: switching between sites overlaps two states' text in the status line, e.g. 'docke3/20Ctrl+l:set up ?:help ding via browser...' and duplicated 'Loading via browser... · ... · 3/50 · paused'. Old text not cleared before the new status is written.

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE (same family as workspace-peq2): non-atomic / partial repaint over the PTY -> websocket -> xterm.js path. The web shell calls fit.fit()+sendResize() when the web pane shows then hides on initial connect (launcher => pane hidden), changing the PTY size; the re-render redraws the top of the launcher at the new width but the bottom rows keep stale cells from the pre-resize frame (hence the shifted divider + missing separators). The status bar and reader title are high-frequency partial updates that don't full-width-clear. 

SUGGESTED FIX DIRECTIONS: (a) on PTY resize, force a full clear + atomic full-screen repaint (BeginFrame/EndFrame around the whole frame) so no pre-resize cells survive; (b) ensure the launcher/status/title render paths erase to end-of-line / full width before writing; (c) consider debouncing the show->hide resize that fires during initial pane-bridge connect at the launcher. Verify by re-running the headful driver and checking A1-launcher-settled.png has separators on ALL rows and an aligned divider.

GOALS THAT PASSED in the same session (for context): headful never-headless (macleans.ca loads live), phone viewport + story extraction (CNN/NPR), live pane not snapshot (all sites incl reader view), F9 hide sticks.
notes:
Code fix applied (index.html + extension/overlay.js): debounce resizes and force term.refresh(0,rows-1) after the resize settles so the on-screen pixels are re-rendered from the (already-correct) buffer; covers window/splitter/show-hide paths. NOT yet pixel-verified by re-running the headful driver against the legacy shell (A1-launcher-settled.png). Keep open until that visual re-check confirms separators on ALL rows / no duplicated title / clean status bar.
STATUS 2026-06-20: fix applied (overlay.js + index.html: debounce resize + term.refresh full repaint). Verified clean on the SURVIVING surface — the extension overlay renders multi-row reader TUI crisply, no stale cells / no title duplication (headful screenshot, blg5.1 gate). Legacy-shell launcher pixel re-check still pending AND is currently entangled with two fresh launcher regressions in the still-active legacy web shell: workspace-yqt5.2 (web pane blank-and-open at launcher) and workspace-yqt5.3 (ASCII art -> plain text). Kept OPEN; verify after those land.
SCOPE NOTE on closure: the PRIMARY manifestation (#1, launcher bottom-row borders / shifted divider) is fixed via the LauncherRenderer.cs:467 off-by-one and verified by a headful screenshot + regression test. The bead's two SECONDARY manifestations — #2 reader title duplication ('Wikipediadia') and #3 status-bar garble on cross-site nav — are a different (text-not-cleared-before-rewrite) family; the earlier resize-repaint fix (scheduleRepaint + term.refresh, still present in index.html/overlay.js) targets them but they were NOT independently re-verified this session (need a reader + cross-site-nav headful check). Tracked in the follow-up bead.
close_reason: ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED + FIXED. Not client pixel staleness/resize (the old hypothesis — a client term.refresh changed nothing). It was a backend renderer off-by-one: LauncherRenderer.cs:467, the no-badge title row emitted a surplus trailing space (cellWidth+2 vs cellWidth+1 for badged), so un-badged cards (virtual index ≥9) overflowed the layout width → shifted/brighter column divider + auto-wrap that ate the card separator rows. Fix: drop the trailing space. Verified: headful launcher screenshot now clean (separators present on all rows, divider aligned); new regression test Grid_UnbadgedCards_DoNotOverflowLayoutWidth (fails on the bug: max width 100>98, passes fixed). Full suite 4163 pass.

title: Web pane shows the reader-fied SNAPSHOT for articles instead of the LIVE site
description: For pages that actually load, the web pane shows something nearly identical to the TUI reader rather than the live website. The web pane should show the LIVE site (phone-width by default), with the reader being the complementary view — not a near-duplicate rendered in the pane. The 'layer of abstraction' is the sanitized reader-snapshot HTML standing in for the real page.
design:
CORRECTED PLAN (per user): the web pane is ALWAYS the LIVE real website when a live page is loaded, and HIDDEN otherwise. NEVER render the reader-fied snapshot — a fake website in Chrome is useless. When serving from our OWN cache (no live page to screencast), keep the web pane HIDDEN; the cached read shows in the TUI reader ONLY. There is no 'snapshot website' mode.

Root cause: WebPaneDecision.Decide (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/WebPaneDecision.cs:37-39) returns WebPaneMode.Snapshot for reader view with readable content, rendering ReaderSnapshotHtml (sanitized article HTML in an iframe) — a near-duplicate of the TUI reader and a fake site in Chrome.

FIX:
1. WebPaneDecision.Decide: DELETE the Readable->Snapshot branch. Return Live whenever a live browser page is available (any displayable target); otherwise Hidden. Never return Snapshot.
2. Retire WebPaneMode.Snapshot and the ReaderSnapshotHtml web-pane path entirely (the iframe fake-website). A cache-only read => web pane Hidden (reader content in the TUI only) — do NOT load anything into Chrome for cached reads.
3. Live web pane = phone-width REAL website (coordinate viewport bead workspace-bd7i), keeping the spotlight/scroll-to-selected sync so the live site follows the article being read.
Cross-ref workspace-8ne3 (headful browser, never headless), workspace-bd7i (phone viewport).
close_reason: Retired WebPaneMode.Snapshot entirely. WebPaneDecision.Decide returns Live whenever there's a displayable target (reader view now streams the LIVE site via the spotlight-followed display page) else Hidden — never a reader-fied snapshot. Deleted ReaderSnapshotHtml + its test, removed Snapshot from the enum, simplified IWebPaneSink.Update(mode), WebPaneHostBridge.BuildModeMessage/ApplyDesiredAsync, BrowserOrchestrator.SyncWebPane; removed the client iframe element + applyMode snapshot branch. Tests updated (WebPaneDecisionTests/WebPaneControlMessageTests). VERIFIED: G5 gate 'reader view streams the LIVE site (snapshot mode retired)' PASS (pane visible, screencast non-blank 303KB, #web-iframe absent). Full suite green.

title: Non-load classification mislabels hard blocks as 'captcha' — needs real definitions + reasons
description: When a site won't load, the app labels it 'captcha' even when it is not (screenshot: Cloudflare 'Sorry, you have been blocked' on macleans.ca shown as 'captcha at macleans.ca'). The user wants real definitions and information about WHY a page isn't loading, not a blanket 'captcha'.
design: Root cause: HumanActionDetector.cs:69 lists 'you have been blocked' as a CAPTCHA indicator, conflating an UNSOLVABLE Cloudflare hard block (HTTP 403/1020/1015, Ray ID, 'Sorry, you have been blocked') with a SOLVABLE captcha challenge. HumanActionVariant (Domain/Enums/Browser) has Captcha/Login/CookieConsent/TwoFactor/Paywall/RegionBlock/RedirectLoop/Generic but no 'bot block / access denied' category. FIX: (1) add HumanActionVariant.BotBlock (access-denied) and detect CF/WAF hard blocks ('Sorry, you have been blocked' + Ray ID + 403/1020/1015 + 'Attention Required' WITHOUT a solvable challenge widget) as BotBlock, not Captcha. (2) Give every variant a real user-facing definition + remediation copy (what it is / why / what to do): Captcha=solve challenge; BotBlock=site blocked automation, open in real browser; Paywall; Login; RegionBlock; RateLimit; NetworkError/DNS; RedirectLoop. (3) Split SOLVABLE (captcha/login/cookie/2FA — auto-resume after user action) from UNSOLVABLE (BotBlock/RegionBlock — offer 'open in real browser', don't spin waiting on a non-existent challenge). (4) Surface the correct reason + one-line explanation in the status bar and the web-pane error screen. Cross-ref: a real headful browser (sibling bead) removes most of these blocks at the source.
close_reason: Added HumanActionVariant.BotBlock (unsolvable hard WAF/bot block, distinct from a solvable captcha). HumanActionDetector.Detect now classifies CF/WAF hard blocks as BotBlock BEFORE captcha scoring: strong markers ('sorry, you have been blocked', CF 'security service' banner, error codes 1020/1015/1010) at any size, CF numeric error status, or HTTP 403 + a weak access-denied phrase on a small page — but it DEFERS to the captcha path when a solvable-challenge widget is present, and to Login when a login form is present (login wins). Per-variant copy: StatusBarRenderer verb 'blocked'; TerminalPageRenderer GetHumanActionCopy + GetInteractiveRefreshBody ('open it in your browser'). BotBlock stays out of IsWatchableVariant so the watcher never spins on it. Tests added: CF hard-block->BotBlock, error-1020, solvable-challenge-stays-captcha, 403+login-form->Login, copy headline + box-width. Also fixed a pre-existing TwoFactor refresh-body overflow (55->48 chars) surfaced by the new width guard. Full suite 4141/0.

title: Broken link-list borders (xterm box-drawing alignment + port divider-dim fix)
description: Link-list borders/dividers render broken (misaligned box-drawing).
design: Two parts: (A) BOX-DRAWING — the link list uses ─│╭╮╰╯┼ and block chars; in xterm.js these can render at inconsistent widths via the web font. Enable xterm customGlyphs (and ensure the canvas/webgl renderer) so box-drawing/block elements are drawn pixel-aligned to the cell grid — the xterm-native equivalent of old 6061826f 'pin box-drawing glyphs to the cell grid'. Configure in src/WireCopy.Web/wwwroot/index.html. (B) DIVIDER — port the shared-renderer fix 4a8801d8 (LinkTreeRenderer.cs: dim the link-list column divider to match the card rules), NOT on this branch. Verify in-app.
close_reason: Borders: ported commit 4a8801d8 — LinkTreeRenderer column divider (|/+) now dimmed with {Dim} to match the horizontal card rules (the brightness mismatch that made borders look broken). Set customGlyphs:true in the xterm config. NOTE (honest scope): this xterm bundle already defaults customGlyphs=true and ships only the DOM renderer (no canvas/webgl addon vendored), so customGlyphs is inert with the DOM renderer; the concrete border fixes are the divider-dim port + the card-height alignment (dlkb.1), with box-drawing relying on DejaVu Sans Mono's correct cell metrics. Vendoring a canvas/webgl addon to make customGlyphs active is a possible follow-up. linktree tests pass; G5 gate TUI renders.

title: Font + launcher card size too small (xterm font + port LauncherRenderer 4->5 card height)
description: Font and launcher cards render too small vs the old branch.
design: Two parts: (A) FONT — src/WireCopy.Web/wwwroot/index.html sets xterm fontSize:14; bump (try 16-18) and/or make responsive to the term-pane size; the old branch restored a larger TUI font (96389db9/75565792). (B) CARD SIZE — port the shared-renderer fix 5c2c4f36 (LauncherRenderer.cs: launcher card height 4->5 rows, aligned with the link list) which is NOT on this branch; update tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/LauncherBoxedGridTests.cs accordingly. Verify in-app with a screenshot.
close_reason: FONT: xterm fontSize 14->16 in index.html (xterm-native equivalent of the old larger-font fix). CARD SIZE: ported commit 5c2c4f36 — LauncherRenderer GridCardHeight 4->5 (separator moved off hardcoded case 3 to the cell's last line; freed interior/top-pad rows fall through to default with selBg+accent when selected) and updated LauncherBoxedGridTests (CellHeight 5). Build clean; launcher/linktree tests 228/0; full suite green; G5 gate confirms the TUI renders in xterm.js.

title: Weird characters on launcher that move with the selector (verify peq2; stateful UTF-8 / glyphs)
description: Stray characters appear on the launcher and move as the selection cursor moves.
design: Likely root cause already addressed by workspace-peq2 (non-atomic repaint -> ghost cells; now wrapped in BeginFrame/EndFrame). STEP 1: re-verify in-app whether the moving characters are gone after peq2. If residual: STEP 2 — stateful UTF-8: TerminalBridge.RunAsync (src/WireCopy.Web/TerminalBridge.cs ~line 92-98) reads up to 8192 raw PTY bytes and forwards them as a binary websocket frame; a multibyte UTF-8 / box-drawing glyph split across two reads relies on xterm.js's internal decoder holding state across writes — confirm it does, else buffer the partial trailing sequence server-side (the old branch fixed the analogous split in the Avalonia TerminalView, 8f388462). STEP 3 — box/block glyphs (wordmark) via xterm customGlyphs (see sibling borders bead).
close_reason: Moving/stray characters: peq2 (already closed) fixed the ghost selection-accent via BeginFrame/EndFrame. For split-multibyte UTF-8 (the 'stray ? marks' class) the client now decodes PTY bytes with the ONE persistent TextDecoder using {stream:true}, so a box-drawing/punctuation glyph split across two websocket frames is buffered and completed on the next frame instead of flushing to a replacement char (xterm-native equivalent of the old stateful-UTF-8 PTY decoder 8f388462). customGlyphs set true. Full suite green; G5 gate TUI renders correctly.

title: Restore TUI visual fidelity in the browser-hosted (xterm.js) shell — regressions vs web-pane-image-mirror
description: Visual regressions reported on this branch vs feat/web-pane-image-mirror: (1) font + launcher card size too small, (2) weird characters on the launcher that move as the selector moves, (3) broken link-list borders. The OLD branch fixed these in the Avalonia/vendored-terminal renderer (epic workspace-sf9o et al). This branch re-architected to a browser-hosted xterm.js-over-PTY shell, so: some SHARED renderer fixes were never ported, and the host-specific font/glyph fixes need xterm.js-native equivalents (config in src/WireCopy.Web/wwwroot/index.html), not the old Avalonia TerminalView changes. Plan split into three children.
design:
OLD fixes and how they map to the new architecture:
- 96389db9 'restore larger font / no tofu' + 75565792 'responsive launcher fill' — Avalonia font; new equiv = xterm fontSize/responsive in index.html.
