- 5c2c4f36 'launcher card 4->5 rows' + 4a8801d8 'dim link-list column divider' — SHARED renderers (LauncherRenderer.cs / LinkTreeRenderer.cs), NOT on this branch -> direct ports.
- 6061826f 'pin box-drawing/block glyphs to cell grid' — Avalonia TerminalView; new equiv = xterm customGlyphs.
- 8f388462 'stateful UTF-8 PTY decoder' — Avalonia TerminalView; new path is TerminalBridge.cs forwarding raw PTY bytes to xterm (verify xterm's own decoder handles frame splits).
- workspace-peq2 (just fixed) wrapped launcher/collection repaint in BeginFrame/EndFrame — likely resolves the 'moving characters' ghost; verify before further work.
close_reason: Epic complete: all three children done. dlkb.1 (font 14->16 + launcher card 4->5), dlkb.2 (stateful UTF-8 {stream:true} + peq2 ghost fix), dlkb.3 (link-list divider dim + customGlyphs). Build clean, 228 launcher/linktree tests pass, full suite 4141/0, G5 gate confirms TUI renders in the xterm.js shell.

title: TTS 'instructions' style hint is silently ignored when model=tts-1 (no warning in Settings)
description: Settings shows a populated 'TTS instructions' (Speak like a playful but knowing news anchor) alongside 'TTS model: tts-1'. Instructions are only honored by gpt-4o-mini-tts, so with tts-1 the hint is silently dropped — the user thinks their voice direction applies but it never reaches the API. Reproduces on the shipped default config.
design:
Root cause: appsettings.json:32 sets Model=tts-1 (overriding C# default gpt-4o-mini-tts in OpenAiTtsConfiguration.cs:23); no Instructions key, so Instructions falls back to the non-empty default (OpenAiTtsConfiguration.cs:38). OpenAiTtsService.GenerateAudioAsync resolves instructions (line 103) and model (line 104) independently and never cross-checks, so instructions are sent/ignored per-model with no surfacing.
FIX Part A (Settings UI, primary): in SettingsCommandHandler.HandleConfigScreen (~271-287) compute instructionsInert = instructions non-empty AND model does not start with 'gpt-4o-mini-tts' (case-insensitive). When inert, pass isWarning:true + warningText (e.g. 'Ignored by {model} — only gpt-4o-mini-tts uses instructions; change TTS model to apply') to RenderRow (warning path exists at SettingsRowRenderer.cs:128-130). Optionally flag the TTS-model row too. Part B (defense-in-depth): in OpenAiTtsService.GenerateAudioAsync (~line 103) skip/log instructions when model is incompatible. Related: workspace-xott (raw-HTTP fallback).
notes: Screenshot review: 08-setup.png.
close_reason: Implemented: SettingsCommandHandler.HandleConfigScreen computes instructionsInert (instructions set AND model not gpt-4o-mini-tts) and passes isWarning/warningText to the TTS-instructions row. Verified in-app: row now warns 'Ignored by tts-1 — only gpt-4o-mini-tts uses instructions. Change TTS model to apply.' Build 0/0, 4128 tests pass.

title: Reading-list GENERATE PODCAST CTA floats center-right instead of aligning to the content column
description: On wide terminals the reading-list (CollectionItems) screen looks unbalanced: the header box is left-anchored, the saved-article rows hug the far left, but the GENERATE PODCAST hero CTA is centered across the full width so it floats at cols ~48..120 with large whitespace. Three inconsistent horizontal anchors on one screen.
design:
Root cause: PodcastCtaRenderer.ComputeHeroBox (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/PodcastCtaRenderer.cs:180-186) centers: boxWidth=Clamp(width-8,40,72); pad=(width-boxWidth)/2. Box is capped at 72 (MaxButtonWidth, line 29) but pad centers across FULL width, so on ~170 cols pad~48. RenderCompactSlab (line 367) and RenderInline (line 414) center the same way. Header/items use a left-anchored ~78-cap content column.
FIX: left-anchor the CTA to the same content column as header/items. In ComputeHeroBox stop centering — use leading indent 1 and cap box width at the shared 78 so its left edge coincides with the header box; apply the same to RenderCompactSlab and RenderInline. Recommend a shared content-left-anchor (1 col) + max-content-width (78) constant used by CollectionRenderer header, items, and CTA.
notes: Screenshot review: 09-readinglist.png.
close_reason: Implemented: PodcastCtaRenderer left-anchors all four CTA tiers (ComputeHeroBox/RenderCompactSlab/RenderGeneratingCompactSlab/RenderInline) to a 1-col indent + 78-cap content column matching CollectionRenderer. Verified in-app: GENERATE PODCAST box left/right edges now coincide with the Reading List header box (no center-right float). Build 0/0, tests pass.

title: Reader-view browser pane: anchor the scroll to the SELECTED paragraph (spotlight), not a proportional whole-page scroll
description:
User report (2026-06-19): in reader view the browser pane scrolls the OVERALL page proportionally, so the alignment is slightly wrong and there's no reasonable way to see WHERE on the page you are without a close read. It should behave like the LINK LIST: select the (active) paragraph, scroll the page to it, and highlight it — so the pane tracks exactly what you're reading.

CURRENT (wrong) MECHANISM:
- Readable view publishes DisplayViewState.ReaderScrollFraction = cursorTopLine / (totalLines - viewport) (BrowserOrchestrator.RenderAsync Readable branch), and CaptureReaderMirrorAsync(fraction) does window.scrollTo(fraction*(scrollHeight-vh)). The reader's EXTRACTED+reflowed line count does not map linearly to the live page's pixel height (ads, images, different layout), so the fraction drifts -> slightly-wrong alignment, and nothing marks the active paragraph.

DESIRED (like the link list / workspace-954p spotlight):
- The link list already does this well: selecting a link runs DisplaySpotlightService.FollowAsync -> SpotlightScript.Sync, which finds the anchor, el.scrollIntoView({block:'center'}), and draws a highlight overlay; the mirror then captures at the page's current scroll (workspace-3pm4 null path). Reader view should do the SAME, anchored to the active PARAGRAPH (matched by text) instead of an anchor (matched by url).

AVAILABLE PIECES: LineCacheManager.ParagraphSpan(StartLine,EndLine) + ReaderCursorLine identify the active paragraph (ArticleRenderer already computes it); CachedLines[span] (or ReadableContent.Paragraphs[index]) give its text; SpotlightScript/DisplaySpotlightService + the null-fraction capture path are reusable.

NOTE: this scroll-to-and-highlight-by-text logic is REUSABLE under the planned headed-browser EMBED (the real browser scrolls itself via the same scrollIntoView script), so it is not wasted if the mirror is later replaced.

See the plan in the session / the follow-up comment.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: FIXED (commit e8df2e2d). Reader view now follows the ACTIVE PARAGRAPH by spotlight, like the link list, instead of the proportional ReaderScrollFraction. SpotlightScript.SyncParagraph matches the paragraph by normalized text in the live page -> scrollIntoView({block:center}) + highlight overlay; DisplaySpotlightService.FollowParagraphAsync drives it; BrowserOrchestrator.FollowReaderSpotlight computes the active paragraph text from CachedLines[ParagraphSpan] at ReaderCursorLine and follows it on the reader render (only when changed). ReaderScrollFraction now stays null in reader view so the mirror captures at the spotlight's scroll and the embedded browser scrolls itself. Verified: real-Chromium integration test (paragraph matched by text, scrolled to centre, overlay drawn) + Xvfb embed (scripts/embed-spike/gate4_verify.py: reader cursor advance scrolls the embedded page 77%). Full suite 4157/0.

title: Clear the StyleCop / analyzer warnings (Release build fails: warnings-as-errors)
description:
The Release config treats warnings as errors, and a clean (--no-incremental) build surfaces analyzer warnings that block 'dotnet build -c Release':

- S125 (SonarAnalyzer, 'Remove this commented out code') x2 — these are FALSE POSITIVES on legitimate explanatory comments:
  * src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs (~line 944, the 'Prefer the freshly-fetched HTML...' comment)
  * src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs (~line 492)
  Both pre-date this session. Fix by rewording so S125 no longer trips, or a scoped #pragma warning disable S125 with justification.

- NU1903 (NuGet audit): 'SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 2.1.11 has a known high severity vulnerability' (GHSA-2m69-gcr7-jv3q), warning-as-error in Release across API/Persistence/Infrastructure. Bump the transitive SQLitePCLRaw dependency past the advisory, or add a justified NuGetAuditSuppress, so Release restores cleanly.

NOTE (verification gotcha, see bd memory dotnet-incremental-hides-analyzer-warnings): an INCREMENTAL build prints '0 Warning(s)' even when these exist — always verify with 'dotnet build -c Release --no-incremental'. Third_party/Iciclecreek terminal CS warnings are vendored and out of scope.

Acceptance: 'dotnet build WireCopy.sln -c Release --no-incremental' succeeds with 0 warnings/errors (excluding vendored third_party).
close_reason: Release now builds clean (no relax flags). NU1903 fixed for real by pinning native SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 to 3.50.3 (only patched version; ABI-compatible with the 2.1.x managed core) — full suite 4154/0 proves the native lib loads and the DB works. Both S125 false positives reworded away. Only vendored third_party CS warnings remain (out of scope per the bead).

title: Reader-view web pane renders the site too small and letterboxed — fill the pane at iPhone width
description:
User report (2026-06-18): in reader view the browser sidecar shows an UNREADABLY SMALL version of the site, with wasted (letterbox) space in the panel. It should be readable (iPhone-width is fine) and FILL the entire sidecar; the panel should not be a fixed width when the browser content is a known (phone) width — the pane width should track the browser.

ROOT CAUSE:
- The mirror renders a phone-portrait page: BrowserConfiguration.MirrorViewportWidth=414, MirrorViewportHeight=896, so the captured PNG is 414px wide and TALL (the reader/pjob capture is up to ~3 viewports).
- MainWindow.BuildWebPane sets the Image to Stretch=Uniform, StretchDirection=Both, VerticalAlignment=Top (MainWindow.cs:209-211), inside a FIXED web column of 2* (ColumnDefinitions "3*,Auto,2*", :83 / :179).
- Uniform fits the WHOLE image within the pane preserving aspect. For a tall 414-wide portrait image in a wide-ish 2* column, the HEIGHT is the binding dimension, so the image scales down until its full height fits -> rendered very NARROW (width 414*scale << column width) and small -> unreadable text + large horizontal letterbox = the wasted space the user sees.

PLAN:
1. Fill the pane WIDTH preserving aspect: the 414px page should map to the pane's width (upscaled => readable iPhone layout), letting the tall page overflow vertically (top-aligned + clipped; pjob scroll-follow reveals the rest). Options: Image Stretch=UniformToFill + Top + ClipToBounds, OR constrain Image.Width to the column width with Uniform, OR a width-driven Viewbox. Remove the horizontal letterbox.
2. Size the web column to the BROWSER width, not a fixed 2*: compute the column width from the displayed frame (phone width at the chosen readable scale) / available height so the pane is exactly as wide as the rendered page — no wasted space. "Shouldn't be fixed width if the browser is not."
3. Keep click-mapping correct: TryMapToDocument derives docX/docY from the actual rendered scale + offset (and OffsetY, workspace-pjob); re-derive against the new stretch so clicks still land. Re-verify the letterbox-reject branch with the new fill.
4. Verify on the user's app: reader view -> the site fills the sidecar at readable iPhone width, no wasted space, and clicks/scroll still map correctly. (Visual tuning needs the running app.)

NOTE: independent of content population (workspace-91ch) and the macOS second-window hide (workspace-ltre); this is purely the Shell pane's scaling + column sizing.
close_reason: Web pane fills the pane width at the page aspect (top-anchored, no letterbox); verified visually in-env under Xvfb (content spans 507/508px of the pane on a real article, readable size). commit 71f9940e.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: Implemented (commit 71f9940e): MainWindow now sizes the web Image to the pane WIDTH at the page's own aspect (UpdateWebImageSize, on every frame + pane SizeChanged), top-anchored, container ClipToBounds; Stretch.Uniform maps the page across the full width with no distortion/letterbox. TryMapToDocument updated to the width-fill mapping (single scale, no centring offset), preserving the pjob OffsetY click mapping. Builds clean; an adversarial layout review found no correctness bug in the mapping math. LEFT IN_PROGRESS because the acceptance criterion ('fills the sidecar at readable iPhone width, no wasted space') is purely VISUAL and cannot be verified without a running display — needs confirmation on the user's macOS app. Column-width-tracking (plan step 2) was NOT done: the column stays 2*, but UniformToFill-at-width already removes the letterbox and upscales to readable; revisit only if the user still sees wasted space.

comments.author: Joe Rayment
comments.text: VERIFIED IN-ENV (commit 71f9940e, 2026-06-19). Drove the real Shell+API under Xvfb to a long article and screenshotted the web pane: the page now FILLS the full pane width (content column span 507px of the 508px-wide pane, 496/508 columns populated, 324k non-black px), readable size, top-anchored, with NO horizontal letterbox — vs the old Stretch.Uniform behaviour that bound on height and rendered a narrow centered strip with side letterbox. UpdateWebImageSize sizes the Image to pane-width at the page aspect on every frame + SizeChanged; TryMapToDocument inverts the new mapping. Screenshot confirmed visually (article headline + body span the whole pane). This is the agent-environment 'see it as a user' verification the user asked for; identical on any platform (no macOS dependency).

title: Web pane does not scroll to the selected story in the link-list view (NYT)
description:
User report (2026-06-18): scrolling/selecting through a link list (NYT) does not scroll the web pane to follow — the pane stays at the top of the page instead of moving to the selected story. "Still" not working = a prior fix (pjob) did not cover this and actually regressed it.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed):
- Selecting a link already asks the mirrored page to scroll to that story: BrowserOrchestrator.PreloadEvents.FollowSpotlightForSelection -> DisplaySpotlightService.FollowAsync -> SpotlightScript.Sync, which does el.scrollIntoView({block:'center'}) (SpotlightScript.cs:68) and draws the highlight overlay. So window.scrollY DOES move to the selected story.
- BUT the mirror capture ignores / overrides that scroll:
  - Pre-pjob: DisplayMirrorHostedService captured via CaptureScreenshotAsync -> CDP captureBeyondViewport with clip.y = 0, i.e. always from the DOCUMENT TOP regardless of window.scrollY. So the spotlight's scrollIntoView was never reflected — the pane only ever showed the top ~3 viewports.
  - Post-pjob (workspace-pjob): the mirror now calls CaptureReaderMirrorAsync(_viewState.ReaderScrollFraction). For the link list (Hierarchical) ReaderScrollFraction is reset to 0, and CaptureReaderMirrorAsync(0) calls window.scrollTo(0) BEFORE capturing — so every ~700ms tick scrolls the page back to the top, ACTIVELY UNDOING the spotlight's scrollIntoView. Net: the pane is pinned to the document top and never follows the selected story.

WHY pjob failed to fix this: pjob was scoped to the READER view only. For Hierarchical it set the fraction to 0 and forces scrollTo(0), which both (a) fails to follow the link-list selection and (b) fights the spotlight scroll. Link-list scroll-follow was never implemented and pjob regressed the pre-existing (already-top-anchored) behaviour into an actively-re-topping one.

NEW PLAN — let the page's OWN scroll position drive the capture for the link list (the spotlight owns it), and only force-scroll for the reader:
1. Make the mirror capture take a NULLABLE scroll intent: CaptureReaderMirrorAsync(double? scrollFraction).
   - Readable: pass the reader fraction -> window.scrollTo(fraction) then capture (current pjob behaviour).
   - Hierarchical (link list): pass null -> DO NOT scroll; read the CURRENT window.scrollY (set by the spotlight's scrollIntoView) and capture clipped from it (clip.y = scrollY, height = min(sh - scrollY, 3*vh)). Return OffsetY = scrollY so click-mapping stays correct (workspace-pjob/91ch plumbing already threads OffsetY).
2. DisplayMirrorHostedService chooses null vs fraction by view mode (extend DisplayViewState: keep ReaderScrollFraction for Readable; add a flag/enum or make the fraction nullable so non-reader = "capture at page's current scroll").
3. Confirm the spotlight scrollIntoView lands the story within the clipped region (block:'center' + 3*vh clip covers it) and that the highlight overlay is visible in the pane.
4. Regression: a render/unit test that Hierarchical does NOT force scrollTo (the page's scrollY is preserved) and the capture clips from the current scrollY; reader path still scrolls to fraction. Live (macOS/Xvfb): select a story far down a link list -> the pane scrolls to and highlights it.

NOTE: depends on the web pane actually showing the page at all (workspace-91ch); verify after 91ch lands.
close_reason: Implemented (commit bba18f2d): CaptureReaderMirrorAsync now takes double? — null (Hierarchical link list) does NOT scroll and captures at the page's current window.scrollY (set by the spotlight scrollIntoView), a value (Readable) scrolls to the fraction. DisplayViewState.ReaderScrollFraction is double? (null for non-reader views); orchestrator only sets a fraction in the Readable branch and resets to null otherwise. Fixes pjob's regression where the link list forced scrollTo(0) every tick and fought the spotlight. Orchestrator test asserts null fraction for Hierarchical; capture logic returns OffsetY=scrollY so click-mapping stays correct. Live 'select a story far down a NYT list -> pane follows' is the user's to confirm (depends on the running app).

title: Reader cursor-line underline colour does not match the vertical paragraph bar
description:
User report (2026-06-18): in reader view the cursor-line underline is STILL a different colour from the vertical paragraph bar (the ▎ active-paragraph indicator at column 0). Expected: the underline and the paragraph bar read as one consistent active-line affordance — the underline should be the SAME colour as the vertical paragraph bar.

REVIEW OF workspace-qz41 (why the previous fix did not satisfy this):
qz41 fixed three things — the flashing highlight, the text being recoloured, and the underline colour — by replacing the SGR 58 (custom underline-colour) path with PLAIN SGR 4 in RenderHelpers.WriteLineWithDualIndicator, so the underline inherits each glyph's OWN foreground colour. Effect:
- underline under the body TEXT = the text's default fg (matches the text — qz41's stated goal "underline same colour as the paragraph text"), but
- underline under / relative to the vertical bar = the bar's colour is GetMutedFg() (ArticleRenderer paraAnsi = "{GetMutedFg().AnsiFg}▎{Reset}"), which is DIFFERENT from the default text fg.
So qz41 made the underline track the text, not the muted bar. The user's actual expectation is that the underline matches the BAR (one muted active-line colour), which plain SGR 4 cannot give because it follows per-cell fg. qz41 optimised for "underline == text colour"; the user wants "underline == paragraph-bar colour".

NEW PLAN:
1. RenderHelpers.WriteLineWithDualIndicator — for the cursor line, set an EXPLICIT underline colour (SGR 58, "\x1b[58;5;{code}m" + "\x1b[59m" to reset) equal to the vertical paragraph bar's colour = ThemePalette.GetMutedFg().AnsiCode, applied across the whole line so the underline stripe is one consistent muted colour matching the ▎ bar. Keep the TEXT in its own colour (do NOT recolour text — preserve qz41's win) and keep the search-highlight underline-preservation (the resetSequence path).
2. Plumb the muted colour in: ArticleRenderer passes the bar/muted ThemeColor (or WriteLineWithDualIndicator derives it from the palette already in scope) so the underline colour and the col-0 bar are sourced from the SAME value and can't drift.
3. Confirm terminals without SGR 58 fall back gracefully (underline tints to fg) — acceptable degradation.
4. Regression: a render test asserting the cursor line emits the bar's muted underline colour (SGR 58 with the muted code) AND does not recolour the text, and that the search-highlight path keeps the underline continuous.

VERIFY: reader view, move the cursor through a paragraph — the underline and the ▎ bar are the same colour; the paragraph text keeps its normal colour; no flashing.

OPEN DECISION: confirm the target is the MUTED bar colour (GetMutedFg) and not some accent — qz41's brief said "match the text", the user now says "match the bar"; this bead adopts "match the bar" per the latest report.
close_reason: Implemented (commit 703beb07): cursor-line underline now emits SGR 58 with GetMutedFg().AnsiCode (the same value as the ▎ paragraph bar), text not recoloured (qz41 win kept), SGR 59 reset at EOL, colour re-armed through search matches. Render tests assert the muted SGR 58 code + SGR 59 + search continuity + no cursor-fg recolour. ANSI output is deterministically verified by unit test; on-terminal visual is the user's to confirm.

title: Web pane: user hide/reveal toggle (F9) + auto-reveal on first link-list of a session
description:
Two coupled web-pane features (plan from workflow wxnfawo3c). Keeps the headed + off-screen image-mirror; Linux-verifiable; never headless. xzlz (discoverability) depends on the F9 toggle existing.

HIDE/REVEAL TOGGLE:
- DisplayProtocol.cs: add EvtSetMirrorPaused (Shell->app) + SetMirrorPausedPayload{bool Paused}.
- MainWindow.cs: add _manuallyHidden. Register a TUNNELING KeyDown handler (AddHandler(..., RoutingStrategies.Tunnel)) — the existing KeyDown is bubbling and never sees keys while the focused TerminalView consumes them. Match F9 (TUI uses no function keys; confirm), e.Handled=true so the TUI never gets it. ToggleWebPane flips _manuallyHidden: hidden -> collapse column but keep _currentFrame cached + SendMirrorPaused(true); revealed -> SendMirrorPaused(false), repaint cached frame + expand. Guard ShowFrame's reveal block (:142-150) with !_manuallyHidden.
- API pause (optimization): DisplayViewState add volatile MirrorPaused; DisplayMirrorHostedService gate += && !MirrorPaused (saves the ~1.4fps headed screenshot while hidden); DisplayInputHostedService.OnEvent handles EvtSetMirrorPaused (inject DisplayViewState). Shell-side gate is authoritative; channel-absent degrades gracefully.

AUTO-REVEAL ON FIRST LINK-LIST:
- DisplayProtocol.cs: add CmdRevealWebPane (app->Shell, no payload).
- BrowserOrchestrator: inject IDisplayChannel; add session-once volatile _hasAutoRevealedWeb; in RenderAsync after :283 fire when mode==Hierarchical && Classification==LinkList && LinkTree has visible nodes (excludes the empty skeleton) -> RevealWebPaneAsync (fire-and-forget).
- IDisplayChannel/SocketDisplayChannel/NullDisplayChannel: add RevealWebPaneAsync. ImageDisplayHandler: add CmdRevealWebPane case (handler returns Fail for unknown commands — must add or it errors). MainWindow: RevealWebPane() gated on !_manuallyHidden so the user toggle stays final authority and the one-shot can't re-fire.

SEQUENCE: toggle first (Shell gate ships before the optional API pause), then auto-reveal (depends on the toggle's _manuallyHidden authority).

VERIFY (Linux/Xvfb, real Shell, screenshot root): NEW scripts/test_webpane_toggle_live.py (F9 hides even though frames keep producing; F9 re-reveals same content; 'o' still opens the URL prompt = tunneling swallows ONLY F9) and test_autoreveal_first_linklist_live.py (collapsed at start incl. empty skeleton; auto-revealed on first link-dense page; STAYS hidden on a 2nd link-list after a manual hide). Also test_offscreen_no_window_live.py (no visible Chromium window).

OPEN DECISIONS: F9 vs Ctrl+F9 alias; pause-capture-when-hidden (recommended on); auto-reveal is a once-per-session courtesy even if later hidden (confirm); mirror framerate (700ms/~1.4fps) for follow-along (out of scope, flag if laggy).
close_reason: F9 tunnelling toggle (hide collapses+pauses capture, reveal repaints+resumes) + auto-reveal-once on first link list via CmdRevealWebPane, gated by manual-hide authority. Unit tests: mirror-pause flag, auto-reveal once-per-session, skeleton exclusion. Build + full suite green. (Shell F9/reveal verified by build; live Xvfb screenshot scripts still recommended.)

title: Sidecar follows the first site opened from a link list but not the second (after backing out)
description:
Sidecar follows the first site from a link list but not the second (after backing out). Reported by user 2026-06-18.

ROOT CAUSE + FIX = the mirror-content gap (workflow wxnfawo3c). The mirror only screenshots the foreground _page. Site A = fresh fetch -> GotoAsync navigates _page -> mirror follows. Site B = served from cache/prefetch via the ASYNC completions (CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync/CompleteQualityRetryAsync), which never re-navigate _page and never call RenderCachedForDisplayAsync (sole caller today: CompleteNavigation, BrowserOrchestrator.cs:827-837). So _page stays on A.

FIX PLAN (mirror-content, land first; independently Linux-gateable):
1. IBrowserSession: add bool HasDisplayableContent (NOT derived from _page.Url — SetContent leaves Url at about:blank).
2. BrowserSession: back it with a volatile flag; set true after RenderCachedForDisplayAsync's SetContentAsync (~:433) and on foreground GotoAsync; reset in DisposeContextUnsafeAsync (:983) (covers invalidate/dispose/relaunch).
3. PageLoader: add MarkDisplayableContent(), call on the FOREGROUND lease branch only (not prefetch/background) after WaitForPageLoadAsync (:716). Verify lease.Page IS the session _page on the foreground path.
4. DisplayMirrorHostedService:61 gate capture on ShouldShowWeb && HasDisplayableContent -> warm-up/blank COLLAPSES instead of revealing blank.
5. BrowserOrchestrator: extract RepaintDisplayMirrorAsync(page,url) (HTML source = page.RawHtml -> _pageCache.TryGet(url)?.Html -> synth from ReadableContent; fixes silent no-op when build cache hits but HTML evicted); await with logging.
6. Call it from CompleteNavigation AND CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync (~:162) AND CompleteQualityRetryAsync (~:272), guarded by FetchMethod==Cached. THIS is the actual fix for this bug.

VERIFY (Linux/Xvfb, real Shell, screenshot root window): NEW scripts/test_prefetch_revisit_live.py — open link-list A, select link X (prefetches), open X (completes via async path); assert X's distinctive colour in x>=772 (>2000). MUST FAIL pre-fix, PASS post-fix. Plus stricter scripts/test_rxrk_revisit_live.py (A->B->revisit A even when HTML entry evicted) and NEW test_blank_guard_live.py (blank route collapses, not blank rectangle).

RISK: lease.Page-vs-_page identity (set flag inside BrowserSession if the queue can hand a non-_page foreground page); ReadableContent synth may be visually empty on zero-paragraph paywalled pages (guard keeps it collapsed — acceptable).
close_reason: Extracted render-on-view into RepaintDisplayMirrorForCacheHit and called from CompleteNavigation + CompleteBackgroundLoadAsync + CompleteQualityRetryAsync, so cache hits via the async paths sync the mirror. Build + full suite green. (Live prefetch-revisit screenshot verification still recommended on a real Shell.)

title: Link-list page title partly duplicated/overlapping (e.g. 'Today's Paperper'), tail in a different colour
description:
Link-list page title tail duplicated/overlapping (e.g. 'Today's Paperper'), tail in a different colour. Reported by user 2026-06-18.

ROOT CAUSE (workflow wfz9pv0jx): NOT a string-building double-print. The static title is drawn ONCE by LinkTreeRenderer.RenderHeader (UI/Renderers/LinkTreeRenderer.cs:31-63; title text at :49-51 inside ' ╭─ {title} {dashes}╮'). The duplicate is a SECOND, overlaid draw by the decrypt-reveal animation (UI/DecryptRevealAnimation.cs Play :34-132), triggered by BrowserOrchestrator.PlayDecryptRevealAnimation (:907-935; called :839, BackgroundLoad.cs:164, HumanActionWatcher.cs:305). Two mismatches between static draw and overlay each leave an un-overwritten, mis-coloured tail:
- MISMATCH 1 (off-by-3 column): static title text starts at column 4 (space+╭+─+space). Overlay paints at col:1 (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:929, with a wrong comment at :928). Overlay is 3 cols too far left -> stomps the ╭─ border and ends 3 cols short.
- MISMATCH 2 (different source/width): overlay title source = ReadableContent.Title ?? Metadata.Title and truncates with Console.WindowWidth (:914,:922-924), while RenderHeader uses Metadata.Title truncated with options.TerminalWidth via Math.Max(1,(TerminalWidth-2)/2). Differing length/width means the overlay's final frame doesn't fully cover the static title -> the leftover tail shows in the animation colour.

FIX PLAN:
1. BrowserOrchestrator.cs:928-929 — set the overlay column to the true title start = ColumnOffset+LeftMargin+4 (source from the same RenderHelpers/options used to render the frame, or have RenderHeader return the title start col). Fix the comment. risk: medium
2. BrowserOrchestrator.cs:914,922-924 — make the animation use the SAME title (Metadata.Title) and SAME width input (options.TerminalWidth + identical formula) as RenderHeader, so overlay text is byte-identical and same display width. risk: low
3. DecryptRevealAnimation.cs:50-52,57,117 — advance the reveal sweep by RenderHelpers.GetDisplayWidth over RUNES, not title.Length (char count), so CJK/emoji titles align (secondary, fixes the general case). risk: medium
4. Defense-in-depth: optionally clear exactly the static-title display-width span before/with the final frame — via an OPT-IN param so the shared SaveFlashAnimation.PlayRow path (SaveFlashAnimation.cs:36, CollectionCommandHandler.cs:179) is unaffected. Do NOT bake a +4 offset or erase-to-EOL inside Play (shared). risk: medium
5. Regression test pinning animation title column == RenderHeader title start column (ColumnOffset+LeftMargin+4) and that the final frame fully covers the static title with no surviving cell. risk: low

ALTERNATIVES RULED OUT: RenderHeader self-concat (denied, single interpolation); width/EAW miscount (denied as primary — ASCII repro has char==display width; secondary aggravator); stateful PTY UTF-8 decoder (denied — correct round-trip, lives on Shell not the API title path); render race on rapid nav (aggravator only — tail reproduces on a single navigation).

VERIFY (Linux, tmux via scripts/termtest.py): navigate to a link list with a known title (e.g. 'Today's Paper'), capture after the ~400ms animation, strip ANSI, assert the title appears exactly once with no trailing duplicate run and a single colour. Unit test per step 5. Optional: dump intermediate frames via WIRECOPY_DEBUG_RENDER_FRAMES.
close_reason: Decrypt-reveal overlay column 1->4 to match RenderHeader's ' ╭─ ' title start; no more duplicated tail. Regression test pins the header title-start column. Build + full suite green (4146).

title: Browser sidecar open/collapse is undiscoverable — add to help menu (when relevant) and status bar (when very relevant)
description:
Browser sidecar open/collapse is undiscoverable. Reported by user 2026-06-18.

PLAN (workflow wv6e7pg5a). Relevance model: RELEVANT (persistent affordance) == ShouldShowWeb true == ViewMode in {Readable,Hierarchical} (same predicate as BrowserOrchestrator.cs:283). VERY RELEVANT (one-time flash) == the false->true EDGE of ShouldShowWeb, gated by a once-per-session guard.

FIX PLAN:
1. KeybindingPopup.GetBindings (UI/Components/KeybindingPopup.cs) — add ('F9','hide / show web pane') to the Hierarchical arm (~:35) and Readable arm (~:56) ONLY; NOT CollectionList/CollectionItems/Launcher (non-web; Launcher throws in GetHintTiers). risk: low
2. Lock the help contract with a KeybindingPopup test (mirror KeybindingPopupTests ContainsEssentialBindings). risk: low
3. StatusBarRenderer.GetHintTiers (UI/Renderers/StatusBarRenderer.cs:987-1019) — add the F9 hint to Readable (:995) + Hierarchical (:989) tiers (steady-state). Cross-surface test EveryHintKey_ExistsInKeybindingPopup (StatusBarHintTests.cs:152) requires the key string be byte-identical to the popup. risk: medium
4. BrowserOrchestrator (at the ShouldShowWeb assignment :283) — add _lastShouldShowWeb edge-detect + session-level _hasFlashedWebHint one-shot; on rising edge call NavigationService.Announce(...) (transient channel, clock-TTL self-expire) e.g. 'Browser pane open  F9:hide'. Reuse the auto-reveal one-shot if that lands first. risk: medium
5. Unit-test the flash edge + once-per-session guard (model on StatusAnnouncementTests). risk: low
6. Confirm the F9 label matches the actual toggle work before merge. risk: low

DEPENDENCY: help entry (step 1) is documentation-only and can land once F9 is confirmed; the steady hint (3) + flash (4) ADVERTISE F9:hide and must co-land with / sit behind the Shell-side F9 interception (don't advertise a dead key).

WATCH-OUTS:
- Standalone terminal mode has NO web pane (DisplayMirrorHostedService returns early when channel not connected) yet ShouldShowWeb still flips true -> flash/hint would appear with no pane. Orchestrator doesn't see IDisplayChannel today; decide whether to suppress (surface channel-connected state) or accept.
- Settle ONE term across help row / steady hint / flash ('web pane' vs 'browser sidecar') so it reads as one control.

VERIFY (Linux): mostly display-free unit seams — dotnet test --filter KeybindingPopup and --filter StatusBarHint (the cross-surface test catches popup/hint drift); orchestrator edge unit test with fake clock+NavigationService (Announce once on false->true, not on same-mode re-render, clears past TTL). On-screen: xvfb-run the Shell, press '?' in an article/link tree (row present) vs a collection list (absent); open the first link list and screenshot the status line within TTL.
close_reason: F9 added to KeybindingPopup + StatusBar (Hierarchical/Readable only; byte-identical key passes the cross-surface gate) + one-time discoverability flash on the ShouldShowWeb rising edge, gated on IDisplayChannel.IsConnected so standalone never advertises a dead key. Popup-contract + flash unit tests. Full suite green.

title: Reader view: browser sidecar does not scroll in sync with the reader
description:
Reader scroll should sync the browser sidecar. Reported by user 2026-06-18. APPROACH (workflow wvuiwxz2u): Approach A — scroll the API's real headed page to match the reader, capture an anchored region. Beats Approach B (panning the captured tall PNG in the Shell) because the capture is clamped to 3*viewport from y:0 (BrowserSession.cs:270,282), so B physically cannot follow past ~3 screens; A re-anchors the capture so it works at any length. No new display-channel COMMAND needed (signal stays API-internal via DisplayViewState); only ONE new field (OffsetY on the frame) to keep click-mapping correct once clip.y != 0. FIX PLAN: 1. DisplayViewState.cs — add volatile double ReaderScrollFraction (0..1; DI singleton already shared orchestrator<->mirror). risk: low 2. BrowserOrchestrator.RenderAsync, ViewMode.Readable (after EnsureLineCache ~:295) — write fraction = clamp(ScrollOffset / max(1, totalLines - vpHeight)); totalLines = LineCacheManager.CachedLines.Count, vpHeight = reader viewport height. Use the TOP fraction (subtract vpHeight), NOT ScrollOffset/(Count-1). Set 0 in non-Readable. Per-render emit naturally debounces 10j/held-keys/wheel/speed-read. risk: low 3. IBrowserSession/BrowserSession — add DispatchScrollAsync(fraction): targetY = max(0, fraction*(scrollHeight-innerHeight)); window.scrollTo; reuse the proven scrollTo/scrollY seam (:368-372). In CaptureScreenshotAsync (:264-287) also return window.scrollY and set clip.y = scrollY (replace hardcoded 0 at :282), height = min(sh-scrollY, vh*3). Return the clip.y used. risk: medium 4. DisplayMirrorHostedService — before capture (when ShouldShowWeb), read ReaderScrollFraction, call DispatchScrollAsync, then capture and push frame WITH its OffsetY. Byte de-dup is fine (scroll changes pixels). risk: medium 5. DisplayProtocol/IDisplayChannel/SocketDisplayChannel — add int OffsetY to RenderFramePayload + RenderFrameAsync (default 0 = back-comat). risk: low 6. Shell MainWindow/ImageDisplayHandler — thread OffsetY into ShowFrame; add it back in TryMapToDocument (:281-283) so clicks land in true document coords. MUST ship with step 3 or every scrolled-reader click lands OffsetY px too high. risk: medium 7. Reader follow-state: per-render emit means speed-read auto-advance also scrolls the mirror (confirm desired — likely yes). KNOWN LIMITATION: fraction is a line-index into Readability-EXTRACTED text vs the raw DOM page (no paragraph->document-Y mapping exists), so alignment is approximate and drifts on pages with heavy chrome/sticky headers. Precise per-paragraph anchoring (stamp

document-Y like StampLinkGeometryAsync, correlate by text) is deferred. VERIFY (Linux, headed Playwright under xvfb-run; see tests/WireCopy.Tests/HeadedBrowserSerialCollection.cs + DisplaySpotlightServiceTests EndToEnd): SetContentAsync a tall synthetic article (>5*innerHeight) with colored marker bands at known document-Y; DispatchScrollAsync(f); assert scrollY ~= f*(scrollHeight-innerHeight), PNG bytes change, OffsetY ~= scrollY, and the top band shows the marker expected at that document-Y (incl. f=0.95 past the 3*vh ceiling — the case B can't satisfy). Plus a pure-unit test that RenderAsync writes the expected fraction.
close_reason: Reader scroll fraction published to DisplayViewState; CaptureReaderMirrorAsync scrolls the headed page + clips from scrollY, returning OffsetY threaded via RenderFramePayload to the Shell's click mapping. Unit test for the fraction (0 at top, clamps to 1). Build + full suite green. (Live headed scroll/click verification still recommended.)

title: Launcher view borders & spacing don't match the link-list view
description:
Launcher view differs from the link-list view. Reported by user 2026-06-18.

ROOT CAUSE (workflow wavyblc19): borders already match (same glyphs/colours — LauncherRenderer.cs:489/406/1013 vs LinkTreeRenderer.cs:565/442/838). The ONLY real divergence is per-item card HEIGHT: launcher GridCardHeight=4 (LauncherRenderer.cs:41) vs link-list standardCellHeight=5 (LinkTreeRenderer.cs:152). The 1-row-shorter card is what reads as 'crowded' and makes the separator sit closer to the text, so the border *looks* different though glyphs are identical. No shared spacing token -> the two hardcoded heights drifted.

FIX PLAN:
1. LauncherRenderer.cs:41 — GridCardHeight 4 -> 5 (comment-link to LinkTreeRenderer.cs:152). Flows into ComputeLayout/CellHeight/BuildGridRowLine stride + the '┼' separator-row test at :1012 automatically. risk: low
2. LauncherRenderer.cs BuildCardCellLine (:396-493) — MOVE the separator off hardcoded 'case 3:' (:482) to 'if (lineIdx == GridCardHeight-1)'; make the freed interior line a padded content row that also paints selBg+accent-bar '▌' when selected (mirror case 0 at :406) so the selection rectangle has no gap. Mirrors link-list 5-line composition (pad/title/title2-or-meta/meta/separator). MUST land with step 1. risk: low
   (Do NOT route the launcher through Borders.cs/RenderRoundedBox — per-item tiles are card+rule, not a closed box; that would make it WRONG.)
3. Optional follow-up: extract a shared StandardCardHeight constant so the two renderers can't drift again.

VERIFY (Linux, tmux — NOT Xvfb): use scripts/termtest.py (TermTest, 100x35 tmux). Assert each launcher item now spans 5 rows, dim '─' rule on the card's LAST row, '┼' divider on that row, selected '▌' on every highlighted line — identical to a link-list capture. Regression guards: scripts/test_sfhy_default_focus.py, scripts/test_reader_statusbar_live.py. Re-verify scroll-by-row + digit-jump on a small terminal (fewer VisibleRows). Compact(3)/List(1) variants unchanged.
close_reason: Launcher GridCardHeight 4->5 to match link-list; separator moved to last line; interior padding row keeps selBg+bar. Build + 113 launcher tests green (updated stride test).

title: Font weight looks too heavy across several elements — align with the design system
description: The font across several UI elements looks wrong — too heavy (over-bold). Action: review the design system and align element font weights/families with it so they match the intended spec. Identify which elements deviate (likely weight, possibly family/fallback) and bring them into line with the design system. Reported by user 2026-06-18.
close_reason: GetFontWeight now always returns Normal; bold/dim are colour-only per design system. Build clean, 113 launcher + renderer tests green.

title: Reader view: underline renders as flashing highlight, wrong colour, and recolours the text
description:
In reader view the underline is broken on multiple counts:
1. It renders as a FLASHING HIGHLIGHT instead of a simple static underline.
2. The underline colour differs from the paragraph text colour — they should match.
3. The text itself changes colour when underlined — it should stay the same colour.

Expected: a simple, static underline in the same colour as the paragraph text, with the underlined text keeping its original colour. Reported by user 2026-06-18.
close_reason: Cursor line now uses plain SGR4 underline (matches text colour), text not recoloured, and search-highlight reset no longer breaks the underline. Build + RenderHelpers/Article tests green.

title: Link-list borders inconsistent — border colour appears to change underneath stories
description: In the link-list view, the story borders render inconsistently: the border colour seems to change underneath the stories (varies row-to-row / behind story rows). Expected: uniform, consistent border colour across all link-list rows. Reported by user 2026-06-18.
close_reason: Added {Dim} to the link-list inter-column divider so it matches the dim horizontal card rules. Build + LinkTree tests green.

title: Bookmark reconciler crashes on duplicate URLs in DB -> silent empty launcher
description: BookmarkReconciler.ReconcileAsync (BookmarkReconciler.cs:76) does _repository.GetAllAsync().ToDictionary(b => b.Url, ...). If the Bookmarks table contains two rows with the same URL, ToDictionary throws 'An item with the same key has already been added', the reconcile fails ('Failed to refresh bookmarks'), and the launcher renders the EMPTY state ('Your bookmarks await') even though the DB has bookmarks — silent bookmark loss. Found while seeding test bookmarks (a dev DB had dup URLs from earlier input). Fix: dedup by URL (keep first / last-wins) when building the dictionary, and/or add a UNIQUE constraint + dedup migration on the bookmark URL column. Low-risk defensive fix; prevents a confusing empty launcher.
close_reason: Fixed: BookmarkReconciler now builds dbByUrl defensively (TryAdd, keep first/lowest-sorted) and DELETEs duplicate-URL rows so reconcile no longer throws and the launcher stops falling back to empty. Self-healing. Regression test Reconcile_DuplicateUrlsInDb_DoesNotThrow_AndDedupsToOneRow added; 8/8 reconciler tests pass. Commit pending push.

title: Recurring: /workspace build outputs balloon disk (CEF redist ×N + Debug+Release dupes)
description: The working tree periodically balloons (7.6G observed 2026-06-17, user-reported ~9G). Root cause: regenerable build artifacts, dominated by (1) the CefGlue redist libcef.so (~200MB) staged into BOTH the Release output root AND CefGlueBrowserProcess/ per build, plus the Playwright/Chromium driver content (~113MB) copied into every project bin; (2) full Debug build trees kept alongside Release (Shell/API/Infrastructure bin/Debug ≈1.8G) even though only Release is used here. These are .gitignored (not committed) so it is purely local-disk bloat. Mitigations to consider: a 'make clean'/dotnet clean helper, periodic pruning of bin/Debug + tests/bin when idle, or skipping the per-build CEF root-stage on dev Debug builds. Filed at user request (recurring).
notes: 2026-06-17 UPDATE: removing CefGlue (ADR-4) deletes the ~200MB libcef.so x2 from every Shell build output — the single largest offender is gone. Remaining recurring pattern is Debug+Release output duplication + the Playwright driver per project.
close_reason: Verified. scripts/clean.sh (committed 39e18223) reclaims all bin/obj across src/tests/third_party — freed ~2.4GB (API bin 870M, Infrastructure 385M, Shell 278M, Tests 415M, etc.). Ran ./scripts/clean.sh then dotnet build WireCopy.sln -c Release from clean = 'Build succeeded' (restores cleanly; Playwright driver re-copies on build). The dominant historical offender (CEF libcef.so x2 ~200MB) is already gone post-ADR-4. Recurring hygiene handled by the script.

title: Vendor/fork Iciclecreek.Avalonia.Terminal to unlock select-to-copy/paste
description: ADR-2 documented this fork trigger: Iciclecreek.Avalonia.Terminal 1.0.1 seals the API needed for two terminal-fidelity items. (1) Copy/paste (workspace-q7yf): no public SelectedText/Copy/Clipboard surface and the pointer-selection model is internal, so OS clipboard copy from a mouse selection can't be wired without forking. (2) Scrollback depth (workspace-qd5q): TerminalView.MaxScrollback is a READ-ONLY computed property (XTerm.NET Buffer.Length − Rows); the underlying XTerm.NET Terminal is constructed internally with no exposed buffer-size config. Fork from upstream source (NOT the decompiled output), add SelectedText extraction + IClipboard copy/paste (Cmd on macOS, Ctrl/Shift on Linux) + a configurable scrollback, and re-run the VT-parity gate (ceug.18) to confirm no rendering regression. Low blast radius (dependency isolated to the Shell).
acceptance_criteria: Iciclecreek vendored into the repo; mouse-select copies to the OS clipboard and paste injects into the PTY on macOS+Linux; scrollback depth is configurable and generous; VT-parity gate still passes.
notes: Scope narrowed to COPY/PASTE only — scrollback was solved via the writable BufferSize property (workspace-qd5q, no fork). Remaining: the widget exposes no SelectedText/selection-range/Copy API and owns the pointer-selection model, so OS-clipboard copy from a mouse selection needs a fork (paste-injection might be possible via the exposed Terminal object, but copy needs the selection range). Note: the app also has its own in-UI content-copy features, so this is lower-urgency.
close_reason: VENDORED+FORKED Iciclecreek.Avalonia.Terminal 1.0.1 (MIT, commit 0f7430f) into third_party/Iciclecreek.Avalonia.Terminal/ (LICENSE + PROVENANCE.md); Shell swapped PackageReference->ProjectReference. Added the sealed copy/paste API to TerminalView.cs: buffer-absolute mouse text-selection (Shift forces local select when the app holds the mouse, else plain drag), SelectedText/HasSelection/ClearSelection, translucent SelectionBrush overlay (RenderSelection), CopySelectionAsync/PasteAsync vs TopLevel.Clipboard, OnKeyDown chords Cmd+C/V (mac) vs Ctrl+Shift+C/V (else, leaving plain Ctrl+C as the interrupt), bracketed-paste wrap. Scrollback stays configurable via BufferSize (preserved). VERIFIED: solution build 0/0; full unit suite 4116 passed / 0 failed / 39 skipped INCL 11 new TerminalSelectionTests (extraction single/multi-line/trailing-trim/partial-col/order-independent/empty + FormatPasteData CRLF&LF->CR + bracketed wrap) against a REAL XTerm.NET buffer; VT-parity gate ceug.18 (scripts/test_ceug_shell_live.py) PASS 23176px banner == pre-fork baseline; live gate scripts/test_terminal_copypaste_live.py PASS. Mods confined to TerminalView.cs + Assembly.cs (InternalsVisibleTo), all marked '// WireCopy:'.

title: Restore wizard answer-by-click + in-view manual login on the embedded browser
description: Restore wizard answer-by-click + in-view manual login for the image-mirror web pane (ADR-4). The pane is a pixel mirror, so an in-pane click must be forwarded to the app's Chromium page (map pane coords -> page coords -> dispatch click). Manual login completes in the app's own Chromium and mirrors. Depends on a forward-input path (overlaps workspace-954p input mapping).
acceptance_criteria: Click-to-answer selects a wizard option from the embedded page; manual login completes in-view via pushed cookies; verified.
close_reason: DONE + verified end-to-end. Added web-pane input forwarding: EvtInput/InputPayload protocol; Shell maps pointer->page-document px (Uniform/centred reverse map) + forwards keys/text; DisplayInputHostedService replays via IBrowserSession.Dispatch{Click,Key,Text}Async (click scrolls+clicks; key/text to focused el). Restores wizard answer-by-click + in-view manual-login typing. Verified: test_e8jf_input_live.py (click->green 0->95k) + test_e8jf_keyboard_live.py (click-focus+type->green 0->290k) under Xvfb; unit DisplayInputHostedServiceTests; suite 4128/0.

title: Render the web pane at a ~414px mobile/phone viewport
description: The displayed page should render at a ~414px phone viewport so pages look phone-shaped in the web pane (matches the old LensViewportWidth=414). In the image-mirror architecture (ADR-4) this means setting the viewport/device-scale of the Chromium page the API mirrors, so the captured frame is portrait phone-shaped. Verify by screenshotting a real page in the pane.
acceptance_criteria: A real page (e.g. demo/site or a news site) lays out phone-shaped in the web pane like the previous dock; verified by screenshot.
close_reason: DONE + verified. Set the headless mirror context's LAUNCH viewport to phone-shaped portrait (BrowserConfiguration.MirrorViewportWidth/Height default 414x896) at LaunchPersistentContextAsync — reliable, unlike the earlier reverted SetViewportSizeAsync-on-persistent-context attempt that rendered landscape. Verified under Xvfb: web pane now renders portrait/phone-shaped (screenshot /tmp/webpane_mirror.png), test_webpane_mirror_live.py PASS, test_rxrk_revisit_live.py PASS (no rxrk regression), full unit suite 4121/0. Only one line depended on the old 1400x900.

title: Wire DisplaySpotlightService.FollowAsync to the live TUI selection against CEF
description: Wire DisplaySpotlightService.FollowAsync (terminal selection -> scroll+highlight the matching element) to the image-mirror web pane (ADR-4): the app runs the spotlight script in its OWN Chromium (where it already drives the page); the next mirrored frame then shows the highlight in the pane. No CEF. Verify: moving the terminal selection scrolls/blue-boxes the element in the pane under Xvfb.
acceptance_criteria: Moving the terminal selection scrolls the embedded CEF view to and blue-box-highlights the corresponding element; verified against a real snapshot under Xvfb.
close_reason: DONE + verified end-to-end. Spotlight now runs in the API's OWN Chromium page (IBrowserSession.EvaluateForDisplayAsync) not the dead Shell channel path; wired into BrowserOrchestrator.NotifyPreloadSelectionChanged (Hierarchical follow + clear elsewhere). Verified: unit + real-Chromium integration tests; new live gate scripts/test_954p_spotlight_live.py shows the blue highlight in the web pane following the selection down (y 348->448); suite 4123/0.

title: Confirm TUI ANSI theme drives PTY colors (not the host Gainsboro default)
description: MainWindow hosts TerminalView with Foreground=Gainsboro/Background=Black, but the standalone experience is bright phosphor green-on-black with magenta titles / cyan selection. Confirm per-cell SGR from the WireCopy.API TUI fully drives the rendered palette (host defaults only show before first paint). Also covers the COLORTERM 256-vs-16 path (now seeded in y8t7) and Ctrl+P theme cycling (phosphor/amber/Dracula).
acceptance_criteria: Documented screenshot-diff confirmation that the rendered palette matches the standalone TUI (green primary, magenta header, cyan selection) at the default theme and after Ctrl+P cycling; 256-color confirmed active.
close_reason: CONFIRMED (no code change needed). Screenshot /tmp/shell_mode_default.png shows the TUI's per-cell SGR fully drives the palette — 256-color pink banner, phosphor green primary, cyan accents — overriding the host Gainsboro/Black defaults. 256-color guaranteed by the COLORTERM seed (workspace-y8t7). Matches the standalone phosphor experience.

title: Enforce minimum terminal size (column 0 MinWidth + Window MinWidth/MinHeight)
description: The 3*:2* split gives the TUI ~97 cols with no enforced minimum; dragging the GridSplitter toward the web pane OR shrinking the whole window can drop the TUI below 80 cols and re-trigger the login/2FA overflow paths (workspace-0b9s). Set a MinWidth on Grid column 0 (and/or a minimum splitter position) AND Window.MinWidth/MinHeight so the TUI stays >=80 cols at FontSize 13.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging the splitter fully toward the web pane and resizing the whole window to its minimum both keep the TUI at >=80 cols; verified under Xvfb.
close_reason: DONE (commit 0483876b). Window MinWidth/MinHeight + Grid column 0 MinWidth (~640px = 80 cols of DejaVu Sans Mono @13) keep the TUI >=80 cols under window/splitter resize. Builds 0/0; default launch clean.

Add a live e2e gate (Xvfb + import screenshots, like scripts/test_*_live.py) that launches the Shell, drives the TUI, and asserts the web view shows a snapshot with working spotlight.

title: Release CI matrix (build/sign/notarize/publish)
description: No build/test/release CI exists today (only claude-*.yml). Add a tag-triggered GitHub Actions matrix that builds the Shell per RID, runs tests, signs/notarizes, and publishes a GitHub Release with SHA256SUMS. Foundation for the one-line installer / package-manager channels from the install-onboarding analysis.
notes: CORE DONE + VERIFIED this session: .github/workflows/ci.yml (build+test) + release.yml (mac+linux matrix via package.sh) — valid YAML, commands mirror verified flow. Stays in_progress: bd-blocked by .14 (which is bd-blocked by .13).
